books.title: Bathing (Ghusl)
books.intro:
The statement of Allah Most High "O you who have believed, when you rise to [perform] prayer, wash your faces and your forearms to the elbows and wipe over your heads and wash your feet to the ankles. And if you are in a state of janabah, then purify yourselves. But if you are ill or on a journey or one of you comes from the place of relieving himself or you have contacted women and do not find water, then seek clean earth and wipe over your faces and hands with it. Allah does not intend to make difficulty for you, but He intends to purify you and complete His favor upon you that you may be grateful."
And His statement: "O you who have believed, do not approach prayer while you are intoxicated until you know what you are saying or in a state of janabah, except those passing through [a place of prayer], until you have washed [your whole body]. And if you are ill or on a journey or one of you comes from the place of relieving himself or you have contacted women and find no water, then seek clean earth and wipe over your faces and your hands [with it]. Indeed, Allah is ever Pardoning and Forgiving."

books.title: Menstrual Periods
books.intro: The statement of Allah Most High "And they ask you about menstruation. Say, 'It is harm, so keep away from wives during menstruation. And do not approach them until they are pure. And when they have purified themselves, then come to them from where Allah has ordained for you. Indeed, Allah loves those who are constantly repentant and loves those who purify themselves.'"

books.title: Rubbing hands and feet with dust (Tayammum)
books.intro: The saying of Allah: "But if you ... cannot find water, then purify yourselves with clean earth by wiping your faces and hands."

books.intro: The words 'Ar-Rahman', 'Ar-Rahim' (i.e., the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful) are two words derived from 'Ar-Rahma' (i.e., the mercy). And the words 'Ar-Rahim' and 'Ar-Raahim' have one meaning as the words 'Al-Alim' and 'Al- 'Aleem' have one and the same meaning (i.e., the Cognizant One).

books.title: Invocations

chapters.title: How the Divine Revelation started being revealed to Allah's Messenger
chapters.intro: And the Statement of Allah جَلَّ ذِكْرُهُ: "Verily, We have sent the revelation to you (O Muhammad ﷺ) as We sent the revelation to Noah and the Prophets after him." (V.<qref chapter="4" verses="163">4:163</qref>)

chapters.title: The statement of the Prophet (saws) "Islam is based on five principles"
chapters.intro:
And belief is both saying and acting, and it increases and decreases. Allah تعالى revealed the following Verses concerning the subject: "That they may grow more in faith along with their (present) faith." (V.48:4) "And We increased them in guidance." (V.18:13) "And Allah increases in guidance those who walk aright [true believers in the Oneness of Allah - who fear Allah much (abstain from all kinds of sins and evil deeds which He has forbidden) and love Allah much (perform all kinds of good deeds which He has ordained)]" (V.19:76). And said, "As for those who accept guidance, He (i.e. Allah جل جلاله) increases their guidance and bestows on them their piety." (V.47:17) "And the believers may increase in faith." (V.74:31) "Which of you has had his faith increased by it? As for those who believe, it has increased their faith." (V.9:124) And, also the Statement of Allah تعالى: "Fear them. But it (only) increased them in faith.'" (V.3:173) And also the Statement of Allah جل جلاله: "And it only added to their faith and to their submissiveness (to Allah)." (V.33:22).

And to love and hate for Allah's sake is a part of faith.

'Umar bin 'Abdul 'Aziz wrote to 'Adi bin 'Adi: "Belief includes Fara'id (enjoined duties), legal laws and Hudud (Allah's boundary limits between lawful and unlawful things) and Sunan [legal ways and deeds (acts) of worship etc.]. And whoever follows (and acts on) all of them completely, has a complete belief, and whoever does not follow them completely (does not act on them), his belief is incomplete. And should I live I will tell you all about them so that you may act on them. And should I die, I am not anxious to have your company."

And the Prophet Ibrahim (Abraham) عليه السلام‎ said, "But to be stronger in faith" (V.2:260). Mu'adh said (to Aswad bin Hilal, one of his companions), "Let us sit for a while so that we may dedicate that period of time to faith." lbn Mas'ud said, "Yaqin is perfect faith." And Ibn 'Umar said, "A person cannot attain true sense of piety unless and until he removes all suspicions from his heart." (i.e. gives up all kinds of polytheism, evil deeds, and doubtful things, and start doing righteous good deeds regularly) [See Fath Al-Bari, Vol. I, Page 54].

And Mujahid said, "He (Allah تعالى) has ordained for you…” (V.42:13) means "0 Muhammad (ﷺ)! We have ordained for you and him (Noah) one religion (i.e. Islamic Monotheism).”

And Ibn 'Abbas explained: "A law and a clear way" (V.5:48) as Islamic way and Sunna (traditions of the Prophet ﷺ).
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ الإِيمَانِ وَقَوْلِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «بُنِيَ الإِسْلاَمُ عَلَى خَمْسٍ»
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَهُوَ قَوْلٌ وَفِعْلٌ، وَيَزِيدُ وَيَنْقُصُ. قَالَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى: {لِيَزْدَادُوا إِيمَانًا مَعَ إِيمَانِهِمْ}، {وَزِدْنَاهُمْ هُدًى}، {وَيَزِيدُ اللَّهُ الَّذِينَ اهْتَدَوْا هُدًى}، {وَالَّذِينَ اهْتَدَوْا زَادَهُمْ هُدًى وَآتَاهُمْ تَقْوَاهُمْ}، {وَيَزْدَادَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِيمَانًا}، وَقَوْلُهُ: {أَيُّكُمْ زَادَتْهُ هَذِهِ إِيمَانًا فَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا فَزَادَتْهُمْ إِيمَانًا}. وَقَوْلُهُ جَلَّ ذِكْرُهُ: {فَاخْشَوْهُمْ فَزَادَهُمْ إِيمَانًا}. وَقَوْلُهُ تَعَالَى: {وَمَا زَادَهُمْ إِلاَّ إِيمَانًا وَتَسْلِيمًا}.
وَالْحُبُّ فِي اللَّهِ وَالْبُغْضُ فِي اللَّهِ مِنَ الإِيمَانِ.
وَكَتَبَ عُمَرُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ إِلَى عَدِيِّ بْنِ عَدِيٍّ إِنَّ لِلإِيمَانِ فَرَائِضَ وَشَرَائِعَ وَحُدُودًا وَسُنَنًا، فَمَنِ اسْتَكْمَلَهَا اسْتَكْمَلَ الإِيمَانَ، وَمَنْ لَمْ يَسْتَكْمِلْهَا لَمْ يَسْتَكْمِلِ الإِيمَانَ، فَإِنْ أَعِشْ فَسَأُبَيِّنُهَا لَكُمْ حَتَّى تَعْمَلُوا بِهَا، وَإِنْ أَمُتْ فَمَا أَنَا عَلَى صُحْبَتِكُمْ بِحَرِيصٍ.
وَقَالَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ: {وَلَكِنْ لِيَطْمَئِنَّ قَلْبِي}.
وَقَالَ مُعَاذٌ: اجْلِسْ بِنَا نُؤْمِنْ سَاعَةً.
وَقَالَ ابْنُ مَسْعُودٍ: الْيَقِينُ الإِيمَانُ كُلُّهُ.
وَقَالَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ: لاَ يَبْلُغُ الْعَبْدُ حَقِيقَةَ التَّقْوَى حَتَّى يَدَعَ مَا حَاكَ فِي الصَّدْرِ.
وَقَالَ مُجَاهِدٌ: {شَرَعَ لَكُمْ} أَوْصَيْنَاكَ يَا مُحَمَّدُ وَإِيَّاهُ دِينًا وَاحِدًا.
وَقَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ: {شِرْعَةً وَمِنْهَاجًا} سَبِيلاً وَسُنَّةً.

chapters.title: Your invocation means your faith
chapters.intro: Your invocation means your faith. And Allah تعالى said: "Say (O Muhammad ﷺ to the disbelievers): My Lord pays attention to you only because of your invocation to Him." (V.<qref chapter="25" verses="77">25:77</qref>).
chapters.title_arabic: باب دُعَاؤُكُمْ إِيمَانُكُمْ
chapters.intro_arabic: لِقَوْلِهِ تَعَالى: {قُلْ مَا يَعْبَؤُا بِكُمْ رَبِّ لَوْلَا دُعَاؤُكُمْ} [<qref chapter="25" verses="77">الفرقان: ٧٧</qref>] وَمَعْنَى الدُّعاءِ في اللُّغَةِ (الإيمان)

chapters.title: (What is said) regarding the deeds of faith
chapters.intro: And the Saying of Allah تعالى: "It is not Al-Birr (piety, righteousness and every act of obedience to Allah) that you turn your faces to east and (or) west (in prayers); but Al-Birr is (the quality of) the one who believes in Allah, the Last Day, the Angels, the Book (Holy Scripture), the Prophets and gives his wealth, in spite of the love for it, to the kinsfolk and to the orphans and to Al Masakin (the poor) and to the wayfarer and to those who ask, and to set slaves free; and perform As-Salat (Iqamat-as-Salat) and gives the Zakat , and who fulfil their covenant when they make it, and who are patient (in severe poverty), and ailment (disease) and at the time of fighting (during the battles). Such are the people of truth, and they are Al Muttaqun." (V.<qref chapter="2" verses="177">2:177</qref>) "Successful indeed are the believers." (V.<qref chapter="23" verses="1">23:1</qref>)
chapters.title_arabic: باب أُمُورِ الإِيمَانِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {لَيْسَ الْبِرَّ أَنْ تُوَلُّوا وُجُوهَكُمْ قِبَلَ الْمَشْرِقِ وَالْمَغْرِبِ وَلَكِنَّ الْبِرَّ مَنْ آمَنَ بِاللَّهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الآخِرِ وَالْمَلاَئِكَةِ وَالْكِتَابِ وَالنَّبِيِّينَ وَآتَى الْمَالَ عَلَى حُبِّهِ ذَوِي الْقُرْبَى وَالْيَتَامَى وَالْمَسَاكِينَ وَابْنَ السَّبِيلِ وَالسَّائِلِينَ وَفِي الرِّقَابِ وَأَقَامَ الصَّلاَةَ وَآتَى الزَّكَاةَ وَالْمُوفُونَ بِعَهْدِهِمْ إِذَا عَاهَدُوا وَالصَّابِرِينَ فِي الْبَأْسَاءِ وَالضَّرَّاءِ وَحِينَ الْبَأْسِ أُولَئِكَ الَّذِينَ صَدَقُوا وَأُولَئِكَ هُمُ الْمُتَّقُونَ}. وَقَوْلِهِ: {قَدْ أَفْلَحَ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ} الآيَةَ.

chapters.title: A Muslim is the one who avoids harming Muslims with his tongue and hands
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْمُسْلِمُ مَنْ سَلِمَ الْمُسْلِمُونَ مِنْ لِسَانِهِ وَيَدِهِ

chapters.title: Whose Islam is the best (Who is the best Muslim)?
chapters.title_arabic: باب أَىُّ الإِسْلاَمِ أَفْضَلُ

chapters.title: To feed (others) is a part of Islam
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِطْعَامُ الطَّعَامِ مِنَ الإِسْلاَمِ

chapters.title: To like for one's (Muslim's) brother what one likes for himself is a part of faith
chapters.title_arabic: باب مِنَ الإِيمَانِ أَنْ يُحِبَّ لأَخِيهِ مَا يُحِبُّ لِنَفْسِهِ

chapters.title: To love the Messenger (Muhammad saws) is a part of faith
chapters.title_arabic: باب حُبُّ الرَّسُولِ صلى الله عليه وسلم مِنَ الإِيمَانِ

chapters.title: Sweetness (delight) of faith

chapters.title: To love the Ansar is a sign of faith
chapters.title_arabic: باب عَلاَمَةُ الإِيمَانِ حُبُّ الأَنْصَارِ

chapters.title: Chapter:
chapters.title_arabic: باب

chapters.title: To flee (run away) from Al-Fitn (afflictions and trials), is a part of religion
chapters.title_arabic: باب مِنَ الدِّينِ الْفِرَارُ مِنَ الْفِتَنِ

chapters.title: The statement of the Prophet (saws): "I know Allah Ta'ala better, than all of you do."
chapters.intro: And knowledge is the act of the heart as it is referred to by the Statement of Allah (جل جلاله), "But He will call you to account for that which your hearts have earned." (V.<qref chapter="2" verses="225">2:225</qref>)
chapters.title_arabic: باب قَوْلِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم ‏«أَنَا أَعْلَمُكُمْ بِاللَّهِ»
chapters.intro_arabic: وَأَنَّ الْمَعْرِفَةَ فِعْلُ الْقَلْبِ لِقَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {وَلَكِنْ يُؤَاخِذُكُمْ بِمَا كَسَبَتْ قُلُوبُكُمْ}

chapters.title: Whoever hates to revert to Kufr (atheism of disbelief) as he hates to be thrown in fire, is a part of faith
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ كَرِهَ أَنْ يَعُودَ فِي الْكُفْرِ كَمَا يَكْرَهُ أَنْ يُلْقَى فِي النَّارِ مِنَ الإِيمَانِ

chapters.title: The grades in superiority of the believers will be according to their good deeds
chapters.title_arabic: باب تَفَاضُلِ أَهْلِ الإِيمَانِ فِي الأَعْمَالِ

chapters.title: Al-Haya (self-respect, modesty bashfulness, honour etc.) is a part of faith
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْحَيَاءُ مِنَ الإِيمَانِ

chapters.title: (The Statement of Allah Jalla Jalalahu) "But if they repent [by rejecting Shirk (polytheism) and accept Islamic Monotheism] and perform As-Salat (Iqamat-as-Salat) and give Zakat then leave their way free."
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ: {فَإِنْ تَابُوا وَأَقَامُوا الصَّلاَةَ وَآتَوُا الزَّكَاةَ فَخَلُّوا سَبِيلَهُمْ}

chapters.title: Whoever says that faith is action (good deeds)
chapters.intro:
Referring to the Statement of Allah تعالى): "And this is the Paradise which you have been made to inherit because of your deeds which you used to do (in the life of the world)", (V.43:72) a number of religious learned men explained the Verse (Statement of Allah تعالى "So by your Lord (O Muhammad ﷺ) We shall certainly call all of them to account for all that they used to do” (V.15:92,93). And the Statement: La ilaha illallah (none has the right to be worshipped but Allah.)

And Allah said, "For the like of this let the workers work." (V.37:61)
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ قَالَ إِنَّ الإِيمَانَ هُوَ الْعَمَلُ
chapters.intro_arabic:
لِقَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {وَتِلْكَ الْجَنَّةُ الَّتِي أُورِثْتُمُوهَا بِمَا كُنْتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ}.
وَقَالَ عِدَّةٌ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْعِلْمِ فِي قَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى: {فَوَرَبِّكَ لَنَسْأَلَنَّهُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ عَمَّا كَانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ} عَنْ قَوْلِ لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ.
وَقَالَ: {لِمِثْلِ هَذَا فَلْيَعْمَلِ الْعَامِلُونَ}.

chapters.title: If one does not embrace Islam truly but does so by compulsion or for fear of being killed (then that man is not a believer)
chapters.intro:
According to the Statement of Allah تعالى: The bedouins say, 'We believe,' Say (O Muhammad ﷺ), 'You believe not but you only say we have surrendered (in Islam).'” (V.49:14)

And if they had embraced Islam truly (sincerely) their Islam would have been as is referred to in the Statement of Allah جل جلاله: "Truly, the religion with Allah is Islam." (V.3:19). "And whoever seeks a religion other than Islam, it will never be accepted of him, and in the Hereafter he will be one of the losers.” (V.3:85).
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ إِذَا لَمْ يَكُنِ الإِسْلاَمُ عَلَى الْحَقِيقَةِ وَكَانَ عَلَى الاِسْتِسْلاَمِ أَوِ الْخَوْفِ مِنَ الْقَتْلِ
chapters.intro_arabic: لِقَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى: {قَالَتِ الأَعْرَابُ آمَنَّا قُلْ لَمْ تُؤْمِنُوا وَلَكِنْ قُولُوا أَسْلَمْنَا}. فَإِذَا كَانَ عَلَى الْحَقِيقَةِ فَهُوَ عَلَى قَوْلِهِ جَلَّ ذِكْرُهُ: {إِنَّ الدِّينَ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ الإِسْلاَمُ}، {وَمَنْ يَبْتَغِ غَيْرَ الإِسْلاَمِ دِينًا فَلَنْ يُقْبَلَ مِنْهُ}

chapters.title: To greet is a part of Islam
chapters.intro:
And 'Ammar said, "Whoever acquires the following three qualities will acquire faith:
1. To treat others as one likes to be treated by others.
2. To greet everybody (known and unknown).
3. To spend (give charitable gifts) in Allah's Cause, in spite of poverty."
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِفْشَاءُ السَّلاَمِ مِنَ الإِسْلاَمِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ عَمَّارٌ: ثَلاَثٌ مَنْ جَمَعَهُنَّ فَقَدْ جَمَعَ الإِيمَانَ الإِنْصَافُ مِنْ نَفْسِكَ، وَبَذْلُ السَّلاَمِ لِلْعَالَمِ، وَالإِنْفَاقُ مِنَ الإِقْتَارِ.

chapters.title: To be ungrateful to one's husband. And disbelief is of (different grades) lesser (or greater) degrees.
chapters.intro: This is narrated by Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri رضي الله عنه on the authority of the Prophet ﷺ.
chapters.title_arabic: باب كُفْرَانِ الْعَشِيرِ وَكُفْرٍ دُونَ كُفْرٍ
chapters.intro_arabic: فِيهِ عَنْ أَبِي سَعِيدٍ الْخُدْرِيِّ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ.

chapters.title: Sins are from ignorance and a sinner is not a disbeliever unless he worships others along with Allah 'Azza wa Jall
chapters.intro: According to the statement of the Prophet ﷺ "You still have some characteristics of ignorance." And the Statement of Allah تعالى "Verily, Allah forgives not that partners should he set up with Him (in worship) but He forgives expect that (anything else) to whom He wills.” (V.<qref chapter="4" verses="48">4:48</qref>).
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْمَعَاصِي مِنْ أَمْرِ الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَلاَ يُكَفَّرُ صَاحِبُهَا بِارْتِكَابِهَا إِلاَّ بِالشِّرْكِ لِقَوْلِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «إِنَّكَ امْرُؤٌ فِيكَ جَاهِلِيَّةٌ». وَقَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {إِنَّ اللَّهَ لاَ يَغْفِرُ أَنْ يُشْرَكَ بِهِ وَيَغْفِرُ مَا دُونَ ذَلِكَ لِمَنْ يَشَاءُ}.

chapters.title: "And if two parties (or groups) from among the believers fall to fighting, then make peace between them both…" Allah has called them "believers"
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ: {وَإِنْ طَائِفَتَانِ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ اقْتَتَلُوا فَأَصْلِحُوا بَيْنَهُمَا} فَسَمَّاهُمُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ

chapters.title: Dhulm (wrong) of one kind can be greater or lesser than that of another
chapters.title_arabic: باب ظُلْمٌ دُونَ ظُلْمٍ

chapters.title: The signs of a hypocrite
chapters.title_arabic: باب عَلاَمَةِ الْمُنَافِقِ

chapters.title: To establish the (Nawafil - voluntary) prayers on the night of Qadr is a part of faith
chapters.title_arabic: باب قِيَامُ لَيْلَةِ الْقَدْرِ مِنَ الإِيمَانِ

chapters.title: Al-Jihad (fighting in Allah's Cause) is a part of faith
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْجِهَادُ مِنَ الإِيمَانِ

chapters.title: It is a part of faith to establish the (Nawafil — voluntary) prayers during the nights of Ramadan
chapters.title_arabic: باب تَطَوُّعُ قِيَامِ رَمَضَانَ مِنَ الإِيمَانِ

chapters.title: To observe fasting (according to Islamic teachings)] during the month of Ramadan (sincerely and faithfully) hoping for Allah's Reward only, is a part of faith
chapters.title_arabic: باب صَوْمُ رَمَضَانَ احْتِسَابًا مِنَ الإِيمَانِ

chapters.title: Religion is very easy
chapters.intro: And the statement of the Prophet ﷺ, "The most beloved religion to Allah جل جلاله is the tolerant Hanifiya [Islamic Monotheism i.e., to believe in the Oneness of Allah and to worship Him (Allah) Alone and not to worship anything else along with Him]."
chapters.title_arabic: باب الدِّينُ يُسْرٌ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَوْلُ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «أَحَبُّ الدِّينِ إِلَى اللَّهِ الْحَنِيفِيَّةُ السَّمْحَةُ».

chapters.title: The (offering of) Salat (prayers) is a part of faith
chapters.intro: And the Statement of Allah تعالى: "And Allah would never make your faith (prayers) to be lost." (V.<qref chapter="2" verses="143">2:143</qref>) (i.e. your prayers which you offered in the past facing the Bait-ul-Maqdis (Jerusalem).
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّلاَةُ مِنَ الإِيمَانِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَوْلُ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {وَمَا كَانَ اللَّهُ لِيُضِيعَ إِيمَانَكُمْ} يَعْنِي صَلاَتَكُمْ عِنْدَ الْبَيْتِ.

chapters.title: (What is said regarding the superiority of) a person who embraces Islam sincerely.
chapters.title_arabic: باب حُسْنِ إِسْلاَمِ الْمَرْءِ

chapters.title: Ad-Din (good, righteous deed - act of worship) loved most by Allah Jalla Jalalahu is that which is done regularly. (And in fact the best religion with Allah is Islam)
chapters.title_arabic: باب أَحَبُّ الدِّينِ إِلَى اللَّهِ أَدْوَمُهُ

chapters.title: Faith increases and decreases
chapters.intro:
And the Statements of Allah جل جلاله, "We increased them in guidance." (V.18:13) "And the believers may increase in faith." (V.74:31) — And Allah تعالى said, "This day I have perfected your religion for you," (V.5:3)

(The last Statement indicates that) if somebody leaves a part of (from) the perfection of the religion then his religion is incomplete.
chapters.title_arabic: باب زِيَادَةِ الإِيمَانِ وَنُقْصَانِهِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {وَزِدْنَاهُمْ هُدًى}، {وَيَزْدَادَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِيمَانًا} وَقَالَ: {الْيَوْمَ أَكْمَلْتُ لَكُمْ دِينَكُمْ} فَإِذَا تَرَكَ شَيْئًا مِنَ الْكَمَالِ فَهُوَ نَاقِصٌ.

chapters.title: To pay Zakat is a part of Islam
chapters.intro: And the Statement of Allah جل جلاله: "And they were commanded not, but that they should worship Allah, and worship none but Him Alone (abstaining from ascribing partners to Him) and to perform As-Salat (Iqamat-as-Salat) and to give Zakat, and that is the right religion." (V.<qref chapter="98" verses="5">98:5</qref>)
chapters.title_arabic: باب الزَّكَاةُ مِنَ الإِسْلاَمِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَوْلُهُ: {وَمَا أُمِرُوا إِلاَّ لِيَعْبُدُوا اللَّهَ مُخْلِصِينَ لَهُ الدِّينَ حُنَفَاءَ وَيُقِيمُوا الصَّلاَةَ وَيُؤْتُوا الزَّكَاةَ وَذَلِكَ دِينُ الْقَيِّمَةِ}

chapters.title: To accompany the funeral processions (up to the place of burial) is a part of faith
chapters.title_arabic: باب اتِّبَاعُ الْجَنَائِزِ مِنَ الإِيمَانِ

chapters.title: (What is said regarding) the fear of a believer that his good deeds may be annulled (lost) without his knowledge
chapters.intro:
And Ibrahim At-Taimi said, "When I compare my talks with my deeds (then I find that my deeds are deficient compared with my talks), I am afraid, my deeds deny what I talk." And Ibn Abi Mulaika said, "I met thirty Companions of the Prophet ﷺ and each of them was afraid of becoming a hypocrite and none of them said that he was as strong in belief as the angel Jibril (Gabriel) or Mikael (Michael)." And Al- Hasan (Al-Basri) said, "It is only a faithful believer who dreads hypocrisy and only a hypocrite who considers himself safe (is not afraid of hypocrisy).”

And one should be afraid, not to persist in hypocrisy and disobedience of Allah (by committing sins) without repenting to Allah جل جلاله immediately, as is referred to in the Statement of Allah تعالى: "And (they) do not persist in what (wrong) they have done, while they know." (V.3:135).
chapters.title_arabic: باب خَوْفِ الْمُؤْمِنِ مِنْ أَنْ يَحْبَطَ عَمَلُهُ وَهُوَ لاَ يَشْعُرُ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَقَالَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ التَّيْمِيُّ مَا عَرَضْتُ قَوْلِي عَلَى عَمَلِي إِلاَّ خَشِيتُ أَنْ أَكُونَ مُكَذَّبًا.
وَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي مُلَيْكَةَ أَدْرَكْتُ ثَلاَثِينَ مِنْ أَصْحَابِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ كُلُّهُمْ يَخَافُ النِّفَاقَ عَلَى نَفْسِهِ، مَا مِنْهُمْ أَحَدٌ يَقُولُ إِنَّهُ عَلَى إِيمَانِ جِبْرِيلَ وَمِيكَائِيلَ، وَيُذْكَرُ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ: مَا خَافَهُ إِلاَّ مُؤْمِنٌ، وَلاَ أَمِنَهُ إِلاَّ مُنَافِقٌ. وَمَا يُحْذَرُ مِنَ الإِصْرَارِ عَلَى النِّفَاقِ وَالْعِصْيَانِ مِنْ غَيْرِ تَوْبَةٍ لِقَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {وَلَمْ يُصِرُّوا عَلَى مَا فَعَلُوا وَهُمْ يَعْلَمُونَ}.

chapters.title: The asking of (angel) Jibrll (Gabriel) from the Prophet (saws) about Iman, Islam, Ihsan and the knowledge of the Hour (Doomsday)
chapters.intro: And their explanation given to him by the Prophet ﷺ. Then the Prophet ﷺ said (to his Companions), "Jibril (Gabriel) عليه السلام came to teach you your religion." So the Prophet ﷺ regarded all that as religion. And all that which the Prophet ﷺ explained to the delegation of Abdul Qais was a part of faith. And the Statement of Allah تعالى: "And whoever seeks a religion other than Islam, it will never be accepted of him." (V.<qref chapter="3" verses="85">3:85</qref>).
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ سُؤَالِ جِبْرِيلَ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ عَنِ الإِيمَانِ وَالإِسْلاَمِ وَالإِحْسَانِ وَعِلْمِ السَّاعَةِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَبَيَانِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ لَهُ ثُمَّ قَالَ: «جَاءَ جِبْرِيلُ- عَلَيْهِ السَّلاَمُ- يُعَلِّمُكُمْ دِينَكُمْ». فَجَعَلَ ذَلِكَ كُلَّهُ دِينًا، وَمَا بَيَّنَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ لِوَفْدِ عَبْدِ الْقَيْسِ مِنَ الإِيمَانِ، وَقَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى: {وَمَنْ يَبْتَغِ غَيْرَ الإِسْلاَمِ دِينًا فَلَنْ يُقْبَلَ مِنْهُ}.

chapters.title: Chapter:
chapters.title_arabic: باب

chapters.title: The superiority of that person who leaves all doubtful (unclear) things for the sake of his religion
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ مَنِ اسْتَبْرَأَ لِدِينِهِ

chapters.title: To pay Al-Khumus (one-fifth of the war booty to be given in Allah's Cause) is a part of faith
chapters.title_arabic: باب أَدَاءُ الْخُمُسِ مِنَ الإِيمَانِ

chapters.title: What is said regarding the statement: "The reward of deeds depends upon the intention and hoping to get rewards from Allah."
chapters.intro:
And every person will get the reward according to what he has intended.

And this includes faith, ablution, As-Salat, Zakat, Hajj, As-Saum and all the Ahkam (orders) of Allah.

Allah تعالى said, "Say: Each one does (deeds) according to Shakilatihi (i.e., his way or his religion or his intentions etc.)..." (V.17:84)

And the spending of a man for his family with the intention of having a reward from Allah جل جلاله, will be regarded as alms.

And the Prophet ﷺ said, "Jihad and intentions." (meaning — Jihad — to fight for Allah's جل جلاله Cause, and when there is no call for it then at least one should have the intention to take part in Jihad).
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا جَاءَ أَنَّ الأَعْمَالَ بِالنِّيَّةِ وَالْحِسْبَةِ وَلِكُلِّ امْرِئٍ مَا نَوَى
chapters.intro_arabic:
فَدَخَلَ فِيهِ الإِيمَانُ وَالْوُضُوءُ وَالصَّلاَةُ وَالزَّكَاةُ وَالْحَجُّ وَالصَّوْمُ وَالأَحْكَامُ.
وَقَالَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى: {قُلْ كُلٌّ يَعْمَلُ عَلَى شَاكِلَتِهِ} عَلَى نِيَّتِهِ.
«نَفَقَةُ الرَّجُلِ عَلَى أَهْلِهِ يَحْتَسِبُهَا صَدَقَةٌ».
وَقَالَ: «وَلَكِنْ جِهَادٌ وَنِيَّةٌ».

chapters.title: The statement of the Prophet (saws): Religion is An-Nasihah (to be sincere and true) to Allah, to His Messenger (Muhammad (saws)), to the Muslim rulers, and to all the Muslims
chapters.intro: And the Statement of Allah تعالى: "If they are sincere (in duty) to Allah and His Messenger (Muhammad ﷺ)." (V.<qref chapter="9" verses="91">9:91</qref>)
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «الدِّينُ النَّصِيحَةُ لِلَّهِ وَلِرَسُولِهِ وَلأَئِمَّةِ الْمُسْلِمِينَ وَعَامَّتِهِمْ»
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى: {إِذَا نَصَحُوا لِلَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ}

chapters.title: The superiority of knowledge
chapters.intro: And the Statement of Allah جل جلاله: "… Allah will exalt in degree those of you who believe, and those who have been granted knowledge. And Allah is Well-Acquainted with what you do." (V.<qref chapter="58" verses="11">58:11</qref>) And the Statement of Allah تعالى: "... My Lord, increase me in knowledge." (V.<qref chapter="20" verses="114">20:114</qref>)
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ فَضْلِ الْعِلْمِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {يَرْفَعِ اللَّهُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مِنْكُمْ وَالَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْعِلْمَ دَرَجَاتٍ وَاللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرٌ}. وَقَوْلِهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ: {رَبِّ زِدْنِي عِلْمًا}.

chapters.title: Whoever is asked about knowledge while he is busy in some conversation, so he finished talking and then answered the questioner
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ سُئِلَ عِلْمًا وَهُوَ مُشْتَغِلٌ فِي حَدِيثِهِ فَأَتَمَّ الْحَدِيثَ ثُمَّ أَجَابَ السَّائِلَ

chapters.title: Whoever raises his voice in (conveying) knowledge
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ رَفَعَ صَوْتَهُ بِالْعِلْمِ

chapters.title: Concerning the variety of words used by narrators conveying different significations regarding the concept of narrating and which has importance for the Hadith scholars only
chapters.intro: Al-Humaidi said: In the opinion of Ibn ‘Uyainah the words haddatha-na, akhbara-na, and anba’-na, I heard that they had the same meaning. Ibn Mas‘ud said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ narrated (haddatha-na) to us and he was truthful, and whatever is told from him was also true. Shaqiq reported from ('an) ‘Abdullah (ibn Mas‘ud): I heard (sami‘tu) from the Prophet ﷺ such a word. Hudhaifah said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ narrated (haddatha-na) to us two hadith. Abul ‘Aliyah reported from Ibn ‘Abbas who reported from the Prophet ﷺ about what he used to report from his Lord. Anas reported from the Prophet ﷺ who reported it from his Lord. Abu Hurairah reported from the Prophet ﷺ who reported it from your Lord, the Blessed and Most High.
chapters.title_arabic: باب قَوْلِ الْمُحَدِّثِ حَدَّثَنَا أَوْ، أَخْبَرَنَا وَأَنْبَأَنَا
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَقَالَ لَنَا الْحُمَيْدِيُّ كَانَ عِنْدَ ابْنِ عُيَيْنَةَ حَدَّثَنَا وَأَخْبَرَنَا وَأَنْبَأَنَا وَسَمِعْتُ وَاحِدًا.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ مَسْعُودٍ حَدَّثَنَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ وَهُوَ الصَّادِقُ الْمَصْدُوقُ.
وَقَالَ شَقِيقٌ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ سَمِعْتُ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ كَلِمَةً.
وَقَالَ حُذَيْفَةُ حَدَّثَنَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ حَدِيثَيْنِ.
وَقَالَ أَبُو الْعَالِيَةِ عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فِيمَا يَرْوِي عَنْ رَبِّهِ.
وَقَالَ أَنَسٌ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يَرْوِيهِ عَنْ رَبِّهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ.
وَقَالَ أَبُو هُرَيْرَةَ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يَرْوِيهِ عَنْ رَبِّكُمْ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ.

chapters.title: The Imam questioning his companions in order to test their knowledge
chapters.title_arabic: باب طَرْحِ الإِمَامِ الْمَسْأَلَةَ عَلَى أَصْحَابِهِ لِيَخْتَبِرَ مَا عِنْدَهُمْ مِنَ الْعِلْمِ

chapters.title: What is said about knowledge
chapters.intro:
And the Statement of Allah تعالى: "And say: My Lord! Increase me in knowledge." (V.20:114)

To recite or read (something) and present it in front of a scholar.

Al-Hasan and al-Thauri and Malik consider such reciting permissible, and some of them have deduced (the sanction for) reciting (a hadith) before the teacher from the hadith of Dimam ibn Tha‘labah who said to the Prophet ﷺ: “Has Allah commanded you that we should say prayers?” He (the Prophet) said: “Yes.” He (Dimam) said: “So this was reciting before the Prophet ﷺ.” Dimam reported this to his people, so they considered it permissible. And Malik deduced it from documents which were read to the people, so they used to say: “So and so have made us witness (to this document).” And it is read out before the teacher, so that the reader will say: “So and so has made me to read it.”

Al-Hasan (al-Basri) reported: “There is no harm in reciting (a hadith) before the teacher.” ‘Ubaidullah ibn Musa narrated to us from Sufyan: “When one recites (a hadith) before a narrator of hadith there is no harm if he says that ‘he (the latter) narrated it to me’.” He (al-Hasan) said: “And I heard Abu ‘Asim reporting from Malik and Sufyan: ‘Reciting before the teacher is the same as the latter reciting it’.”
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا جَاءَ فِي الْعِلْمِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَقَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى: {وَقُلْ رَبِّ زِدْنِي عِلْمًا} الْقِرَاءَةُ وَالْعَرْضُ عَلَى الْمُحَدِّثِ.
وَرَأَى الْحَسَنُ وَالثَّوْرِيُّ وَمَالِكٌ الْقِرَاءَةَ جَائِزَةً، وَاحْتَجَّ بَعْضُهُمْ فِي الْقِرَاءَةِ عَلَى الْعَالِمِ بِحَدِيثِ ضِمَامِ بْنِ ثَعْلَبَةَ قَالَ لِلنَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ آللَّهُ أَمَرَكَ أَنْ نُصَلِّيَ الصَّلَوَاتِ قَالَ: «نَعَمْ».
قَالَ فَهَذِهِ قِرَاءَةٌ عَلَى النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ أَخْبَرَ ضِمَامٌ قَوْمَهُ بِذَلِكَ فَأَجَازُوهُ. وَاحْتَجَّ مَالِكٌ بِالصَّكِّ يُقْرَأُ عَلَى الْقَوْمِ فَيَقُولُونَ أَشْهَدَنَا فُلاَنٌ. وَيُقْرَأُ ذَلِكَ قِرَاءَةً عَلَيْهِمْ، وَيُقْرَأُ عَلَى الْمُقْرِئِ فَيَقُولُ الْقَارِئُ أَقْرَأَنِي فُلاَنٌ. حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ سَلاَمٍ حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ الْوَاسِطِيُّ عَنْ عَوْفٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ لاَ بَأْسَ بِالْقِرَاءَةِ عَلَى الْعَالِمِ. وَأَخْبَرَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يُوسُفَ الْفِرَبْرِيُّ وَحَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْمَاعِيلَ الْبُخَارِيُّ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عُبَيْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ مُوسَى عَنْ سُفْيَانَ قَالَ إِذَا قُرِئَ عَلَى الْمُحَدِّثِ فَلاَ بَأْسَ أَنْ يَقُولَ حَدَّثَنِي. قَالَ وَسَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَاصِمٍ يَقُولُ عَنْ مَالِكٍ وَسُفْيَانَ الْقِرَاءَةُ عَلَى الْعَالِمِ وَقِرَاءَتُهُ سَوَاءٌ.
[تحفة 18529، 18761 أ، 19246 أ].

chapters.title: What is said regarding the hand to hand exchange (of books of knowledge), and the writing of knowledge by religious scholars to different countries
chapters.intro: Anas said that ‘Uthman got the Qur’an transcribed and sent its copies to far-off places. ‘Abdullah bin ‘Umar, Yahya bin Sa‘id, and Malik consider it permissible, and some people of Hijaz supported this opinion depending on the narration of the Prophet ﷺ, when the Prophet ﷺ got some instructions written to be given to the commander of the army, and told him (the commander) not to read them till he had reached such and such place. When that commander reached that place he read out what had been written to the people and informed them of the orders of the Prophet ﷺ.
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يُذْكَرُ فِي الْمُنَاوَلَةِ وَكِتَابِ أَهْلِ الْعِلْمِ بِالْعِلْمِ إِلَى الْبُلْدَانِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَقَالَ أَنَسٌ نَسَخَ عُثْمَانُ الْمَصَاحِفَ، فَبَعَثَ بِهَا إِلَى الآفَاقِ. وَرَأَى عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ عُمَرَ وَيَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ وَمَالِكٌ ذَلِكَ جَائِزًا. وَاحْتَجَّ بَعْضُ أَهْلِ الْحِجَازِ فِي الْمُنَاوَلَةِ بِحَدِيثِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ حَيْثُ كَتَبَ لأَمِيرِ السَّرِيَّةِ كِتَابًا وَقَالَ: «لاَ تَقْرَأْهُ حَتَّى تَبْلُغَ مَكَانَ كَذَا وَكَذَا». فَلَمَّا بَلَغَ ذَلِكَ الْمَكَانَ قَرَأَهُ عَلَى النَّاسِ، وَأَخْبَرَهُمْ بِأَمْرِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ.
[تحفة 9783].

chapters.title: Whoever sat at the farther end of a gathering. And whoever found a place amongst a gathering and took his seat there
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ قَعَدَ حَيْثُ يَنْتَهِي بِهِ الْمَجْلِسُ وَمَنْ رَأَى فُرْجَةً فِي الْحَلْقَةِ فَجَلَسَ فِيهَا‏.‏

chapters.title: The Statement of the Prophet (saws): lt is probable that a person who receives a piece of information indirectly may comprehend it better than he who has heard it directly from its source
chapters.title_arabic: باب قَوْلِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم ‏"‏رُبَّ مُبَلَّغٍ أَوْعَى مِنْ سَامِعٍ ‏"‏

chapters.title: It is essential to know a thing first before saying or acting upon it.
chapters.intro:
According to the Statement of Allah تعالى: "So know (O Muhammad ﷺ) that La ilaha illallah... (none has the right to be worshipped but Allah)," (V.47:19) So Allah stated that one should acquire knowledge first. And religious scholars are the inheritors of the Prophets, i.e., they inherit knowledge. And whoever gains knowledge is lucky and gains a great thing. And whoever followed a way to seek (religious) knowledge, Allah جل جلاله will make easy for him the way to Paradise. Allah تعالى said, "... It is only those who have knowledge among His slaves that fear Allah..." (V.35:28) And Allah said, "... But none will understand them except those who have knowledge." (V.29:43). (And also Allah’s Statement): "And they will say: Had we but listened or used our intelligence, we would not have been among the dwellers of the blazing Fire." (V.67:10) And Allah تعالى also said, "... Are those who know equal to those who know not?..." (V.39:9)

And the Prophet ﷺ said, “If Allah wants to do good to a person, He makes him comprehend the religion and verily, knowledge is attained by learning.” Abu Dhar, pointing towards his neck, said, “If you put the sword on this (my neck to kill me) and then I think that, before this sword could work, I can say even one sentence which I heard from the Prophet ﷺ, I would surely say it. (Indicating that one should convey the knowledge that one has acquired to others).” And Ibn ‘Abbas said, “You should be Rabbaniyyin (religious scholars) forgiving, wise, and learned men.” And it is said that a Rabbaniy (religious scholar) is the one who starts teaching people simple subjects of knowledge before touching big (difficult) ones.
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ الْعِلْمُ قَبْلَ الْقَوْلِ وَالْعَمَلِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
لِقَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {فَاعْلَمْ أَنَّهُ لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ} فَبَدَأَ بِالْعِلْمِ، وَأَنَّ الْعُلَمَاءَ هُمْ وَرَثَةُ الأَنْبِيَاءِ- وَرَّثُوا الْعِلْمَ- مَنْ أَخَذَهُ أَخَذَ بِحَظٍّ وَافِرٍ، وَمَنْ سَلَكَ طَرِيقًا يَطْلُبُ بِهِ عِلْمًا سَهَّلَ اللَّهُ لَهُ طَرِيقًا إِلَى الْجَنَّةِ.
وَقَالَ جَلَّ ذِكْرُهُ: {إِنَّمَا يَخْشَى اللَّهَ مِنْ عِبَادِهِ الْعُلَمَاءُ} وَقَالَ: {وَمَا يَعْقِلُهَا إِلاَّ الْعَالِمُونَ}، {وَقَالُوا لَوْ كُنَّا نَسْمَعُ أَوْ نَعْقِلُ مَا كُنَّا فِي أَصْحَابِ السَّعِيرِ}.
وَقَالَ: {هَلْ يَسْتَوِي الَّذِينَ يَعْلَمُونَ وَالَّذِينَ لاَ يَعْلَمُونَ}.
وَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «مَنْ يُرِدِ اللَّهُ بِهِ خَيْرًا يُفَقِّهْهُ فِي الدِّينِ، وَإِنَّمَا الْعِلْمُ بِالتَّعَلُّمِ».
وَقَالَ أَبُو ذَرٍّ لَوْ وَضَعْتُمُ الصَّمْصَامَةَ عَلَى هَذِهِ وَأَشَارَ إِلَى قَفَاهُ- ثُمَّ ظَنَنْتُ أَنِّي أُنْفِذُ كَلِمَةً سَمِعْتُهَا مِنَ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ قَبْلَ أَنْ تُجِيزُوا عَلَيَّ لأَنْفَذْتُهَا.
وَقَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ: {كُونُوا رَبَّانِيِّينَ} حُكَمَاءَ فُقَهَاءَ. وَيُقَالُ الرَّبَّانِيُّ الَّذِي يُرَبِّي النَّاسَ بِصِغَارِ الْعِلْمِ قَبْلَ كِبَارِهِ.

chapters.title: The Prophet (saws) used to take care of the people in preaching by selecting a suitable time so that they might not run away (never made them averse or bored them with religious talk and knowledge all the time)
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا كَانَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم يَتَخَوَّلُهُمْ بِالْمَوْعِظَةِ وَالْعِلْمِ كَىْ لاَ يَنْفِرُوا

chapters.title: Whoever fixed a special day for giving (a religious talk) to the students
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ جَعَلَ لأَهْلِ الْعِلْمِ أَيَّامًا مَعْلُومَةً

chapters.title: If Allah Jalla Jalalahu wants to do good to a person, He makes him comprehend (the religion). [The understanding of the Qur'an and As-Sunna (legal ways) of the Prophet (Muhammad (saws))]
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ يُرِدِ اللَّهُ بِهِ خَيْرًا يُفَقِّهْهُ فِي الدِّينِ

chapters.title: (The superiority of) comprehending knowledge
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْفَهْمِ فِي الْعِلْمِ

chapters.title: Wish to be like the one who has knowledge and Al-Hikmah [wisdom i.e., the knowledge of the Qur'an and the Sunna (legal ways) of the Prophet (saws)]
chapters.intro: And ‘Umar رضي الله عنه said, “Everyone must acquire sound religious knowledge early before he becomes a chief.” (Abu ‘Abdullah said:) The Companions of the Prophet ﷺ had studied in spite of the fact that they were old in age.
chapters.title_arabic: باب الاِغْتِبَاطِ فِي الْعِلْمِ وَالْحِكْمَةِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَقَالَ عُمَرُ تَفَقَّهُوا قَبْلَ أَنْ تُسَوَّدُوا.
قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ : وَبَعْدَ أَنْ تُسَوَّدُوا وَقَدْ تَعَلَّمَ أَصْحَابُ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فِي كِبَرِ سِنِّهِمْ

chapters.title: What has been said about the journey of Prophet Musa ('alaihi as-salam) (when he went) in the sea to meet Al-Khidr
chapters.intro: And the Statement of Allah: “…May I follow you so that you teach me” (V.<qref chapter="18" verses="66">18:66</qref>)
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا ذُكِرَ فِي ذَهَابِ مُوسَى صلى الله عليه وسلم فِي الْبَحْرِ إِلَى الْخَضِرِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى: {هَلْ أَتَّبِعُكَ عَلَى أَنْ تُعَلِّمَنِي مِمَّا عُلِّمْتَ رُشْدًا}.

chapters.title: The statement of the Prophet (saws): "O Allah! Bestow on him (Ibn Abbas) the knowledge of the Book (the Qur'an)
chapters.title_arabic: باب قَوْلِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم ‏"‏ اللَّهُمَّ عَلِّمْهُ الْكِتَابَ ‏"‏

chapters.title: At what age may a youth be listened to (i.e. quotation of the Hadith from a boy be acceptable)
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَتَى يَصِحُّ سَمَاعُ الصَّغِيرِ

chapters.title: To go out in search of knowledge
chapters.intro: And Jabir bin ‘Abdullah travelled for one month to get a single Hadith from ‘Abdullah bin Unais.
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْخُرُوجِ فِي طَلَبِ الْعِلْمِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَرَحَلَ جَابِرُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ مَسِيرَةَ شَهْرٍ إِلَى عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أُنَيْسٍ فِي حَدِيثٍ وَاحِدٍ.

chapters.title: The superiority of a person who learns (Islam, becomes a religious scholar) and then teaches it to others
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ مَنْ عَلِمَ وَعَلَّمَ

chapters.title: (What is said regarding) the disappearance of the (religious) knowledge and the appearance of (religious) ignorance
chapters.intro: And Rabi‘a said, “It is not wise for a person who has been gifted with a part of the (religious) knowledge to ruin himself (by abstaining from teaching it to others).”
chapters.title_arabic: باب رَفْعِ الْعِلْمِ وَظُهُورِ الْجَهْلِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ رَبِيعَةُ لاَ يَنْبَغِي لأَحَدٍ عِنْدَهُ شَيْءٌ مِنَ الْعِلْمِ أَنْ يُضَيِّعَ نَفْسَهُ.

chapters.title: The superiority of (religious) knowledge
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ الْعِلْمِ

chapters.title: To give a religious verdict while riding an animal or standing on anything else
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْفُتْيَا وَهُوَ وَاقِفٌ عَلَى الدَّابَّةِ وَغَيْرِهَا

chapters.title: Whoever gave a religious verdict by beckoning or by nodding
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ أَجَابَ الْفُتْيَا بِإِشَارَةِ الْيَدِ وَالرَّأْسِ

chapters.title: The Prophet (saws) urged the people (mission) of 'Abdul Qais to memorize the faith and the (religious) knowledge (as he explained to them) and to inform (convey) to their people whom they left behind (at home)
chapters.intro: Narrated Malik bin Al-Huwairith that the Prophet ﷺ said to them, “Go back to your people and teach (religious knowledge) to them.”
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ تَحْرِيضِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ وَفْدَ عَبْدِ الْقَيْسِ عَلَى أَنْ يَحْفَظُوا الإِيمَانَ وَالْعِلْمَ وَيُخْبِرُوا مَنْ وَرَاءَهُمْ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ مَالِكُ بْنُ الْحُوَيْرِثِ قَالَ لَنَا النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «ارْجِعُوا إِلَى أَهْلِيكُمْ، فَعَلِّمُوهُمْ».

chapters.title: To travel seeking an answer to a problematic matter, and to teach it to one's family
chapters.title_arabic: باب الرِّحْلَةِ فِي الْمَسْأَلَةِ النَّازِلَةِ وَتَعْلِيمِ أَهْلِهِ

chapters.title: To fix the duties in rotation for learning (religious) knowledge
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّنَاوُبِ فِي الْعِلْمِ

chapters.title: To be furious while preaching or teaching if one sees what one hates
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْغَضَبِ فِي الْمَوْعِظَةِ وَالتَّعْلِيمِ إِذَا رَأَى مَا يَكْرَهُ

chapters.title: Whoever knelt down before the Imam or a (religious) preacher
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ بَرَكَ عَلَى رُكْبَتَيْهِ عِنْدَ الإِمَامِ أَوِ الْمُحَدِّثِ

chapters.title: Repeating ones talk thrice in order to make others understand
chapters.intro: The Prophet ﷺ said: “Beware from giving a false statement,” and he kept on repeating it. Ibn ‘Umar said that the Prophet ﷺ said thrice, “Haven’t I conveyed Allah’s Message (to you?)”
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ أَعَادَ الْحَدِيثَ ثَلاَثًا لِيُفْهَمَ عَنْهُ
chapters.intro_arabic:
فَقَالَ: «أَلاَ وَقَوْلُ الزُّورِ». فَمَا زَالَ يُكَرِّرُهَا.
وَقَالَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ قَالَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «هَلْ بَلَّغْتُ». ثَلاَثًا.

chapters.title: A man teaching (religion to) his woman-slave and his family
chapters.title_arabic: باب تَعْلِيمِ الرَّجُلِ أَمَتَهُ وَأَهْلَهُ

chapters.title: The preaching (and teaching) of the (religious) knowledge to women by the Imam (Chief)
chapters.title_arabic: باب عِظَةِ الإِمَامِ النِّسَاءَ وَتَعْلِيمِهِنَّ

chapters.title: Eagerness to (learn) the Hadith
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْحِرْصِ عَلَى الْحَدِيثِ

chapters.title: How the (religious) knowledge will be taken away
chapters.intro: And 'Umar bin 'Abdul 'Aziz wrote to Abu Bakr bin Hazm, "Look for the knowledge of Hadith and get it written, as I am afraid that religious knowledge will vanish and the religious learned men will pass away (die). Do not accept anything save the Hadiths of the Prophet. Circulate knowledge and teach the ignorant, for knowledge does not vanish except when it is kept secretly (to oneself)."
chapters.title_arabic: باب كَيْفَ يُقْبَضُ الْعِلْمُ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَكَتَبَ عُمَرُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ إِلَى أَبِي بَكْرِ بْنِ حَزْمٍ انْظُرْ مَا كَانَ مِنْ حَدِيثِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فَاكْتُبْهُ، فَإِنِّي خِفْتُ دُرُوسَ الْعِلْمِ وَذَهَابَ الْعُلَمَاءِ، وَلاَ تَقْبَلْ إِلاَّ حَدِيثَ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، وَلْتُفْشُوا الْعِلْمَ، وَلْتَجْلِسُوا حَتَّى يُعَلَّمَ مَنْ لاَ يَعْلَمُ، فَإِنَّ الْعِلْمَ لاَ يَهْلِكُ حَتَّى يَكُونَ سِرًّا. حَدَّثَنَا الْعَلاَءُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْجَبَّارِ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْعَزِيزِ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ دِينَارٍ بِذَلِكَ، يَعْنِي حَدِيثَ عُمَرَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ إِلَى قَوْلِهِ ذَهَابَ الْعُلَمَاءِ.

chapters.title: Should a day be fixed for women in order to teach them religion (apart from men)?
chapters.title_arabic: باب هَلْ يُجْعَلُ لِلنِّسَاءِ يَوْمٌ عَلَى حِدَةٍ فِي الْعِلْمِ

chapters.title: Whoever heard something (but did not understand it) and then asked again till he understood it completely
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ سَمِعَ شَيْئًا، فَرَاجَعَ حَتَّى يَعْرِفَهُ

chapters.title: It is incumbent on those who are present [in a religious meeting (or conference)] to convey the knowledge to those who are absent
chapters.intro: This statement has come from the Prophet ﷺ on the authority of Ibn ‘Abbas رضي الله عنهما.
chapters.title_arabic: باب لِيُبَلِّغِ الْعِلْمَ الشَّاهِدُ الْغَائِبَ
chapters.intro_arabic: قَالَهُ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ.

chapters.title: The sin of a person who tells a lie against the Prophet (saws)
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِثْمِ مَنْ كَذَبَ عَلَى النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم

chapters.title: The writing of knowledge
chapters.title_arabic: باب كِتَابَةِ الْعِلْمِ

chapters.title: The knowledge and its teaching and preaching at night
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْعِلْمِ وَالْعِظَةِ بِاللَّيْلِ

chapters.title: To speak about (religious) knowledge at night
chapters.title_arabic: باب السَّمَرِ بِالْعِلْمِ

chapters.title: (What is said regarding) the memorization of (religious) knowledge
chapters.title_arabic: باب حِفْظِ الْعِلْمِ

chapters.title: To be quiet (and listen) to religious learned men
chapters.title_arabic: باب الإِنْصَاتِ لِلْعُلَمَاءِ

chapters.title: When a religious learned man is asked, "Who is the most learned person." it is better for him to attribute or entrust absolute knowledge to Allah 'Azza wa Jall and to say. "Allah is the Most Learned (than anybody else)"
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يُسْتَحَبُّ لِلْعَالِمِ إِذَا سُئِلَ أَىُّ النَّاسِ أَعْلَمُ فَيَكِلُ الْعِلْمَ إِلَى اللَّهِ

chapters.title: Whosoever, while standing, asked a religious learned man who was sitting (on a pulpit or a similar thing, about something)
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ سَأَلَ وَهْوَ قَائِمٌ عَالِمًا جَالِسًا

chapters.title: To ask about a religious matter and to give a religious verdict (at Mina during Hajj) while doing the Rami of Jimar (throwing of pebbles at the Jimar in Mina during Hajj)
chapters.title_arabic: باب السُّؤَالِ وَالْفُتْيَا عِنْدَ رَمْىِ الْجِمَارِ

chapters.title: The Statement of Allah Ta'ala: "And of knowledge you (mankind) have been given only a little"
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {وَمَا أُوتِيتُمْ مِنَ الْعِلْمِ إِلاَّ قَلِيلاً}

chapters.title: Whosoever left some optional things simply for the fear that some people may not be able to understand them and may fall into something more difficult
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ مَنْ تَرَكَ بَعْضَ الاِخْتِيَارِ مَخَافَةَ أَنْ يَقْصُرَ فَهْمُ بَعْضِ النَّاسِ عَنْهُ فَيَقَعُوا فِي أَشَدَّ مِنْهُ

chapters.title: Whoever selected some people to teach them (religious) knowledge preferring them over others for fear that the others may not understand it
chapters.intro: And ‘Ali said, “You should preach to the people according to their mental calibre so that they may not convey wrong things about Allah and His Messenger ﷺ.”
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ خَصَّ بِالْعِلْمِ قَوْمًا دُونَ قَوْمٍ كَرَاهِيَةَ أَنْ لاَ يَفْهَمُوا
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ عَلِيٌّ حَدِّثُوا النَّاسَ، بِمَا يَعْرِفُونَ، أَتُحِبُّونَ أَنْ يُكَذَّبَ، اللَّهُ وَرَسُولُهُ

chapters.title: (What is said as regards): To be shy (Al-Haya) while learning (religious) knowledge
chapters.intro: And Mujahid said, “Neither a shy nor a proud person can learn the religious knowledge.” And ‘Aishah رضي الله عنها said, “How excellent the women of the Ansar are! They do not feel shy while learning sound knowledge in religion.”
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْحَيَاءِ فِي الْعِلْمِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَقَالَ مُجَاهِدٌ لاَ يَتَعَلَّمُ الْعِلْمَ مُسْتَحْيٍ وَلاَ مُسْتَكْبِرٌ.
وَقَالَتْ عَائِشَةُ نِعْمَ النِّسَاءُ نِسَاءُ الأَنْصَارِ لَمْ يَمْنَعْهُنَّ الْحَيَاءُ أَنْ يَتَفَقَّهْنَ فِي الدِّينِ.
[تحفة 17996 أ].

chapters.title: Whosoever felt shy (to ask something) and then requested another person to ask on his behalf
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنِ اسْتَحْيَا فَأَمَرَ غَيْرَهُ بِالسُّؤَالِ

chapters.title: Teaching religious knowledge and giving religious verdicts in a masjid
chapters.title_arabic: باب ذِكْرِ الْعِلْمِ وَالْفُتْيَا فِي الْمَسْجِدِ

chapters.title: Whosoever answered the questioner more than what was asked
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ أَجَابَ السَّائِلَ بِأَكْثَرَ مِمَّا سَأَلَهُ

chapters.title: What has been revealed regarding ablution?
chapters.intro:
And the Statement of Allah جل جلاله: “O you who believe! When you intend to offer Salat (prayer), wash your faces and your hands (forearms) up to the elbows, rub (by passing wet hands over) your heads, and (wash) your feet up to the ankles...” (V.5:6) Abu ‘Abdullah said: The Prophet ﷺ had made clear that it is obligatory (while performing) ablution to wash the (above-mentioned) body parts once. And the Prophet ﷺ also did perform the ablution by washing these parts twice and thrice, but he never washed them more than three times.

And the religious learned men disliked exceeding the limits set by the Prophet ﷺ while performing ablution and to surpass the action of the Prophet ﷺ.
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ مَا جَاءَ فِي الْوُضُوءِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {إِذَا قُمْتُمْ إِلَى الصَّلاَةِ فَاغْسِلُوا وُجُوهَكُمْ وَأَيْدِيَكُمْ إِلَى الْمَرَافِقِ وَامْسَحُوا بِرُءُوسِكُمْ وَأَرْجُلَكُمْ إِلَى الْكَعْبَيْنِ} قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ وَبَيَّنَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ أَنَّ فَرْضَ الْوُضُوءِ مَرَّةً مَرَّةً، وَتَوَضَّأَ أَيْضًا مَرَّتَيْنِ وَثَلاَثًا، وَلَمْ يَزِدْ عَلَى ثَلاَثٍ، وَكَرِهَ أَهْلُ الْعِلْمِ الإِسْرَافَ فِيهِ وَأَنْ يُجَاوِزُوا فِعْلَ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ.

chapters.title: Chapter. No Salat (prayer) Is Accepted Without Ablution (i.e, To Remove, The Small Hadith By Ablution Or The Big Hadith By Taking A Bath)
chapters.title_arabic: باب لاَ تُقْبَلُ صَلاَةٌ بِغَيْرِ طُهُورٍ

chapters.title: Chapter. The Superiority Of Ablution. And Al-ghurr-ul-muhajjalun (the Parts Of The Body Of The Muslims Washed In Ablution Will Shine On The Day Of Ressurrection And The Angels Will Call Them By That Name) From The Traces Of Ablution
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ الْوُضُوءِ، وَالْغُرُّ الْمُحَجَّلُونَ مِنْ آثَارِ الْوُضُوءِ

chapters.title: One should not repeat ablution if in doubt unless and until he is convinced (that he has lost his ablution by having Hadath)
chapters.title_arabic: باب لاَ يَتَوَضَّأُ مِنَ الشَّكِّ حَتَّى يَسْتَيْقِنَ

chapters.title: To perform a light ablution
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّخْفِيفِ فِي الْوُضُوءِ

chapters.title: The completion (or perfection) of ablution (one should wash all the parts perfectly)
chapters.intro: And Ibn ‘Umar said, “The completion of ablution means to clean the parts perfectly.”
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِسْبَاغِ الْوُضُوءِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ إِسْبَاغُ الْوُضُوءِ الإِنْقَاءُ.

chapters.title: To wash the face with both hands by a handful of water
chapters.title_arabic: باب غَسْلِ الْوَجْهِ بِالْيَدَيْنِ مِنْ غَرْفَةٍ وَاحِدَةٍ

chapters.title: To recite "In the name of Allah," during every action and on having sexual relations with one's wife
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّسْمِيَةِ عَلَى كُلِّ حَالٍ وَعِنْدَ الْوِقَاعِ

chapters.title: What to say while going to the lavatory (water closet)
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يَقُولُ عِنْدَ الْخَلاَءِ

chapters.title: Providing water at lavatories (for washing the private parts after answering the call of nature)
chapters.title_arabic: باب وَضْعِ الْمَاءِ عِنْدَ الْخَلاَءِ

chapters.title: While urinating or defecating, never face the Qiblah except when you are screened by a building or a wall or something like that
chapters.title_arabic: باب لاَ تُسْتَقْبَلُ الْقِبْلَةُ بِغَائِطٍ أَوْ بَوْلٍ إِلاَّ عِنْدَ الْبِنَاءِ جِدَارٍ أَوْ نَحْوِهِ

chapters.title: Defecating while sitting over two bricks
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ تَبَرَّزَ عَلَى لَبِنَتَيْنِ

chapters.title: The going out of women for answering the call of nature
chapters.title_arabic: باب خُرُوجِ النِّسَاءِ إِلَى الْبَرَازِ

chapters.title: To defecate in houses
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّبَرُّزِ فِي الْبُيُوتِ

chapters.title: To wash the private parts with water after answering the call of nature
chapters.title_arabic: باب الاِسْتِنْجَاءِ بِالْمَاءِ

chapters.title: Getting water carried by somebody else for purification (washing one's private parts).
chapters.intro: And Abu Ad-Darda’ said (to the people of Iraq), “Is not the man whose nicknames are Sahib An-Na‘lain, Sahib At-Tahir, and Sahib Al-Wisad (‘Abdullah bin Mas‘ud) amongst you?” [‘Abdullah bin Mas‘ud used to carry the Na‘lain (shoes) and Tahir (water for purification and ablution) and Wisad (carpet) for the Prophet ﷺ, so he was called by those names].
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ حُمِلَ مَعَهُ الْمَاءُ لِطُهُورِهِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ أَبُو الدَّرْدَاءِ: أَلَيْسَ فِيكُمْ صَاحِبُ النَّعْلَيْنِ وَالطَّهُورِ وَالْوِسَادِ.

chapters.title: To carry an 'Anaza (spear-headed stick) along with the water for washing the private parts after answering the call of nature.
chapters.title_arabic: باب حَمْلِ الْعَنَزَةِ مَعَ الْمَاءِ فِي الاِسْتِنْجَاءِ

chapters.title: It is forbidden to clean the private parts with the right hand
chapters.title_arabic: باب النَّهْىِ عَنْ الاِسْتِنْجَاءِ، بِالْيَمِينِ

chapters.title: While passing urine one should not hold his penis with his right hand.
chapters.title_arabic: باب لاَ يُمْسِكُ ذَكَرَهُ بِيَمِينِهِ إِذَا بَالَ

chapters.title: To clean the private parts with stones
chapters.title_arabic: باب الاِسْتِنْجَاءِ بِالْحِجَارَةِ

chapters.title: Do not clean the private parts with dung
chapters.title_arabic: باب لاَ يُسْتَنْجَى بِرَوْثٍ

chapters.title: The washing of the body parts (i.e., the parts which are washed in ablution) once only while performing ablution
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْوُضُوءِ مَرَّةً مَرَّةً

chapters.title: The washing of the body parts twice while performing ablution
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْوُضُوءِ مَرَّتَيْنِ مَرَّتَيْنِ

chapters.title: The washing of the parts thrice while performing ablution
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْوُضُوءِ ثَلاَثًا ثَلاَثًا

chapters.title: The cleaning of the nose by putting water in it and then blowing it out during ablution
chapters.title_arabic: باب الاِسْتِنْثَارِ فِي الْوُضُوءِ
chapters.intro_arabic: ذَكَرَهُ عُثْمَانُ وَعَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ زَيْدٍ وَابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمْ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ.

chapters.title: To clean the private parts with odd number of stones
chapters.title_arabic: باب الاِسْتِجْمَارِ وِتْرًا

chapters.title: Washing both feet, and it is not sufficient to pass wet hands over the feet
chapters.title_arabic: باب غَسْلِ الرِّجْلَيْنِ وَلاَ يَمْسَحُ عَلَى الْقَدَمَيْنِ

chapters.title: To rinse the mouth with water while performing ablution
chapters.intro: This statement has come from the Prophet ﷺ on the authority of Ibn ‘Abbas and ‘Abdullah bin Zaid رضي الله عنهم.
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْمَضْمَضَةِ فِي الْوُضُوءِ
chapters.intro_arabic: قَالَهُ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ وَعَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ زَيْدٍ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمْ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ.

chapters.title: The washing of heels during ablution
chapters.intro: Whenever Ibn Sirin performed ablution, he used to wash the place that was under the ring.
chapters.title_arabic: باب غَسْلِ الأَعْقَابِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَكَانَ ابْنُ سِيرِينَ يَغْسِلُ مَوْضِعَ الْخَاتَمِ إِذَا تَوَضَّأَ.

chapters.title: Washing the feet when one is wearing the shoes, and it is not sufficient for one to pass a wet hand over the shoes (but one should take off the shoes and wash one's feet)
chapters.title_arabic: باب غَسْلِ الرِّجْلَيْنِ فِي النَّعْلَيْنِ وَلاَ يَمْسَحُ عَلَى النَّعْلَيْنِ

chapters.title: While performing ablution or taking a bath one should start from the right side of the body
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّيَمُّنِ فِي الْوُضُوءِ وَالْغُسْلِ

chapters.title: To look for water (for ablution) when the time for the prayer is due
chapters.intro: ‘Aishah رضي الله عنها said: Once the Fajr prayer was due and water was searched for (for ablution) but it was not found. Thereupon the Divine Revelation of Tayammum was revealed. (Tayammum means to put or strike lightly on clean earth with one’s hands and then pass the palm of each on the back of the other, blow off the dust, and then pass them on the face).
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْتِمَاسِ الْوَضُوءِ إِذَا حَانَتِ الصَّلاَةُ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَتْ عَائِشَةُ حَضَرَتِ الصُّبْحُ فَالْتُمِسَ الْمَاءُ، فَلَمْ يُوجَدْ، فَنَزَلَ التَّيَمُّمُ.

chapters.title: What is said regarding the water with which human hair has been washed
chapters.intro: ‘Ata’ saw no harm in making threads and ropes out of human hair. The utilization of the thing which is licked or eaten by a dog, and the passing of dogs through the mosque. Az-Zuhri said, “It is permissible for one to perform ablution with water that has been licked by a dog, provided that there is no water except that.” Sufyan said, “This is the true religious verdict: Allah جل جلاله said: “And you find no water, then perform Tayammum.” (V.<qref chapter="4" verses="43">4:43</qref>).
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْمَاءِ الَّذِي يُغْسَلُ بِهِ شَعَرُ الإِنْسَانِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَكَانَ عَطَاءٌ لاَ يَرَى بِهِ بَأْسًا أَنْ يُتَّخَذَ مِنْهَا الْخُيُوطُ وَالْحِبَالُ، وَسُؤْرِ الْكِلاَبِ وَمَمَرِّهَا فِي الْمَسْجِدِ.
وَقَالَ الزُّهْرِيُّ إِذَا وَلَغَ فِي إِنَاءٍ لَيْسَ لَهُ وَضُوءٌ غَيْرُهُ يَتَوَضَّأُ بِهِ.
وَقَالَ سُفْيَانُ هَذَا الْفِقْهُ بِعَيْنِهِ، يَقُولُ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى: {فَلَمْ تَجِدُوا مَاءً فَتَيَمَّمُوا} وَهَذَا مَاءٌ، وَفِي النَّفْسِ مِنْهُ شَيْءٌ، يَتَوَضَّأُ بِهِ وَيَتَيَمَّمُ.

chapters.title: If a dog drinks from the utensil of any one of you then it is essential to wash it seven times
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا شَرِبَ الْكَلْبُ فِي إِنَاءِ أَحَدِكُمْ فَلْيَغْسِلْهُ سَبْعًا

chapters.title: Whosoever considers not to repeat ablution except if something is discharged or passed from exit (front or back private parts)
chapters.intro: As is mentioned in the Statement of Allah تعالى: “… Or any of you comes from answering the call of nature…” (V.<qref chapter="5" verses="6">5:6</qref>). And ‘Ata’ said, “If a worm comes out of one’s anus or if a drop of discharge equal to the size of a louse comes out of one’s penis (then it is essential to repeat the ablution).” Jabir bin ‘Abdullah said, “If one laughs in Salat (prayer), he must repeat his Salat and not the ablution.” Al-Hasan said, “If someone takes out (cuts) some of his hair, cuts his nails, or removes his leather socks, he is not to repeat his ablution.” Abu Hurairah said, “It is not necessary to repeat ablution except on Hadath.” And Jabir stated, “The Prophet ﷺ was in the battle of Dhat-ur-Riqa’ and a person was shot with an arrow and he bled profusely, but he bowed and prostrated and continued his Salat.” Al-Hasan said, “The Muslims used to offer Salat regularly with their wounds.” Tawus, Muhammad bin ‘Ali, ‘Ata, and the people of Hijaz say, “Bleeding does not necessitate the repetition of ablution.” Ibn ‘Umar squeezed one of his pimples and blood came out, but he did not repeat his ablution. Ibn Abi Aufa spat out blood but carried on with his Salat. Ibn ‘Umar and Al-Hasan said, “If anyone lets his blood out, then it is necessary for him to wash the cut area only.”
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ لَمْ يَرَ الْوُضُوءَ إِلاَّ مِنَ الْمَخْرَجَيْنِ، مِنَ الْقُبُلِ وَالدُّبُرِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَقَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {أَوْ جَاءَ أَحَدٌ مِنْكُمْ مِنَ الْغَائِطِ} وَقَالَ عَطَاءٌ فِيمَنْ يَخْرُجُ مِنْ دُبُرِهِ الدُّودُ أَوْ مِنْ ذَكَرِهِ نَحْوُ الْقَمْلَةِ يُعِيدُ الْوُضُوءَ.
وَقَالَ جَابِرُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ إِذَا ضَحِكَ فِي الصَّلاَةِ أَعَادَ الصَّلاَةَ، وَلَمْ يُعِدِ الْوُضُوءَ.
وَقَالَ الْحَسَنُ إِنْ أَخَذَ مِنْ شَعَرِهِ وَأَظْفَارِهِ أَوْ خَلَعَ خُفَّيْهِ فَلاَ وُضُوءَ عَلَيْهِ.
وَقَالَ أَبُو هُرَيْرَةَ لاَ وُضُوءَ إِلاَّ مِنْ حَدَثٍ. وَيُذْكَرُ عَنْ جَابِرٍ أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ كَانَ فِي غَزْوَةِ ذَاتِ الرِّقَاعِ فَرُمِيَ رَجُلٌ بِسَهْمٍ، فَنَزَفَهُ الدَّمُ فَرَكَعَ وَسَجَدَ، وَمَضَى فِي صَلاَتِهِ.
وَقَالَ الْحَسَنُ مَا زَالَ الْمُسْلِمُونَ يُصَلُّونَ فِي جِرَاحَاتِهِمْ.
وَقَالَ طَاوُسٌ وَمُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ وَعَطَاءٌ وَأَهْلُ الْحِجَازِ لَيْسَ فِي الدَّمِ وُضُوءٌ. وَعَصَرَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ بَثْرَةً فَخَرَجَ مِنْهَا الدَّمُ، وَلَمْ يَتَوَضَّأْ. وَبَزَقَ ابْنُ أَبِي أَوْفَى دَمًا فَمَضَى فِي صَلاَتِهِ.
وَقَالَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ وَالْحَسَنُ فِيمَنْ يَحْتَجِمُ لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ إِلاَّ غَسْلُ مَحَاجِمِهِ.

chapters.title: (What is said regarding) a man who helps his companion to perform ablution (by pouring water for him)
chapters.title_arabic: باب الرَّجُلِ يُوَضِّئُ صَاحِبَهُ

chapters.title: The recitation of Quran or doing other invocations etc. after Hadath
chapters.title_arabic: باب قِرَاءَةِ الْقُرْآنِ بَعْدَ الْحَدَثِ وَغَيْرِهِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَقَالَ مَنْصُورٌ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ لاَ بَأْسَ بِالْقِرَاءَةِ فِي الْحَمَّامِ، وَبِكَتْبِ الرِّسَالَةِ عَلَى غَيْرِ وُضُوءٍ.
وَقَالَ حَمَّادٌ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ إِنْ كَانَ عَلَيْهِمْ إِزَارٌ فَسَلِّمْ، وَإِلاَّ فَلاَ تُسَلِّمْ.

chapters.title: Whoever does not repeat ablution except after fallinginto deep sleep - losing consciousness completely
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ لَمْ يَتَوَضَّأْ إِلاَّ مِنَ الْغَشْىِ الْمُثْقِلِ

chapters.title: To pass wet hands over the whole head during ablution
chapters.intro: As is referred to by the Statement of Allah تعالى: “… Rub (by passing wet hands over) your heads…” (V.<qref chapter="5" verses="6">5:6</qref>). And Ibn Al-Musaiyab said, “This order is both for men and women.” And Malik was asked, “Is the passing of a wet hand over a part of the head sufficient (and that is not sufficient)?” He took his verdict from the narration of ‘Abdullah bin Zaid which follows.
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَسْحِ الرَّأْسِ كُلِّهِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
لِقَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {وَامْسَحُوا بِرُءُوسِكُمْ}.
وَقَالَ ابْنُ الْمُسَيَّبِ الْمَرْأَةُ بِمَنْزِلَةِ الرَّجُلِ تَمْسَحُ عَلَى رَأْسِهَا. وَسُئِلَ مَالِكٌ أَيُجْزِئُ أَنْ يَمْسَحَ بَعْضَ الرَّأْسِ فَاحْتَجَّ بِحَدِيثِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ زَيْدٍ.

chapters.title: The washing of feet unto the ankles
chapters.title_arabic: باب غَسْلِ الرِّجْلَيْنِ إِلَى الْكَعْبَيْنِ

chapters.title: The using of the remaining water after ablution
chapters.intro: And Jarir bin ‘Abdullah ordered the members of his family to perform ablution with the water in which he had put his Siwak (a piece of a root of a tree called Al-Arak used as a toothbrush).
chapters.title_arabic: باب اسْتِعْمَالِ فَضْلِ وَضُوءِ النَّاسِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَأَمَرَ جَرِيرُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ أَهْلَهُ أَنْ يَتَوَضَّئُوا بِفَضْلِ سِوَاكِهِ.

chapters.title: Chapter:
chapters.title_arabic: باب

chapters.title: Rinsing one's mouth and putting water in one's nose and cleaning it by blowing the water out with a single handful of water
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ مَضْمَضَ وَاسْتَنْشَقَ مِنْ غَرْفَةٍ وَاحِدَةٍ

chapters.title: The passing of wet hands over the head once only (while performing ablution)
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَسْحِ الرَّأْسِ مَرَّةً

chapters.title: The performance of ablution by a man along with his wife. The utilization of water remaining after a woman has performed ablution. Umar performed ablution with warm water and with water brought from the house of a Christian woman
chapters.intro: 'Umar performed ablution with warm water and with water brought from the house of a Christian woman.
chapters.title_arabic: باب وُضُوءِ الرَّجُلِ مَعَ امْرَأَتِهِ وَفَضْلِ وَضُوءِ الْمَرْأَةِ‏
chapters.intro_arabic: وَتَوَضَّأَ عُمَرُ بِالْحَمِيمِ مِنْ بَيْتِ نَصْرَانِيَّةٍ.

chapters.title: The sprinkling of remaining water after performing ablution on an unconscious person by the prophet (saw)
chapters.title_arabic: باب صَبِّ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم وَضُوءَهُ عَلَى الْمُغْمَى عَلَيْهِ

chapters.title: To take a bath or perform ablution from a Mikhdab (utensil), a tumbler, or a wooden or stone pot
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْغُسْلِ وَالْوُضُوءِ فِي الْمِخْضَبِ وَالْقَدَحِ وَالْخَشَبِ وَالْحِجَارَةِ

chapters.title: To perform ablution from an earthen-ware pot
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْوُضُوءِ مِنَ التَّوْرِ

chapters.title: To perform ablution with one Mudd of water. (Mudd is practically 2/3 of the kilogram)
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْوُضُوءِ بِالْمُدِّ

chapters.title: To pass wet hands over Khuffain [two leather socks covering the ankles]
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْمَسْحِ عَلَى الْخُفَّيْنِ

chapters.title: If one puts on (Khuff) just after performing ablution (ther is no need to wash one's feet again in ablution) (24 hours for non-travellers and three days for travelers)
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا أَدْخَلَ رِجْلَيْهِ وَهُمَا طَاهِرَتَانِ

chapters.title: Not repeating ablution after eating mutton and As-Sawiq
chapters.intro: Abu Bakr, ‘Umar, and ‘Uthman ate such food but did not repeat ablution.
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ لَمْ يَتَوَضَّأْ مِنْ لَحْمِ الشَّاةِ وَالسَّوِيقِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَأَكَلَ أَبُو بَكْرٍ وَعُمَرُ وَعُثْمَانُ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمْ لَحْمًا فَلَمْ يَتَوَضَّئُوا.

chapters.title: Rinsing one's mouth (with water) after eating As-Sawiq without repaying ablution
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ مَضْمَضَ مِنَ السَّوِيقِ وَلَمْ يَتَوَضَّأْ

chapters.title: Whether to rinse the mouth after drinking milk
chapters.title_arabic: باب هَلْ يُمَضْمِضُ مِنَ اللَّبَنِ

chapters.title: Ablution after sleep. And whoever considers it unnecessary to repeat ablution after dozing once or twice or after nodding once in slumber
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ الْوُضُوءِ مِنَ النَّوْمِ وَمَنْ لَمْ يَرَ مِنَ النَّعْسَةِ وَالنَّعْسَتَيْنِ أَوِ الْخَفْقَةِ وُضُوءًا

chapters.title: To perform ablution even on having no Hadath
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْوُضُوءِ مِنْ غَيْرِ حَدَثٍ

chapters.title: One of the major sins is not to protect oneself (one's clothes and body) from one's urine (i.e., from being soiled with it)
chapters.title_arabic: باب مِنَ الْكَبَائِرِ أَنْ لاَ يَسْتَتِرَ مِنْ بَوْلِهِ

chapters.title: What is said regarding washing out urine
chapters.intro: And the Prophet ﷺ remarked about the person in the grave that he never saved himself from being soiled with his urine. And the Prophet ﷺ mentioned only the urine of human beings.
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا جَاءَ فِي غَسْلِ الْبَوْلِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ لِصَاحِبِ الْقَبْرِ: «كَانَ لاَ يَسْتَتِرُ مِنْ بَوْلِهِ». وَلَمْ يَذْكُرْ سِوَى بَوْلِ النَّاسِ.

chapters.title: Chapter:
chapters.title_arabic: باب

chapters.title: The Prophet (saw) and the people left the bedouin undisturbed till he finished urinating in the mosque
chapters.title_arabic: باب تَرْكِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم وَالنَّاسِ الأَعْرَابِيَّ حَتَّى فَرَغَ مِنْ بَوْلِهِ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ

chapters.title: The pouring of water over the urine in the mosque
chapters.title_arabic: باب صَبِّ الْمَاءِ عَلَى الْبَوْلِ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ

chapters.title: The spilling of water over the place where there is urine
chapters.intro:
Narrated Anas bin Malik:

A Bedouin came and passed urine in one corner of the mosque. The people shouted at him but the Prophet stopped them till he finished urinating. The Prophet ordered them to spill a bucket of water over that place and they did so.
chapters.title_arabic: باب يُهَرِيقُ الْمَاءَ عَلَى الْبَوْلِ
chapters.intro_arabic: حَدَّثَنَا خَالِدٌ، قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا سُلَيْمَانُ، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ سَعِيدٍ، قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَنَسَ بْنَ مَالِكٍ، قَالَ جَاءَ أَعْرَابِيٌّ فَبَالَ فِي طَائِفَةِ الْمَسْجِدِ، فَزَجَرَهُ النَّاسُ، فَنَهَاهُمُ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم، فَلَمَّا قَضَى بَوْلَهُ أَمَرَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم بِذَنُوبٍ مِنْ مَاءٍ، فَأُهْرِيقَ عَلَيْهِ‏.‏

chapters.title: The urine of children
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَوْلِ الصِّبْيَانِ

chapters.title: To pass urine while standing and sitting
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْبَوْلِ قَائِمًا وَقَاعِدًا

chapters.title: To urinate beside one's companion while screened by a wall
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْبَوْلِ عِنْدَ صَاحِبِهِ وَالتَّسَتُّرِ بِالْحَائِطِ

chapters.title: To urinate near the dumps of some people
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْبَوْلِ عِنْدَ سُبَاطَةِ قَوْمٍ

chapters.title: The washing out of blood
chapters.title_arabic: باب غَسْلِ الدَّمِ

chapters.title: The washing out of semen with water and rubbing it off (when it is dry) and the washing out of what comes out of women (i.e., discharge)
chapters.title_arabic: باب غَسْلِ الْمَنِيِّ وَفَرْكِهِ وَغَسْلِ مَا يُصِيبُ مِنَ الْمَرْأَةِ

chapters.title: If the (traces of) Janaba (semen) or other spots are not removed completely on washing
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا غَسَلَ الْجَنَابَةَ أَوْ غَيْرَهَا فَلَمْ يَذْهَبْ أَثَرُهُ

chapters.title: (What is said) about the urine of camels, sheep and other animals and about their folds
chapters.intro: Abu Musa offered prayer at Dar-il-Barid (post office) and there was animal dung in it though a vast strip of land was near it. Abu Musa said: Both these places are similar (for offering of the prayers).
chapters.title_arabic: باب أَبْوَالِ الإِبِلِ وَالدَّوَابِّ وَالْغَنَمِ وَمَرَابِضِهَا
chapters.intro_arabic: وَصَلَّى أَبُو مُوسَى فِي دَارِ الْبَرِيدِ وَالسِّرْقِينِ وَالْبَرِّيَّةُ إِلَى جَنْبِهِ فَقَالَ هَاهُنَا وَثَمَّ سَوَاءٌ.

chapters.title: An-Najasat (impure and filthy things) which fall in cooking butter (ghee - which is obtained by evaporating moisture from butter) and water
chapters.intro: Az-Zuhri said, “There is no harm in using water if its taste, odour, or colour is not changed.” Hammad said, “There is no harm if the feathers of dead birds fell in it.” About the bones of dead animals like an elephant, Az-Zuhri said, “I met some of the old learned religious men who were using them (bones) as combs and as containers for oiling, etc., and they found no harm in that.” Ibn Sirin and Ibrahim said, “There is no harm in the trade of ivory.”
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يَقَعُ مِنَ النَّجَاسَاتِ فِي السَّمْنِ وَالْمَاءِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَقَالَ الزُّهْرِيُّ لاَ بَأْسَ بِالْمَاءِ مَا لَمْ يُغَيِّرْهُ طَعْمٌ أَوْ رِيحٌ أَوْ لَوْنٌ.
وَقَالَ حَمَّادٌ لاَ بَأْسَ بِرِيشِ الْمَيْتَةِ.
وَقَالَ الزُّهْرِيُّ فِي عِظَامِ الْمَوْتَى نَحْوَ الْفِيلِ وَغَيْرِهِ أَدْرَكْتُ نَاسًا مِنْ سَلَفِ الْعُلَمَاءِ يَمْتَشِطُونَ بِهَا، وَيَدَّهِنُونَ فِيهَا، لاَ يَرَوْنَ بِهِ بَأْسًا.
وَقَالَ ابْنُ سِيرِينَ وَإِبْرَاهِيمُ وَلاَ بَأْسَ بِتِجَارَةِ الْعَاجِ.

chapters.title: Urinating in stagnant water
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْبَوْلِ فِي الْمَاءِ الدَّائِمِ

chapters.title: If a dead body or a polluted thing is put on the back of a person offering Salat (prayer), his Salat will not be annulled (rejected by Allah)
chapters.intro: In prayer, Ibn ‘Umar used to take off his clothes whenever he saw blood on them and used to continue his prayer. Ibn Al-Musaiyyab and Ash-Sha‘bi said, “Whenever a person offers his Salat (prayers) while wearing clothes stained with blood or Janaba or offers Salat facing in a direction other than the Qiblah (unintentionally) or with Tayammum and finds water before the time of that Salat is over, he does not have to repeat his Salat in any of the above-mentioned cases.”
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا أُلْقِيَ عَلَى ظَهْرِ الْمُصَلِّي قَذَرٌ أَوْ جِيفَةٌ لَمْ تَفْسُدْ عَلَيْهِ صَلاَتُهُ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَكَانَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ إِذَا رَأَى فِي ثَوْبِهِ دَمًا وَهُوَ يُصَلِّي وَضَعَهُ وَمَضَى فِي صَلاَتِهِ.

chapters.title: Spitting or blowing out the nose or doing similar action in one's own garment
chapters.intro: Narrated Miswar bin Makhrama and Marwan: Allah’s Messenger ﷺ set out at the time of Al-Hudaibiya (treaty), and mentioned the rest of Hadith and when Allah’s Messenger ﷺ spitted, the spittle would fall in the hand of one of them (the Prophet’s Companions) who would rub it on his face and skin.
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْبُزَاقِ وَالْمُخَاطِ وَنَحْوِهِ فِي الثَّوْبِ
chapters.intro_arabic: قَالَ عُرْوَةُ عَنِ الْمِسْوَرِ وَمَرْوَانَ خَرَجَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ زَمَنَ حُدَيْبِيَةَ فَذَكَرَ الْحَدِيثَ. وَمَا تَنَخَّمَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ نُخَامَةً إِلاَّ وَقَعَتْ فِي كَفِّ رَجُلٍ مِنْهُمْ فَدَلَكَ بِهَا وَجْهَهُ وَجِلْدَهُ.

chapters.title: It is unlawful to perform ablution with Nabidh (water in which dates or grapes etc, are soaked and is not yet fermented) or with any other intoxicant
chapters.intro: Hasan and Abul-‘Aliya disliked it. ‘Ata’ said: I prefer to do Tayammum instead of doing ablution with milk or Nabidh.
chapters.title_arabic: باب لاَ يَجُوزُ الْوُضُوءُ بِالنَّبِيذِ وَلاَ الْمُسْكِرِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَكَرِهَهُ الْحَسَنُ وَأَبُو الْعَالِيَةِ.
وَقَالَ عَطَاءٌ التَّيَمُّمُ أَحَبُّ إِلَيَّ مِنَ الْوُضُوءِ بِالنَّبِيذِ وَاللَّبَنِ.

chapters.title: Washing blood by a women off her father's face
chapters.intro: Abul-‘Aliya said: Rub my leg as it is aching.
chapters.title_arabic: باب غَسْلِ الْمَرْأَةِ أَبَاهَا الدَّمَ عَنْ وَجْهِهِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ أَبُو الْعَالِيَةِ امْسَحُوا عَلَى رِجْلِي فَإِنَّهَا مَرِيضَةٌ.

chapters.title: Siwak (to clean the teeth with Siwak which is a tooth-brush in the form of a pencil from the roots of the Arak tree
chapters.intro: Ibn ‘Abbas said, “Once I passed the night with the Prophet ﷺ and saw him cleaning his teeth (with Siwak).”
chapters.title_arabic: باب السِّوَاكِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ بِتُّ عِنْدَ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فَاسْتَنَّ.

chapters.title: To give Siwak to the oldest person of the group
chapters.title_arabic: باب دَفْعِ السِّوَاكِ إِلَى الأَكْبَرِ

chapters.title: The superiority of a person who sleeps with ablution.
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ مَنْ بَاتَ عَلَى الْوُضُوءِ

chapters.title: The performance of ablution before taking a bath
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْوُضُوءِ قَبْلَ الْغُسْلِ

chapters.title: Taking a bath by a man along with his wife
chapters.title_arabic: باب غُسْلِ الرَّجُلِ مَعَ امْرَأَتِهِ

chapters.title: Taking a bath with a Sa' of water or so (One Sa' = 3 kilograms approx.)
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْغُسْلِ بِالصَّاعِ وَنَحْوِهِ

chapters.title: Pouring water thrice on one's head
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ أَفَاضَ عَلَى رَأْسِهِ ثَلاَثًا

chapters.title: To wash the body (parts) once only
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْغُسْلِ مَرَّةً وَاحِدَةً

chapters.title: Starting one's bath by scenting oneself with Hilab or some other scent
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ بَدَأَ بِالْحِلاَبِ أَوِ الطِّيبِ عِنْدَ الْغُسْلِ

chapters.title: To rinse the mouth and to clean the nose by putting water in it and then blowing it out while taking the bath of Janaba
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْمَضْمَضَةِ وَالاِسْتِنْشَاقِ فِي الْجَنَابَةِ

chapters.title: The rubbing of hands with earth in ore to clean them thoroughly
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَسْحِ الْيَدِ بِالتُّرَابِ لِيَكُونَ أَنْقَى

chapters.title: Can a Junub (a person who has yet to take bath after the sexual act or wet dream) put his hands in a pot (containing water) before washing them if they are not polluted with a dirty thing except Janaba?
chapters.title_arabic: باب هَلْ يُدْخِلُ الْجُنُبُ يَدَهُ فِي الإِنَاءِ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَغْسِلَهَا إِذَا لَمْ يَكُنْ عَلَى يَدِهِ قَذَرٌ غَيْرُ الْجَنَابَةِ

chapters.title: Interval during ablution or bath
chapters.title_arabic: باب تَفْرِيقِ الْغُسْلِ وَالْوُضُوءِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَيُذْكَرُ عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ أَنَّهُ غَسَلَ قَدَمَيْهِ بَعْدَ مَا جَفَّ وَضُوءُهُ.

chapters.title: Pouring water with one's right hand over the left one, during the bath
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ أَفْرَغَ بِيَمِينِهِ عَلَى شِمَالِهِ فِي الْغُسْلِ

chapters.title: Having sexual intercourse and repeating it. And engaging with one's own wives and taking a single bath (after doing so)
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا جَامَعَ ثُمَّ عَادَ، وَمَنْ دَارَ عَلَى نِسَائِهِ فِي غُسْلٍ وَاحِدٍ

chapters.title: The washing away of emotional urethral discharge and performing ablution after it
chapters.title_arabic: باب غَسْلِ الْمَذْىِ وَالْوُضُوءِ مِنْهُ

chapters.title: Whoever scented himself and then took a bath while the effect of scent remained even after bathing
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ تَطَيَّبَ ثُمَّ اغْتَسَلَ وَبَقِيَ أَثَرُ الطِّيبِ

chapters.title: To rub the hair thoroughly (while taking a bath) till one feels that one has made his skin wet (underneath the hair) and the one pours water over it
chapters.title_arabic: باب تَخْلِيلِ الشَّعَرِ حَتَّى إِذَا ظَنَّ أَنَّهُ قَدْ أَرْوَى بَشَرَتَهُ أَفَاضَ عَلَيْهِ

chapters.title: Whoever performed the ablution of Janaba and then washed his body but did not wash once again the parts which were washed in ablution
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ مَنْ تَوَضَّأَ فِي الْجَنَابَةِ ثُمَّ غَسَلَ سَائِرَ جَسَدِهِ، وَلَمْ يُعِدْ، غَسْلَ مَوَاضِعِ الْوُضُوءِ مَرَّةً أُخْرَى

chapters.title: If someone while in the mosque remembers that he is Junub, he should leave (the mosque to take a bath) and should not perform Tayammum
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا ذَكَرَ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ أَنَّهُ جُنُبٌ يَخْرُجُ كَمَا هُوَ وَلاَ يَتَيَمَّمُ

chapters.title: The removing of water from one's body with one's hands after taking the bath of Janaba
chapters.title_arabic: باب نَفْضِ الْيَدَيْنِ مِنَ الْغُسْلِ عَنِ الْجَنَابَةِ

chapters.title: Starting from the right side of one's head while taking a bath
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ بَدَأَ بِشِقِّ رَأْسِهِ الأَيْمَنِ فِي الْغُسْلِ

chapters.title: Whosoever took a bath alone (in seclusion) completely naked
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنِ اغْتَسَلَ عُرْيَانًا وَحْدَهُ فِي الْخَلْوَةِ، وَمَنْ تَسَتَّرَ فَالتَّسَتُّرُ أَفْضَلُ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ بَهْزٌ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ جَدِّهِ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «اللَّهُ أَحَقُّ أَنْ يُسْتَحْيَا مِنْهُ مِنَ النَّاسِ».

chapters.title: To screen oneself from the people while taking a bath
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّسَتُّرِ فِي الْغُسْلِ عِنْدَ النَّاسِ

chapters.title: If a woman has a wet dream (nocturnal sexual discharge)
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا احْتَلَمَتِ الْمَرْأَةُ

chapters.title: (What is said regarding) the sweat of a Junub. And a Muslim never becomes impure
chapters.title_arabic: باب عَرَقِ الْجُنُبِ وَأَنَّ الْمُسْلِمَ لاَ يَنْجُسُ

chapters.title: A Junub person) can go out and walk in the market or anywhere else
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْجُنُبُ يَخْرُجُ وَيَمْشِي فِي السُّوقِ وَغَيْرِهِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ عَطَاءٌ يَحْتَجِمُ الْجُنُبُ وَيُقَلِّمُ أَظْفَارَهُ، وَيَحْلِقُ رَأْسَهُ، وَإِنْ لَمْ يَتَوَضَّأْ.

chapters.title: A Junub can stay at home without taking a bath but with ablution
chapters.title_arabic: باب كَيْنُونَةِ الْجُنُبِ فِي الْبَيْتِ إِذَا تَوَضَّأَ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَغْتَسِلَ

chapters.title: Sleeping of a Junub person
chapters.title_arabic: باب نَوْمِ الْجُنُبِ

chapters.title: A Junub person should perform ablution before sleeping
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْجُنُبِ يَتَوَضَّأُ ثُمَّ يَنَامُ

chapters.title: When male and female organs come in close contact (bath becomes compulsory)
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا الْتَقَى الْخِتَانَانِ

chapters.title: Washing away what comes out from the private parts of a woman (woman's discharge) if one gets soiled with that
chapters.title_arabic: باب غَسْلِ مَا يُصِيبُ مِنْ فَرْجِ الْمَرْأَةِ

chapters.title: How the menses started
chapters.intro: And the statement of the Prophet ﷺ, “This is a thing which Allah has ordained for the daughters of Adam.” Some people said that the menses first came for Bani Israel. But what the Prophet ﷺ said is more comprehensive (the truth).
chapters.title_arabic: باب كَيْفَ كَانَ بَدْءُ الْحَيْضِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَوْلِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم ‏"‏ هَذَا شَىْءٌ كَتَبَهُ اللَّهُ عَلَى بَنَاتِ آدَمَ ‏"‏‏.‏ وَقَالَ بَعْضُهُمْ كَانَ أَوَّلُ مَا أُرْسِلَ الْحَيْضُ عَلَى بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ، وَحَدِيثُ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم أَكْثَرُ‏.‏

chapters.title: Menses (a thing) ordained (by Allah and instructions) for women when they get their menses
chapters.title_arabic: باب الأَمْرِ بِالنُّفَسَاءِ إِذَا نُفِسْنَ

chapters.title: The washing of the husband's head and the combing of his hair by a menstruating wife
chapters.title_arabic: باب غَسْلِ الْحَائِضِ رَأْسَ زَوْجِهَا وَتَرْجِيلِهِ

chapters.title: To recite the Qur'an while lying in the lap of one's own menstruating wife
chapters.intro: Abu Wa’il used to send his menstruating maid servant to bring the Qur’an from Abi Razin by carrying it from the hanger (of its case).
chapters.title_arabic: باب قِرَاءَةِ الرَّجُلِ فِي حَجْرِ امْرَأَتِهِ وَهْىَ حَائِضٌ
