chapters.intro_arabic: وَكَانَ أَبُو وَائِلٍ يُرْسِلُ خَادِمَهُ وَهْيَ حَائِضٌ إِلَى أَبِي رَزِينٍ، فَتَأْتِيهِ بِالْمُصْحَفِ فَتُمْسِكُهُ بِعِلاَقَتِهِ.

chapters.title: Using the word Nifas for menses
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ سَمَّى النِّفَاسَ حَيْضًا

chapters.title: Fondling a menstruating wife
chapters.title_arabic: باب مُبَاشَرَةِ الْحَائِضِ

chapters.title: A menstruating women should leave observing Saum (fasting)
chapters.title_arabic: باب تَرْكِ الْحَائِضِ الصَّوْمَ

chapters.title: A menstruating women should perform all the ceremonies of Hajj except the Tawaf around the Ka'bah
chapters.intro: Ibrahim said, “There is no harm in reciting a Verse of the Qur’an by a menstruating woman.” Ibn ‘Abbas considers that there was no harm in the recitation of the Qur’an by a Junub. The Prophet ﷺ used to remember (glorify) Allah at all times. Umm ‘Atiyya said that they were ordered to let the menstruating women come out (on festivals) to say Takbirat (Allahu-Akbar), and to invoke Allah. Ibn ‘Abbas narrated on the authority of Abu Sufyan: Heraclius asked for the letter of the Prophet ﷺ and read it. It began: “In the Name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful. O people of the Scripture (Jews and Christians): Come to a word that is just between us and you that we worship none but Allah …” (V.<qref chapter="3" verses="64">3:64</qref>). And ‘Ata quoted from Jabir, “Aishah رضي الله عنها got her menses and she performed all the ceremonies of Hajj except the Tawaf round the Ka‘bah and she did not offer Salat (prayers).” Al-Hakam said, “I slaughter the animal even if I were Junub.” And Allah جل جلاله said: “Eat not (O believers) of that (meat) on which Allah’s Name has not been pronounced (at the time of slaughtering of the animal).” (V.<qref chapter="6" verses="121">6:121</qref>).
chapters.title_arabic: باب تَقْضِي الْحَائِضُ الْمَنَاسِكَ كُلَّهَا إِلاَّ الطَّوَافَ بِالْبَيْتِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَقَالَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ لاَ بَأْسَ أَنْ تَقْرَأَ الآيَةَ. وَلَمْ يَرَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ بِالْقِرَاءَةِ لِلْجُنُبِ بَأْسًا. وَكَانَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يَذْكُرُ اللَّهَ عَلَى كُلِّ أَحْيَانِهِ.
وَقَالَتْ أُمُّ عَطِيَّةَ كُنَّا نُؤْمَرُ أَنْ يَخْرُجَ الْحُيَّضُ، فَيُكَبِّرْنَ بِتَكْبِيرِهِمْ وَيَدْعُونَ.
وَقَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ أَخْبَرَنِي أَبُو سُفْيَانَ أَنَّ هِرَقْلَ دَعَا بِكِتَابِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فَقَرَأَ فَإِذَا فِيهِ: «بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحْمَنِ الرَّحِيمِ وَ{يَا أَهْلَ الْكِتَابِ تَعَالَوْا إِلَى كَلِمَةٍ}». الآيَةَ.
وَقَالَ عَطَاءٌ عَنْ جَابِرٍ حَاضَتْ عَائِشَةُ فَنَسَكَتِ الْمَنَاسِكَ غَيْرَ الطَّوَافِ بِالْبَيْتِ، وَلاَ تُصَلِّي.
وَقَالَ الْحَكَمُ إِنِّي لأَذْبَحُ وَأَنَا جُنُبٌ.
وَقَالَ اللَّهُ: {وَلاَ تَأْكُلُوا مِمَّا لَمْ يُذْكَرِ اسْمُ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ}.

chapters.title: Al-Isthihada [bleeding (from the womb) in between a woman's periods]
chapters.title_arabic: باب الاِسْتِحَاضَةِ

chapters.title: Washing out the menstrual blood
chapters.title_arabic: باب غَسْلِ دَمِ الْمَحِيضِ

chapters.title: The I'tikaf of a woman who is bleeding in between her periods
chapters.title_arabic: باب الاِعْتِكَافِ لِلْمُسْتَحَاضَةِ

chapters.title: Can a woman offer her Salat (prayers) in the clothes in which she has her menses?
chapters.title_arabic: باب هَلْ تُصَلِّي الْمَرْأَةُ فِي ثَوْبٍ حَاضَتْ فِيهِ

chapters.title: Putting perfume by woman at the time of taking a bath after finishing from the menses
chapters.title_arabic: باب الطِّيبِ لِلْمَرْأَةِ عِنْدَ غُسْلِهَا مِنَ الْمَحِيضِ

chapters.title: A woman should rub her own body thoroughly during a bath after the menses
chapters.intro: How to take a bath after menses and rub the place soiled with blood with a perfumed piece of cloth.
chapters.title_arabic: باب دَلْكِ الْمَرْأَةِ نَفْسَهَا إِذَا تَطَهَّرَتْ مِنَ الْمَحِيضِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَكَيْفَ تَغْتَسِلُ، وَتَأْخُذُ فِرْصَةً مُمَسَّكَةً فَتَتَّبِعُ بِهَا أَثَرَ الدَّمِ

chapters.title: To take a bath after finishing from the menses
chapters.title_arabic: باب غُسْلِ الْمَحِيضِ

chapters.title: The combing of head-hair while taking the bath after finishing from her menses
chapters.title_arabic: باب امْتِشَاطِ الْمَرْأَةِ عِنْدَ غُسْلِهَا مِنَ الْمَحِيضِ

chapters.title: A woman should undo her head-hair while taking the bath after finishing from her menses.
chapters.title_arabic: باب نَقْضِ الْمَرْأَةِ شَعَرَهَا عِنْدَ غُسْلِ الْمَحِيضِ

chapters.title: "(A little lump of flesh) some formed and some unformed."
chapters.title_arabic: باب مُخَلَّقَةٍ وَغَيْرِ مُخَلَّقَةٍ

chapters.title: How a menstruating woman should assume Ihram for Hajj or for Umra
chapters.title_arabic: باب كَيْفَ تُهِلُّ الْحَائِضُ بِالْحَجِّ وَالْعُمْرَةِ

chapters.title: The beginning and the ending of menstrual periods
chapters.intro: Some women used to send the pads of cotton with traces of yellowish discharge to ‘Aishah رضي الله عنها (for her verdict to know whether they had become clean from menses or not). And ‘Aishah رضي الله عنها would say, “Do not hurry till you see the cotton pad is white (meaning the perfect disappearance of menses).” The daughter of Zaid bin Thabit was told that some women used to ask for candles at midnight to see whether the menses had stopped or not. On that, the daughter of Zaid said that the ladies (the wives of the Prophet’s Companions) had never done so, and she blamed them (the former women).
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِقْبَالِ الْمَحِيضِ وَإِدْبَارِهِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَكُنَّ نِسَاءٌ يَبْعَثْنَ إِلَى عَائِشَةَ بِالدُّرْجَةِ فِيهَا الْكُرْسُفُ فِيهِ الصُّفْرَةُ فَتَقُولُ لاَ تَعْجَلْنَ حَتَّى تَرَيْنَ الْقَصَّةَ الْبَيْضَاءَ. تُرِيدُ بِذَلِكَ الطُّهْرَ مِنَ الْحَيْضَةِ. وَبَلَغَ ابْنَةَ زَيْدِ بْنِ ثَابِتٍ أَنَّ نِسَاءً يَدْعُونَ بِالْمَصَابِيحِ مِنْ جَوْفِ اللَّيْلِ يَنْظُرْنَ إِلَى الطُّهْرِ فَقَالَتْ مَا كَانَ النِّسَاءُ يَصْنَعْنَ هَذَا. وَعَابَتْ عَلَيْهِنَّ.

chapters.title: There is no Salat (prayer) to be offered by a menstruating woman in lieu of the missed Salat during her menses.
chapters.intro: And the Prophet ﷺ said, “The lady (in her menses) must leave her Salat (prayers).”
chapters.title_arabic: باب لاَ تَقْضِي الْحَائِضُ الصَّلاَةَ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ جَابِرٌ وَأَبُو سَعِيدٍ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ تَدَعُ الصَّلاَةَ.

chapters.title: Sleeping with a menstruating woman (one's wife) while she is wearing her clothes (that are worn during menses)
chapters.title_arabic: باب النَّوْمِ مَعَ الْحَائِضِ وَهْىَ فِي ثِيَابِهَا

chapters.title: Whoever kept a special dress for menses besides other dresses for the clean period
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنِ اتَّخَذَ ثِيَابَ الْحَيْضِ سِوَى ثِيَابِ الطُّهْرِ

chapters.title: The participation of menstruating women in the two Eid festivals and in religious gatherings of Muslims and their isolation from the Musalla (prayer place)
chapters.title_arabic: باب شُهُودِ الْحَائِضِ الْعِيدَيْنِ، وَدَعْوَةَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ، وَيَعْتَزِلْنَ الْمُصَلَّى

chapters.title: If a woman gets menses thrice a month
chapters.intro: Can we believe a woman if she says she is menstruating or pregnant, and whatever is related to menses as is referred to by the Statement of Allah جل جلاله: “…And it is not lawful for them to conceal what Allah has created in their wombs…” (V.<qref chapter="2" verses="228">2:228</qref>). ‘Ali and Shuraih said, “If a woman presents witness from her relatives who are good Muslims that she is getting menses thrice a month then she is to be believed.” ‘Ata said, “Her previous menstrual cycles are to be taken into consideration.” The same was said by Ibrahim. ‘Ata said, “Menses can last from one to fifteen days.” Ibn Sirin was asked about a woman who noticed blood five days after her menstrual cycle. He replied: The women knew better about that.
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ إِذَا حَاضَتْ فِي شَهْرٍ ثَلاَثَ حِيَضٍ وَمَا يُصَدَّقُ النِّسَاءُ فِي الْحَيْضِ وَالْحَمْلِ فِيمَا يُمْكِنُ مِنَ الْحَيْضِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
لِقَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {وَلاَ يَحِلُّ لَهُنَّ أَنْ يَكْتُمْنَ مَا خَلَقَ اللَّهُ فِي أَرْحَامِهِنَّ}. وَيُذْكَرُ عَنْ عَلِيٍّ وَشُرَيْحٍ إِنِ امْرَأَةٌ جَاءَتْ بِبَيِّنَةٍ مِنْ بِطَانَةِ أَهْلِهَا مِمَّنْ يُرْضَى دِينُهُ، أَنَّهَا حَاضَتْ ثَلاَثًا فِي شَهْرٍ. صُدِّقَتْ.
وَقَالَ عَطَاءٌ أَقْرَاؤُهَا مَا كَانَتْ، وَبِهِ قَالَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ.
وَقَالَ عَطَاءٌ الْحَيْضُ يَوْمٌ إِلَى خَمْسَ عَشْرَةَ.
وَقَالَ مُعْتَمِرٌ عَنْ أَبِيهِ سَأَلْتُ ابْنَ سِيرِينَ عَنِ الْمَرْأَةِ تَرَى الدَّمَ بَعْدَ قَرْئِهَا بِخَمْسَةِ أَيَّامٍ قَالَ النِّسَاءُ أَعْلَمُ بِذَلِكَ.

chapters.title: Yellowish discharge not during the menses
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصُّفْرَةِ وَالْكُدْرَةِ فِي غَيْرِ أَيَّامِ الْحَيْضِ

chapters.title: Al-Istihadah (bleeding in between the periods is from a blood vessel)
chapters.title_arabic: باب عِرْقِ الاِسْتِحَاضَةِ

chapters.title: If a woman gets her menses after Tawaf-al-Ifada
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْمَرْأَةِ تَحِيضُ بَعْدَ الإِفَاضَةِ

chapters.title: When a woman having bleeding in between her periods notices signs of cleanliness from her menses
chapters.intro: Ibn ‘Abbas said: She should take a bath and offer Salat (prayers) even if (she were clean) for an hour and she can have (sexual relation with her husband) after the prayer, and As-Salat (the prayer) is more superior and important (than anything else).
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا رَأَتِ الْمُسْتَحَاضَةُ الطُّهْرَ
chapters.intro_arabic: قَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ تَغْتَسِلُ وَتُصَلِّي وَلَوْ سَاعَةً، وَيَأْتِيهَا زَوْجُهَا إِذَا صَلَّتْ، الصَّلاَةُ أَعْظَمُ.

chapters.title: The offering of a funeral prayer for a woman who had died (or after) delivery and its (i.e., funeral prayer's) legal way of performing
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّلاَةِ عَلَى النُّفَسَاءِ وَسُنَّتِهَا

chapters.title: Chapter:
chapters.title_arabic: باب

chapters.title: Chapter
chapters.title_arabic: باب

chapters.title: What to do if neither water nor earth is available
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا لَمْ يَجِدْ مَاءً وَلاَ تُرَابًا

chapters.title: The performance of Tayammum by a non-traveller (is permissible) when water is not available and when one is afraid that the time of Salat (prayer) may elapse
chapters.intro: 'Ata' supported that opinion. Al-Hasan says, "If a patient has water but there is no one to hand it over to him, then he can perform Tayammum ." Ibn 'Umar came from his land at Al-Jurf and the time for the `Asr prayer became due while he was at Marbad-An-Na`am (sheepfold), so he (performed Tayammum) and prayed there and then entered Medina when the sun was still high but he did not repeat that prayer.
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّيَمُّمِ فِي الْحَضَرِ، إِذَا لَمْ يَجِدِ الْمَاءَ، وَخَافَ فَوْتَ الصَّلاَةِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَبِهِ قَالَ عَطَاءٌ.
وَقَالَ الْحَسَنُ فِي الْمَرِيضِ عِنْدَهُ الْمَاءُ وَلاَ يَجِدُ مَنْ يُنَاوِلُهُ يَتَيَمَّمُ.
وَأَقْبَلَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ مِنْ أَرْضِهِ بِالْجُرُفِ، فَحَضَرَتِ الْعَصْرُ بِمَرْبَدِ النَّعَمِ فَصَلَّى، ثُمَّ دَخَلَ الْمَدِينَةَ وَالشَّمْسُ مُرْتَفِعَةٌ فَلَمْ يُعِدْ.

chapters.title: Can a person blow off the dust from his hands in performing Tayammum (before passing them over his face)
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْمُتَيَمِّمُ هَلْ يَنْفُخُ فِيهِمَا

chapters.title: Tayammum is for the hands and the face
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّيَمُّمُ لِلْوَجْهِ وَالْكَفَّيْنِ

chapters.title: Clean earth is sufficient for a Muslim as a substitute for water for ablution (if he does not find water)
chapters.intro: Al-Hasan said, “Tayammum is sufficient unless one does Hadath.” Ibn ‘Abbas led the Salat (prayer) with Tayammum. Yahya bin Sa‘id said, “There is no harm in offering Salat on a moorland (a barren salty land) and performing Tayammum with it.”
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّعِيدُ الطَّيِّبُ وَضُوءُ الْمُسْلِمِ ، يَكْفِيهِ مِنَ الْمَاءِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَقَالَ الْحَسَنُ يُجْزِئُهُ التَّيَمُّمُ مَا لَمْ يُحْدِثْ. وَأَمَّ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ وَهُوَ مُتَيَمِّمٌ.
وَقَالَ يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ لاَ بَأْسَ بِالصَّلاَةِ عَلَى السَّبَخَةِ وَالتَّيَمُّمِ بِهَا.

chapters.title: A Junub can perform Tayammum if he is afraid of disease, death or thirst
chapters.intro: It is said that once ‘Amr bin Al-‘As became Junub in a very cold night. He performed Tayammum and recited the following Verse from the Qur'an: “…And do not kill yourself (nor kill one another). Surely, Allah is Most Merciful to you.” (V.<qref chapter="4" verses="29">4:29</qref>) When that was reported to the Prophet ﷺ, he did not object to it.
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا خَافَ الْجُنُبُ عَلَى نَفْسِهِ الْمَرَضَ أَوِ الْمَوْتَ أَوْ خَافَ الْعَطَشَ، تَيَمَّمَ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَيُذْكَرُ أَنَّ عَمْرَو بْنَ الْعَاصِ أَجْنَبَ فِي لَيْلَةٍ بَارِدَةٍ فَتَيَمَّمَ وَتَلاَ: {وَلاَ تَقْتُلُوا أَنْفُسَكُمْ إِنَّ اللَّهَ كَانَ بِكُمْ رَحِيمًا} فَذَكَرَ لِلنَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فَلَمْ يُعَنِّفْ.

chapters.title: Tayammum with one light stroke (on the earth)
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّيَمُّمُ ضَرْبَةٌ

chapters.title: Chapter
chapters.title_arabic: باب

chapters.title: How As-Salat (the Prayer) was prescribed on the night of Al-Isra (miraculous night journey) of the Prophet (pbuh) to Jerusalem (and then to the heavens)
chapters.intro: Ibn ‘Abbas said: Abu Sufyan, when telling the narration of Heraclius, mentioned: “The Prophet ﷺ ordered us to offer our Salat (prayers) regularly and to be true and chaste.”
chapters.title_arabic: باب كَيْفَ فُرِضَتِ الصَّلاَةُ فِي الإِسْرَاءِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ حَدَّثَنِي أَبُو سُفْيَانَ فِي حَدِيثِ هِرَقْلَ فَقَالَ يَأْمُرُنَا- يَعْنِي النَّبِيَّ- صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ بِالصَّلاَةِ وَالصِّدْقِ وَالْعَفَافِ.

chapters.title: It is obligatory to wear clothes while offering As-Salat (the prayers)
chapters.intro:
And the Statement of Allah تعالى: “Take your adornment (by wearing your clean clothes covering completely the Aurah): [It means, while offering Salat (prayer), a male must cover himself with clothes from the umbilicus of his abdomen up to his knees, and it is better that both his shoulders should be covered. A female must cover all her body and feet, except the face, and it is better that both her hands are also covered.] While praying and going around (the Tawaf of) the Ka‘bah (V.7:31).

And offering Salat while wearing a single garment wrapped around the body, Salma bin Al-Akwa‘ narrated: The Prophet ﷺ said, “He should pin it, even if with a thorn.”

Offering Salat with the same garment in which one has had sexual intercourse is permissible if one does not see anything dirty on it.

The Prophet ﷺ ordered that no one should go around the Ka‘bah (perform Tawaf) in a naked state.
chapters.title_arabic: باب وُجُوبِ الصَّلاَةِ فِي الثِّيَابِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَقَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {خُذُوا زِينَتَكُمْ عِنْدَ كُلِّ مَسْجِدٍ}.
وَمَنْ صَلَّى مُلْتَحِفًا فِي ثَوْبٍ وَاحِدٍ، وَيُذْكَرُ عَنْ سَلَمَةَ بْنِ الأَكْوَعِ أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ قَالَ: «يَزُرُّهُ وَلَوْ بِشَوْكَةٍ». فِي إِسْنَادِهِ نَظَرٌ، وَمَنْ صَلَّى فِي الثَّوْبِ الَّذِي يُجَامِعُ فِيهِ مَا لَمْ يَرَ أَذًى، وَأَمَرَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ أَنْ لاَ يَطُوفَ بِالْبَيْتِ عُرْيَانٌ.

chapters.title: To tie Izar (dress worn below the waist) at one's back while offering Salat (prayers)
chapters.intro: Sahl said, “Some people offered Salat (prayer) with the Prophet ﷺ with their Izar tied to their necks.”
chapters.title_arabic: باب عَقْدِ الإِزَارِ عَلَى الْقَفَا فِي الصَّلاَةِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ أَبُو حَازِمٍ عَنْ سَهْلٍ صَلَّوْا مَعَ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ عَاقِدِي أُزْرِهِمْ عَلَى عَوَاتِقِهِمْ.

chapters.title: To offer As-Salat (the prayers) with a single garment wrapped round the body
chapters.intro: Umm Hani رضي الله عنها said that the Prophet ﷺ wrapped his body with a single garment and crossed its ends over his shoulders.
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّلاَةِ فِي الثَّوْبِ الْوَاحِدِ مُلْتَحِفًا بِهِ
chapters.intro_arabic: قَالَ الزُّهْرِيُّ فِي حَدِيثِهِ الْمُلْتَحِفُ الْمُتَوَشِّحُ، وَهْوَ الْمُخَالِفُ بَيْنَ طَرَفَيْهِ عَلَى عَاتِقَيْهِ، وَهْوَ الاِشْتِمَالُ عَلَى مَنْكِبَيْهِ. قَالَ وَقَالَتْ أُمُّ هَانِئٍ الْتَحَفَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ بِثَوْبٍ، وَخَالَفَ بَيْنَ طَرَفَيْهِ عَلَى عَاتِقَيْهِ.

chapters.title: If someone offers Salat (prayer) wrapped in a single garment, he should cross its corners round his shoulders
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا صَلَّى فِي الثَّوْبِ الْوَاحِدِ فَلْيَجْعَلْ عَلَى عَاتِقَيْهِ

chapters.title: If the garment is tight (over the body)
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا كَانَ الثَّوْبُ ضَيِّقًا

chapters.title: To offer As-Salat (the prayers) in a Syrian cloak (made by infidels)
chapters.intro: Al-Hasan said that there was no harm in wearing clothes woven by a Magian. And Ma'mar said that he had seen Az-Zuhri wearing Yemenite garments dyed with urine. And 'Ali offered Salat (prayer) in a new unwashed garment.
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّلاَةِ فِي الْجُبَّةِ الشَّأْمِيَّةِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَقَالَ الْحَسَنُ فِي الثِّيَابِ يَنْسُجُهَا الْمَجُوسِيُّ لَمْ يَرَ بِهَا بَأْسًا.
وَقَالَ مَعْمَرٌ رَأَيْتُ الزُّهْرِيَّ يَلْبَسُ مِنْ ثِيَابِ الْيَمَنِ مَا صُبِغَ بِالْبَوْلِ.
وَصَلَّى عَلِيٌّ فِي ثَوْبٍ غَيْرِ مَقْصُورٍ.

chapters.title: It is disliked to the naked during As-Salat (the prayers)
chapters.title_arabic: باب كَرَاهِيَةِ التَّعَرِّي فِي الصَّلاَةِ وَغَيْرِهَا

chapters.title: To offer Salat (prayer) with a shirt, trousers, a Tubban or a Qaba (an outer garment with full length sleeves)
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّلاَةِ فِي الْقَمِيصِ وَالسَّرَاوِيلِ وَالتُّبَّانِ وَالْقَبَاءِ

chapters.title: What may be used to cover the private parts of the body
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يَسْتُرُ مِنَ الْعَوْرَةِ

chapters.title: To pray without a Rida
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّلاَةِ بِغَيْرِ رِدَاءٍ

chapters.title: What is said about the thigh
chapters.intro: Narrated Ibn Abbas and Jarhad and Muhammad bin Jahsh The Prophet ﷺ said, “The thigh is Aurah (i.e., it is illegal to keep it bare).” And Anas bin Malik said, “The Prophet ﷺ uncovered his thigh.” The narration of Anas is dependable, but it would be safer to take Jarhad’s narration into consideration in order to get rid of the difference between them. Abu Musa said, “The Prophet ﷺ covered his knees when Uthman entered.” Zaid bin Thabit said, “Divine Revelation came to Allah’s Messenger ﷺ while his thigh was on my thigh and it became so heavy that I was afraid that it might break my thigh.”
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يُذْكَرُ فِي الْفَخِذِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَيُرْوَى عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ وَجَرْهَدٍ وَمُحَمَّدِ بْنِ جَحْشٍ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «الْفَخِذُ عَوْرَةٌ».
وَقَالَ أَنَسٌ حَسَرَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ عَنْ فَخِذِهِ. وَحَدِيثُ أَنَسٍ أَسْنَدُ، وَحَدِيثُ جَرْهَدٍ أَحْوَطُ حَتَّى يُخْرَجَ مِنِ اخْتِلاَفِهِمْ.
وَقَالَ أَبُو مُوسَى غَطَّى النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ رُكْبَتَيْهِ حِينَ دَخَلَ عُثْمَانُ.
وَقَالَ زَيْدُ بْنُ ثَابِتٍ أَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ عَلَى رَسُولِهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ وَفَخِذُهُ عَلَى فَخِذِي فَثَقُلَتْ عَلَيَّ حَتَّى خِفْتُ أَنْ تُرَضَّ فَخِذِي.

chapters.title: In how many (what sort of) clothes a woman should offer Salat (prayer)
chapters.intro: ‘Ikrima said, “If she can cover all her body with one garment, it is sufficient.”
chapters.title_arabic: باب فِي كَمْ تُصَلِّي الْمَرْأَةُ فِي الثِّيَابِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ عِكْرِمَةُ لَوْ وَارَتْ جَسَدَهَا فِي ثَوْبٍ لأَجَزْتُهُ.

chapters.title: If a person offered Salat (prayer) in a dress with marks and looked at those marks during the Salat
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا صَلَّى فِي ثَوْبٍ لَهُ أَعْلاَمٌ وَنَظَرَ إِلَى عَلَمِهَا

chapters.title: If someone offers Salat (prayer) in a garment bearing marks of a cross or picture, will he Salat be annulled? And what is forbidden thereof
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ إِنْ صَلَّى فِي ثَوْبٍ مُصَلَّبٍ أَوْ تَصَاوِيرَ هَلْ تَفْسُدُ صَلاَتُهُ وَمَا يُنْهَى عَنْ ذَلِكَ

chapters.title: Whoever offered Salat (prayer)in a silk Farruj (an outer garment opened at the back) and then took it off.
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ صَلَّى فِي فَرُّوجِ حَرِيرٍ ثُمَّ نَزَعَهُ

chapters.title: (It is permissible) to offer Salat (prayer) in a red garment
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّلاَةِ فِي الثَّوْبِ الأَحْمَرِ

chapters.title: (It is permissible) to offer Salat (prayer) on roofs, a pulpit or wood
chapters.intro: Al-Hasan finds no objection for one to offer Salat (prayer) over snow or bridges, even if urine were flowing underneath, or over, or in front of them as long as there was a Sutra (any object put in front of the praying person to act as symbolic barrier between him and others) in front of the person. Abu Hurairah رضي الله عنه offered Salat on the roof of the mosque with the Imam, and Ibn ‘Umar رضي الله عنه offered Salat on snow.
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّلاَةِ فِي السُّطُوحِ وَالْمِنْبَرِ وَالْخَشَبِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ وَلَمْ يَرَ الْحَسَنُ بَأْسًا أَنْ يُصَلَّى عَلَى الْجَمْدِ وَالْقَنَاطِرِ، وَإِنْ جَرَى تَحْتَهَا بَوْلٌ أَوْ فَوْقَهَا أَوْ أَمَامَهَا، إِذَا كَانَ بَيْنَهُمَا سُتْرَةٌ.
وَصَلَّى أَبُو هُرَيْرَةَ عَلَى ظَهْرِ الْمَسْجِدِ بِصَلاَةِ الإِمَامِ.
وَصَلَّى ابْنُ عُمَرَ عَلَى الثَّلْجِ.

chapters.title: If the clothes of a prayer person in prostration touched his wife [would that make his Salat (prayer) invalid]?
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا أَصَابَ ثَوْبُ الْمُصَلِّي امْرَأَتَهُ إِذَا سَجَدَ

chapters.title: To offer As-Salat (the prayers) on the Hasir (a mat that is made of the leaves of date-palm trees and is as long as or longer than a man's stature)
chapters.intro: Jabir and Abu Sa‘id offered Salat (prayers) standing on board a ship. Al-Hasan said, “If it is not hard for one’s companions, one may offer Salat standing and turn himself with its (ship’s) turnings; otherwise pray sitting.”
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّلاَةِ عَلَى الْحَصِيرِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَصَلَّى جَابِرٌ وَأَبُو سَعِيدٍ فِي السَّفِينَةِ قَائِمًا.
وَقَالَ الْحَسَنُ تُصَلِّي قَائِمًا مَا لَمْ تَشُقَّ عَلَى أَصْحَابِكَ، تَدُورُ مَعَهَا وَإِلاَّ فَقَاعِدًا.

chapters.title: To offer As-Salat (the prayers) on a Khumra (a small mat, hardly sufficient for the face and hands while prostrating during Salat)
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّلاَةِ عَلَى الْخُمْرَةِ

chapters.title: To offer As-Salat (the prayers) on the bed
chapters.intro: Anas offered Salat (prayer) on his bed. Anas said: We used to offer As-Salat (the prayer) with the Prophet ﷺ and prostrate on our clothes.
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّلاَةِ عَلَى الْفِرَاشِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَصَلَّى أَنَسٌ عَلَى فِرَاشِهِ.
وَقَالَ أَنَسٌ كُنَّا نُصَلِّي مَعَ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فَيَسْجُدُ أَحَدُنَا عَلَى ثَوْبِهِ.

chapters.title: To prostrate on a garment in scorching heat
chapters.intro: Al-Hasan said: People used to prostrate on their turbans and head-covers with their hands in their sleeves (because of scorching heat).
chapters.title_arabic: باب السُّجُودِ عَلَى الثَّوْبِ فِي شِدَّةِ الْحَرِّ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ الْحَسَنُ كَانَ الْقَوْمُ يَسْجُدُونَ عَلَى الْعِمَامَةِ وَالْقَلَنْسُوَةِ وَيَدَاهُ فِي كُمِّهِ.

chapters.title: To offer Salat (prayer) with the shoes on
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّلاَةِ فِي النِّعَالِ

chapters.title: To offer As-Salat (the prayers) wearing Khuff (leather socks)
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّلاَةِ فِي الْخِفَافِ

chapters.title: If someone does not prostrate properly
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا لَمْ يُتِمَّ السُّجُودَ

chapters.title: During Prostrations one should show his armpits and separate his forearms from his body
chapters.title_arabic: باب يُبْدِي ضَبْعَيْهِ وَيُجَافِي فِي السُّجُودِ

chapters.title: Superiority of (praying) facing the Qiblah with the toes toward it as well
chapters.intro: Abu Humaid said that referring to what the Prophet ﷺ said or used to do.
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ اسْتِقْبَالِ الْقِبْلَةِ
chapters.intro_arabic: يَسْتَقْبِلُ بِأَطْرَافِ رِجْلَيْهِ. قَالَ أَبُو حُمَيْدٍ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ.

chapters.title: The Qiblah for the people of Al-Madina, Sham and the East.
chapters.intro: The Qiblah is neither to the East nor to the West for the people of Al-Madina as the Prophet said to them, "Do not face Qiblah (Ka’bah at Makkah) during defecation and urination in an open space. Face either east or west."
chapters.title_arabic: باب قِبْلَةِ أَهْلِ الْمَدِينَةِ وَأَهْلِ الشَّأْمِ وَالْمَشْرِقِ
chapters.intro_arabic: لَيْسَ فِي الْمَشْرِقِ وَلاَ فِي الْمَغْرِبِ قِبْلَةٌ، لِقَوْلِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «لاَ تَسْتَقْبِلُوا الْقِبْلَةَ بِغَائِطٍ أَوْ بَوْلٍ وَلَكِنْ شَرِّقُوا أَوْ غَرِّبُوا».

chapters.title: The Statement of Allah " And take you (people) the Maqam (place) of Ibrahim (Abraham) (or the stone on which Abrahim stood while he was building the Kabah as a place of prayer (for some of your Salat e.g., two Raka after the Tawaf of Kabah)"
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {وَاتَّخِذُوا مِنْ مَقَامِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ مُصَلًّى}

chapters.title: [During the obligatory Salat (prayers)] one should face the Qiblah (Kabah at Makkah) wherever one may be
chapters.intro: Narrated Abu Hurairah رضي الله عنه: The Prophet said, "Face the Qiblah (Ka'bah at Makkah) and say Allahu Akbar."
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّوَجُّهِ نَحْوَ الْقِبْلَةِ حَيْثُ كَانَ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ أَبُو هُرَيْرَةَ قَالَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «اسْتَقْبِلِ الْقِبْلَةَ وَكَبِّرْ».

chapters.title: What has been said about (facing) the Qiblah (Kabah at Makkah) and wherever considered that there was no need to repeat the Salat (prayer) if someone offered prayers by mistake facing a direction other than that of the Qiblah
chapters.intro: When the Prophet ﷺ did Taslim after offering two Rak'a of Zuhr prayer he then faced the people and then completed the rest of the prayer.
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ مَا جَاءَ فِي الْقِبْلَةِ، وَمَنْ لاَ يَرَى الإِعَادَةَ عَلَى مَنْ سَهَا فَصَلَّى إِلَى غَيْرِ الْقِبْلَةِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَدْ سَلَّمَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فِي رَكْعَتَيِ الظُّهْرِ، وَأَقْبَلَ عَلَى النَّاسِ بِوَجْهِهِ، ثُمَّ أَتَمَّ مَا بَقِيَ.

chapters.title: To scrape off the sputum from the mosque with the hand (using some tool or other, or using n tool)
chapters.title_arabic: باب حَكِّ الْبُزَاقِ بِالْيَدِ مِنَ الْمَسْجِدِ

chapters.title: To scrape the nasal secretion off the mosque with gravel
chapters.intro: And Ibn 'Abbas said, "If you tread on (any) wet, filthy thing, wash it away and if it is dry don't wash it."
chapters.title_arabic: باب حَكِّ الْمُخَاطِ بِالْحَصَى مِنَ الْمَسْجِدِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ إِنْ وَطِئْتَ عَلَى قَذَرٍ رَطْبٍ فَاغْسِلْهُ، وَإِنْ كَانَ يَابِسًا فَلاَ.

chapters.title: It is forbidden to spit on the right side while in Salat (prayers)
chapters.title_arabic: باب لاَ يَبْصُقْ عَنْ يَمِينِهِ، فِي الصَّلاَةِ

chapters.title: One should spit on the left side or under one's left foot
chapters.title_arabic: باب لِيَبْزُقْ عَنْ يَسَارِهِ، أَوْ تَحْتَ قَدَمِهِ الْيُسْرَى

chapters.title: The expiation for spitting in the mosque
chapters.title_arabic: باب كَفَّارَةِ الْبُزَاقِ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ

chapters.title: The burying of the expectoration in the mosque
chapters.title_arabic: باب دَفْنِ النُّخَامَةِ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ

chapters.title: If the spit or sputum comes out suddenly then one should spit in the corner of one's garnet
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا بَدَرَهُ الْبُزَاقُ فَلْيَأْخُذْ بِطَرَفِ ثَوْبِهِ

chapters.title: Preaching of the Imam to the people regarding the proper offering of As-Salat (the prayers) and the mention of the Qiblah (Kabah at Makkah)
chapters.title_arabic: باب عِظَةِ الإِمَامِ النَّاسَ فِي إِتْمَامِ الصَّلاَةِ، وَذِكْرِ الْقِبْلَةِ

chapters.title: It is permissible to say, "Masjid (mosque) of Bani so-and-so?
chapters.title_arabic: باب هَلْ يُقَالُ مَسْجِدُ بَنِي فُلاَنٍ

chapters.title: The distribution (of goods or wealh) and the hanging of a cluster of dates in the mosque.
chapters.intro: Abu ‘Abdullah (Bukhari) said: Qinw means bunch and its dual form is qinwan, and its plural is also qinwan, as with the words sinw (singular) and sinwan (dual and plural).
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْقِسْمَةِ وَتَعْلِيقِ الْقِنْوِ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ
chapters.intro_arabic: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْقِنْوُ الْعِذْقُ، وَالاِثْنَانِ قِنْوَانِ، وَالْجَمَاعَةُ أَيْضًا قِنْوَانٌ مِثْلَ صِنْوٍ وَصِنْوَانٍ.

chapters.title: Receivng and invitation to dinner in the mosque and accepting it
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ دَعَا لِطَعَامٍ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ وَمَنْ أَجَابَ فِيهِ

chapters.title: To give the judicial verdicts in the mosque and to perform the Al-Lian between men and women (husbands and wives) there
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْقَضَاءِ وَاللِّعَانِ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ بَيْنَ الرِّجَالِ وَالنِّسَاءِ

chapters.title: If someone enters a house, should he offer prayers where he likes, or as he is told? And he should not look out to seek information about the place or do spying
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا دَخَلَ بَيْتًا يُصَلِّي حَيْثُ شَاءَ، أَوْ حَيْثُ أُمِرَ، وَلاَ يَتَجَسَّسُ

chapters.title: About (taking) the mosques in the houses
chapters.intro: And Al-Bara' bin 'Azib offered Salat (prayers) in the mosque in his house with other people in congregation.
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْمَسَاجِدِ فِي الْبُيُوتِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَصَلَّى الْبَرَاءُ بْنُ عَازِبٍ فِي مَسْجِدِهِ فِي دَارِهِ جَمَاعَةً.

chapters.title: While entering the mosques etc. one should start with the right foot
chapters.intro: And 'Abdullah bin 'Umar used to enter the mosque by putting in his right foot first and while leaving he used to put out his left foot first.
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّيَمُّنِ فِي دُخُولِ الْمَسْجِدِ وَغَيْرِهِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَكَانَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ يَبْدَأُ بِرِجْلِهِ الْيُمْنَى، فَإِذَا خَرَجَ بَدَأَ بِرِجْلِهِ الْيُسْرَى.

chapters.title: Is it permissible to dig the graves of pagans of the period of Ignorance, and to use that place as a mosque?
chapters.intro: And the saying of the Prophet ﷺ "Allah cursed the Jews because they built the places of worship at the graves of their Prophets." And what is said regarding the disapproval of offering Salat (prayers) at graves. And 'Umar saw Anas bin Malik offering Salat at a grave and shouted, "The grave! The grave!! (meaning: Do not offer Salat there)." But he did not order him to repeat his Salat.
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ هَلْ تُنْبَشُ قُبُورُ مُشْرِكِي الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ، وَيُتَّخَذُ مَكَانَهَا مَسَاجِدَ
chapters.intro_arabic:
لِقَوْلِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «لَعَنَ اللَّهُ الْيَهُودَ، اتَّخَذُوا قُبُورَ أَنْبِيَائِهِمْ مَسَاجِدَ». وَمَا يُكْرَهُ مِنَ الصَّلاَةِ فِي الْقُبُورِ.
وَرَأَى عُمَرُ أَنَسَ بْنَ مَالِكٍ يُصَلِّي عِنْدَ قَبْرٍ فَقَالَ الْقَبْرَ الْقَبْرَ. وَلَمْ يَأْمُرْهُ بِالإِعَادَةِ.

chapters.title: To offer As-Salat (the prayer) in a sheep-fold
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّلاَةِ فِي مَرَابِضِ الْغَنَمِ

chapters.title: To offer As-Salat (the prayer) in the camel yards (the places where the camels are stationed)
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّلاَةِ فِي مَوَاضِعِ الإِبِلِ

chapters.title: Whoever offered Salat (prayer) with furnace or fire or any other worshipable thing in front of him but he intended Salat solely for Allah
chapters.intro: Az-Zuhri narrated that Anas said that the Prophet ﷺ said: "While I was offering Salat (prayer), the (Hell) Fire was displayed in front of me."
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ صَلَّى وَقُدَّامَهُ تَنُّورٌ أَوْ نَارٌ أَوْ شَىْءٌ مِمَّا يُعْبَدُ، فَأَرَادَ بِهِ اللَّهَ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ الزُّهْرِيُّ: أَخْبَرَنِي أَنَسٌ قَالَ: قَالَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «عُرِضَتْ عَلَيَّ النَّارُ وَأَنَا أُصَلِّي».

chapters.title: The dislikeness of offering As-Salat (the prayers) in grave-yards
chapters.title_arabic: باب كَرَاهِيَةِ الصَّلاَةِ فِي الْمَقَابِرِ

chapters.title: (What is said about) offering Salat (Prayer) at the places where the earth had sunk down and Allah's punishment had fallen
chapters.intro: It is said that 'Ali رضي الله عنه disliked offering As-Salat (the prayers) in the land of Babylon which had sunk down.
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّلاَةِ فِي مَوَاضِعِ الْخَسْفِ وَالْعَذَابِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَيُذْكَرُ أَنَّ عَلِيًّا رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ كَرِهَ الصَّلاَةَ بِخَسْفِ بَابِلَ.

chapters.title: To offer As-Salat (the prayer) in a church or in a temple etc.
chapters.intro: 'Umar رضي الله عنه said, "We do not enter your churches because of the statues and pictures." Ibn 'Abbas used to offer Salat (prayers) in the church provided there were no statues in it.
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّلاَةِ فِي الْبِيعَةِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَقَالَ عُمَرُ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ إِنَّا لاَ نَدْخُلُ كَنَائِسَكُمْ مِنْ أَجْلِ التَّمَاثِيلِ الَّتِي فِيهَا الصُّوَرَ.
وَكَانَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ يُصَلِّي فِي الْبِيعَةِ إِلاَّ بِيعَةً فِيهَا تَمَاثِيلُ.

chapters.title: Chapter
chapters.title_arabic: باب

chapters.title: The sayings of the Prophet (pbuh) "The earth has been made for me a Masjid (place for praying) and a thing to purify (to perform Tayammum)."
chapters.title_arabic: باب قَوْلِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم ‏"‏ جُعِلَتْ لِيَ الأَرْضُ مَسْجِدًا وَطَهُورًا ‏"‏

chapters.title: Sleeping of a woman in the mosque (and residing in it)
chapters.title_arabic: باب نَوْمِ الْمَرْأَةِ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ

chapters.title: Sleeping of a men in the mosque
chapters.intro: And narrated Anas, "Some people of the tribe of 'Ukl came to the Prophet ﷺ and joined the men of As-Suffa." 'Abdul Rahman bin Abi Bakr said, "Ashab-as-Suffa (Suffa companions) were poor people."
chapters.title_arabic: باب نَوْمِ الرِّجَالِ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَقَالَ أَبُو قِلاَبَةَ عَنْ أَنَسٍ قَدِمَ رَهْطٌ مِنْ عُكْلٍ عَلَى النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فَكَانُوا فِي الصُّفَّةِ.
وَقَالَ عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ أَبِي بَكْرٍ كَانَ أَصْحَابُ الصُّفَّةِ الْفُقَرَاءَ.

chapters.title: To offer As-Salat (the prayer) whe returning from a journey
chapters.intro: Ka'b bin Malik said: "Whenever the Prophet ﷺ returned from a journey, he entered the mosque and offered prayers in it."
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّلاَةِ إِذَا قَدِمَ مِنْ سَفَرٍ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ كَعْبُ بْنُ مَالِكٍ كَانَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ إِذَا قَدِمَ مِنْ سَفَرٍ بَدَأَ بِالْمَسْجِدِ فَصَلَّى فِيهِ.

chapters.title: If one entered a mosque, one should offer two Rak'a (Tahayyat-al-Masjid) before sitting
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا دَخَلَ الْمَسْجِدَ فَلْيَرْكَعْ رَكْعَتَيْنِ

chapters.title: Al-Hadath (passing wind) in the mosque
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْحَدَثِ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ

chapters.title: The construction of (the prophet's pbuh) mosque
chapters.intro: Abu Sa'id said, "The roof of the mosque was made of the leaves of date-palms." 'Umar ordered the Prophet's ﷺ mosque to be expanded (built) and said, "Protect the people from rain. Beware of red and yellow decorations, for they put the people to trial." Anas reciting a part of a Hadith said, "They will boast of them (mosques) rather than coming frequently to them for offering prayers." Ibn 'Abbas said, "You (Muslims) will surely decorate your mosques as the Jews and Christians decorated (their churches and temples)."
chapters.title_arabic: باب بُنْيَانِ الْمَسْجِدِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَقَالَ أَبُو سَعِيدٍ كَانَ سَقْفُ الْمَسْجِدِ مِنْ جَرِيدِ النَّخْلِ. وَأَمَرَ عُمَرُ بِبِنَاءِ الْمَسْجِدِ وَقَالَ أَكِنَّ النَّاسَ مِنَ الْمَطَرِ، وَإِيَّاكَ أَنْ تُحَمِّرَ أَوْ تُصَفِّرَ، فَتَفْتِنَ النَّاسَ.
وَقَالَ أَنَسٌ يَتَبَاهَوْنَ بِهَا، ثُمَّ لاَ يَعْمُرُونَهَا إِلاَّ قَلِيلاً.
وَقَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ لَتُزَخْرِفُنَّهَا كَمَا زَخْرَفَتِ الْيَهُودُ وَالنَّصَارَى.

chapters.title: To co-operate in building a mosque
chapters.intro:
"It is not for Mushrikun (polytheists, idolaters, pagans, disbelievers in the Oneness of Allah) to maintain the Mosques of Allah (i.e., to pray and worship Allah therein, to look after their cleanliness and their building), while they witness against their ownselves of disbelief. The works of such are in vain, and in Fire shall they abide.

The Mosques of Allah shall be maintained only by those who believe in Allah and the Last Day, perform Salat (Iqamat-as-Salat) and give Zakat and fear none but Allah. It is they who are on true guidance." (V.9:17,18).
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّعَاوُنِ فِي بِنَاءِ الْمَسْجِدِ
chapters.intro_arabic: {مَا كَانَ لِلْمُشْرِكِينَ أَنْ يَعْمُرُوا مَسَاجِدَ اللَّهِ شَاهِدِينَ عَلَى أَنْفُسِهِمْ بِالْكُفْرِ أُولَئِكَ حَبِطَتْ أَعْمَالُهُمْ وَفِي النَّارِ هُمْ خَالِدُونَ إِنَّمَا يَعْمُرُ مَسَاجِدَ اللَّهِ مَنْ آمَنَ بِاللَّهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الآخِرِ وَأَقَامَ الصَّلاَةَ وَآتَى الزَّكَاةَ وَلَمْ يَخْشَ إِلاَّ اللَّهَ فَعَسَى أُولَئِكَ أَنْ يَكُونُوا مِنَ الْمُهْتَدِينَ}.

chapters.title: Employing the carpenter and the technical hand (artsan) in making the wooden pulpit or building the mosque
chapters.title_arabic: باب الاِسْتِعَانَةِ بِالنَّجَّارِ وَالصُّنَّاعِ فِي أَعْوَادِ الْمِنْبَرِ وَالْمَسْجِدِ

chapters.title: (The superiority of) whoever built a mosque
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ بَنَى مَسْجِدًا

chapters.title: While passing through a mosque, (one should better) hold the arrowheads (with the hand)
chapters.title_arabic: باب يَأْخُذُ بِنُصُولِ النَّبْلِ إِذَا مَرَّ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ

chapters.title: Passing through the mosque (is permissible)
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْمُرُورِ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ

chapters.title: (What is said about) reciting poetry in the mosque?
chapters.title_arabic: باب الشِّعْرِ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ

chapters.title: The presence of spearmen (with their spears) in the mosque (is permissible)
chapters.title_arabic: باب أَصْحَابِ الْحِرَابِ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ

chapters.title: Mentioning about sales and purchases on the pulpit in the mosque
chapters.title_arabic: باب ذِكْرِ الْبَيْعِ وَالشِّرَاءِ عَلَى الْمِنْبَرِ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ

chapters.title: Asking a debtor to repay what he owes and catching the debtor in the mosque
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّقَاضِي وَالْمُلاَزَمَةِ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ

chapters.title: Sweeping (cleaning) of the mosque and removing rags, dirt and sticks from it
chapters.title_arabic: باب كَنْسِ الْمَسْجِدِ وَالْتِقَاطِ الْخِرَقِ وَالْقَذَى وَالْعِيدَانِ

chapters.title: The order of banning the trade of alcoholic drinks was issues in the mosque
chapters.title_arabic: باب تَحْرِيمِ تِجَارَةِ الْخَمْرِ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ

chapters.title: Servants for the mosque
chapters.intro: Ibn 'Abbas referred to the Verse: "... I have vowed to You what (the child that) is in my womb to be dedicated for Your services (free from all wordly work, to serve Your place of worship)..." (V.<qref chapter="3" verses="35">3:35</qref>)
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْخَدَمِ لِلْمَسْجِدِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ: {نَذَرْتُ لَكَ مَا فِي بَطْنِي مُحَرَّرًا} لِلْمَسْجِدِ يَخْدُمُهُ.

chapters.title: To fasten a prisoner or a debtor in the mosque
chapters.title_arabic: باب الأَسِيرِ أَوِ الْغَرِيمِ يُرْبَطُ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ

chapters.title: To take a bath on embracing Islam and fasten a prisoner in the mosque
chapters.intro: Shuraih used to order the offender or debtor to be fastened to one of the pillars of the mosque.
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ الاِغْتِسَالِ إِذَا أَسْلَمَ، وَرَبْطِ الأَسِيرِ أَيْضًا فِي الْمَسْجِدِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَكَانَ شُرَيْحٌ يَأْمُرُ الْغَرِيمَ أَنْ يُحْبَسَ إِلَى سَارِيَةِ الْمَسْجِدِ.

chapters.title: To pitch a tent in the mosque for patients, etc
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْخَيْمَةِ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ لِلْمَرْضَى وَغَيْرِهِمْ

chapters.title: To take the camel inside the mosque if necessary
chapters.intro: And Ibn Abbas said: The Prophet ﷺ performed the Tawaf while riding a camel.
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِدْخَالِ الْبَعِيرِ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ لِلْعِلَّةِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ طَافَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ عَلَى بَعِيرٍ.

chapters.title: Chaper
chapters.title_arabic: باب

chapters.title: Al-Khaukhah (a small door) and a path in the mosque
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْخَوْخَةِ وَالْمَمَرِّ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ

chapters.title: The doors and locks of the Kabah and the mosques
chapters.intro: Narrated Ibn ‘Juraij: Ibn Abi Mulaika said to me, “O ‘Abdul Malik! I wish that you had seen the mosque of Ibn ‘Abbas and its doors.”
chapters.title_arabic: باب الأَبْوَابِ وَالْغَلَقِ لِلْكَعْبَةِ وَالْمَسَاجِدِ
chapters.intro_arabic: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ وَقَالَ لِي عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ قَالَ: قَالَ لِي ابْنُ أَبِي مُلَيْكَةَ يَا عَبْدَ الْمَلِكِ، لَوْ رَأَيْتَ مَسَاجِدَ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ وَأَبْوَابَهَا.

chapters.title: The entering of a pagan in the mosque
chapters.title_arabic: باب دُخُولِ الْمُشْرِكِ الْمَسْجِدَ

chapters.title: Raising the voice in the mosque
chapters.title_arabic: باب رَفْعِ الصَّوْتِ فِي الْمَسَاجِدِ

chapters.title: The religious gathering in circles and sitting in the mosque
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْحِلَقِ وَالْجُلُوسِ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ

chapters.title: To lie flat (on the back) in the mosque
chapters.title_arabic: باب الاِسْتِلْقَاءِ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ وَمَدِّ الرِّجْلِ

chapters.title: (If) a mosque (is built) on a road, it should not be a cause of harm for the people
chapters.intro: Al-Hasan, Ayyub and Malik held the same view.
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْمَسْجِدِ يَكُونُ فِي الطَّرِيقِ مِنْ غَيْرِ ضَرَرٍ بِالنَّاسِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَبِهِ قَالَ الْحَسَنُ وَأَيُّوبُ وَمَالِكٌ.

chapters.title: To offer As-Salat (the prayers) in a mosque situated in a market
chapters.intro: Ibn ‘Aun offered prayers in a mosque situated in a house and the gate used to be closed while they were inside.
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّلاَةِ فِي مَسْجِدِ السُّوقِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَصَلَّى ابْنُ عَوْنٍ فِي مَسْجِدٍ فِي دَارٍ يُغْلَقُ عَلَيْهِمُ الْبَابُ.

chapters.title: To clasp one's hands by interlocking the fingers in the mosque or outside the mosque
chapters.title_arabic: باب تَشْبِيكِ الأَصَابِعِ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ وَغَيْرِهِ

chapters.title: The mosques which are on the way to Al-Madina and the places where the Prophet (pbuh) had offered Salat (Prayers)
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ الْمَسَاجِدِ الَّتِي عَلَى طُرُقِ الْمَدِينَةِ، وَالْمَوَاضِعِ الَّتِي صَلَّى فِيهَا النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ

chapters.title: The Sutra of the Imam is also a Sutra for those who are behind him
chapters.title_arabic: باب سُتْرَةُ الإِمَامِ سُتْرَةُ مَنْ خَلْفَهُ

chapters.title: What should be the distance between the person offering Salat (prayer) and the Sutra?
chapters.title_arabic: باب قَدْرِ كَمْ يَنْبَغِي أَنْ يَكُونَ بَيْنَ الْمُصَلِّي وَالسُّتْرَةِ

chapters.title: To offer As-Salat (the prayer) using a Harba (a short spear) (as a Sutra)
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّلاَةِ إِلَى الْحَرْبَةِ

chapters.title: To offer As-Salat (the prayer) using an 'Anaza (a spear-headed stick) (as a Sutra)
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّلاَةِ إِلَى الْعَنَزَةِ

chapters.title: Sutra (for the prayer)in Makkah and elsewhere.
chapters.title_arabic: باب السُّتْرَةِ بِمَكَّةَ وَغَيْرِهَا

chapters.title: To offer As-Salat (the prayer) facing a pillar
chapters.intro: ‘Umar said, “The people offering As-Salat (the prayer) have got more right to pray behind the pillars of the mosque than those who are talking.” When ‘Umar saw a person Salat (prayer) between two pillars, he brought him close to a pillar and told him to pray behind it.
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّلاَةِ إِلَى الأُسْطُوَانَةِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ عُمَرُ الْمُصَلُّونَ أَحَقُّ بِالسَّوَارِي مِنَ الْمُتَحَدِّثِينَ إِلَيْهَا. وَرَأَى عُمَرُ رَجُلاً يُصَلِّي بَيْنَ أُسْطُوَانَتَيْنِ فَأَدْنَاهُ إِلَى سَارِيَةٍ فَقَالَ صَلِّ إِلَيْهَا.

chapters.title: To offer non-congregational As-Salat (the prayers) between the pillars
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّلاَةِ بَيْنَ السَّوَارِي فِي غَيْرِ جَمَاعَةٍ

chapters.title: Chapter
chapters.title_arabic: باب

chapters.title: To offer As-Salat (the prayer) facing a Rahila camel, a tree or a camel saddle as a Sutra).
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّلاَةِ إِلَى الرَّاحِلَةِ وَالْبَعِيرِ وَالشَّجَرِ وَالرَّحْلِ

chapters.title: To offer As-Salat (the prayer) facing a bed
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّلاَةِ إِلَى السَّرِيرِ

chapters.title: The person offering Salat (prayer) should repulse that person who tries to pass in front of him
chapters.intro: While sitting in Tashah-hud [a specific sitting position adopted by a person during the Salat (prayer)] and while in the Ka‘bah Ibn ‘Umar repulsed a man (who tried to pass in front of him). He used to say, “Use force if that person refuses to retreat.”
chapters.title_arabic: باب يَرُدُّ الْمُصَلِّي مَنْ مَرَّ بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَرَدَّ ابْنُ عُمَرَ فِي التَّشَهُّدِ وَفِي الْكَعْبَةِ وَقَالَ إِنْ أَبَى إِلاَّ أَنْ تُقَاتِلَهُ فَقَاتِلْهُ.

chapters.title: The sin of a person who passes in front of a person offering Salat
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِثْمِ الْمَارِّ بَيْنَ يَدَىِ الْمُصَلِّي

chapters.title: A man facing a man while offering Salat (prayer)
chapters.intro: 'Uthman disliked to face a praying person if it diverted his attention. Zaid bin Thabit said, "But if it does not have such an effect, a man does not cancel the Salat (prayers) of another man."
chapters.title_arabic: باب اسْتِقْبَالِ الرَّجُلِ صَاحِبَهُ أَوْ غَيْرَهُ فِي صَلاَتِهِ وَهُوَ يُصَلِّي
chapters.intro_arabic: وَكَرِهَ عُثْمَانُ أَنْ يُسْتَقْبَلَ الرَّجُلُ وَهُوَ يُصَلِّي، وَإِنَّمَا هَذَا إِذَا اشْتَغَلَ بِهِ، فَأَمَّا إِذَا لَمْ يَشْتَغِلْ فَقَدْ قَالَ زَيْدُ بْنُ ثَابِتٍ مَا بَالَيْتُ إِنَّ الرَّجُلَ لاَ يَقْطَعُ صَلاَةَ الرَّجُلِ.

chapters.title: To offer As-Salat (the prayer) behind a sleeping person
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّلاَةِ خَلْفَ النَّائِمِ

chapters.title: To offer Nawafil (non-obligatory prayers) behind a sleeping woman
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّطَوُّعِ خَلْفَ الْمَرْأَةِ

chapters.title: Whoever says "Nothing annuls As-Salat (the prayer) (i.e. nothing of what others do, not the praying person himself)."
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ قَالَ لاَ يَقْطَعُ الصَّلاَةَ شَىْءٌ

chapters.title: If a small girl is carried on one's neck during the Salat (prayer)
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا حَمَلَ جَارِيَةً صَغِيرَةً عَلَى عُنُقِهِ فِي الصَّلاَةِ

chapters.title: To offer Salat (prayer) facing a bed occupied by a menstruatng woman
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا صَلَّى إِلَى فِرَاشٍ فِيهِ حَائِضٌ

chapters.title: Is it permissible to touch or push one's wife in prostration in order to prostrate properly?
chapters.title_arabic: باب هَلْ يَغْمِزُ الرَّجُلُ امْرَأَتَهُ عِنْدَ السُّجُودِ لِكَىْ يَسْجُدَ

chapters.title: A woman can remove troublesome or offensive things from a person in Salat (prayer)
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْمَرْأَةِ تَطْرَحُ عَنِ الْمُصَلِّي، شَيْئًا مِنَ الأَذَى

chapters.title: The times of As-Salat (the prayers) and the superiority of offering Salat (prayers) in time
chapters.intro: And the Statement of Allah: "...Verily, As-Salat (the prayer) is enjoined on the believers at fixed hours." (V <qref chapter="4" verses="103">4:103</qref>)
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَوَاقِيتِ الصَّلاَةِ وَفَضْلِهَا
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَوْلِهِ: {إِنَّ الصَّلاَةَ كَانَتْ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ كِتَابًا مَوْقُوتًا} وَقَّتَهُ عَلَيْهِمْ.

chapters.title: The Statement of Allah عز وجل: (And remain always) turning in repentance to Him (only), and be afraid and dutiful to Him; and perform As-Salat (Iqamat-as-Salat) and be not of Al-Mushrikun (the disbelievers in the Oneness of Allah, polytheists, idolaters). (V.<qref chapter="30" verses="31">30:31</qref>)
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ: {مُنِيبِينَ إِلَيْهِ وَاتَّقُوهُ وَأَقِيمُوا الصَّلاَةَ وَلاَ تَكُونُوا مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ}

chapters.title: To give the Bai'ah (pledge) for Iqamat-as-Salat [the offering of As-Salat (the prayers).
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْبَيْعَةِ عَلَى إِقَامَةِ الصَّلاَةِ

chapters.title: As-Salat (the prayers) is expiation (of sins)
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّلاَةُ كَفَّارَةٌ

chapters.title: Superiority of offering As-Salat (the prayers) at the stated times
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ الصَّلاَةِ لِوَقْتِهَا

chapters.title: The five Salat (prayers) are expiations (of sins)
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّلَوَاتُ الْخَمْسُ كَفَّارَةٌ

chapters.title: Not offering As-Salat (the prayers) at its stated fixed time
chapters.title_arabic: باب تَضْيِيعِ الصَّلاَةِ عَنْ وَقْتِهَا

chapters.title: A person in Salat (prayer) is speaking in private to his Lord (Allah)
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْمُصَلِّي يُنَاجِي رَبَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ

chapters.title: In severe heat, offer Zuhr prayers when it becomes (a bit) cooler
chapters.title_arabic: باب الإِبْرَادِ بِالظُّهْرِ فِي شِدَّةِ الْحَرِّ

chapters.title: When going on a journey, pray Zuhr prayer when it becomes cooler
chapters.title_arabic: باب الإِبْرَادِ بِالظُّهْرِ فِي السَّفَرِ

chapters.title: The time of Zuhr prayer is when the sun declines (just after mid-day)
chapters.intro: Jabir said: The Prophet ﷺ used to offer the Zuhr prayer just after mid-day (as the sun declines at noon.)
chapters.title_arabic: باب وَقْتِ الظُّهْرِ عِنْدَ الزَّوَالِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ جَابِرٌ كَانَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يُصَلِّي بِالْهَاجِرَةِ.

chapters.title: To deny the Zuhr (prayer) up to the 'Asr (prayer) time
chapters.title_arabic: باب تَأْخِيرِ الظُّهْرِ إِلَى الْعَصْرِ

chapters.title: The time of the Asr prayer
chapters.intro: Narrated Hisham (that Aishah رضي الله عنها said) "Sunshine used to be still inside my chamber (i.e., at the time of Asr prayer).
chapters.title_arabic: باب وَقْتِ الْعَصْرِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقالَ أَبُو أٌسَامَةَ عَنْ هِشَامٍ: مِنْ قَعْرِ حُجْرَتِها

chapters.title: The sin of one who misses the 'Asr prayer (intentionally)
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِثْمِ مَنْ فَاتَتْهُ الْعَصْرُ

chapters.title: One who omits (does not offer) the 'Asr prayer (intentionally)
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ تَرَكَ الْعَصْرَ

chapters.title: Superiority of the 'Asr prayer
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ صَلاَةِ الْعَصْرِ

chapters.title: Whoever got (or was able to offer) only one Rak'a of the 'Asr prayer before sunset
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ أَدْرَكَ رَكْعَةً مِنَ الْعَصْرِ قَبْلَ الْغُرُوبِ

chapters.title: The time of the Maghrib prayer (evening prayer)
chapters.intro: ‘Ata' said: "A person who is sick can offer Maghrib and Isha' prayers together."
chapters.title_arabic: باب وَقْتِ الْمَغْرِبِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ عَطَاءٌ يَجْمَعُ الْمَرِيضُ بَيْنَ الْمَغْرِبِ وَالْعِشَاءِ.

chapters.title: Whoever disliked to call the Maghrib prayer as the 'Isha' prayer
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ كَرِهَ أَنْ يُقَالَ لِلْمَغْرِبِ الْعِشَاءُ

chapters.title: The mention of 'Isha' and 'Atama and whoever took the two names as one and the same
chapters.intro:
Narrated Abu Hurairah رضي الله عنه: The Prophet ﷺ said, "The most difficult and the hardest Salat (prayers) for the hypocrites are the Isha' (prayer) and the Fajr (prayer)." He added, "Had they known what is (the reward of) the Atama ('Isha') and the Fajr (prayers) they would have come to attend them even if they had to crawl."

Few discussions about Maghrib, 'Atama i.e. 'Isha', which are merely repetition of the topic of this chapter, are mentioned here in the Arabic text.

Abu Abdullah (Bukhari) said: It is better that one should call it Isha because of the words of Allah, the Most High: “…and after the Isha prayer” (the Quran, 24:58). It has been related of Abu Musa that he said: We used to take turns to be with the Prophet ﷺ at Isha prayer and he would say it late. Ibn Abbas and Aishah said: The Prophet ﷺ delayed the Isha prayer. Some reported from Aishah that the Prophet ﷺ delayed the Atamah prayer. Jabir said: The Prophet ﷺ used to say the Isha prayer. Abu Barzah said: The Prophet ﷺ used to delay the Isha prayer. Anas said: The Prophet ﷺ delayed the late Isha prayer. Ibn Umar, Abu Ayyub and Ibn Abbas said: The Prophet ﷺ said the Maghrib and the Isha prayers.
chapters.title_arabic: باب ذِكْرِ الْعِشَاءِ وَالْعَتَمَةِ وَمَنْ رَآهُ وَاسِعًا
chapters.intro_arabic:
قَالَ أَبُو هُرَيْرَةَ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «أَثْقَلُ الصَّلاَةِ عَلَى الْمُنَافِقِينَ الْعِشَاءُ وَالْفَجْرُ».
وَقَالَ: «لَوْ يَعْلَمُونَ مَا فِي الْعَتَمَةِ وَالْفَجْرِ». قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ وَالاِخْتِيَارُ أَنْ يَقُولَ الْعِشَاءُ لِقَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى: {وَمِنْ بَعْدِ صَلاَةِ الْعِشَاءِ}. وَيُذْكَرُ عَنْ أَبِي مُوسَى قَالَ كُنَّا نَتَنَاوَبُ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ عِنْدَ صَلاَةِ الْعِشَاءِ فَأَعْتَمَ بِهَا.
وَقَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ وَعَائِشَةُ أَعْتَمَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ بِالْعِشَاءِ.
وَقَالَ بَعْضُهُمْ عَنْ عَائِشَةَ أَعْتَمَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ بِالْعَتَمَةِ.
وَقَالَ جَابِرٌ كَانَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يُصَلِّي الْعِشَاءَ.
وَقَالَ أَبُو بَرْزَةَ كَانَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يُؤَخِّرُ الْعِشَاءَ.
وَقَالَ أَنَسٌ أَخَّرَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ الْعِشَاءَ الآخِرَةَ.
وَقَالَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ وَأَبُو أَيُّوبَ وَابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمْ صَلَّى النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ الْمَغْرِبَ وَالْعِشَاءَ.

chapters.title: The time of the Isha prayer. If the people get together (pray earlier), and if they come late (delay it)
chapters.title_arabic: باب وَقْتِ الْعِشَاءِ إِذَا اجْتَمَعَ النَّاسُ أَوْ تَأَخَّرُوا

chapters.title: Superiority of the 'Isha prayer
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ الْعِشَاءِ

chapters.title: What is disliked about sleeping before the 'Isha prayer
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يُكْرَهُ مِنَ النَّوْمِ قَبْلَ الْعِشَاءِ

chapters.title: Sleeping before the 'Isha prayer if (one is) over-whelmed by it (sleep)
chapters.title_arabic: باب النَّوْمِ قَبْلَ الْعِشَاءِ لِمَنْ غُلِبَ

chapters.title: Time of the 'Isha prayer is up to the middle of the night
chapters.intro: And Abu Barza said that the Prophet ﷺ used to prefer to pray 'Isha' late.
chapters.title_arabic: باب وَقْتِ الْعِشَاءِ إِلَى نِصْفِ اللَّيْلِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ أَبُو بَرْزَةَ كَانَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يَسْتَحِبُّ تَأْخِيرَهَا.

chapters.title: Superiority of the Fajr (early morning) prayer
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ صَلاَةِ الْفَجْرِ

chapters.title: Time of the Fajr (early morning) prayer
chapters.title_arabic: باب وَقْتِ الْفَجْرِ

chapters.title: Whoever got (or was able to offer) one Rak'a of the Fajr prayer (in time). [One Rak'a means, one standing, one bowing, and two prostrations].
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ أَدْرَكَ مِنَ الْفَجْرِ رَكْعَةً

chapters.title: Whoever got (or was able to offer) one Rak'a of a prayer (in time)
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ أَدْرَكَ مِنَ الصَّلاَةِ رَكْعَةً

chapters.title: What is said regarding the offering of As-Salat (the prayers) between the Fajr prayer and sunrise.
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّلاَةِ بَعْدَ الْفَجْرِ حَتَّى تَرْتَفِعَ الشَّمْسُ

chapters.title: One should not try to offer As-Salat (the prayers) just before sunset
chapters.title_arabic: باب لاَ يَتَحَرَّى الصَّلاَةَ قَبْلَ غُرُوبِ الشَّمْسِ

chapters.title: Whoever did not dislike to offer optional prayers except after the compulsory prayers of 'Asr and Fajr only
chapters.intro: This has been narrated by 'Umar, Ibn 'Umar, Abu Sa'id and Abu Hurairah.
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ لَمْ يَكْرَهِ الصَّلاَةَ إِلاَّ بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ وَالْفَجْرِ
chapters.intro_arabic: رَوَاهُ عُمَرُ وَابْنُ عُمَرَ وَأَبُو سَعِيدٍ وَأَبُو هُرَيْرَةَ.

chapters.title: To offer the missed Salat (prayers) and the like after the 'Asr prayer
chapters.intro: And narrated Umm Salama رضي الله عنها: The Prophet ﷺ offered two Rak'a after the 'Asr prayer and said, "Some people of the tribe of 'Abdul-Qais made me busy and did not let me offer the two Rak'a after the Zuhr prayer."
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يُصَلَّى بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ مِنَ الْفَوَائِتِ وَنَحْوِهَا
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ كُرَيْبٌ عَنْ أُمِّ سَلَمَةَ صَلَّى النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ رَكْعَتَيْنِ وَقَالَ: «شَغَلَنِي نَاسٌ مِنْ عَبْدِ الْقَيْسِ عَنِ الرَّكْعَتَيْنِ بَعْدَ الظُّهْرِ».

chapters.title: To offer (the 'Asr prayers) earlier on a cloudy day
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّبْكِيرِ بِالصَّلاَةِ فِي يَوْمِ غَيْمٍ

chapters.title: The Adhan for the Salat (prayer) after its stated time is over
chapters.title_arabic: باب الأَذَانِ بَعْدَ ذَهَابِ الْوَقْتِ

chapters.title: Whoever led the people in Salat (prayer) after its time was over
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ صَلَّى بِالنَّاسِ جَمَاعَةً بَعْدَ ذَهَابِ الْوَقْتِ

chapters.title: One who forgets a Salat (prayer) should offer it when he remembers it, and should not repeat anything except that particular prayer
chapters.intro: Ibrahim said: If one missed unintentionally one prayer 20 years ago then he should offer only that Salat (prayer).
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ نَسِيَ صَلاَةً فَلْيُصَلِّ إِذَا ذَكَرَهَا وَلاَ يُعِيدُ إِلاَّ تِلْكَ الصَّلاَةَ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ مَنْ تَرَكَ صَلاَةً وَاحِدَةً عِشْرِينَ سَنَةً لَمْ يُعِدْ إِلاَّ تِلْكَ الصَّلاَةَ الْوَاحِدَةَ.

chapters.title: The Qada of prayers (Qada means to perform or offer or do a missed religious obligation after its stated time)
chapters.title_arabic: باب قَضَاءِ الصَّلَوَاتِ الأُولَى فَالأُولَى

chapters.title: What is disliked about talking after the 'Isha prayer
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يُكْرَهُ مِنَ السَّمَرِ بَعْدَ الْعِشَاءِ

chapters.title: Talking about the Islamic jurisprudence and good things after the 'Isha prayer
chapters.title_arabic: باب السَّمَرِ فِي الْفِقْهِ وَالْخَيْرِ بَعْدَ الْعِشَاءِ

chapters.title: To talk with the family and the guests after the 'sha prayer.
chapters.title_arabic: باب السَّمَرِ مَعَ الضَّيْفِ وَالأَهْلِ

chapters.title: How the Adhan for Salat (Prayer) was started
chapters.intro:
And the Statement of Allah جل جلاله:

"And when you proclaim the call for As-Salat [call for the prayer (Adhan)], they take it (but) as a mockery and fun; that is because they are a people who understand not." (V.5:58).

And also the Statement of Allah تعالى:

"...When the call (Adhan) is proclaimed for the Salat (prayer) on the day of Friday..." (V.62:9).
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَدْءُ الأَذَانِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَوْلُهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ: {وَإِذَا نَادَيْتُمْ إِلَى الصَّلاَةِ اتَّخَذُوهَا هُزُوًا وَلَعِبًا ذَلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ قَوْمٌ لاَ يَعْقِلُونَ} وَقَوْلُهُ: {إِذَا نُودِيَ لِلصَّلاَةِ مِنْ يَوْمِ الْجُمُعَةِ}.

chapters.title: Pronouncing the wording of Adhan for Salat (prayers) twice (in doubles)
chapters.title_arabic: باب الأَذَانُ مَثْنَى مَثْنَى

chapters.title: To pronounce the wording of Iqama once (in singles) except Qad-qamat-is-Salat
chapters.title_arabic: باب الإِقَامَةُ وَاحِدَةٌ، إِلاَّ قَوْلَهُ قَدْ قَامَتِ الصَّلاَةُ

chapters.title: Superiority of the Adhan
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ التَّأْذِينِ

chapters.title: Raising the voice in pronouncing the Adhan
chapters.intro: 'Umar bin 'Abdul 'Aziz said (to his Mu'adh-dhin i.e., call-maker), "Pronounce the Adhan clearly and in a straightforward manner, otherwise we will dismiss you."
chapters.title_arabic: باب رَفْعِ الصَّوْتِ بِالنِّدَاءِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ عُمَرُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ أَذِّنْ أَذَانًا سَمْحًا وَإِلاَّ فَاعْتَزِلْنَا.

chapters.title: To suspend fighting on hearing the Adhan
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يُحْقَنُ بِالأَذَانِ مِنَ الدِّمَاءِ

chapters.title: What to say on hearing the Adhan
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يَقُولُ إِذَا سَمِعَ الْمُنَادِي

chapters.title: Invocation at the time of Adhan
chapters.title_arabic: باب الدُّعَاءِ عِنْدَ النِّدَاءِ

chapters.title: To draw lots for pronouncing the Adhan
chapters.intro: And it is said that some people differed regarding the pronunciation of Adhan (i.e., who should pronounce the Adhan). So Sa'd drew lots amongst them.
chapters.title_arabic: باب الاِسْتِهَامِ فِي الأَذَانِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَيُذْكَرُ أَنَّ أَقْوَامًا اخْتَلَفُوا فِي الأَذَانِ فَأَقْرَعَ بَيْنَهُمْ سَعْدٌ.

chapters.title: Talking during the Adhan
chapters.intro: Sulaiman bin Surad talked while pronouncing Adhan. Al-Hasan said, "There is no harm if the Mu'adh-dhin laughs while pronouncing the Adhan or Iqama."
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْكَلاَمِ فِي الأَذَانِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَتَكَلَّمَ سُلَيْمَانُ بْنُ صُرَدٍ فِي أَذَانِهِ.
وَقَالَ الْحَسَنُ لاَ بَأْسَ أَنْ يَضْحَكَ وَهْوَ يُؤَذِّنُ أَوْ يُقِيمُ.

chapters.title: The Adhan pronounced by a blind man (is permissible) when there is a person to inform him about the time of the Salat (prayer)
chapters.title_arabic: باب أَذَانِ الأَعْمَى إِذَا كَانَ لَهُ مَنْ يُخْبِرُهُ

chapters.title: The Adhan after Al-Fajr (dawn)
chapters.title_arabic: باب الأَذَانِ بَعْدَ الْفَجْرِ

chapters.title: The Adhan before Al-Fajr (dawn)
chapters.title_arabic: باب الأَذَانِ قَبْلَ الْفَجْرِ

chapters.title: How long should the interval between the Adhan and the Iqama be? (And something concerning) the person who wants for the Iqama
chapters.title_arabic: باب كَمْ بَيْنَ الأَذَانِ وَالإِقَامَةِ وَمَنْ يَنْتَظِرُ الإِقَامَةَ

chapters.title: Whoever waits for the Iqama of the prayer
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنِ انْتَظَرَ الإِقَامَةَ

chapters.title: Between every two calls (Adhan and Iqama) there is a Salat (prayer) (that is optional) for the one who wants to offer it.
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيْنَ كُلِّ أَذَانَيْنِ صَلاَةٌ لِمَنْ شَاءَ

chapters.title: Whoever said that there should be one Mu'adh-dhin in the journey
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ قَالَ لِيُؤَذِّنْ فِي السَّفَرِ مُؤَذِّنٌ وَاحِدٌ

chapters.title: If there are many travellers, Adhan and Iqama should be pronounced, (the same is to be observed) in 'Arafat and Al-Muzdalifa too
chapters.intro: On a very cold or a rainy night, the Mu'adh-dhin’s saying: As-Salatu fir-Rihal [Offer Salat (prayer) at your Ar-Rihal (homes or camps)]."
chapters.title_arabic: باب الأَذَانِ لِلْمُسَافِرِ إِذَا كَانُوا جَمَاعَةً، وَالإِقَامَةِ، وَكَذَلِكَ بِعَرَفَةَ وَجَمْعٍ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَوْلِ الْمُؤَذِّنِ الصَّلاَةُ فِي الرِّحَالِ‏.‏ فِي اللَّيْلَةِ الْبَارِدَةِ أَوِ الْمَطِيرَةِ

chapters.title: Should the Mu'adh-dhin turn his mouth (face) and look from side to side during the Adhan?
chapters.intro: It is said that (during the Adhan) Bilal used to put his (two) fingers in his (two) ears, whereas Ibn 'Umar never put the fingers in his ears. Ibrahim said that there was no harm in pronouncing the Adhan for the Salat (prayer) without ablution. 'Ata' said, "Ablution is essential and it is Sunna (legal way)." 'Aishah رضي الله عنها said, "The Prophet ﷺ used to remember Allah during all times."
chapters.title_arabic: باب هَلْ يَتَتَبَّعُ الْمُؤَذِّنُ فَاهُ هَا هُنَا وَهَا هُنَا، وَهَلْ يَلْتَفِتُ فِي الأَذَانِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَيُذْكَرُ عَنْ بِلاَلٍ أَنَّهُ جَعَلَ إِصْبَعَيْهِ فِي أُذُنَيْهِ. وَكَانَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ لاَ يَجْعَلُ إِصْبَعَيْهِ فِي أُذُنَيْهِ.
وَقَالَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ لاَ بَأْسَ أَنْ يُؤَذِّنَ عَلَى غَيْرِ وُضُوءٍ.
وَقَالَ عَطَاءٌ الْوُضُوءُ حَقٌّ وَسُنَّةٌ.
وَقَالَتْ عَائِشَةُ كَانَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يَذْكُرُ اللَّهَ عَلَى كُلِّ أَحْيَانِهِ.

chapters.title: The saying of a person "We have missed As-Salat (the prayer)"
chapters.intro: According to Ibn Sirin, it is disliked to say: "We have missed As-Salat (the prayer)." It is better to say: "We have not been able to offer (get) As-Salat", but the Prophet's statement (i.e., we missed As-Salat), is more correct.
chapters.title_arabic: باب قَوْلِ الرَّجُلِ فَاتَتْنَا الصَّلاَةُ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَكَرِهَ ابْنُ سِيرِينَ أَنْ يَقُولَ فَاتَتْنَا الصَّلاَةُ وَلَكِنْ لِيَقُلْ لَمْ نُدْرِكْ. وَقَوْلُ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ أَصَحُّ.

chapters.title: One should not run for As-Salat (the prayer) but present himself with calmness and solemnity
chapters.intro: "Offer prayer (in congregation) whatever you are able to pray and complete what you have missed." Abu Qatada narrated this from the Prophet ﷺ.
chapters.title_arabic: باب لاَ يَسْعَى إِلَى الصَّلاَةِ، وَلْيَأْتِ بِالسَّكِينَةِ وَالْوَقَارِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ: «مَا أَدْرَكْتُمْ فَصَلُّوا، وَمَا فَاتَكُمْ فَأَتِمُّوا». قَالَهُ أَبُو قَتَادَةَ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ.

chapters.title: When should the people get up for the Salat (prayer) if they see the Imam (the person leading Salat) during the Iqama?
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَتَى يَقُومُ النَّاسُ إِذَا رَأَوُا الإِمَامَ عِنْدَ الإِقَامَةِ

chapters.title: One should not stand for As-Salat (the prayer) hurriedly but with calmness and solemnity.
chapters.title_arabic: باب لاَ يَسْعَى إِلَى الصَّلاَةِ مُسْتَعْجِلاً، وَلْيَقُمْ بِالسَّكِينَةِ وَالْوَقَارِ

chapters.title: Can one go out of the mosque (after the Adhan, or the Iqama) if there is a genuine excuse?
chapters.title_arabic: باب هَلْ يَخْرُجُ مِنَ الْمَسْجِدِ لِعِلَّةٍ

chapters.title: If the Imam says, "Remain at your places till I return", then wait for him
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا قَالَ الإِمَامُ مَكَانَكُمْ. حَتَّى رَجَعَ انْتَظَرُوهُ

chapters.title: The saying of a man to the Prophet (pbuh), "We have not prayed."
chapters.title_arabic: باب قَوْلِ الرَّجُلِ مَا صَلَّيْنَا

chapters.title: If the Imam is confronted with a problem after the Iqama
chapters.title_arabic: باب الإِمَامِ تَعْرِضُ لَهُ الْحَاجَةُ بَعْدَ الإِقَامَةِ

chapters.title: To talk after the Iqama
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْكَلاَمِ إِذَا أُقِيمَتِ الصَّلاَةُ

chapters.title: Congregational Salat (prayer) is obligatory.
chapters.intro: Al-Hasan said, "If somebody is forbidden by his mother from going to the congregational 'Isha' prayer because of mercy and pity for him, he should not obey her."
chapters.title_arabic: باب وُجُوبِ صَلاَةِ الْجَمَاعَةِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ الْحَسَنُ إِنْ مَنَعَتْهُ أُمُّهُ عَنِ الْعِشَاءِ فِي الْجَمَاعَةِ شَفَقَةً لَمْ يُطِعْهَا.

chapters.title: Superiority of the congregational Salat (prayer)
chapters.intro: Whenever Al-Aswad missed the congregational Salat (prayer) he used to go to another mosque (to offer the Salat in congregation). Once, Anas came to a mosque where the Salat was finished; he pronounced the Adhan and then Iqama and offered the Salat in congregation.
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ صَلاَةِ الْجَمَاعَةِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَكَانَ الأَسْوَدُ إِذَا فَاتَتْهُ الْجَمَاعَةُ ذَهَبَ إِلَى مَسْجِدٍ آخَرَ.
وَجَاءَ أَنَسٌ إِلَى مَسْجِدٍ قَدْ صُلِّيَ فِيهِ، فَأَذَّنَ وَأَقَامَ وَصَلَّى جَمَاعَةً.

chapters.title: Superiority of the Fajr (early morning) prayer in congregation.
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ صَلاَةِ الْفَجْرِ فِي جَمَاعَةٍ

chapters.title: The superiority of offering the Zuhr prayer early
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ التَّهْجِيرِ إِلَى الظُّهْرِ

chapters.title: Every step towards good deeds is rewarded
chapters.title_arabic: باب احْتِسَابِ الآثَارِ

chapters.title: The superiority of the 'Isha' prayer in congregation
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ الْعِشَاءِ فِي الْجَمَاعَةِ

chapters.title: Two or more than two persons are considered as a group (for the congregational prayers)
chapters.title_arabic: باب اثْنَانِ فَمَا فَوْقَهُمَا جَمَاعَةٌ

chapters.title: (The reward of a person) who waits for As-Salat (the prayer) in the mosque and the superiority of mosques
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ جَلَسَ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ يَنْتَظِرُ الصَّلاَةَ، وَفَضْلِ الْمَسَاجِدِ

chapters.title: The superiority of going to the mosque (every) morning and in the afternoon and evening [for the congregational Salat (prayers)]
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ مَنْ غَدَا إِلَى الْمَسْجِدِ وَمَنْ رَاحَ

chapters.title: No Salat (prayer) (is to be offered) except the compulsory Salat after the Iqama has been pronounced for that compulsory Salat
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا أُقِيمَتِ الصَّلاَةُ فَلاَ صَلاَةَ إِلاَّ الْمَكْتُوبَةَ

chapters.title: The limit set for a patient to attend the congregational Salat (prayer)?
chapters.title_arabic: باب حَدِّ الْمَرِيضِ أَنْ يَشْهَدَ الْجَمَاعَةَ

chapters.title: It is permissible to pray at one's dwelling during rain or if there is a genuine excuse
chapters.title_arabic: باب الرُّخْصَةِ فِي الْمَطَرِ وَالْعِلَّةِ أَنْ يُصَلِّيَ فِي رَحْلِهِ

chapters.title: Can the Imam offer the Salat (prayer) with only those who are present (for the prayer)? And can be deliver a Khutba (religious talk) on Friday if it is raining?
chapters.title_arabic: باب هَلْ يُصَلِّي الإِمَامُ بِمَنْ حَضَرَ وَهَلْ يَخْطُبُ يَوْمَ الْجُمُعَةِ فِي الْمَطَرِ

chapters.title: (What should one do) if the meal has been served and the Iqama has been pronounced for As-Salat(the prayer)
chapters.intro: And Ibn 'Umar used to start with the supper first. Abu Ad-Darda' said, "It is a sign of comprehension (of a person) to fulfil or turn to his needs first so as to offer prayer attentively with a clear mind."
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا حَضَرَ الطَّعَامُ وَأُقِيمَتِ الصَّلاَةُ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَكَانَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ يَبْدَأُ بِالْعَشَاءِ.
وَقَالَ أَبُو الدَّرْدَاءِ مِنْ فِقْهِ الْمَرْءِ إِقْبَالُهُ عَلَى حَاجَتِهِ حَتَّى يُقْبِلَ عَلَى صَلاَتِهِ وَقَلْبُهُ فَارِغٌ.

chapters.title: When the Imam is called for As-Salat (the prayer) while he has in his hands something to eat.
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا دُعِيَ الإِمَامُ إِلَى الصَّلاَةِ وَبِيَدِهِ مَا يَأْكُلُ

chapters.title: If somebody was busy with his domestic work and Iqama was pronounced and then he came out [for offering the Salat (prayer)]
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ كَانَ فِي حَاجَةِ أَهْلِهِ فَأُقِيمَتِ الصَّلاَةُ فَخَرَجَ

chapters.title: Offering Salat (prayer) in front of the people with the sole intention of teaching them the Salat of the Prophet (pbuh) and his Sunnah (legal ways etc.)
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ صَلَّى بِالنَّاسِ وَهْوَ لاَ يُرِيدُ إِلاَّ أَنْ يُعَلِّمَهُمْ صَلاَةَ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم وَسُنَّتَهُ

chapters.title: The religious learned men are entitled to precedence in leading the Salat (prayer)
chapters.title_arabic: باب أَهْلُ الْعِلْمِ وَالْفَضْلِ أَحَقُّ بِالإِمَامَةِ

chapters.title: Whoever stood by the side of the Imam because of a genuine cause [in Salat (prayer)]
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ قَامَ إِلَى جَنْبِ الإِمَامِ لِعِلَّةٍ

chapters.title: If somebody is leading the Salat (prayer) and (in the meanwhile) the first (usual) Imam comes, the Salat is valid whether the former retreats or does not retreat
chapters.intro: This was narrated by 'Aishah رضي الله عنها who heard this from the Prophet ﷺ.
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ دَخَلَ لِيَؤُمَّ النَّاسَ فَجَاءَ الإِمَامُ الأَوَّلُ فَتَأَخَّرَ الأَوَّلُ أَوْ لَمْ يَتَأَخَّرْ جَازَتْ صَلاَتُهُ‏
chapters.intro_arabic: فِيهِ عَائِشَةُ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم

chapters.title: If some people are equally proficient in the recitation of the Quran (and religious knowledge), the oldest of them should lead As-Salat (the prayer)
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا اسْتَوَوْا فِي الْقِرَاءَةِ فَلْيَؤُمَّهُمْ أَكْبَرُهُمْ

chapters.title: If the Imam visited some people and led them in Salat (prayer)
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا زَارَ الإِمَامُ قَومًا فَأَمَّهُمْ

chapters.title: The Imam is appointed to be followed
chapters.intro: The Prophet ﷺ in his fatal illness led the people in Salat (prayer) while he was sitting (and the people were standing). Ibn Mas'ud said, "If anyone raises his head (while in prostration) before the Imam, he must prostrate again and must remain in prostration for a period equal to that he has lost by lifting his head before the Imam, and then he should follow the Imam." And Al-Hasan said, "A person who is offering a two Rak'a Salat with the Imam but (because of the rush of people) is unable to prostrate, then he should prostrate twice for the last Rak'a and make up for his Rak'a with its prostrations. And if somebody forgets to prostrate and stands up then he should prostrate."
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِنَّمَا جُعِلَ الإِمَامُ لِيُؤْتَمَّ بِهِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَصَلَّى النَّبِيُّ ﷺ فِي مَرَضِهِ الَّذِي تُوُفِّيَ فِيهِ بِالنَّاسِ وَهُوَ جَالِسٌ وَقَالَ ابْنُ مَسْعُودٍ: إِذَا رَفَعَ قَبْلَ الإِمَامِ يَعُودُ، فَيَمْكُثُ بِقَدْرِ مَا رَفَعَ، ثُمَّ يَتْبَعُ الإِمَامَ. وَقَالَ الحَسَنُ فِيمَنْ يَرْكَعُ مَعَ الإِمَامِ رَكْعَتَيْنِ وَلاَ يَقْدِرُ عَلَى السُّجُودِ: يَسْجُدُ لِلرَّكْعَةِ الآخِرَةِ سَجْدَتَيْنِ، ثُمَّ يَقْضِي الرَّكْعَةَ الأُولَى بِسُجُودِهَا. وَفِيمَنْ نَسِيَ سَجْدَةً حَتَّى قَامَ: يَسْجُدُ.

chapters.title: When should those who are behind the Imam prostrate?
chapters.intro: Anas said, "Prostrate when the Imam prostrates."
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَتَى يَسْجُدُ مَنْ خَلْفَ الإِمَامِ
chapters.intro_arabic: قَالَ أَنَسٌ فَإِذَا سَجَدَ فَاسْجُدُوا

chapters.title: The sin of the one who raises his head before the Imam (raises his head)
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِثْمِ مَنْ رَفَعَ رَأْسَهُ قَبْلَ الإِمَامِ

chapters.title: A slave or a manumitted slave can lead the Salat (prayer)
chapters.intro: 'Aishah رضي الله عنها was led in the Salat (prayer) by her slave Dhakwan who used to recite from the Mushaf [the written Qur'an (not from memory)]. Can an illegitimate boy, a bedouin, or a boy who has not reached the age of puberty lead the Salat? (It is permissible according to) the statement of the Prophet ﷺ that the Imam should be a person who knows the Qur'an more than the others.
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِمَامَةِ الْعَبْدِ وَالْمَوْلَى
chapters.intro_arabic: وَكَانَتْ عَائِشَةُ يَؤُمُّهَا عَبْدُهَا ذَكْوَانُ مِنَ الْمُصْحَفِ. وَوَلَدِ الْبَغِيِّ وَالأَعْرَابِيِّ وَالْغُلاَمِ الَّذِي لَمْ يَحْتَلِمْ، لِقَوْلِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «يَؤُمُّهُمْ أَقْرَؤُهُمْ لِكِتَابِ اللَّهِ».

chapters.title: If the Imam does not offer the Salat (prayer) perfectly and the followers offer it perfectly
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا لَمْ يُتِمَّ الإِمَامُ وَأَتَمَّ مَنْ خَلْفَهُ

chapters.title: Offering prayers behind a man who is a victim of Al-Fitan (trials and affections) or a heretic.
chapters.intro: Al-Hasan said, "You can offer prayers behind that Imam and the sin of heresy will be against him."
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِمَامَةِ الْمَفْتُونِ وَالْمُبْتَدِعِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ الْحَسَنُ صَلِّ وَعَلَيْهِ بِدْعَتُهُ.

chapters.title: To stand on the right side of the Imam on the same line if only two persons (counting the Imam) are offering Salat (prayer) in congregation
chapters.title_arabic: باب يَقُومُ عَنْ يَمِينِ الإِمَامِ، بِحِذَائِهِ سَوَاءً إِذَا كَانَا اثْنَيْنِ

chapters.title: If a man stood on the left side of the Imam and the Imam drew him to his right side, then the Salat of none of them would be invalid
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا قَامَ الرَّجُلُ عَنْ يَسَارِ الإِمَامِ، فَحَوَّلَهُ الإِمَامُ إِلَى يَمِينِهِ لَمْ تَفْسُدْ صَلاَتُهُمَا

chapters.title: If the Imam has not had the intention of leading the prayer and then some persons join him and he leads them
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا لَمْ يَنْوِ الإِمَامُ أَنْ يَؤُمَّ ثُمَّ جَاءَ قَوْمٌ فَأَمَّهُمْ

chapters.title: If the Imam prolongs the Salat (prayer) and somebody has an urgent work or need and so he leaves the congregation and offers Salat alone.
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا طَوَّلَ الإِمَامُ وَكَانَ لِلرَّجُلِ حَاجَةٌ فَخَرَجَ فَصَلَّى

chapters.title: The shortening of the Qiyam (standing) by the Imam[in Salat (prayer)] but performing the bowings and the prostrations perfectly
chapters.title_arabic: باب تَخْفِيفِ الإِمَامِ فِي الْقِيَامِ وَإِتْمَامِ الرُّكُوعِ وَالسُّجُودِ

chapters.title: When offering Salat (prayer) alone, one can prolong the Salat as much as one wishes
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا صَلَّى لِنَفْسِهِ فَلْيُطَوِّلْ مَا شَاءَ

chapters.title: Complaining against one's Imam if he prolongs the prayer.
chapters.intro: Abu Usaid said, "O my son! You have prolonged the prayer."
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ شَكَا إِمَامَهُ إِذَا طَوَّلَ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ أَبُو أُسَيْدٍ طَوَّلْتَ بِنَا يَا بُنَيَّ.

chapters.title: The shortening and perfection of the prayer (by the Imam)
chapters.title_arabic: باب الإِيجَازِ فِي الصَّلاَةِ وَإِكْمَالِهَا

chapters.title: Whoever cuts short As-Salat (the prayer) on hearing the cries of a child.
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ أَخَفَّ الصَّلاَةَ عِنْدَ بُكَاءِ الصَّبِيِّ

chapters.title: If one offers Salat (prayer) and the leads the people in Salat.
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا صَلَّى ثُمَّ أَمَّ قَوْمًا

chapters.title: One who repeats the Takbir (Allahu Akbar) of the Imam so that the people may hear it.
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ أَسْمَعَ النَّاسَ تَكْبِيرَ الإِمَامِ

chapters.title: If a person follows the Imam and the others follow that person (then it is all right)
chapters.intro: The Prophet ﷺ said, "You should follow me and the people behind you should follow you (in the prayers)."
chapters.title_arabic: باب الرَّجُلُ يَأْتَمُّ بِالإِمَامِ وَيَأْتَمُّ النَّاسُ بِالْمَأْمُومِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَيُذْكَرُ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «ائْتَمُّوا بِي وَلْيَأْتَمَّ بِكُمْ مَنْ بَعْدَكُمْ».

chapters.title: Can the Imam depend on the people's saying if he is in doubt (about a certain matter)?
chapters.title_arabic: باب هَلْ يَأْخُذُ الإِمَامُ إِذَا شَكَّ بِقَوْلِ النَّاسِ

chapters.title: If the Imam weeps in As-Salat (the prayers) (will his Salat be valid?)
chapters.intro: 'Abdullah bin Shaddad said, "I heard 'Umar weeping while I was in the last row and 'Umar was reciting: '...I only complain of my grief and sorrow to Allah...' " (V.<qref chapter="12" verses="86">12:86</qref>)
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا بَكَى الإِمَامُ فِي الصَّلاَةِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ شَدَّادٍ سَمِعْتُ نَشِيجَ عُمَرَ وَأَنَا فِي آخِرِ الصُّفُوفِ يَقْرَأُ: {إِنَّمَا أَشْكُو بَثِّي وَحُزْنِي إِلَى اللَّهِ}.

chapters.title: Straightening the rows at the time of Iqama and after it (immediately)
chapters.title_arabic: باب تَسْوِيَةِ الصُّفُوفِ عِنْدَ الإِقَامَةِ وَبَعْدَهَا

chapters.title: Facing of the Imam towards his followers while straightening the rows.
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِقْبَالِ الإِمَامِ عَلَى النَّاسِ عِنْدَ تَسْوِيَةِ الصُّفُوفِ

chapters.title: The first row
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّفِّ الأَوَّلِ

chapters.title: The straightening of the rows is amongst those obligatory and good things which make your As-Salat (the prayer) a correct and perfect one.
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِقَامَةِ الصَّفِّ مِنْ تَمَامِ الصَّلاَةِ

chapters.title: The sin of a person who does not complete the rows (who is out of alignment) for the prayer
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِثْمِ مَنْ لَمْ يُتِمَّ الصُّفُوفَ

chapters.title: To stand shoulder to shoulder and foot to foot in a row
chapters.intro: And An-Nu'man bin Bashir said, "I saw that every one of us used to put his heel with the heel of his companion."
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِلْزَاقِ الْمَنْكِبِ بِالْمَنْكِبِ وَالْقَدَمِ بِالْقَدَمِ فِي الصَّفِّ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ النُّعْمَانُ بْنُ بَشِيرٍ رَأَيْتُ الرَّجُلَ مِنَّا يُلْزِقُ كَعْبَهُ بِكَعْبِ صَاحِبِهِ.

chapters.title: If a person stands by the left side of the Imam, and the Imam dress him to the right from behind, the Salat (prayer) is correct.
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا قَامَ الرَّجُلُ عَنْ يَسَارِ الإِمَامِ، وَحَوَّلَهُ الإِمَامُ خَلْفَهُ إِلَى يَمِينِهِ، تَمَّتْ صَلاَتُهُ

chapters.title: One woman can form a row
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْمَرْأَةُ وَحْدَهَا تَكُونُ صَفًّا

chapters.title: The right side of the mosque and the place to the right of the Imam
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَيْمَنَةِ الْمَسْجِدِ وَالإِمَامِ

chapters.title: If there is a wall or a Sutra between the Imam and followers
