chapters.intro: Al-Hasan said, "There is no harm in offering Salat (prayer) if there is a river between you and the Imam." Abu Mijlaz said, "One can follow the Imam even if there is a road or a wall between the Imam and followers provided the Takbir (Allahu Akbar) is audible."
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا كَانَ بَيْنَ الإِمَامِ وَبَيْنَ الْقَوْمِ حَائِطٌ أَوْ سُتْرَةٌ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَقَالَ الْحَسَنُ لاَ بَأْسَ أَنْ تُصَلِّيَ وَبَيْنَكَ وَبَيْنَهُ نَهْرٌ.
وَقَالَ أَبُو مِجْلَزٍ يَأْتَمُّ بِالإِمَامِ وَإِنْ كَانَ بَيْنَهُمَا طَرِيقٌ أَوْ جِدَارٌ إِذَا سَمِعَ تَكْبِيرَ الإِمَامِ.

chapters.title: The night prayer.
chapters.title_arabic: باب صَلاَةِ اللَّيْلِ

chapters.title: The necessity of saying the Takbir, i.e., Allahu Akbar (Allah is the Most Great) and the commencement of As-Salat (the prayer)
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِيجَابِ التَّكْبِيرِ وَافْتِتَاحِ الصَّلاَةِ

chapters.title: To raise both hands on saying the first Takbir simultaneously with opening the Salat (prayer)
chapters.title_arabic: باب رَفْعِ الْيَدَيْنِ فِي التَّكْبِيرَةِ الأُولَى مَعَ الاِفْتِتَاحِ سَوَاءً

chapters.title: To raise both hands while saying the Takbir [on opening As-Salat (the prayer)], and while bowing and on raising up the head (after bowing)
chapters.title_arabic: باب رَفْعِ الْيَدَيْنِ إِذَا كَبَّرَ وَإِذَا رَكَعَ وَإِذَا رَفَعَ

chapters.title: To what level should one raise one's hand.
chapters.intro: In the presence of his companions Abu Humaid said: "The Prophet ﷺ raised his hands up to his shoulders."
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِلَى أَيْنَ يَرْفَعُ يَدَيْهِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ أَبُو حُمَيْدٍ فِي أَصْحَابِهِ رَفَعَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ حَذْوَ مَنْكِبَيْهِ.

chapters.title: To raise one's hands after finishing the second Rak'a (on standing for the third Rak'a)
chapters.title_arabic: باب رَفْعِ الْيَدَيْنِ إِذَا قَامَ مِنَ الرَّكْعَتَيْنِ

chapters.title: To place the right hand on the left [in As-Salt (the prayers)]
chapters.title_arabic: باب وَضْعِ الْيُمْنَى عَلَى الْيُسْرَى

chapters.title: Submissiveness in As-Salat (the prayer)
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْخُشُوعِ فِي الصَّلاَةِ

chapters.title: What to say after the Takbir
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يَقُولُ بَعْدَ التَّكْبِيرِ

chapters.title: Chapter
chapters.title_arabic: باب

chapters.title: To cast a look at the Imam during As-Salat (the prayer)
chapters.intro: 'Aishah رضي الله عنها said: The Prophet ﷺ was narrating about the Salat (prayer) of eclipse and said, "I saw Hell, and one of its sides was destroying the other. (And that was) when you saw me retreating (during the Salat)."
chapters.title_arabic: باب رَفْعِ الْبَصَرِ إِلَى الإِمَامِ فِي الصَّلاَةِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَتْ عَائِشَةُ قَالَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فِي صَلاَةِ الْكُسُوفِ: «فَرَأَيْتُ جَهَنَّمَ يَحْطِمُ بَعْضُهَا بَعْضًا حِينَ رَأَيْتُمُونِي تَأَخَّرْتُ».

chapters.title: Looking towards the sky during As-Salat (the prayer)
chapters.title_arabic: باب رَفْعِ الْبَصَرِ إِلَى السَّمَاءِ فِي الصَّلاَةِ

chapters.title: To look hither and thither in As-Salat (the prayer)
chapters.title_arabic: باب الاِلْتِفَاتِ فِي الصَّلاَةِ

chapters.title: Is it permissible to one to look around in Salat (prayer) if something happens to one? Or can one look at something like expectoration in the directn of the Qiblah?
chapters.intro: Sahl said: "Abu Bakr رضي الله عنه turned and saw the Prophet ﷺ [during the Salat (prayer)]."
chapters.title_arabic: باب هَلْ يَلْتَفِتُ لأَمْرٍ يَنْزِلُ بِهِ أَوْ يَرَى شَيْئًا أَوْ بُصَاقًا فِي الْقِبْلَةِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ سَهْلٌ الْتَفَتَ أَبُو بَكْرٍ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ فَرَأَى النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ.

chapters.title: Recitation of the Qur'an (Surat Al-Fatiha) is compulsory for the Imam and the followers, at the home and on journey, in all As-Salat (the prayers) whether the recitation is done silently or aloud.
chapters.title_arabic: باب وُجُوبِ الْقِرَاءَةِ لِلإِمَامِ وَالْمَأْمُومِ فِي الصَّلَوَاتِ كُلِّهَا فِي الْحَضَرِ وَالسَّفَرِ وَمَا يُجْهَرُ فِيهَا وَمَا يُخَافَتُ

chapters.title: The recitation of the Qur'an in a Zuhr prayer
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْقِرَاءَةِ فِي الظُّهْرِ

chapters.title: The recitation of the Qur'an in the 'Asr prayer
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْقِرَاءَةِ فِي الْعَصْرِ

chapters.title: The recitation of the Qur'an in the Magrib prayer
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْقِرَاءَةِ فِي الْمَغْرِبِ

chapters.title: To recite aloud in the Maghrib prayer.
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْجَهْرِ فِي الْمَغْرِبِ

chapters.title: To recite aloud in the 'Isha' prayer.
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْجَهْرِ فِي الْعِشَاءِ

chapters.title: To recite in the 'Isha prayer, with As-Sajda(prostration)
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْقِرَاءَةِ فِي الْعِشَاءِ بِالسَّجْدَةِ

chapters.title: Recitation in the 'Isha prayer
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْقِرَاءَةِ فِي الْعِشَاءِ

chapters.title: Prolonging the first two Rak'a and shortening the last two
chapters.title_arabic: باب يُطَوِّلُ فِي الأُولَيَيْنِ وَيَحْذِفُ فِي الأُخْرَيَيْنِ

chapters.title: The recitation of the Qur'an in the Fajr prayer
chapters.intro: And Umm Salama said: "The Prophet ﷺ recited Surat At-Tur."
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْقِرَاءَةِ فِي الْفَجْرِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَتْ أُمُّ سَلَمَةَ قَرَأَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ بِالطُّورِ.

chapters.title: To recite aloud in the Fajr (early morning) prayer.
chapters.intro: And Umm Salama said, "I was performing Tawaf behind the people while the Prophet ﷺ was offering Salat (praying) and reciting Surat At-Tur."
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْجَهْرِ بِقِرَاءَةِ صَلاَةِ الْفَجْرِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَتْ أُمُّ سَلَمَةَ طُفْتُ وَرَاءَ النَّاسِ وَالنَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يُصَلِّي وَيَقْرَأُ بِالطُّورِ.

chapters.title: To recite two Surah in one Rak'a and to recite the last verses of some Surah, or to elite the Surah in their reverse order, or to recite the beginning of a Surah
chapters.intro: Narrated 'Abdullah bin As-Sa'ib: The Prophet ﷺ recited Surat Al-Mu’minun in the Fajr prayer and when he reached the story of Musa (Moses) and Harun (Aaron) or 'Isa (Jesus), he got a cough and bowed. 'Umar recited 120 Verses from Surat Al-Baqarah in the first Rak'a and in the second Rak'a, he recited a Surah from the Mathani (Surah of less than 100 Verses). Ahnaf recited in the first Rak'a Surat Al-Kahf and in the second Rak'a Surat Yusuf (Joseph) or Surat Yunus (Jonah) and said that he had offered the Fajr prayer with 'Umar reciting the same Surah. Ibn Mas'ud recited 40 Verses from Surat Al-Anfal (in the first Rak'a) and in the second Rak'a, a Surah from the Mufassal Surah [the Surah starting from Qaf to the end of the Qur’an]. Qatada said about a person who recited one Surah divided between two Rak'as or repeated the same Surah in both Rak'as that he can do so as all those Verses are from Allah’s Book.
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْجَمْعِ بَيْنَ السُّورَتَيْنِ فِي الرَّكْعَةِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَالْقِرَاءَةِ بِالْخَوَاتِيمِ، وَبِسُورَةٍ قَبْلَ سُورَةٍ، وَبِأَوَّلِ سُورَةٍ. وَيُذْكَرُ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ السَّائِبِ قَرَأَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ فِي الصُّبْحِ حَتَّى إِذَا جَاءَ ذِكْرُ مُوسَى وَهَارُونَ أَوْ ذِكْرُ عِيسَى، أَخَذَتْهُ سَعْلَةٌ فَرَكَعَ. وَقَرَأَ عُمَرُ فِي الرَّكْعَةِ الأُولَى بِمِائَةٍ وَعِشْرِينَ آيَةً مِنَ الْبَقَرَةِ، وَفِي الثَّانِيَةِ بِسُورَةٍ مِنَ الْمَثَانِي. وَقَرَأَ الأَحْنَفُ بِالْكَهْفِ فِي الأُولَى، وَفِي الثَّانِيَةِ بِيُوسُفَ أَوْ يُونُسَ، وَذَكَرَ أَنَّهُ صَلَّى مَعَ عُمَرَ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ الصُّبْحَ بِهِمَا. وَقَرَأَ ابْنُ مَسْعُودٍ بِأَرْبَعِينَ آيَةً مِنَ الأَنْفَالِ، وَفِي الثَّانِيَةِ بِسُورَةٍ مِنَ الْمُفَصَّلِ.

وَقَالَ قَتَادَةُ فِيمَنْ يَقْرَأُ سُورَةً وَاحِدَةً فِي رَكْعَتَيْنِ أَوْ يُرَدِّدُ سُورَةً وَاحِدَةً فِي رَكْعَتَيْنِ كُلٌّ كِتَابُ اللَّهِ.

chapters.title: To recite only Surah Al-Fathiha in the last two Rak'a during a four Rak'a Salat (prayer)
chapters.title_arabic: باب يَقْرَأُ فِي الأُخْرَيَيْنِ بِفَاتِحَةِ الْكِتَابِ

chapters.title: To recite (the Qur'an) quietly in the Zuhr and 'Asr prayers
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ خَافَتَ الْقِرَاءَةَ فِي الظُّهْرِ وَالْعَصْرِ

chapters.title: (In a quiet prayer) if the Imam recites a Verse or so audibly
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا أَسْمَعَ الإِمَامُ الآيَةَ

chapters.title: To prolong the first Rak'a
chapters.title_arabic: باب يُطَوِّلُ فِي الرَّكْعَةِ الأُولَى

chapters.title: Saying of Amin aloud by the Imam
chapters.intro: 'Ata' said: 'Amin is an invocation. Ibn Az-Zubair and the persons behind him said 'Amin loudly till the mosque echoed. Abu Hurairah used to say to the Imam "Do not say 'Amin before me (i.e. before I join the row for the prayer)." Nafi' said: "Ibn 'Umar never missed 'Amin, and urged the people to say it. I heard something good about it from him."
chapters.title_arabic: باب جَهْرِ الإِمَامِ بِالتَّأْمِينِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَقَالَ عَطَاءٌ آمِينَ دُعَاءٌ. أَمَّنَ ابْنُ الزُّبَيْرِ وَمَنْ وَرَاءَهُ حَتَّى إِنَّ لِلْمَسْجِدِ لَلَجَّةً. وَكَانَ أَبُو هُرَيْرَةَ يُنَادِي الإِمَامَ لاَ تَفُتْنِي بِآمِينَ.
وَقَالَ نَافِعٌ كَانَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ لاَ يَدَعُهُ وَيَحُضُّهُمْ، وَسَمِعْتُ مِنْهُ فِي ذَلِكَ خَيْرًا.

chapters.title: Superiority of saying Amin
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ التَّأْمِينِ

chapters.title: Saying of Amin aloud by the followers
chapters.title_arabic: باب جَهْرِ الْمَأْمُومِ بِالتَّأْمِينِ

chapters.title: If someone bowed behind the rows, [on entering the mosque and before joining the rows of Salat (prayer)]
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا رَكَعَ دُونَ الصَّفِّ

chapters.title: Itmam At-Takbir (i.e., to end the number of Takbir or to say the Takbir perfectly) on bowing. [See Fath Al-Bari]
chapters.intro: Ibn 'Abbas narrated it from the Prophet ﷺ. One of the narrators of the Hadith is Malik bin Huwairith رضي الله عنه.
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِتْمَامِ التَّكْبِيرِ فِي الرُّكُوعِ
chapters.intro_arabic: قَالَهُ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ. فِيهِ مَالِكُ بْنُ الْحُوَيْرِثِ.

chapters.title: Itmam At-Takbir (i.e., to end the number of Takbir, or to say the Takbir perfectly) on prostrating. [See Fath Al-Bari]
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِتْمَامِ التَّكْبِيرِ فِي السُّجُودِ

chapters.title: Saying the Takbir on raising from the prostration
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّكْبِيرِ إِذَا قَامَ مِنَ السُّجُودِ

chapters.title: To put the hands (palms) on both knees while bowing.
chapters.intro: While in the company of his companions, Abu Humaid said, "The Prophet ﷺ used to put both his hands on his knees."
chapters.title_arabic: باب وَضْعِ الأَكُفِّ عَلَى الرُّكَبِ فِي الرُّكُوعِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ أَبُو حُمَيْدٍ فِي أَصْحَابِهِ أَمْكَنَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يَدَيْهِ مِنْ رُكْبَتَيْهِ.

chapters.title: Not performing bowing perfectly
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا لَمْ يُتِمَّ الرُّكُوعَ

chapters.title: To keep the back straight in bowing.
chapters.intro: In the presence of his companions, Abu Humaid said, "The Prophet ﷺ bowed and kept his back straight."
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ اسْتِوَاءِ الظَّهْرِ فِي الرُّكُوعِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ أَبُو حُمَيْدٍ فِي أَصْحَابِهِ رَكَعَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ثُمَّ هَصَرَ ظَهْرَهُ.

chapters.title: And what is said regarding the limit of the completion of bowing and of keeping the back straight and the calmness with which it is performed.
chapters.title_arabic: باب حَدِّ إِتْمَامِ الرُّكُوعِ وَالاِعْتِدَالِ فِيهِ وَالاِطْمَأْنِينَةِ

chapters.title: The order of the Prophet (pbuh) to a person who did not perform his bowing perfectly that he should repeat his Salat (prayer)
chapters.title_arabic: باب أَمْرِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم الَّذِي لاَ يُتِمُّ رُكُوعَهُ بِالإِعَادَةِ

chapters.title: Invocation in bowing.
chapters.title_arabic: باب الدُّعَاءِ فِي الرُّكُوعِ

chapters.title: What the Imam and the followers say on raising their heads from bowing.
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يَقُولُ الإِمَامُ وَمَنْ خَلْفَهُ إِذَا رَفَعَ رَأْسَهُ مِنَ الرُّكُوعِ

chapters.title: The superiority of saying Allahumma Rabbana lakal hand (O Allah, Our Lord!, All the praises and thanks are for You)
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ اللَّهُمَّ رَبَّنَا لَكَ الْحَمْدُ

chapters.title: Chapter
chapters.title_arabic: باب

chapters.title: To stand Straight with calmness on raising the head from bowing
chapters.intro: And Abu Humaid said: The Prophet ﷺ rose (from bowing) and stood straight till all the vertebra of his spinal column came to a natural position.
chapters.title_arabic: باب الاِطْمَأْنِينَةِ حِينَ يَرْفَعُ رَأْسَهُ مِنَ الرُّكُوعِ
chapters.intro_arabic: قَالَ أَبُو حُمَيْدٍ رَفَعَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ وَاسْتَوَى جَالِسًا حَتَّى يَعُودَ كُلُّ فَقَارٍ مَكَانَهُ.

chapters.title: One should say Takbir while going in prostration.
chapters.intro: Nafi' said: "Ibn 'Umar used to place both his hands (on the ground) before his knees."
chapters.title_arabic: باب يَهْوِي بِالتَّكْبِيرِ حِينَ يَسْجُدُ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ نَافِعٌ كَانَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ يَضَعُ يَدَيْهِ قَبْلَ رُكْبَتَيْهِ.

chapters.title: Superiority of prostrating.
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ السُّجُودِ

chapters.title: During the prostrations one should keep one's arms away from one's sides and the abdomen should be kept away from the thighs.
chapters.title_arabic: باب يُبْدِي ضَبْعَيْهِ وَيُجَافِي فِي السُّجُودِ

chapters.title: One should keep the toes in the direction of the Qiblah
chapters.intro: Abu Humaid As-Sa'idi narrated this from the Prophet ﷺ.
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ يَسْتَقْبِلُ بِأَطْرَافِ رِجْلَيْهِ الْقِبْلَةَ
chapters.intro_arabic: قَالَهُ أَبُو حُمَيْدٍ السَّاعِدِيُّ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ.

chapters.title: If one does not perform the prostrations perfectly
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا لَمْ يُتِمَّ السُّجُودَ

chapters.title: To prostrate on seven bones
chapters.title_arabic: باب السُّجُودِ عَلَى سَبْعَةِ أَعْظُمٍ

chapters.title: To prostrate on the nose
chapters.title_arabic: باب السُّجُودِ عَلَى الأَنْفِ

chapters.title: To prostrate on the nose and in the mud
chapters.title_arabic: باب السُّجُودِ عَلَى الأَنْفِ وَالسُّجُودِ عَلَى الطِّينِ

chapters.title: To tie the clothes and wrap them properly [in Salat (prayer)]; and whoever gathered his clothes for fear that his private parts may become exposed.
chapters.title_arabic: باب عَقْدِ الثِّيَابِ وَشَدِّهَا وَمَنْ ضَمَّ إِلَيْهِ ثَوْبَهُ إِذَا خَافَ أَنْ تَنْكَشِفَ عَوْرَتُهُ

chapters.title: One should not tuck put the hair [during Salat (prayers)]
chapters.title_arabic: باب لاَ يَكُفُّ شَعَرًا

chapters.title: One should not tuck up his garment in As-Salat (the prayer)
chapters.title_arabic: باب لاَ يَكُفُّ ثَوْبَهُ فِي الصَّلاَةِ

chapters.title: To invoke and glorify Allah in prostration
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّسْبِيحِ وَالدُّعَاءِ فِي السُّجُودِ

chapters.title: To sit for a while between the two prostrations
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْمُكْثِ بَيْنَ السَّجْدَتَيْنِ

chapters.title: One should not put the forearms on the ground during prostrations
chapters.intro: Abu Humaid said: The Prophet ﷺ prostrated and put his hands (on the ground) with the forearms away from the ground and away from the body.
chapters.title_arabic: باب لاَ يَفْتَرِشُ ذِرَاعَيْهِ فِي السُّجُودِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ أَبُو حُمَيْدٍ سَجَدَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ وَوَضَعَ يَدَيْهِ غَيْرَ مُفْتَرِشٍ وَلاَ قَابِضِهِمَا.

chapters.title: Sitting Straight in a Witr payer (i.e., an odd Rak'a) and then getting up.
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنِ اسْتَوَى قَاعِدًا فِي وِتْرٍ مِنْ صَلاَتِهِ ثُمَّ نَهَضَ

chapters.title: How to support oneself on the ground while standing after finishing the Rak'a (after the two prostrations)
chapters.title_arabic: باب كَيْفَ يَعْتَمِدُ عَلَى الأَرْضِ إِذَا قَامَ مِنَ الرَّكْعَةِ

chapters.title: Saying Takbir on rising from the two prostrations
chapters.intro: Ibn Az-Zubair used to say the Takbir on rising.
chapters.title_arabic: باب يُكَبِّرُ وَهْوَ يَنْهَضُ مِنَ السَّجْدَتَيْنِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَكَانَ ابْنُ الزُّبَيْرِ يُكَبِّرُ فِي نَهْضَتِهِ‏.‏

chapters.title: The Prophet's Sunna (legal way) for the sitting in the Tashah-hud [in the Salat (prayer)].
chapters.intro: Umm Ad-Darda' used to sit in the Salat (prayer) like men and she was a woman well-versed (in religious knowledge).
chapters.title_arabic: باب سُنَّةِ الْجُلُوسِ فِي التَّشَهُّدِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَكَانَتْ أُمُّ الدَّرْدَاءِ تَجْلِسُ فِي صَلاَتِهَا جِلْسَةَ الرَّجُلِ، وَكَانَتْ فَقِيهَةً.

chapters.title: Whoever considered that the first Tashah-hud is not compulsory
chapters.intro: As the Prophet ﷺ stood up after the second Rak'a (without sitting for Tashahhud) and did not perform it.
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ لَمْ يَرَ التَّشَهُّدَ الأَوَّلَ وَاجِبًا
chapters.intro_arabic: لأَنَّ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ قَامَ مِنَ الرَّكْعَتَيْنِ وَلَمْ يَرْجِعْ

chapters.title: (Saying of the) Tashah-hud in the first sitting.
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّشَهُّدِ فِي الأُولَى

chapters.title: (Saying of the) Tashah-hud in the last Rak'a
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّشَهُّدِ فِي الآخِرَةِ

chapters.title: Invocation before the Taslim
chapters.title_arabic: باب الدُّعَاءِ قَبْلَ السَّلاَمِ

chapters.title: What optional invocation may be selected after the Tashah-hud, and it is not obligatory.
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يُتَخَيَّرُ مِنَ الدُّعَاءِ بَعْدَ التَّشَهُّدِ وَلَيْسَ بِوَاجِبٍ

chapters.title: No cleaning (rubbing) one's forehead and nose till one has completed As-Salat (the prayer)
chapters.intro: And Abu 'Abdullah said: I saw Al-Humaidi quoting this Hadith (No.798) to support his argument that the forehead should not be cleaned (rubbed) in As-Salat (prayer).
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ لَمْ يَمْسَحْ جَبْهَتَهُ وَأَنْفَهُ حَتَّى صَلَّى
chapters.intro_arabic: قالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ : رَأَيْتُ الحُمَيْدِيَّ يَحْتَجُّ بِهٰذَا الحَدِيثِ أَنْ لَا يَمْسَحَ الجَبْهَةَ فِي الصَّلاةِ.

chapters.title: Taslim [turning the face to the right and then to the left and saying "As-Salamu alikum wa rah mat-ullah" at the end of the Salat (prayers)]
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّسْلِيمِ

chapters.title: To finish the Salat (prayer) with Taslim along with the Imam
chapters.intro: Ibn 'Umar رضي الله عنهما liked for those offering Salat (prayers) behind the Imam to say Taslim (immediately) after the Imam had said it.
chapters.title_arabic: باب يُسَلِّمُ حِينَ يُسَلِّمُ الإِمَامُ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَكَانَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمَا يَسْتَحِبُّ إِذَا سَلَّمَ الإِمَامُ أَنْ يُسَلِّمَ مَنْ خَلْفَهُ.

chapters.title: Whoever did not say (a Taslim) in addition to the Taslim of the Imam but thought that Taslim of the Salat (prayer) was sufficient
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ لَمْ يَرَ رَدَّ السَّلاَمِ عَلَى الإِمَامِ وَاكْتَفَى بِتَسْلِيمِ الصَّلاَةِ

chapters.title: The Dhikr (remembering Allah by Glorifying, Praising and Magnifying Him) after As-Salat (the prayer)
chapters.title_arabic: باب الذِّكْرِ بَعْدَ الصَّلاَةِ

chapters.title: The Imam should face the followers after finishing the prayer with Taslim.
chapters.title_arabic: باب يَسْتَقْبِلُ الإِمَامُ النَّاسَ إِذَا سَلَّمَ

chapters.title: The staying of the Imam at his Musalla (praying place) after (finishing the prayer with) Taslim
chapters.title_arabic: باب مُكْثِ الإِمَامِ فِي مُصَلاَّهُ بَعْدَ السَّلاَمِ

chapters.title: Whoever led the people in Salat (prayer) and remembered an rent matter or necessity and had to pass over the people (to carry out that)
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ صَلَّى بِالنَّاسِ فَذَكَرَ حَاجَةً فَتَخَطَّاهُمْ

chapters.title: To leave or depart from the right and from the left after finishing from the Salat (prayers).
chapters.intro: Anas bin Malik used to leave off from his right and from his left, and he used to criticize all those who always aimed to leave from their right side only.
chapters.title_arabic: باب الاِنْفِتَالِ وَالاِنْصِرَافِ عَنِ الْيَمِينِ، وَالشِّمَالِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَكَانَ أَنَسٌ يَنْفَتِلُ عَنْ يَمِينِهِ وَعَنْ يَسَارِهِ، وَيَعِيبُ عَلَى مَنْ يَتَوَخَّى، أَوْ مَنْ يَعْمِدُ الاِنْفِتَالَ عَنْ يَمِينِهِ.

chapters.title: What has been said about uncooked garlic, onion and leek.
chapters.intro: And the statement of the Prophet ﷺ "Whoever has eaten garlic or onion because of hunger or otherwise should not come near our mosque."
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا جَاءَ فِي الثُّومِ النَّىِّ وَالْبَصَلِ وَالْكُرَّاثِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَوْلِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «مَنْ أَكَلَ الثُّومَ أَوِ الْبَصَلَ مِنَ الْجُوعِ أَوْ غَيْرِهِ فَلاَ يَقْرَبَنَّ مَسْجِدَنَا».

chapters.title: The ablution for boys (youngsters). When they should perform Ghusl (take a bath) and Tuhur (purification). Their attendance at congregational prayers and their rows in the prayers.
chapters.title_arabic: باب وُضُوءِ الصِّبْيَانِ, وَمَتَى يَجِبُ عَلَيْهِمُ الْغَسْلُ وَالطُّهُورُ وَحُضُورِهِمِ الْجَمَاعَةَ وَالْعِيدَيْنِ وَالْجَنَائِزَ وَصُفُوفِهِمْ.

chapters.title: Going of women to the mosque at night and in darkness
chapters.title_arabic: باب خُرُوجِ النِّسَاءِ إِلَى الْمَسَاجِدِ بِاللَّيْلِ وَالْغَلَسِ

chapters.title: The waiting of the people for the religious learned Imam to get up (after the prayer to depart)
chapters.title_arabic: باب انْتِظَارِ النَّاسِ قِيَامَ الإِمَامِ الْعَالِمِ

chapters.title: The Salat (prayer) of women behind men
chapters.title_arabic: باب صَلاَةِ النِّسَاءِ خَلْفَ الرِّجَالِ

chapters.title: Returning of the women immediately after the Fajr prayer and their staying in the mosque for a short period only
chapters.title_arabic: باب سُرْعَةِ انْصِرَافِ النِّسَاءِ مِنَ الصُّبْحِ، وَقِلَّةِ مَقَامِهِنَّ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ

chapters.title: A women shall ask her husband's permission (on wishing) to go to the mosque.
chapters.title_arabic: باب اسْتِئْذَانِ الْمَرْأَةِ زَوْجَهَا بِالْخُرُوجِ إِلَى الْمَسْجِدِ

chapters.title: Women praying behind men
chapters.title_arabic: باب صَلاَةِ النِّسَاءِ خَلْفَ الرِّجَالِ

chapters.title: The prescription of Friday [Salat-ul-Jumu'ah (Jumu'ah prayer)] and Khutba (religious talk) according to the Statement of Allah تعالى: "When the call is proclaimed for the Salat (prayer) of Friday (Jumu'ah prayer) come to the remembrance of Allah [Jumu'ah religious talk (Khutba) and Salat (prayer)] and leave off business (and every other thing)." (V.<qref chapter="62" verses="9">62:9</qref>)
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَرْضِ الْجُمُعَةِ لِقَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {إِذَا نُودِيَ لِلصَّلاَةِ مِنْ يَوْمِ الْجُمُعَةِ فَاسْعَوْا إِلَى ذِكْرِ اللَّهِ وَذَرُوا الْبَيْعَ ذَلِكُمْ خَيْرٌ لَكُمْ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ}.

chapters.title: The superiority of taking a bath on friday
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ الْغُسْلِ يَوْمَ الْجُمُعَةِ، وَهَلْ عَلَى الصَّبِيِّ شُهُودُ يَوْمِ الْجُمُعَةِ أَوْ عَلَى النِّسَاءِ

chapters.title: To perfume before going for the Friday (prayer)
chapters.title_arabic: باب الطِّيبِ لِلْجُمُعَةِ

chapters.title: The superiority of Jumu'ah (prayer and Khutba)
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ الْجُمُعَةِ

chapters.title: Chapter
chapters.title_arabic: باب

chapters.title: To use (hair) oil for the Friday prayer
chapters.title_arabic: باب الدُّهْنِ لِلْجُمُعَةِ

chapters.title: To wear the best clothes (for the Jumu'ah prayer)
chapters.title_arabic: باب يَلْبَسُ أَحْسَنَ مَا يَجِدُ

chapters.title: To clean the teeth with Siwak on Friday
chapters.intro: And Abu Sa'id said that the Prophet ﷺ used to clean his teeth.
chapters.title_arabic: باب السِّوَاكِ يَوْمَ الْجُمُعَةِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ أَبُو سَعِيدٍ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يَسْتَنُّ.

chapters.title: Whoever cleans his teeth with Siwak
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ تَسَوَّكَ بِسِوَاكِ غَيْرِهِ

chapters.title: What should be recited in the Fajr prayer on Friday
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يُقْرَأُ فِي صَلاَةِ الْفَجْرِ يَوْمَ الْجُمُعَةِ

chapters.title: To offer Jumu'ah prayer and Khutba in villages and towns
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْجُمُعَةِ فِي الْقُرَى وَالْمُدْنِ

chapters.title: Is the bath necessary for those who do not present themselves for the Jumu'ah prayer
chapters.intro: And Ibn 'Umar said, "A bath is compulsory for those on whom the Jumu'ah (prayer) is obligatory."
chapters.title_arabic: باب هَلْ عَلَى مَنْ لَمْ يَشْهَدِ الْجُمُعَةَ غُسْلٌ مِنَ النِّسَاءِ وَالصِّبْيَانِ وَغَيْرِهِمْ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ إِنَّمَا الْغُسْلُ عَلَى مَنْ تَجِبُ عَلَيْهِ الْجُمُعَةُ.

chapters.title: Chapter
chapters.title_arabic: باب

chapters.title: Not to attend the Jumu'ah (prayer) if it is raining.
chapters.title_arabic: باب الرُّخْصَةِ إِنْ لَمْ يَحْضُرِ الْجُمُعَةَ فِي الْمَطَرِ

chapters.title: For whom is the Jumu'ah (prayer) compulsory?
chapters.intro:
(What judgment is inferred from) the Statement of Allah جل جلاله:

"...When the call is proclaimed for the Salat (prayer) on Friday (Jumu'ah prayer), come to the remembrance of Allah [Jumu'ah religious talk (Khutba) and Salat (prayer)]." (V.62:9)

And 'Ata said, "If you are in a village and the Adhan is pronounced for the Salat-ul-Jumu'ah (Jumu'ah prayer), it is obligatory for you to present yourself for the Jumu'ah (prayer) whether you hear the Adhan or not. And at times, Anas used to establish the Salat-ul-Jumu'ah (Jumu'ah prayer) at his palace and sometimes he did not, while he was at a place called Az-Zawiya, situated at a distance of two parasangs (about six miles from Basrah)."
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ مِنْ أَيْنَ تُؤْتَى الْجُمُعَةُ، وَعَلَى مَنْ تَجِبُ؟
chapters.intro_arabic:
لِقَوْلِ اللَّهِ : {إِذَا نُودِيَ لِلصَّلاَةِ مِنْ يَوْمِ الْجُمُعَةِ فَٱ سْعَوْا إلَٰى ذِكْرِ ٱللهِ}

وقالَ عَطاءٌ : إِذا كُنْتَ فِي قَرْيَةٍ جامِعَةٍ نُودِيَ بِالصَّلاةِ مِنْ يَوْمِ الجُمُعَةِ فَحَقٌّ عَلَيْكَ أنْ تَشْهَدَها، سَمِعْتَ النَّدَاءَ أو لَمْ تَسْمَعْهُ. وَكَانَ أَنَسٌ رَضِيَ اللهُ عَنْهُ فِي قَصْرِهِ أَحْياناً يُجَمِّعُ وأحْياناً لا يُجَمِّعُ، وَهُوَ بِالزَّاوِيَةِ عَلَى فَرْسَخَينِ.

chapters.title: The time for the Jumu'ah (prayer) is due when the sun declines (i.e. just after mid-day)
chapters.intro: The same was said by 'Umar, 'Ali, An-Nu'man bin Bashir, and 'Amr bin Huraith رضي الله عنهم.
chapters.title_arabic: باب وَقْتُ الْجُمُعَةِ إِذَا زَالَتِ الشَّمْسُ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَكَذَلِكَ يُرْوَى عَنْ عُمَرَ وَعَلِيٍّ وَالنُّعْمَانِ بْنِ بَشِيرٍ وَعَمْرِو بْنِ حُرَيْثٍ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمْ.

chapters.title: If it becomes very hot on Fridays
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا اشْتَدَّ الْحَرُّ يَوْمَ الْجُمُعَةِ

chapters.title: To go walking unhurriedly
chapters.intro:
And the Statement of Allah تعالى: "...Come to the remembrance of Allah [Jumu'ah religious talk (Khutba) and Salat (prayer)]" (V.62:9) and whoever said that the meaning of "come" is "to prepare and go for the Salat (prayer)" as is inferred from the Statement of Allah تعالى: "And strives for it, with the necessary effort due for it..." (V.17:19)

And Ibn 'Abbas رضي الله عنهما said: "Selling is forbidden at that time." And 'Ata' said, "All types of work are forbidden [at the time of the Salat (prayer)]." And narrated Az-Zuhri: If the Adhan is pronounced by the Mu'adh-dhin on Friday [for Salat-ul-Jumu'ah (Jumu'ah prayer)], anyone on a journey should attend the Salat (prayer).
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْمَشْىِ إِلَى الْجُمُعَةِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَقَوْلِ اللَّهِ- جَلَّ ذِكْرُهُ- {فَاسْعَوْا إِلَى ذِكْرِ اللَّهِ}.
وَمَنْ قَالَ السَّعْيُ الْعَمَلُ وَالذَّهَابُ لِقَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى: {وَسَعَى لَهَا سَعْيَهَا}.
وَقَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمَا يَحْرُمُ الْبَيْعُ حِينَئِذٍ.
وَقَالَ عَطَاءٌ تَحْرُمُ الصِّنَاعَاتُ كُلُّهَا.
وَقَالَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ سَعْدٍ عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ إِذَا أَذَّنَ الْمُؤَذِّنُ يَوْمَ الْجُمُعَةِ وَهْوَ مُسَافِرٌ فَعَلَيْهِ أَنْ يَشْهَدَ.

chapters.title: One should not separate two persons on Fridays
chapters.title_arabic: باب لاَ يُفَرَّقُ بَيْنَ اثْنَيْنِ يَوْمَ الْجُمُعَةِ

chapters.title: A man should not make his brother get up to sit in his place
chapters.title_arabic: باب لاَ يُقِيمُ الرَّجُلُ أَخَاهُ يَوْمَ الْجُمُعَةِ وَيَقْعُدُ فِي مَكَانِهِ

chapters.title: Adhan on Friday (for the Jumu'ah prayer)
chapters.title_arabic: باب الأَذَانِ يَوْمَ الْجُمُعَةِ

chapters.title: One Mu'adh-dhin on Friday
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْمُؤَذِّنِ الْوَاحِدِ يَوْمَ الْجُمُعَةِ

chapters.title: The Imam repeats the wordings of the Adhan when he hears it
chapters.title_arabic: باب يُؤَذِّنُ الإِمَامُ عَلَى الْمِنْبَرِ إِذَا سَمِعَ النِّدَاءَ

chapters.title: To sit on the pulpit while Adhan is pronounced
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْجُلُوسِ عَلَى الْمِنْبَرِ عِنْدَ التَّأْذِينِ

chapters.title: Adhan before delivering the Khutba
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّأْذِينِ عِنْدَ الْخُطْبَةِ

chapters.title: (To deliver) the Khutba on the pulpit
chapters.intro: And Anas رضي الله عنه said: "The Prophet ﷺ delivered the Khutba on the pulpit."
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْخُطْبَةِ عَلَى الْمِنْبَرِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ أَنَسٌ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ خَطَبَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ عَلَى الْمِنْبَرِ.

chapters.title: To deliver the Khutba while standing
chapters.intro: And Anas رضي الله عنه said: "While the Prophet ﷺ was delivering the Khutba (religious talk) standing..."
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْخُطْبَةِ قَائِمًا
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ أَنَسٌ بَيْنَا النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يَخْطُبُ قَائِمًا.

chapters.title: The facing of the Imam towards the people
chapters.intro: And Ibn 'Umar and Anas رضي الله عنهم faced the Imam.
chapters.title_arabic: باب يَسْتَقْبِلُ الإِمَامُ الْقَوْمَ وَاسْتِقْبَالِ النَّاسِ الإِمَامَ إِذَا خَطَبَ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَاسْتَقْبَلَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ وَأَنَسٌ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمْ الإِمَامَ.

chapters.title: Saying "Ammaba'du" in the Khutba
chapters.intro: Ibn 'Abbas quoted this from the Prophet ﷺ.
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ قَالَ فِي الْخُطْبَةِ بَعْدَ الثَّنَاءِ أَمَّا بَعْدُ
chapters.intro_arabic: رَوَاهُ عِكْرِمَةُ عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ.

chapters.title: To sit in between the two Khutba
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْقَعْدَةِ بَيْنَ الْخُطْبَتَيْنِ يَوْمَ الْجُمُعَةِ

chapters.title: To listen to the Khutba (religious talk) on Friday
chapters.title_arabic: باب الاِسْتِمَاعِ إِلَى الْخُطْبَةِ

chapters.title: During the Khutba, two Rak'a Salat before sitting (Tahayyat-ul-Masjid)
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا رَأَى الإِمَامُ رَجُلاً جَاءَ وَهْوَ يَخْطُبُ أَمَرَهُ أَنْ يُصَلِّيَ رَكْعَتَيْنِ

chapters.title: When the Imam is delivering the Khutba, a light two Rak'a Salat (prayer) (Tahayyat-ul Masjid)
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ جَاءَ وَالإِمَامُ يَخْطُبُ صَلَّى رَكْعَتَيْنِ خَفِيفَتَيْنِ

chapters.title: To raise hands during the Khutba (religious talk)
chapters.title_arabic: باب رَفْعِ الْيَدَيْنِ فِي الْخُطْبَةِ

chapters.title: Istisqa' in the Khutba on Friday
chapters.title_arabic: باب الاِسْتِسْقَاءِ فِي الْخُطْبَةِ يَوْمَ الْجُمُعَةِ

chapters.title: One should keep quiet and listen while the Imam is delivering the Khutba
chapters.intro: And if a person says to his companion: "Be quiet and listen," even then he is doing something wrong (which will reduce his reward). Narrated Salman: The Prophet ﷺ said, "Whenever the Imam is delivering the Khutba (religious talk), it is essential for the audience to keep quiet and listen."
chapters.title_arabic: باب الإِنْصَاتِ يَوْمَ الْجُمُعَةِ وَالإِمَامُ يَخْطُبُ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَإِذَا قَالَ لِصَاحِبِهِ أَنْصِتْ. فَقَدْ لَغَا.
وَقَالَ سَلْمَانُ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يُنْصِتُ إِذَا تَكَلَّمَ الإِمَامُ.

chapters.title: An hour (opportune-lucky time) on Friday
chapters.title_arabic: باب السَّاعَةِ الَّتِي فِي يَوْمِ الْجُمُعَةِ

chapters.title: If some people leave while the Imam during the Salat-ul-Jumu'ah
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا نَفَرَ النَّاسُ عَنِ الإِمَامِ فِي صَلاَةِ الْجُمُعَةِ فَصَلاَةُ الإِمَامِ وَمَنْ بَقِيَ جَائِزَةٌ

chapters.title: To offer Salat before and after the Jumu'ah prayer
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّلاَةِ بَعْدَ الْجُمُعَةِ وَقَبْلَهَا

chapters.title: When the Salat is ended, you may disperse through the land, and seek of the Bounty of Allah
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {فَإِذَا قُضِيَتِ الصَّلاَةُ فَانْتَشِرُوا فِي الأَرْضِ وَابْتَغُوا مِنْ فَضْلِ اللَّهِ}

chapters.title: The afternoon nap after the Jumu'ah (prayer)
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْقَائِلَةِ بَعْدَ الْجُمُعَةِ

chapters.title: The Salat-ul-Khauf
chapters.intro: And the Statement of Allah تعالى: "And when you (Muslims) travel in the land, there is no sin on you if you shorten As-Salat (prayer) if you fear that the disbelievers may put you in trial (attack you etc.), verily the disbelievers are ever unto you open enemies. When you (O Messenger Muhammad ﷺ) are among them, and lead them in As-Salat (the prayer), let one party of them stand up (in Salat) with you taking their arms with them; when they finish their prostrations, let them take their position in the rear, and let the other party come up which has not yet offered Salat and let them offer Salat with you taking all precautions, and bearing arms. Those who disbelieve wish, if you were negligent of your arms and your baggage, to attack you in a single rush, but there is no sin on you if you put away your arms because of the inconvenience of rain or because you are ill, but take (every) precaution for yourselves. Verily! Allah has prepared a humiliating torment for the disbelievers. (V.<qref chapter="4" verses="101-102">4:101-102</qref>)
chapters.title_arabic: باب صَلاَةِ الْخَوْفِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {وَإِذَا ضَرَبْتُمْ فِي الأَرْضِ فَلَيْسَ عَلَيْكُمْ جُنَاحٌ أَنْ تَقْصُرُوا مِنَ الصَّلاَةِ إِنْ خِفْتُمْ أَنْ يَفْتِنَكُمُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا إِنَّ الْكَافِرِينَ كَانُوا لَكُمْ عَدُوًّا مُبِينًا وَإِذَا كُنْتَ فِيهِمْ فَأَقَمْتَ لَهُمُ الصَّلاَةَ فَلْتَقُمْ طَائِفَةٌ مِنْهُمْ مَعَكَ وَلْيَأْخُذُوا أَسْلِحَتَهُمْ فَإِذَا سَجَدُوا فَلْيَكُونُوا مِنْ وَرَائِكُمْ وَلْتَأْتِ طَائِفَةٌ أُخْرَى لَمْ يُصَلُّوا فَلْيُصَلُّوا مَعَكَ وَلْيَأْخُذُوا حِذْرَهُمْ وَأَسْلِحَتَهُمْ وَدَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لَوْ تَغْفُلُونَ عَنْ أَسْلِحَتِكُمْ وَأَمْتِعَتِكُمْ فَيَمِيلُونَ عَلَيْكُمْ مَيْلَةً وَاحِدَةً وَلاَ جُنَاحَ عَلَيْكُمْ إِنْ كَانَ بِكُمْ أَذًى مِنْ مَطَرٍ أَوْ كُنْتُمْ مَرْضَى أَنْ تَضَعُوا أَسْلِحَتَكُمْ وَخُذُوا حِذْرَكُمْ إِنَّ اللَّهَ أَعَدَّ لِلْكَافِرِينَ عَذَابًا مُهِينًا}.

chapters.title: The Salat-ul-Khauf while standing or riding
chapters.title_arabic: باب صَلاَةِ الْخَوْفِ رِجَالاً وَرُكْبَانًا رَاجِلٌ قَائِمٌ

chapters.title: To guard one another during the Salat-ul-Khauf
chapters.title_arabic: باب يَحْرُسُ بَعْضُهُمْ بَعْضًا فِي صَلاَةِ الْخَوْفِ

chapters.title: As-Salat at the time of besieging a fort and at the time of meeting the enemy
chapters.intro: Al-Auza'i said, "If there are signs of victory and it is impossible to offer the Salat (prayer) then everyone should offer his Salat (prayer) with signs. If he cannot do even that then he can delay the Salat (prayer) till the battle is over or till they feel secure, then they can offer two Rak'at prayers. But if they are unable to complete the Salat (prayer) then they can perform one bowing and two prostrations (one Rak'a), and if they are unable to do even this, then saying Allahu-Akbar is not sufficient for them, and so they should postpone the Salat (prayer) till they feel secure." Mak-hul agrees to it (i.e. this verdict). And Anas bin Malik said, "I reached at dawn during the siege of Tustar and the fighting was at its peak. The Muslims were not able to offer the Fajr prayer and we did not offer Salat (prayer) till a part of the day had passed and then we offered the Salat with Abu Musa and we were granted victory by Allah." Anas further said, "It would not make me happy even if I got the whole world and whatever is in it instead of that Salat (prayer) (i.e. the one they missed because of fighting)."
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّلاَةِ عِنْدَ مُنَاهَضَةِ الْحُصُونِ وَلِقَاءِ الْعَدُوِّ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَقَالَ الأَوْزَاعِيُّ إِنْ كَانَ تَهَيَّأَ الْفَتْحُ، وَلَمْ يَقْدِرُوا عَلَى الصَّلاَةِ صَلَّوْا إِيمَاءً كُلُّ امْرِئٍ لِنَفْسِهِ، فَإِنْ لَمْ يَقْدِرُوا عَلَى الإِيمَاءِ أَخَّرُوا الصَّلاَةَ، حَتَّى يَنْكَشِفَ الْقِتَالُ أَوْ يَأْمَنُوا، فَيُصَلُّوا رَكْعَتَيْنِ، فَإِنْ لَمْ يَقْدِرُوا صَلَّوْا رَكْعَةً وَسَجْدَتَيْنِ، لاَ يُجْزِئُهُمُ التَّكْبِيرُ وَيُؤَخِّرُوهَا حَتَّى يَأْمَنُوا وَبِهِ قَالَ مَكْحُولٌ.
وَقَالَ أَنَسٌ حَضَرْتُ عِنْدَ مُنَاهَضَةِ حِصْنِ تُسْتَرَ عِنْدَ إِضَاءَةِ الْفَجْرِ، وَاشْتَدَّ اشْتِعَالُ الْقِتَالِ، فَلَمْ يَقْدِرُوا عَلَى الصَّلاَةِ، فَلَمْ نُصَلِّ إِلاَّ بَعْدَ ارْتِفَاعِ النَّهَارِ، فَصَلَّيْنَاهَا وَنَحْنُ مَعَ أَبِي مُوسَى، فَفُتِحَ لَنَا.
وَقَالَ أَنَسٌ وَمَا يَسُرُّنِي بِتِلْكَ الصَّلاَةِ الدُّنْيَا وَمَا فِيهَا.

chapters.title: The chaser and the chased can offer Salat (prayer) while riding , and by signs
chapters.intro: Al-Walid said: I told Al-Auza'i about the Salat (prayer) of Shurahbil bin As-Samt and his companions on the backs of animals. On that, he said, "That was the case with us if we feared that the time of Salat would be over." Al-Walid (disagreed with Al-Auza'i) deriving his verdict from the statement of the Prophet ﷺ: "None should offer the 'Asr prayer but at Bani Quraiza."
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ صَلاَةِ الطَّالِبِ وَالْمَطْلُوبِ رَاكِبًا وَإِيمَاءً
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ الْوَلِيدُ ذَكَرْتُ لِلأَوْزَاعِيِّ صَلاَةَ شُرَحْبِيلَ بْنِ السِّمْطِ وَأَصْحَابِهِ عَلَى ظَهْرِ الدَّابَّةِ فَقَالَ كَذَلِكَ الأَمْرُ عِنْدَنَا إِذَا تُخُوِّفَ الْفَوْتُ، وَاحْتَجَّ الْوَلِيدُ بِقَوْلِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «لاَ يُصَلِّيَنَّ أَحَدٌ الْعَصْرَ إِلاَّ فِي بَنِي قُرَيْظَةَ».

chapters.title: Offering As-Salat (the prayers) while attacking the enemy and in battles
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّبْكِيرِ وَالْغَلَسِ بِالصُّبْحِ وَالصَّلاَةِ عِنْدَ الإِغَارَةِ وَالْحَرْبِ

chapters.title: The two Eid and sprucing oneself up on them
chapters.title_arabic: باب فِي الْعِيدَيْنِ وَالتَّجَمُّلِ فِيهِ

chapters.title: A display of spears and shields on 'Eid Festival day
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْحِرَابِ وَالدَّرَقِ يَوْمَ الْعِيدِ

chapters.title: The legal way of the celebrations on the two 'Eid festivals
chapters.title_arabic: باب سُنَّةِ الْعِيدَيْنِ لأَهْلِ الإِسْلاَمِ

chapters.title: Eating on the day of Fitr before ethe 'Eid-al-Fitr
chapters.title_arabic: باب الأَكْلِ يَوْمَ الْفِطْرِ قَبْلَ الْخُرُوجِ

chapters.title: Eating on the day of Nahr (10th of Dhul-Hijjah)
chapters.title_arabic: باب الأَكْلِ يَوْمَ النَّحْرِ

chapters.title: To proceed to a masjid without a pulpit
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْخُرُوجِ إِلَى الْمُصَلَّى بِغَيْرِ مِنْبَرٍ

chapters.title: Eid prayer before the Khutba and no Adhan or Iqama for it
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْمَشْىِ وَالرُّكُوبِ إِلَى الْعِيدِ بِغَيْرِ أَذَانٍ وَلاَ إِقَامَةٍ

chapters.title: The Khutba after the Eid prayer
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْخُطْبَةِ بَعْدَ الْعِيدِ

chapters.title: It is disliked to carry arms on 'Eid and in the Haram
chapters.intro: And Al-Hasan said: (In the lifetime of the Prophet ﷺ) It was forbidden to carry arms on the day of 'Eid except if there was fear from the enemy.
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يُكْرَهُ مِنْ حَمْلِ السِّلاَحِ فِي الْعِيدِ وَالْحَرَمِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ الْحَسَنُ نُهُوا أَنْ يَحْمِلُوا السِّلاَحَ يَوْمَ عِيدٍ إِلاَّ أَنْ يَخَافُوا عَدُوًّا.

chapters.title: To offer the Eid prayer early
chapters.intro: 'Abdullah bin Busr said: We used to finish the 'Eid prayer (in the lifetime of the Prophet ﷺ) at the time of Tasbih (Duha or Ishraq prayer) i.e. after sunrise.
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّبْكِيرِ إِلَى الْعِيدِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ بُسْرٍ إِنْ كُنَّا فَرَغْنَا فِي هَذِهِ السَّاعَةِ، وَذَلِكَ حِينَ التَّسْبِيحِ.

chapters.title: Superiority of deeds on the days of Tashriq
chapters.intro: Ibn 'Abbas said, "Remember Allah during the well-known days; i.e., the first ten days of Dhul-Hijjah, and also the fixed number of appointed days; i.e. the days of Tashriq." Ibn 'Umar and Abu Hurairah used to go out to the market saying Takbir during the first ten days of Dhul-Hijjah and the people would say Takbir after their Takbirs. Muhammad bin 'Ali used to say Takbir after Nawafil.
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ الْعَمَلِ فِي أَيَّامِ التَّشْرِيقِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَقَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ وَاذْكُرُوا اللَّهَ فِي أَيَّامٍ مَعْلُومَاتٍ أَيَّامُ الْعَشْرِ، وَالأَيَّامُ الْمَعْدُودَاتُ أَيَّامُ التَّشْرِيقِ.
وَكَانَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ وَأَبُو هُرَيْرَةَ يَخْرُجَانِ إِلَى السُّوقِ فِي أَيَّامِ الْعَشْرِ يُكَبِّرَانِ، وَيُكَبِّرُ النَّاسُ بِتَكْبِيرِهِمَا.
وَكَبَّرَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ خَلْفَ النَّافِلَةِ.

chapters.title: To say Takbir on the days of Mina and while proceeding to Arafat
chapters.intro: 'Umar رضي الله عنه during his stay at Mina, used to say Takbir in his tent (with such a loud voice) that the people in the mosque would hear it and they too would start saying Takbir and the people in the market too would do the same and then the whole Mina would quiver with Takbir. During those days, Ibn 'Umar used to say Takbir at Mina and after the (compulsory) Salat (prayers) and also while in bed in his tent, while sitting, and while walking. He used to do so during all those days. Maimuna used to say Takbir on the day of Nahr. The women used to say Takbir behind Aban bin 'Uthman and 'Umar bin 'Abdul Aziz, along with the men in the mosque during the nights of Tashriq.
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّكْبِيرِ أَيَّامَ مِنًى وَإِذَا غَدَا إِلَى عَرَفَةَ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَكَانَ عُمَرُ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ يُكَبِّرُ فِي قُبَّتِهِ بِمِنًى فَيَسْمَعُهُ أَهْلُ الْمَسْجِدِ، فَيُكَبِّرُونَ وَيُكَبِّرُ أَهْلُ الأَسْوَاقِ، حَتَّى تَرْتَجَّ مِنًى تَكْبِيرًا.
وَكَانَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ يُكَبِّرُ بِمِنًى تِلْكَ الأَيَّامَ وَخَلْفَ الصَّلَوَاتِ، وَعَلَى فِرَاشِهِ وَفِي فُسْطَاطِهِ، وَمَجْلِسِهِ وَمَمْشَاهُ تِلْكَ الأَيَّامَ جَمِيعًا.
وَكَانَتْ مَيْمُونَةُ تُكَبِّرُ يَوْمَ النَّحْرِ. وَكُنَّ النِّسَاءُ يُكَبِّرْنَ خَلْفَ أَبَانَ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ وَعُمَرَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزَ لَيَالِيَ التَّشْرِيقِ مَعَ الرِّجَالِ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ.

chapters.title: As-Salat using a small spear as a Sutra
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّلاَةِ إِلَى الْحَرْبَةِ يَوْمَ الْعِيدِ

chapters.title: To put the 'Anaza (spear-headed stick) or Harba in front of the Imam on Eid day
chapters.title_arabic: باب حَمْلِ الْعَنَزَةِ أَوِ الْحَرْبَةِ بَيْنَ يَدَىِ الإِمَامِ يَوْمَ الْعِيدِ

chapters.title: The coming out of the ladies and menstruating women to the Masjid
chapters.title_arabic: باب خُرُوجِ النِّسَاءِ وَالْحُيَّضِ إِلَى الْمُصَلَّى

chapters.title: The attendance of boys at Musalla
chapters.title_arabic: باب خُرُوجِ الصِّبْيَانِ إِلَى الْمُصَلَّى

chapters.title: The Imam faces the people while delivering the Khutna (religious talk) of Eid
chapters.intro: Abu Sa'id said, "The Prophet ﷺ stood facing the people."
chapters.title_arabic: باب اسْتِقْبَالِ الإِمَامِ النَّاسَ فِي خُطْبَةِ الْعِيدِ
chapters.intro_arabic: قَالَ أَبُو سَعِيدٍ قَامَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ مُقَابِلَ النَّاسِ.

chapters.title: The mark of the Musall
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْعَلَمِ الَّذِي بِالْمُصَلَّى

chapters.title: The preaching to the woman by the Imam on Eid day
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَوْعِظَةِ الإِمَامِ النِّسَاءَ يَوْمَ الْعِيدِ

chapters.title: If a women has no veil to use for Eid
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا لَمْ يَكُنْ لَهَا جِلْبَابٌ فِي الْعِيدِ

chapters.title: Menstruating women should keep away from the Mussalla
chapters.title_arabic: باب اعْتِزَالِ الْحُيَّضِ الْمُصَلَّى

chapters.title: An-Nahr and Adh-Dhabh at the Masjid on the day of Nahr
chapters.title_arabic: باب النَّحْرِ وَالذَّبْحِ يَوْمَ النَّحْرِ بِالْمُصَلَّى

chapters.title: If the Imam is asked about something while he is delivering the Khutba
chapters.title_arabic: باب كَلاَمِ الإِمَامِ وَالنَّاسِ فِي خُطْبَةِ الْعِيدِ، وَإِذَا سُئِلَ الإِمَامُ عَنْ شَيْءٍ وَهْوَ يَخْطُبُ

chapters.title: Whoever returned (after offering the Eid prayer) on the day of Eid through a way different from that by which he went
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ خَالَفَ الطَّرِيقَ إِذَا رَجَعَ يَوْمَ الْعِيدِ

chapters.title: Whoever missed the Eid prayer should offer two Rak'a prayer
chapters.intro: And similarly the women and those who are at home and in the villages should do so, as is confirmed by the statement of the Prophet ﷺ: "O Muslims, this is our 'Eid." At Az-Zawiya, Anas bin Malik ordered his slave Ibn Abi Ghaniya to collect his (Anas’s) family and offspring. Anas led a Salat (prayer) similar to that offered by the people of any town and recited Takbir similar to theirs. 'Ikrima said, "The villagers should gather on the day of 'Eid and offer two Rak'a as the Imam does." 'Ata' said, "Whoever misses the Salat-al-'Eid ('Eid prayer) should offer two Rak'a prayer."
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا فَاتَهُ الْعِيدُ يُصَلِّي رَكْعَتَيْنِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَكَذَلِكَ النِّسَاءُ، وَمَنْ كَانَ فِي الْبُيُوتِ وَالْقُرَى لِقَوْلِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «هَذَا عِيدُنَا أَهْلَ الإِسْلاَمِ». وَأَمَرَ أَنَسُ بْنُ مَالِكٍ مَوْلاَهُمُ ابْنَ أَبِي عُتْبَةَ بِالزَّاوِيَةِ، فَجَمَعَ أَهْلَهُ وَبَنِيهِ، وَصَلَّى كَصَلاَةِ أَهْلِ الْمِصْرِ وَتَكْبِيرِهِمْ.
وَقَالَ عِكْرِمَةُ أَهْلُ السَّوَادِ يَجْتَمِعُونَ فِي الْعِيدِ يُصَلُّونَ رَكْعَتَيْنِ كَمَا يَصْنَعُ الإِمَامُ.
وَقَالَ عَطَاءٌ إِذَا فَاتَهُ الْعِيدُ صَلَّى رَكْعَتَيْنِ.

chapters.title: Salat before or after the Eid prayer
chapters.intro: Ibn 'Abbas disliked to offer Salat (prayer) before 'Eid prayer.
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّلاَةِ قَبْلَ الْعِيدِ وَبَعْدَهَا
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ أَبُو الْمُعَلَّى سَمِعْتُ سَعِيدًا عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ كَرِهَ الصَّلاَةَ قَبْلَ الْعِيدِ.

chapters.title: What is said regarding the Witr prayer
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا جَاءَ فِي الْوِتْرِ

chapters.title: The timing of the Salat-ul-Witr (Witr prayer)
chapters.intro: Abu Hurairah said, "The Prophet ﷺ told me to offer Witr prayer before sleeping."
chapters.title_arabic: باب سَاعَاتِ الْوِتْرِ
chapters.intro_arabic: قَالَ أَبُو هُرَيْرَةَ أَوْصَانِي النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ بِالْوِتْرِ قَبْلَ النَّوْمِ.

chapters.title: The Prophet (pbuh) used to wake his family up for the Witr prayer
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِيقَاظِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم أَهْلَهُ بِالْوِتْرِ

chapters.title: Witr as the last Salat (at night)
chapters.title_arabic: باب لِيَجْعَلْ آخِرَ صَلاَتِهِ وِتْرًا

chapters.title: To offer the Witr Salat while riding on an animal
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْوِتْرِ عَلَى الدَّابَّةِ

chapters.title: Offering prayers of Witr while on a journey
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْوِتْرِ فِي السَّفَرِ

chapters.title: To recite Qunut before and after bowing
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْقُنُوتِ قَبْلَ الرُّكُوعِ وَبَعْدَهُ

chapters.title: Going out of the Prophet (pbuh) to offer Istisqa' prayer
chapters.title_arabic: باب الاِسْتِسْقَاءِ وَخُرُوجِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم فِي الاِسْتِسْقَاءِ

chapters.title: Invocation of the Prophet (pbuh)
chapters.title_arabic: باب دُعَاءِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم ‏"‏ اجْعَلْهَا عَلَيْهِمْ سِنِينَ كَسِنِي يُوسُفَ ‏"‏

chapters.title: Request of the people to the Imam to offer the Istisqa' prayer
chapters.title_arabic: باب سُؤَالِ النَّاسِ الإِمَامَ الاِسْتِسْقَاءَ إِذَا قَحَطُوا

chapters.title: Turning cloak inside out while offering the Istisqa' prayer
chapters.title_arabic: باب تَحْوِيلِ الرِّدَاءِ فِي الاِسْتِسْقَاءِ

chapters.title: The taking of revenge by Allah over His creatures by drought
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ انْتِقَامِ الرَّبِّ جَلَّ وَعَزَّ مِنْ خَلْقِهِ بِالْقَحْطِ إِذَا انْتُهِكَ مَحَارِمُ اللَّهِ

chapters.title: Istisqa' in the main mosque (of the town)
chapters.title_arabic: باب الاِسْتِسْقَاءِ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ الْجَامِعِ

chapters.title: To invoke Allah for rain in the Khutba of Friday facing a direction other than the Qiblah
chapters.title_arabic: باب الاِسْتِسْقَاءِ فِي خُطْبَةِ الْجُمُعَةِ غَيْرَ مُسْتَقْبِلِ الْقِبْلَةِ

chapters.title: Istisqa' (to invoke Allah for the rain) on the pulpit
chapters.title_arabic: باب الاِسْتِسْقَاءِ عَلَى الْمِنْبَرِ

chapters.title: Whoever To invoke Allah for rain in the Jumu'ah prayer
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنِ اكْتَفَى بِصَلاَةِ الْجُمُعَةِ فِي الاِسْتِسْقَاءِ

chapters.title: Invocation (for stoppage of rain)
chapters.title_arabic: باب الدُّعَاءِ إِذَا تَقَطَّعَتِ السُّبُلُ مِنْ كَثْرَةِ الْمَطَرِ

chapters.title: The saying that "The Prophet (pbuh) did not turn his cloak inside out during the invocation for rain on Friday."
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ مَا قِيلَ إِنَّ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ لَمْ يُحَوِّلْ رِدَاءَهُ فِي الاِسْتِسْقَاءِ يَوْمَ الْجُمُعَةِ

chapters.title: If the people request the Imam to invoke Allah for rain, the Imam should not refuse
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا اسْتَشْفَعُوا إِلَى الإِمَامِ لِيَسْتَسْقِيَ لَهُمْ لَمْ يَرُدُّهُمْ

chapters.title: If Al-Mushrikun intercede the Muslim to invoke Allah for rain during drought
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا اسْتَشْفَعَ الْمُشْرِكُونَ بِالْمُسْلِمِينَ عِنْدَ الْقَحْطِ

chapters.title: To say, "Around us and not on us." when it rains excessively
chapters.title_arabic: باب الدُّعَاءِ إِذَا كَثُرَ الْمَطَرُ حَوَالَيْنَا وَلاَ عَلَيْنَا

chapters.title: To invoke Allah for rain while standing
chapters.title_arabic: باب الدُّعَاءِ فِي الاِسْتِسْقَاءِ قَائِمًا

chapters.title: To recite aloud while offering the prayer of Istisqa'
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْجَهْرِ بِالْقِرَاءَةِ فِي الاِسْتِسْقَاءِ

chapters.title: How the Prophet (pbuh) turned his back towards the people [while offering the Salat (prayer) for rain]
chapters.title_arabic: باب كَيْفَ حَوَّلَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم ظَهْرَهُ إِلَى النَّاسِ

chapters.title: The Istisqa' prayer consists of two Rak'a
chapters.title_arabic: باب صَلاَةِ الاِسْتِسْقَاءِ رَكْعَتَيْنِ

chapters.title: To offer the Istisqa' prayer at the Musalla
chapters.title_arabic: باب الاِسْتِسْقَاءِ فِي الْمُصَلَّى

chapters.title: Facing the Qiblah while offering the Istisqa' prayer
chapters.title_arabic: باب اسْتِقْبَالِ الْقِبْلَةِ فِي الاِسْتِسْقَاءِ

chapters.title: While offering the Istisqa' prayer, people should raise hands along with the Imam
chapters.title_arabic: باب رَفْعِ النَّاسِ أَيْدِيَهُمْ مَعَ الإِمَامِ فِي الاِسْتِسْقَاءِ

chapters.title: The raising of both hands by the Imam during Istisqa' while invoking Allah for rain
chapters.title_arabic: باب رَفْعِ الإِمَامِ يَدَهُ فِي الاِسْتِسْقَاءِ

chapters.title: What should be said (or what to say) if it rains
chapters.intro: Ibn 'Abbas رضي الله عنه said regarding ka-sayyib-in, it is rain. Others said: saba yasubu, and (derived from it is) asaba.
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يُقَالُ إِذَا أَمْطَرَتْ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَقَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ: {كَصَيِّبٍ} الْمَطَرُ.
وَقَالَ غَيْرُهُ صَابَ وَأَصَابَ يَصُوبُ.

chapters.title: One who stood in the rain till the water started trickling down his beard
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ تَمَطَّرَ فِي الْمَطَرِ حَتَّى يَتَحَادَرَ عَلَى لِحْيَتِهِ

chapters.title: If the wind blows (what should one do or say?)
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا هَبَّتِ الرِّيحُ

chapters.title: "I was granted victory with As-Saba"
chapters.title_arabic: باب قَوْلِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم ‏"‏ نُصِرْتُ بِالصَّبَا ‏"‏

chapters.title: Earthquakes and (other) signs (of the Day of Judgement)
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا قِيلَ فِي الزَّلاَزِلِ وَالآيَاتِ

chapters.title: "And instead for the provision He gives you, you deny"
chapters.intro: Ibn 'Abbas رضي الله عنهما said that it means that you should be thankful to Allah (but instead of being grateful you are ungrateful to Allah).
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {وَتَجْعَلُونَ رِزْقَكُمْ أَنَّكُمْ تُكَذِّبُونَ}
chapters.intro_arabic: قَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ شُكْرَكُمْ.

chapters.title: Except Allah nobody nobody knows when it will rain
chapters.intro: And Abu Hurairah narrated that the Prophet ﷺ said, "There are five things which nobody knows except Allah."
chapters.title_arabic: باب لاَ يَدْرِي مَتَى يَجِيءُ الْمَطَرُ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ أَبُو هُرَيْرَةَ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «خَمْسٌ لاَ يَعْلَمُهُنَّ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ».

chapters.title: As-Salat (the prayer) during a solar eclipse
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّلاَةِ فِي كُسُوفِ الشَّمْسِ

chapters.title: To give Sadaqa during the eclipse
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّدَقَةِ فِي الْكُسُوفِ

chapters.title: Making a loud announcement of As-Salat (the prayer) in congregation for eclipse
chapters.title_arabic: باب النِّدَاءِ بِالصَّلاَةُ جَامِعَةٌ فِي الْكُسُوفِ

chapters.title: A Khutba (delivered) by the Imam on the eclipse
chapters.intro: 'Aishah and Asma' رضي الله عنهما said that the Prophet ﷺ delivered a Khutba (religious talk) (on such an occasion).
chapters.title_arabic: باب خُطْبَةِ الإِمَامِ فِي الْكُسُوفِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَتْ عَائِشَةُ وَأَسْمَاءُ خَطَبَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ.

chapters.title: Should one say: The sun Kasafat or Khasafat?
chapters.intro: Allah تعالى says: “And the moon Khasafat (eclipsed).” (V.<qref chapter="75" verses="8">75:8</qref>)
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ هَلْ يَقُولُ كَسَفَتِ الشَّمْسُ أَوْ خَسَفَتْ؟
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى: {وَخَسَفَ الْقَمَرُ}

chapters.title: "Allah frightens His slaves with Kusuf (eclipse)
chapters.intro: And this has been narrated by Abu Musa from the Prophet ﷺ.
chapters.title_arabic: باب قَوْلِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم ‏: «يُخَوِّفُ اللَّهُ عِبَادَهُ بِالْكُسُوفِ»
chapters.intro_arabic: قَالَهُ أَبُو مُوسَى عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ.

chapters.title: To seek refuge with Allah from the torment in the grave during eclipse
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّعَوُّذِ مِنْ عَذَابِ الْقَبْرِ فِي الْكُسُوفِ

chapters.title: To prolong the prostrations in the eclipse Salat
chapters.title_arabic: باب طُولِ السُّجُودِ فِي الْكُسُوفِ

chapters.title: To offer the Eclipse Salat (prayer) in congregation
chapters.intro: Ibn 'Abbas رضي الله عنهما offered the eclipse prayer with the people (in congregation) by the side of the Zamzam well. 'Ali bin 'Abdullah bin 'Abbas رضي الله عنهم also offered that Salat in congregation and Ibn 'Umar رضي الله عنهما also offered it (in the same way).
chapters.title_arabic: باب صَلاَةِ الْكُسُوفِ جَمَاعَةً
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَصَلَّى ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ لَهُمْ فِي صُفَّةِ زَمْزَمَ.
وَجَمَعَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَبَّاسٍ. وَصَلَّى ابْنُ عُمَرَ.

chapters.title: Offering of Eclipse prayer by women along with men
chapters.title_arabic: باب صَلاَةِ النِّسَاءِ مَعَ الرِّجَالِ فِي الْكُسُوفِ

chapters.title: Manumision (of slaves) during the solar eclipse
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ أَحَبَّ الْعَتَاقَةَ فِي كُسُوفِ الشَّمْسِ

chapters.title: To offer the eclipse prayer in the mosque
chapters.title_arabic: باب صَلاَةِ الْكُسُوفِ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ

chapters.title: The solar eclipse does not occur because of someone's death or life
chapters.intro: And this has been narrated by Abu Bakra, Al-Mughira, Abu Musa, Ibn 'Abbas, and Ibn 'Umar رضي الله عنهم.
chapters.title_arabic: باب لاَ تَنْكَسِفُ الشَّمْسُ لِمَوْتِ أَحَدٍ وَلاَ لِحَيَاتِهِ
chapters.intro_arabic: رَوَاهُ أَبُو بَكْرَةَ وَالْمُغِيرَةُ وَأَبُو مُوسَى وَابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ وَابْنُ عُمَرَ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمْ.

chapters.title: To remember Allah during the eclipse
chapters.intro: This is narrated by Ibn 'Abbas رضي الله عنهما.
chapters.title_arabic: باب الذِّكْرِ فِي الْكُسُوفِ
chapters.intro_arabic: رَوَاهُ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمَا.

chapters.title: Invocation during the eclipse
chapters.intro: And this is narrated by Abu Musa and 'Aishah رضي الله عنهما from the Prophet ﷺ.
chapters.title_arabic: باب الدُّعَاءِ فِي الْخُسُوفِ
chapters.intro_arabic: قَالَهُ أَبُو مُوسَى وَعَائِشَةُ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمَا عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ.

chapters.title: The saying of Amma ba'du during the Khutba of the eclipse
chapters.title_arabic: باب قَوْلِ الإِمَامِ فِي خُطْبَةِ الْكُسُوفِ أَمَّا بَعْدُ

chapters.title: The prayer f the lunar eclipse
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّلاَةِ فِي كُسُوفِ الْقَمَرِ

chapters.title: The first Rak'a of the eclipse prayer is longer
chapters.title_arabic: باب الرَّكْعَةُ الأُولَى فِي الْكُسُوفِ أَطْوَلُ

chapters.title: To recite aloud in the eclipse Salat
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْجَهْرِ بِالْقِرَاءَةِ فِي الْكُسُوفِ

chapters.title: Prostrations during the recitation of the Qur'an
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا جَاءَ فِي سُجُودِ الْقُرْآنِ وَسُنَّتِهَا

chapters.title: To prostrate during the recitation of Salat Tanzil - As-Sajda (No. 32)
chapters.title_arabic: باب سَجْدَةِ تَنْزِيلُ السَّجْدَةُ

chapters.title: To prostrate while reciting Surat Sad (No. 38)
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ سَجْدَةِ {ص}

chapters.title: The prostration in An-Najm (No. 53)
chapters.intro: Ibn 'Abbas رضي الله عنهما narrates this from the Prophet ﷺ.
chapters.title_arabic: باب سَجْدَةِ النَّجْمِ
chapters.intro_arabic: قَالَهُ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمَا عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ.

chapters.title: The prostrattion of Muslims along with Al-Mushrikun; and a Mushrik is Najasun (impure) and does not perform ablution;
chapters.intro: Ibn 'Umar رضي الله عنه used to prostrate without ablution.
chapters.title_arabic: باب سُجُودِ الْمُسْلِمِينَ مَعَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ. وَالْمُشْرِكُ نَجَسٌ لَيْسَ لَهُ وُضُوءٌ.
chapters.intro_arabic: وَكَانَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمَا يَسْجُدُ عَلَى وُضُوءٍ.

chapters.title: Whoever recited the verses of prostration and did not prostrate
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ قَرَأَ السَّجْدَةَ وَلَمْ يَسْجُدْ

chapters.title: Prostration while reciting Idhat-Sama'un-Shaqqat
chapters.title_arabic: باب سَجْدَةِ ‏<qref chapter="84" verses="1">{‏إِذَا السَّمَاءُ انْشَقَّتْ‏}</qref>‏

chapters.title: Whoever prostrated with the prostration of the reciter
chapters.intro: And Ibn Mas'ud asked Tamim bin Hadhlam, while he was a boy, to recite Surah and said to him, "Prostrate as you are our Imam."
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ سَجَدَ لِسُجُودِ الْقَارِئِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ ابْنُ مَسْعُودٍ لِتَمِيمِ بْنِ حَذْلَمٍ- وَهْوَ غُلاَمٌ- فَقَرَأَ عَلَيْهِ سَجْدَةً، فَقَالَ اسْجُدْ فَإِنَّكَ إِمَامُنَا فِيهَا.

chapters.title: The overcrowding of the people when the Imam recites As-Sajda
chapters.title_arabic: باب ازْدِحَامِ النَّاسِ إِذَا قَرَأَ الإِمَامُ السَّجْدَةَ

chapters.title: Whoever thinks that Allah has not made prostration of recitation compulsory
chapters.intro: And 'Imran bin Hussain was asked if a man heard As-Sajda but was not sitting to listen to it (would the prostration be compulsory for him?) He said, "In my opinion, prostration is not compulsory for him even if he were sitting to listen to it." And Salman (who once heard Surat As-Sajda but did not prostrate) said, "I did not come with the intention of listening to it", and 'Uthman رضي الله عنه said, "The prostration is compulsory for the person who listens to it." And Az-Zuhri said, "Do not perform the prostration of recitation without ablution, and when you are a non-traveler, face the Qiblah while performing the prostration of recitation and if you are riding perform it in whatever direction you are facing." And As-Sa'ib bin Yazid did not perform the prostrations of recitation while a story-teller or a preacher was reciting the Verses of prostration.
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ رَأَى أَنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ لَمْ يُوجِبِ السُّجُودَ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَقِيلَ لِعِمْرَانَ بْنِ حُصَيْنٍ الرَّجُلُ يَسْمَعُ السَّجْدَةَ وَلَمْ يَجْلِسْ لَهَا قَالَ أَرَأَيْتَ لَوْ قَعَدَ لَهَا كَأَنَّهُ لاَ يُوجِبُهُ عَلَيْهِ.
وَقَالَ سَلْمَانُ مَا لِهَذَا غَدَوْنَا.
وَقَالَ عُثْمَانُ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ إِنَّمَا السَّجْدَةُ عَلَى مَنِ اسْتَمَعَهَا.
وَقَالَ الزُّهْرِيُّ لاَ يَسْجُدُ إِلاَّ أَنْ يَكُونَ طَاهِرًا، فَإِذَا سَجَدْتَ وَأَنْتَ فِي حَضَرٍ فَاسْتَقْبِلِ الْقِبْلَةَ، فَإِنْ كُنْتَ رَاكِبًا فَلاَ عَلَيْكَ حَيْثُ كَانَ وَجْهُكَ.
وَكَانَ السَّائِبُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ لاَ يَسْجُدُ لِسُجُودِ الْقَاصِّ.

chapters.title: Whoever recited the Verse of Sajda during the Salat (prayer) and prostrated
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ قَرَأَ السَّجْدَةَ فِي الصَّلاَةِ فَسَجَدَ بِهَا

chapters.title: Whoever does not find a place for prostration (with the Imam) because of overcrowding
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ لَمْ يَجِدْ مَوْضِعًا لِلسُّجُودِ مِنَ الزِّحَامِ

chapters.title: For what period of stay one should offer shortened prayers
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا جَاءَ فِي التَّقْصِيرِ وَكَمْ يُقِيمُ حَتَّى يَقْصُرَ

chapters.title: As-Salat (the prayers) at Mina (during Hajj)
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّلاَةِ بِمِنًى

chapters.title: How long did the Prophet (pbup) stay during the Hajj?
chapters.title_arabic: باب كَمْ أَقَامَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم فِي حَجَّتِهِ

chapters.title: The length of the journey that makes it permissible to offer a shortened prayer?
chapters.intro: The Prophet ﷺ called a journey of one day and one night as travelling. Ibn 'Umar, Ibn 'Abbas رضي الله عنهما used to shorten the Salat and stop fasting in a journey of four Burud, i.e. sixteen Farsakh (distance of 3 miles equals one Farsakh).
chapters.title_arabic: باب فِي كَمْ يَقْصُرُ الصَّلاَةَ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَسَمَّى النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يَوْمًا وَلَيْلَةً سَفَرًا. وَكَانَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ وَابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمْ يَقْصُرَانِ وَيُفْطِرَانِ فِي أَرْبَعَةِ بُرُدٍ وَهْيَ سِتَّةَ عَشَرَ فَرْسَخًا.

chapters.title: When a traveller leaves his original place, he can shorten his Salat (prayer)
chapters.intro: **Once 'Ali (bin Abi Talib) رضي الله عنه left (Kufa) and started shortening the Salat (prayers) although the houses (of Kufa) were in sight. On his return, he was told, "This is Kufa." (So that he would no longer shorten the Salat). He said, "No, [I will go on shortening the
chapters.title_arabic: باب يَقْصُرُ إِذَا خَرَجَ مِنْ مَوْضِعِهِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَخَرَجَ عَلِيٌّ- عَلَيْهِ السَّلاَمُ- فَقَصَرَ وَهْوَ يَرَى الْبُيُوتَ فَلَمَّا رَجَعَ قِيلَ لَهُ هَذِهِ الْكُوفَةُ. قَالَ لاَ حَتَّى نَدْخُلَهَا.

chapters.title: Three Rak'a of Maghrib prayer during the journey
chapters.title_arabic: باب يُصَلِّي الْمَغْرِبَ ثَلاَثًا فِي السَّفَرِ

chapters.title: To offer Nawafil on the back of animals
chapters.title_arabic: باب صَلاَةِ التَّطَوُّعِ عَلَى الدَّوَابِّ وَحَيْثُمَا تَوَجَّهَتْ بِهِ

chapters.title: To offer the Salat by signs (while riding) on an animal
chapters.title_arabic: باب الإِيمَاءِ عَلَى الدَّابَّةِ

chapters.title: To get down to offer compulsory Salat
chapters.title_arabic: باب يَنْزِلُ لِلْمَكْتُوبَةِ

chapters.title: To offer the Nawafil while riding a donkey
chapters.title_arabic: باب صَلاَةِ التَّطَوُّعِ عَلَى الْحِمَارِ

chapters.title: Whoever did not offer the Nawafil before and after the (compulsory) Salat during a journey
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ لَمْ يَتَطَوَّعْ فِي السَّفَرِ دُبُرَ الصَّلاَةِ وَقَبْلَهَا

chapters.title: Whoever offered Nawafil not after the compulsory Salat but before it
chapters.intro: The Prophet ﷺ offered two Rak'a before the Fajr prayers on a journey.
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ تَطَوَّعَ فِي السَّفَرِ فِي غَيْرِ دُبُرِ الصَّلَوَاتِ وَقَبْلَهَا
chapters.intro_arabic: وَرَكَعَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ رَكْعَتَيِ الْفَجْرِ فِي السَّفَرِ.

chapters.title: To offer Maghrib and "Isha" prayers together on a journey
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْجَمْعِ فِي السَّفَرِ بَيْنَ الْمَغْرِبِ وَالْعِشَاءِ

chapters.title: Should the Adhan and Iqama be pronounced when the Maghrib and Isha prayers are offered together
chapters.title_arabic: باب هَلْ يُؤَذِّنُ أَوْ يُقِيمُ إِذَا جَمَعَ بَيْنَ الْمَغْرِبِ وَالْعِشَاءِ

chapters.title: To delay the Salat-uz-Zuhr till the Asr prayers
chapters.intro: Ibn 'Abbas رضي الله عنهما narrated this on the authority of the Prophet ﷺ.
chapters.title_arabic: باب يُؤَخِّرُ الظُّهْرَ إِلَى الْعَصْرِ إِذَا ارْتَحَلَ قَبْلَ أَنْ تَزِيغَ الشَّمْسُ
chapters.intro_arabic: فِيهِ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ.

chapters.title: Whenever a person travels after midday, he should offer the Zuhr prayers and the ride for the journey
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا ارْتَحَلَ بَعْدَ مَا زَاغَتِ الشَّمْسُ صَلَّى الظُّهْرَ ثُمَّ رَكِبَ

chapters.title: To offer Salat (prayers) while sitting
chapters.title_arabic: باب صَلاَةِ الْقَاعِدِ

chapters.title: To offer Salat (prayers) by signs while sitting
chapters.title_arabic: باب صَلاَةِ الْقَاعِدِ بِالإِيمَاءِ

chapters.title: Whoever cannot offer Salat while sitting, can offer Salat while lying on his side
chapters.intro: ‘Ata’ said, “If one is unable to turn towards the Qiblah then he can offer the Salat (prayer) in whatever direction his face may be.”
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا لَمْ يُطِقْ قَاعِدًا صَلَّى عَلَى جَنْبٍ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ عَطَاءٌ إِنْ لَمْ يَقْدِرْ أَنْ يَتَحَوَّلَ إِلَى الْقِبْلَةِ صَلَّى حَيْثُ كَانَ وَجْهُهُ.

chapters.title: Whoever starts his Salat sitting (because of ailment) and then during the Salat (prayer) feels better, can finish the rest while standing
chapters.intro: Al-Hasan said, “If the sick person wishes he can offer two Rak'a while standing and two Rak'a while sitting.”
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا صَلَّى قَاعِدًا ثُمَّ صَحَّ أَوْ وَجَدَ خِفَّةً تَمَّمَ مَا بَقِيَ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ الْحَسَنُ إِنْ شَاءَ الْمَرِيضُ صَلَّى رَكْعَتَيْنِ قَائِمًا وَرَكْعَتَيْنِ قَاعِدًا.

chapters.title: The Tahajjud Prayer at night
chapters.intro: And the Statement of Allah جل جلاله : “And in some parts of the night (also) offer the Salat (prayer) with it (i.e., recite the Qur'an in the prayer), as an additional prayer (Tahajjud optional prayer — Nawafil) for you... (O Muhammad ﷺ)” (V.<qref chapter="17" verses="79">17:79</qref>).
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّهَجُّدِ بِاللَّيْلِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَوْلِهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ: {وَمِنَ اللَّيْلِ فَتَهَجَّدْ بِهِ نَافِلَةً لَكَ}.

chapters.title: The superiority of Tahajjud prayer
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ قِيَامِ اللَّيْلِ

chapters.title: To perform a long prostration in the Tahajjud
chapters.title_arabic: باب طُولِ السُّجُودِ فِي قِيَامِ اللَّيْلِ

chapters.title: Leaving the night prayer by a patient
chapters.title_arabic: باب تَرْكِ الْقِيَامِ لِلْمَرِيضِ

chapters.title: The Prophet's (pbuh) exhorting to Tahajjud and Nawafil without making them compulsory
chapters.intro: The Prophet ﷺ went to ‘Ali and Fatima رضي الله عنهما at night and knocked their door to exhort them to perform Salat (i.e. night prayers).
chapters.title_arabic: باب تَحْرِيضِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم عَلَى صَلاَةِ اللَّيْلِ وَالنَّوَافِلِ مِنْ غَيْرِ إِيجَابٍ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَطَرَقَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فَاطِمَةَ وَعَلِيًّا- عليهما السلام- لَيْلَةً لِلصَّلاَةِ.

chapters.title: Standing of the Prophet (pbuh) at night
chapters.intro: ‘Aishah رضي الله عنها narrated that the Prophet ﷺ used to offer Salat (prayer) till his feet used to be swollen (oedematous).
chapters.title_arabic: باب قِيَامِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم حَتَّى تَرِمَ قَدَمَاهُ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَتْ عَائِشَةُ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهَا حَتَّى تَفَطَّرَ قَدَمَاهُ. وَالْفُطُورُ الشُّقُوقُ، {انْفَطَرَتْ} انْشَقَّتْ.

chapters.title: Sleeping in the last hours of the night
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ نَامَ عِنْدَ السَّحَرِ

chapters.title: Whoever took the Suhur and did not sleep before offering Fajr prayers
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ تَسَحَّرَ فَلَمْ يَنَمْ حَتَّى صَلَّى الصُّبْحَ

chapters.title: To prolong the standing posture in the Tahajjud
chapters.title_arabic: باب طُولِ الْقِيَامِ فِي صَلاَةِ اللَّيْلِ

chapters.title: How was the Salat of the Prophet (pbuh) and how many Rak'a he used to offer at night?
chapters.title_arabic: باب كَيْفَ كَانَ صَلاَةُ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم وَكَمْ كَانَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يُصَلِّي مِنَ اللَّيْلِ

chapters.title: The waking up of the Prophet ﷺ from his sleep for the night prayer and what (how much) was cancelled from the night prayer
chapters.intro:
And the Statement of Allah تعالى: “O you wrapped in garments (i.e. Prophet Muhammad ﷺ)! Stand (to pray) all night, except a little. Half of it, or a little less than that, or a little more; and recite the Qur'an (aloud) in a slow (pleasant tone and) style. Verily, We shall send down to you a weighty Word (i.e. obligations, laws). Verily, the rising by night (for Tahajjud prayer) is very hard and most potent and good for governing (oneself), and most suitable for (understanding) the Word (of Allah). Verily, there is for you by day prolonged occupation with ordinary duties.” (V.73:1-7)

And Allah's Statement: “…He knows that you are unable to pray the whole night, so He has turned to you (in mercy). So, recite you of the Qur’an as much as may be easy for you. He knows that there will be some among you sick, others travelling through the land, seeking of Allah’s Bounty, yet others fighting in Allah’s Cause. So recite as much of the Qur’an as may be easy (for you), and perform As-Salat (Iqamat-as-Salat) and give Zakat, and lend to Allah, a goodly loan, and whatever good you send before you for yourselves, (i.e. Nawafil — non-obligatory acts of worship: prayers, charity, fasting, Hajj and Umra, etc.), you will certainly find it with Allah, better and greater in reward...” (V.73:20)
chapters.title_arabic: باب قِيَامِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم بِاللَّيْلِ وَنَوْمِهِ وَمَا نُسِخَ مِنْ قِيَامِ اللَّيْلِ

chapters.title: Satan'a tying of knots at the back of the head if one does not offer the ight prayer
chapters.title_arabic: باب عَقْدِ الشَّيْطَانِ عَلَى قَافِيَةِ الرَّأْسِ إِذَا لَمْ يُصَلِّ بِاللَّيْلِ

chapters.title: If one sleeps and does not offer the night prayer
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا نَامَ وَلَمْ يُصَلِّ بَالَ الشَّيْطَانُ فِي أُذُنِهِ

chapters.title: Offering Salat (prayer) and invoking Allah in the last hours of the night
chapters.intro: And Allah جل جلاله says: “They used to sleep but little by night, [invoking their Lord (Allah) and praying with fear and hope]. And in the hours before dawn, they were (found) asking (Allah) for forgiveness.” (V.<qref chapter="51" verses="17,18">51:17,18</qref>)
chapters.title_arabic: باب الدُّعَاءِ وَالصَّلاَةِ مِنْ آخِرِ اللَّيْلِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ: {كَانُوا قَلِيلاً مِنَ اللَّيْلِ مَا يَهْجَعُونَ} أَيْ مَا يَنَامُونَ {وَبِالأَسْحَارِ هُمْ يَسْتَغْفِرُونَ}.

chapters.title: Sleeping in the first part of the night and getting up in its last part
chapters.intro: Salman asked Abi Ad-Darda’ to sleep, and when it was the last part of the night, he told him to get up. (When this news reached the Prophet ﷺ), he said, “Salman said the truth.”
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ نَامَ أَوَّلَ اللَّيْلِ وَأَحْيَا آخِرَهُ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ سَلْمَانُ لأَبِي الدَّرْدَاءِ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمَا نَمْ. فَلَمَّا كَانَ مِنْ آخِرِ اللَّيْلِ قَالَ قُمْ. قَالَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «صَدَقَ سَلْمَانُ».

chapters.title: The Salat (prayer) of the Prophet (pbuh) at night in Ramadan and (in) other months
chapters.title_arabic: باب قِيَامِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم بِاللَّيْلِ فِي رَمَضَانَ وَغَيْرِهِ

chapters.title: The Superiority of remaining with ablution during the day and night
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ الطُّهُورِ بِاللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهَارِ وَفَضْلِ الصَّلاَةِ بَعْدَ الْوُضُوءِ بِاللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهَارِ

chapters.title: It is disliked to exaggerate in matters of worship
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يُكْرَهُ مِنَ التَّشْدِيدِ فِي الْعِبَادَةِ

chapters.title: It is disliked for a person to leave the night Salat
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يُكْرَهُ مِنْ تَرْكِ قِيَامِ اللَّيْلِ لِمَنْ كَانَ يَقُومُهُ

chapters.title: Chapter:
chapters.title_arabic: باب

chapters.title: The superiority of one who wakes up at night and offers the Salat with a loud voice
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ مَنْ تَعَارَّ مِنَ اللَّيْلِ فَصَلَّى

chapters.title: Regularity of two Rak'a (Sunna) of the Fajr
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْمُدَاوَمَةِ عَلَى رَكْعَتَىِ الْفَجْرِ

chapters.title: To lie down on the right side after offering two Rak'a (Sunna) of the Fajr prayer
chapters.title_arabic: باب الضِّجْعَةِ عَلَى الشِّقِّ الأَيْمَنِ بَعْدَ رَكْعَتَىِ الْفَجْرِ

chapters.title: Whoever talks after offering two Rak'a (Sunna) (of the Fajr prayer)
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ تَحَدَّثَ بَعْدَ الرَّكْعَتَيْنِ وَلَمْ يَضْطَجِعْ

chapters.title: What is said about the Nawafil being offered as two Rak'a followed by two and so on
chapters.intro:
And this has been narrated by ‘Ammar, Abu Dhar, Anas, Jabir bin Zaid, ‘Ikrima, and Az-Zuhri رضي الله عنهم.

Yahya bin Sa’id Al-Ansari said, “I saw the religious learned men of our town (Al-Madina) offering two Rak‘a of Nawafil and finishing them with Taslim during the day.”
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ الْحَدِيثِ بَعْدَ رَكْعَتَيِ الْفَجْرِ
chapters.intro_arabic: قَالَ مُحَمَّدٌ وَيُذْكَرُ ذَلِكَ عَنْ عَمَّارٍ وَأَبِي ذَرٍّ وَأَنَسٍ وَجَابِرِ بْنِ زَيْدٍ وَعِكْرِمَةَ وَالزُّهْرِيِّ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمْ. وَقَالَ يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ الأَنْصَارِيُّ مَا أَدْرَكْتُ فُقَهَاءَ أَرْضِنَا إِلاَّ يُسَلِّمُونَ فِي كُلِّ اثْنَتَيْنِ مِنَ النَّهَارِ.

chapters.title: To talk after offering two Rak'a (Sunna of the Fajr prayer)
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ تَعَاهُدِ رَكْعَتَيِ الْفَجْرِ وَمَنْ سَمَّاهُمَا تَطَوُّعًا

chapters.title: To offer two Rak'at before the Fajr prayer
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ مَا يُقْرَأُ فِي رَكْعَتَيِ الْفَجْرِ

chapters.title: What is recited in the two Rak'a (Sunna) of the Fajr
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ مَا جَاءَ فِي التَّطَوُّعِ مَثْنَى مَثْنَى

chapters.title: To offer the Nawafil after the compulsory (congregational) Salat (prayers)
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّطَوُّعِ بَعْدَ الْمَكْتُوبَةِ

chapters.title: Whoever did not offer the Salat after the compulsory (congregational) Salat (prayers
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ لَمْ يَتَطَوَّعْ بَعْدَ الْمَكْتُوبَةِ

chapters.title: To offer the Salat-ud-Duha in journey
chapters.title_arabic: باب صَلاَةِ الضُّحَى فِي السَّفَرِ

chapters.title: Whoever did not offer the Duha prayer and thought it permissible (to offer it)
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ لَمْ يُصَلِّ الضُّحَى وَرَآهُ وَاسِعًا

chapters.title: To offer Salat-ud-Duha when not traveling
chapters.intro: ‘Itban bin Malik narrated that on the authority of the Prophet ﷺ.
chapters.title_arabic: باب صَلاَةِ الضُّحَى فِي الْحَضَرِ
chapters.intro_arabic: قَالَهُ عِتْبَانُ بْنُ مَالِكٍ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ.

chapters.title: To offer two Rak'a before the Zuhr prayer
chapters.title_arabic: باب الرَّكْعَتَيْنِ قَبْلَ الظُّهْرِ

chapters.title: The (optional) salat before the Maghrib prayers
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّلاَةِ قَبْلَ الْمَغْرِبِ

chapters.title: To offer Nawafil in congregation
chapters.intro: This is narrated by Anas and ‘Aishah رضي الله عنهما on the authority of the Prophet ﷺ.
chapters.title_arabic: باب صَلاَةِ النَّوَافِلِ جَمَاعَةً
chapters.intro_arabic: ذَكَرَهُ أَنَسٌ وَعَائِشَةُ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمَا عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ.

chapters.title: To offer the Nawafil prayers at home
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّطَوُّعِ فِي الْبَيْتِ

chapters.title: The superiority of offering As-Salat in the Mosque of Makkah and Al-Madina
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ الصَّلاَةِ فِي مَسْجِدِ مَكَّةَ وَالْمَدِينَةِ

chapters.title: The Masjid Quba (the Mosque of Quba)
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَسْجِدِ قُبَاءٍ

chapters.title: Whoever visited the mosque of Quba every Saturday
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ أَتَى مَسْجِدَ قُبَاءٍ كُلَّ سَبْتٍ

chapters.title: To go to the Mosque of Quba
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِتْيَانِ مَسْجِدِ قُبَاءٍ مَاشِيًا وَرَاكِبًا

chapters.title: The superiority of the place between the pulpit and the grave (of the Prophet (pbuh))
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ مَا بَيْنَ الْقَبْرِ وَالْمِنْبَرِ

chapters.title: The Mosque of Bait-ul-Maqdis (Jerusalem)
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَسْجِدِ بَيْتِ الْمَقْدِسِ

chapters.title: To take the help of the hands while offering Salat
chapters.intro: Ibn ‘Abbas رضي الله عنهما said, “A person can take the help of any portion of his body,” and Abu Ishaq took off and then put on his cap during the Salat (prayer). And ‘Ali رضي الله عنه used to place his right palm over the left wrist except when he wanted to scratch his skin or arrange his clothes (he would lift it then).
chapters.title_arabic: باب اسْتِعَانَةِ الْيَدِ فِي الصَّلاَةِ إِذَا كَانَ مِنْ أَمْرِ الصَّلاَةِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمَا يَسْتَعِينُ الرَّجُلُ فِي صَلاَتِهِ مِنْ جَسَدِهِ بِمَا شَاءَ. وَوَضَعَ أَبُو إِسْحَاقَ قَلَنْسُوَتَهُ فِي الصَّلاَةِ وَرَفَعَهَا. وَوَضَعَ عَلِيٌّ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ كَفَّهُ عَلَى رُصْغِهِ الأَيْسَرِ، إِلاَّ أَنْ يَحُكَّ جِلْدًا أَوْ يُصْلِحَ ثَوْبًا.

chapters.title: What speech is prohibited during As-Salat
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يُنْهَى عَنْهُ مِنَ الْكَلاَمِ فِي الصَّلاَةِ

chapters.title: The saying of Subhan Allah and Al-handulillah during As-Salat
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يَجُوزُ مِنَ التَّسْبِيحِ وَالْحَمْدِ فِي الصَّلاَةِ لِلرِّجَالِ

chapters.title: Whoever named some people or greeted somebody during As-Salat
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ سَمَّى قَوْمًا أَوْ سَلَّمَ فِي الصَّلاَةِ عَلَى غَيْرِهِ مُوَاجَهَةً وَهُوَ لاَ يَعْلَمُ

chapters.title: Clapping [during the Salat] is permissible only for the women
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّصْفِيقُ لِلنِّسَاءِ

chapters.title: Whoever came back or went forward during the Salat (prayer) because of some urgent need
chapters.intro: This has been narrated by Sahl bin Sa‘d on the authority of the Prophet ﷺ.
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ رَجَعَ الْقَهْقَرَى فِي صَلاَتِهِ، أَوْ تَقَدَّمَ بِأَمْرٍ يَنْزِلُ بِهِ
chapters.intro_arabic: رَوَاهُ سَهْلُ بْنُ سَعْدٍ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ.

chapters.title: If a mother calls her son while he is offering As-Salat
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا دَعَتِ الأُمُّ وَلَدَهَا فِي الصَّلاَةِ

chapters.title: The levelling of small stones during As-Salat
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَسْحِ الْحَصَى فِي الصَّلاَةِ

chapters.title: Spreading the clothes over the site of prostration
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَسْطِ الثَّوْبِ فِي الصَّلاَةِ لِلسُّجُودِ

chapters.title: What kind of actions are permissible during As-Salat
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يَجُوزُ مِنَ الْعَمَلِ فِي الصَّلاَةِ

chapters.title: If an animal runs away while one is in Salat
chapters.intro: Qatada said, “If a thief takes away the clothes of a person in Salat (prayer) then he can leave the Salat (prayer) and follow the thief.”
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا انْفَلَتَتِ الدَّابَّةُ فِي الصَّلاَةِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ قَتَادَةُ إِنْ أُخِذَ ثَوْبُهُ يَتْبَعُ السَّارِقَ وَيَدَعُ الصَّلاَةَ.

chapters.title: Blowing and splitting while in As-Salat (the Prayer)
chapters.intro: And ‘Abdullah bin ‘Amr narrated that the Prophet ﷺ during the eclipse Salat (prayer), blew during his prostration.
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يَجُوزُ مِنَ الْبُصَاقِ وَالنَّفْخِ فِي الصَّلاَةِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَيُذْكَرُ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو نَفَخَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فِي سُجُودِهِ فِي كُسُوفٍ.

chapters.title: If a man claps during the Salat because of ignorance, his Salat will not be invalid
chapters.intro: (This has been narrated by Sahl bin Sa‘d on the authority of the Prophet ﷺ).
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ مَنْ صَفَّقَ جَاهِلاً مِنَ الرِّجَالِ فِي صَلاَتِهِ لَمْ تَفْسُدْ صَلاَتُهُ
