chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ لَمْ يَرَ عَلَيْهِ صَوْمًا إِذَا اعْتَكَفَ

chapters.title: Vow in the pre-Islamic period to perform I'tikaf
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا نَذَرَ فِي الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ أَنْ يَعْتَكِفَ ثُمَّ أَسْلَمَ

chapters.title: I'tikaf in the middle ten days of Ramadan
chapters.title_arabic: باب الاِعْتِكَافِ فِي الْعَشْرِ الأَوْسَطِ مِنْ رَمَضَانَ

chapters.title: Whoever intended I'tikaf and then changed his mind
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ أَرَادَ أَنْ يَعْتَكِفَ ثُمَّ بَدَا لَهُ أَنْ يَخْرُجَ

chapters.title: A Mu'takif can let his head in the house for washing
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْمُعْتَكِفِ يُدْخِلُ رَأْسَهُ الْبَيْتَ لِلْغَسْلِ

chapters.title: What has come in the Statement of Allah تعالى: “Then when the (Jumu‘ah) Salat is ended, you may disperse through the land, and seek of the Bounty of Allah.... And Allah is the Best of Providers.” (V.<qref chapter="62" verses="10,11">62:10,11</qref>)
chapters.intro: And also His Statement: “Eat not up your property among yourselves unjustly except it be a trade amongst you, by mutual consent...” (V.<qref chapter="4" verses="29">4:29</qref>)
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ مَا جَاءَ فِي قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {فَإِذَا قُضِيَتِ الصَّلاَةُ فَانْتَشِرُوا فِي الأَرْضِ وَابْتَغُوا مِنْ فَضْلِ اللَّهِ وَاذْكُرُوا اللَّهَ كَثِيرًا لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ وَإِذَا رَأَوْا تِجَارَةً أَوْ لَهْوًا انْفَضُّوا إِلَيْهَا وَتَرَكُوكَ قَائِمًا قُلْ مَا عِنْدَ اللَّهِ خَيْرٌ مِنَ اللَّهْوِ وَمِنَ التِّجَارَةِ وَاللَّهُ خَيْرُ الرَّازِقِينَ}
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَوْلِهِ: {لاَ تَأْكُلُوا أَمْوَالَكُمْ بَيْنَكُمْ بِالْبَاطِلِ إِلاَّ أَنْ تَكُونَ تِجَارَةً عَنْ تَرَاضٍ مِنْكُمْ}.

chapters.title: Legal, illegal, and doubtful things
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْحَلاَلُ بَيِّنٌ وَالْحَرَامُ بَيِّنٌ وَبَيْنَهُمَا مُشَبَّهَاتٌ

chapters.title: Explanation of doubtful (unclear) things
chapters.intro: Hassan bin Abi Sinan said, “I found nothing easier than to be pious and Allah-fearing; (to achieve this aim) leave all doubtful (unclear) things and do what is completely clear of doubt.”
chapters.title_arabic: باب تَفْسِيرِ الْمُشَبَّهَاتِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ حَسَّانُ بْنُ أَبِي سِنَانٍ مَا رَأَيْتُ شَيْئًا أَهْوَنَ مِنَ الْوَرَعِ، دَعْ مَا يَرِيبُكَ إِلَى مَا لاَ يَرِيبُكَ.

chapters.title: What doubtful (unclear) things should be avoided?
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يُتَنَزَّهُ مِنَ الشُّبُهَاتِ

chapters.title: Whoever does not consider dark suggestions
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ لَمْ يَرَ الْوَسَاوِسَ وَنَحْوَهَا مِنَ الْمُشَبَّهَاتِ

chapters.title: The Statement of Allah azza wajal: "And when they see some merchandise or some amusement they disperse headlong to it ..."
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {وَإِذَا رَأَوْا تِجَارَةً أَوْ لَهْوًا انْفَضُّوا إِلَيْهَا}

chapters.title: One who does not care from where he earns
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ لَمْ يُبَالِ مِنْ حَيْثُ كَسَبَ الْمَالَ

chapters.title: Trade of cloth and other things
chapters.intro:
And the Statement of Allah عز وجل: “Men whom neither trade nor sale (business) diverts them from the Remembrance of Allah (with heart and tongue)...” (V.24:37)

Qatada said, “The people used to do sale and trade; but whenever they were to perform any of Allah’s obligations, then trade and sale would not divert them from Allah’s worship, but they would rather fulfil that obligation (to Allah).”
chapters.title_arabic: باب التِّجَارَةِ فِي الْبَرِّ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَقَوْلِهِ: {رِجَالٌ لاَ تُلْهِيهِمْ تِجَارَةٌ وَلاَ بَيْعٌ عَنْ ذِكْرِ اللَّهِ}.
وَقَالَ قَتَادَةُ كَانَ الْقَوْمُ يَتَبَايَعُونَ، وَيَتَّجِرُونَ، وَلَكِنَّهُمْ إِذَا نَابَهُمْ حَقٌّ مِنْ حُقُوقِ اللَّهِ لَمْ تُلْهِهِمْ تِجَارَةٌ وَلاَ بَيْعٌ عَنْ ذِكْرِ اللَّهِ، حَتَّى يُؤَدُّوهُ إِلَى اللَّهِ.

chapters.title: Going out for Trading
chapters.intro: And the Statement of Allah عز وجل: “You may disperse through the land and seek of the Bounty of Allah (by working)...” (V.<qref chapter="62" verses="10">62:10</qref>)
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْخُرُوجِ فِي التِّجَارَةِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {فَانْتَشِرُوا فِي الأَرْضِ وَابْتَغُوا مِنْ فَضْلِ اللَّهِ}.

chapters.title: Trading in sea
chapters.intro:
And Matar said, “There is no harm in it, and whatever Allah has mentioned about it in the Qur’an, is but the truth.” Then he (Matar) recited:

“...And you see the ships ploughing through it, that you may seek (thus) of His Bounty (by transporting the goods from place to place)...” (V.16:14)
chapters.title_arabic: باب التِّجَارَةِ فِي الْبَحْرِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَقَالَ مَطَرٌ لاَ بَأْسَ بِهِ وَمَا ذَكَرَهُ اللَّهُ فِي الْقُرْآنِ إِلاَّ بِحَقٍّ ثُمَّ تَلاَ: {وَتَرَى الْفُلْكَ مَوَاخِرَ فِيهِ وَلِتَبْتَغُوا مِنْ فَضْلِهِ} وَالْفُلْكُ السُّفُنُ، الْوَاحِدُ وَالْجَمْعُ سَوَاءٌ.
وَقَالَ مُجَاهِدٌ تَمْخَرُ السُّفُنُ الرِّيحَ وَلاَ تَمْخَرُ الرِّيحَ مِنَ السُّفُنِ إِلاَّ الْفُلْكُ الْعِظَامُ.

chapters.title: “And when they see some merchandise or some amusement [beating of Tambur (drum) etc.], they disperse headlong to it...” (V.<qref chapter="62" verses="11">62:11</qref>). And Allah’s Statement: “Men whom neither trade nor sale divert them from the Remembrance of Allah...” (V.<qref chapter="24" verses="37">24:37</qref>)
chapters.intro: Qatada said, “The people used to trade, but whenever they were to perform any of Allah’s obligations, then neither trade nor sale would divert them from the Remembrance of Allah, but they would rather fulfil that obligation.”
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ: {وَإِذَا رَأَوْا تِجَارَةً أَوْ لَهْوًا انْفَضُّوا إِلَيْهَا} وَقَوْلُهُ جَلَّ ذِكْرُهُ: {رِجَالٌ لاَ تُلْهِيهِمْ تِجَارَةٌ وَلاَ بَيْعٌ عَنْ ذِكْرِ اللَّهِ}
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ قَتَادَةُ كَانَ الْقَوْمُ يَتَّجِرُونَ، وَلَكِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا إِذَا نَابَهُمْ حَقٌّ مِنْ حُقُوقِ اللَّهِ لَمْ تُلْهِهِمْ تِجَارَةٌ وَلاَ بَيْعٌ عَنْ ذِكْرِ اللَّهِ، حَتَّى يُؤَدُّوهُ إِلَى اللَّهِ.

chapters.title: Allah's Statement: "... Spend of the good things which you have earned ..."
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {أَنْفِقُوا مِنْ طَيِّبَاتِ مَا كَسَبْتُمْ}

chapters.title: Whoever liked to expand in his sustenance
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ أَحَبَّ الْبَسْطَ فِي الرِّزْقِ

chapters.title: The Prophet saws purchased (foodgrains) on credit
chapters.title_arabic: باب شِرَاءِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم بِالنَّسِيئَةِ

chapters.title: The earnings of a person and his manual labour
chapters.title_arabic: باب كَسْبِ الرَّجُلِ وَعَمَلِهِ بِيَدِهِ

chapters.title: One should be lenient and generous in bargaining
chapters.title_arabic: باب السُّهُولَةِ وَالسَّمَاحَةِ فِي الشِّرَاءِ وَالْبَيْعِ، وَمَنْ طَلَبَ حَقًّا فَلْيَطْلُبْهُ فِي عَفَافٍ

chapters.title: Whoever gave time to a rich person to pay at his convenience
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ أَنْظَرَ مُوسِرًا

chapters.title: A person in hard circumstances to pay debt (when able to repay)
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ أَنْظَرَ مُعْسِرًا

chapters.title: To explain the good and bad points of the transaction
chapters.intro:
Al-‘Adda’ bin Khalid said, “The Prophet ﷺ got this statement written for me: ‘This is what Muhammad, Allah’s Messenger ﷺ bought from ‘Adda’ bin Khalid as a Muslim sells to another Muslim and that it (i.e., the bought slave) is neither sick, nor bad-behaved nor stolen.’”

Qatada said that *Al-Gha’ila* means: adultery, theft, or (the slave) who runs away.

It was said to Ibrahim, “Some brokers name their stables and the stables of Khurasan and Sigstan and say, ‘It (i.e., the animal) arrived from Khurasan only yesterday (or) it came from Sigstan today.’” Ibrahim hated that very much.

‘Uqba bin ‘Amir said, “It is illegal for one to sell a thing if one knows that it has a defect, unless one informs the buyer of that defect.”
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا بَيَّنَ الْبَيِّعَانِ وَلَمْ يَكْتُمَا وَنَصَحَا
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَيُذْكَرُ عَنِ الْعَدَّاءِ بْنِ خَالِدٍ قَالَ كَتَبَ لِي النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «هَذَا مَا اشْتَرَى مُحَمَّدٌ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ مِنَ الْعَدَّاءِ بْنِ خَالِدٍ، بَيْعَ الْمُسْلِمِ الْمُسْلِمَ، لاَ دَاءَ، وَلاَ خِبْثَةَ، وَلاَ غَائِلَةَ».
وَقَالَ قَتَادَةُ الْغَائِلَةُ الزِّنَا وَالسَّرِقَةُ وَالإِبَاقُ. وَقِيلَ لإِبْرَاهِيمَ إِنَّ بَعْضَ النَّخَّاسِينَ يُسَمِّي آرِيَّ خُرَاسَانَ وَسِجِسْتَانَ فَيَقُولُ جَاءَ أَمْسِ مِنْ خُرَاسَانَ، جَاءَ الْيَوْمَ مِنْ سِجِسْتَانَ. فَكَرِهَهُ كَرَاهِيَةً شَدِيدَةً.
وَقَالَ عُقْبَةُ بْنُ عَامِرٍ لاَ يَحِلُّ لاِمْرِئٍ يَبِيعُ سِلْعَةً، يَعْلَمُ أَنَّ بِهَا دَاءً، إِلاَّ أَخْبَرَهُ.

chapters.title: Selling of mixed dates
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيْعِ الْخِلْطِ مِنَ التَّمْرِ

chapters.title: What is said about the meat seller and the butcher
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا قِيلَ فِي اللَّحَّامِ وَالْجَزَّارِ

chapters.title: The loss (of blessings) if one tells lies or hides the facts in a deal
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يَمْحَقُ الْكَذِبُ وَالْكِتْمَانُ فِي الْبَيْعِ

chapters.title: The Statement of Allah Ta'ala: "... Eat not Riba doubled and multiplied."
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لاَ تَأْكُلُوا الرِّبَا أَضْعَافًا مُضَاعَفَةً وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ}

chapters.title: The sin of Riba, its witness and its writer
chapters.intro:
And the Statement of Allah تعالى: “Those who eat Riba (usury) will not stand (on the Day of Resurrection) except like standing of a person beaten by Shaitan (Satan) leading him to insanity.

That is because they say: Trading is only like Riba (usury). Whereas Allah has permitted trading and forbidden Riba (usury). “So, whosoever receives an admonition from his Lord and stops eating Riba (usury) shall not be punished for the past; his case is for Allah (to judge); but whoever returns [to Riba (usury)], such are the dwellers of the Fire—they will abide therein.” (V.2:275)
chapters.title_arabic: باب آكِلِ الرِّبَا وَشَاهِدِهِ وَكَاتِبِهِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى: {الَّذِينَ يَأْكُلُونَ الرِّبَا لاَ يَقُومُونَ إِلاَّ كَمَا يَقُومُ الَّذِي يَتَخَبَّطُهُ الشَّيْطَانُ مِنَ الْمَسِّ ذَلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ قَالُوا إِنَّمَا الْبَيْعُ مِثْلُ الرِّبَا وَأَحَلَّ اللَّهُ الْبَيْعَ وَحَرَّمَ الرِّبَا فَمَنْ جَاءَهُ مَوْعِظَةٌ مِنْ رَبِّهِ فَانْتَهَى فَلَهُ مَا سَلَفَ وَأَمْرُهُ إِلَى اللَّهِ وَمَنْ عَادَ فَأُولَئِكَ أَصْحَابُ النَّارِ هُمْ فِيهَا خَالِدُونَ}.

chapters.title: The Riba-giver
chapters.intro:
As is referred to in the Statement of Allah تعالى: “O you who believe! Be afraid of Allah and give up what remains (due to you) from Riba (usury) (from now onwards), if you are (really) believers. And if you do not do it, then take a notice of war from Allah and His Messenger but if you repent, you shall have your capital sums. Deal not unjustly (by asking more than your capital sums), and you shall not be dealt with unjustly (by receiving less than your capital sums). And if the debtor is in a hard time (has no money), then grant him time till it is easy for him to repay, but if you remit it by way of charity, that is better for you if you did but know. And be afraid of the Day when you shall be brought back to Allah. Then every person shall be paid what he earned, and they shall not be dealt with unjustly.” (V.2:278-281)

Ibn Abbas said, “This was the last Verse revealed to the Prophet ﷺ.”
chapters.title_arabic: باب مُوكِلِ الرِّبَا
chapters.intro_arabic: لِقَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى: {يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اتَّقُوا اللَّهَ وَذَرُوا مَا بَقِيَ مِنَ الرِّبَا إِنْ كُنْتُمْ مُؤْمِنِينَ فَإِنْ لَمْ تَفْعَلُوا فَأْذَنُوا بِحَرْبٍ مِنَ اللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ وَإِنْ تُبْتُمْ فَلَكُمْ رُءُوسُ أَمْوَالِكُمْ لاَ تَظْلِمُونَ وَلاَ تُظْلَمُونَ وَإِنْ كَانَ ذُو عُسْرَةٍ فَنَظِرَةٌ إِلَى مَيْسَرَةٍ وَأَنْ تَصَدَّقُوا خَيْرٌ لَكُمْ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ وَاتَّقُوا يَوْمًا تُرْجَعُونَ فِيهِ إِلَى اللَّهِ ثُمَّ تُوَفَّى كُلُّ نَفْسٍ مَا كَسَبَتْ وَهُمْ لاَ يُظْلَمُونَ} قَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ هَذِهِ آخِرُ آيَةٍ نَزَلَتْ عَلَى النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ.

chapters.title: "Allah will destroy Riba and will give increase for Sadaqat"
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ: {يَمْحَقُ اللَّهُ الرِّبَا وَيُرْبِي الصَّدَقَاتِ وَاللَّهُ لاَ يُحِبُّ كُلَّ كَفَّارٍ أَثِيمٍ}

chapters.title: Giving oaths (swearing) while selling
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يُكْرَهُ مِنَ الْحَلِفِ فِي الْبَيْعِ

chapters.title: What is said about the goldsmiths
chapters.intro: Narrated Ibn ‘Abbas رضي الله عنهما: The Prophet ﷺ said, “Do not cut the shrubs of Makkah.” Al-‘Abbas said, “Except Al-Idhkhir (a kind of grass). It is used by their blacksmith and for their houses.” The Prophet ﷺ said, “Except Al-Idhkhir.”
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا قِيلَ فِي الصَّوَّاغِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَقَالَ طَاوُسٌ عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمَا قَالَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «لاَ يُخْتَلَى خَلاَهَا».
وَقَالَ الْعَبَّاسُ إِلاَّ الإِذْخِرَ، فَإِنَّهُ لِقَيْنِهِمْ وَبُيُوتِهِمْ.
فَقَالَ: «إِلاَّ الإِذْخِرَ».

chapters.title: The mentioning of blacksmiths
chapters.title_arabic: باب ذِكْرِ الْقَيْنِ وَالْحَدَّادِ

chapters.title: The mentioning of the tailor
chapters.title_arabic: باب ذِكْرِ الْخَيَّاطِ

chapters.title: The weaver
chapters.title_arabic: باب ذِكْرِ النَّسَّاجِ

chapters.title: The carpenter
chapters.title_arabic: باب النَّجَّارِ

chapters.title: The purchase by the ruler himself
chapters.intro: Ibn ‘Umar رضي الله عنهما said, “The Prophet ﷺ bought a camel from ‘Umar.” Ibn ‘Umar رضي الله عنهما purchased (goods) by himself. ‘Abdur-Rahman bin Abu Bakr رضي الله عنهما said: “A Mushrik came with sheep and the Prophet ﷺ bought a sheep from him and (the Prophet ﷺ also bought) a camel from Jabir.”
chapters.title_arabic: باب شِرَاءِ الْحَوَائِجِ بِنَفْسِهِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَقَالَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمَا اشْتَرَى النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ جَمَلاً مِنْ عُمَرَ.
وَقَالَ عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ أَبِي بَكْرٍ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمَا جَاءَ مُشْرِكٌ بِغَنَمٍ، فَاشْتَرَى النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ مِنْهُ شَاةً. وَاشْتَرَى مِنْ جَابِرٍ بَعِيرًا.

chapters.title: The purchase of animals and donkeys
chapters.intro:
If somebody buys an animal or a camel and the seller is still riding over it, will the bargain be regarded as settled before the seller gets down from it?

Ibn ‘Umar رضي الله عنهما said, “The Prophet ﷺ told ‘Umar to sell that unmanageable (untamed) camel to him.”
chapters.title_arabic: باب شِرَاءِ الدَّوَابِّ وَالْحَمِيرِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَإِذَا اشْتَرَى دَابَّةً أَوْ جَمَلاً وَهُوَ عَلَيْهِ، هَلْ يَكُونُ ذَلِكَ قَبْضًا قَبْلَ أَنْ يَنْزِلَ وَقَالَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمَا قَالَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ لِعُمَرَ: «بِعْنِيهِ». يَعْنِي جَمَلاً صَعْبًا.

chapters.title: The markets of the Pre-Islamic Period of Ignorance
chapters.title_arabic: باب الأَسْوَاقِ الَّتِي كَانَتْ فِي الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ فَتَبَايَعَ بِهَا النَّاسُ فِي الإِسْلاَمِ

chapters.title: Purchasing of camel suffering from disease
chapters.title_arabic: باب شِرَاءِ الإِبِلِ الْهِيمِ أَوِ الأَجْرَبِ
chapters.intro_arabic: الْهَائِمُ الْمُخَالِفُ لِلْقَصْدِ فِي كُلِّ شَيْءٍ.

chapters.title: Selling of arms during the period of Al-Fitnah
chapters.intro: ‘Imran bin Husain hated the selling (of arms) during Al-Fitnah (trial, affliction).
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيْعِ السِّلاَحِ فِي الْفِتْنَةِ وَغَيْرِهَا
chapters.intro_arabic: وَكَرِهَ عِمْرَانُ بْنُ حُصَيْنٍ بَيْعَهُ فِي الْفِتْنَةِ.

chapters.title: The perfume seller and the seller of musk
chapters.title_arabic: باب فِي الْعَطَّارِ وَبَيْعِ الْمِسْكِ

chapters.title: Al-Hajjam (i.e. the one who practises cupping)
chapters.title_arabic: باب ذِكْرِ الْحَجَّامِ

chapters.title: The trade of cloth, wearing of which is undesirable
chapters.title_arabic: باب التِّجَارَةِ فِيمَا يُكْرَهُ لُبْسُهُ لِلرِّجَالِ وَالنِّسَاءِ

chapters.title: The owner of a thing has to suggest a price
chapters.title_arabic: باب صَاحِبُ السِّلْعَةِ أَحَقُّ بِالسَّوْمِ

chapters.title: For what perios to confirm or cancel the bargain?
chapters.title_arabic: باب كَمْ يَجُوزُ الْخِيَارُ

chapters.title: If the time for the option is not fixed, will the deal be considered as legal?
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا لَمْ يُوَقِّتْ فِي الْخِيَارِ، هَلْ يَجُوزُ الْبَيْعُ

chapters.title: To cancel or confirm the bargain
chapters.intro: Ibn ‘Umar, Shuraih, Ash-Sha‘bi, Tawus, ‘Ata’, and Ibn Abi Mulaika agree upon this judgement.
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْبَيِّعَانِ بِالْخِيَارِ مَا لَمْ يَتَفَرَّقَا
chapters.intro_arabic: وَبِهِ قَالَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ وَشُرَيْحٌ وَالشَّعْبِيُّ وَطَاوُسٌ وَعَطَاءٌ وَابْنُ أَبِي مُلَيْكَةَ.

chapters.title: The option of cancelling the bargain
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا خَيَّرَ أَحَدُهُمَا صَاحِبَهُ بَعْدَ الْبَيْعِ فَقَدْ وَجَبَ الْبَيْعُ

chapters.title: If the seller has the option of cancelling the bargain
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا كَانَ الْبَائِعُ بِالْخِيَارِ، هَلْ يَجُوزُ الْبَيْعُ

chapters.title: To buy a thing and give it as a present
chapters.intro: Tawus said that if somebody bought a thing with mutual agreement and then sold it, then that was his property and the profit would be for him.
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ إِذَا اشْتَرَى شَيْئًا فَوَهَبَ مِنْ سَاعَتِهِ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَتَفَرَّقَا وَلَمْ يُنْكِرِ الْبَائِعُ عَلَى الْمُشْتَرِي، أَوِ اشْتَرَى عَبْدًا فَأَعْتَقَهُ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ طَاوُسٌ فِيمَنْ يَشْتَرِي السِّلْعَةَ عَلَى الرِّضَا ثُمَّ بَاعَهَا وَجَبَتْ لَهُ، وَالرِّبْحُ لَهُ.

chapters.title: What is disliked as regards cheating in business
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يُكْرَهُ مِنَ الْخِدَاعِ فِي الْبَيْعِ

chapters.title: What is said about markets
chapters.intro:
And narrated ‘Abdur-Rahman bin ‘Auf: On our arrival in Al-Madina, I asked whether there was a market of trading. Somebody said, “There is the market of Qainuqa‘.”

Narrated Anas: ‘Abdur-Rahman said, “Show me the market.” And ‘Umar said, “Trading in the market diverted my attention (from better things).”
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا ذُكِرَ فِي الأَسْوَاقِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَقَالَ عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ عَوْفٍ لَمَّا قَدِمْنَا الْمَدِينَةَ قُلْتُ هَلْ مِنْ سُوقٍ فِيهِ تِجَارَةٌ قَالَ سُوقُ قَيْنُقَاعَ.
وَقَالَ أَنَسٌ قَالَ عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ دُلُّونِي عَلَى السُّوقِ.
وَقَالَ عُمَرُ أَلْهَانِي الصَّفْقُ بِالأَسْوَاقِ.

chapters.title: The dislike of raising voices in the market
chapters.title_arabic: باب كَرَاهِيَةِ السَّخَبِ فِي السُّوقِ

chapters.title: Weighing goods by the seller or the giver
chapters.intro:
And the Statement of Allah عز وجل: “And when they have to give by measure or weight to (other) men, give less than due.” (V.83:3)

The Prophet ﷺ said, “When you receive what you buy by measure, let it be exact full measure.”

Narrated ‘Uthman رضي الله عنه that the Prophet ﷺ told him, “If you are the seller, you have to measure, and if you are the buyer, then let the seller measure for you.”
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْكَيْلِ عَلَى الْبَائِعِ وَالْمُعْطِي
chapters.intro_arabic:
لِقَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {وَإِذَا كَالُوهُمْ أَوْ وَزَنُوهُمْ يُخْسِرُونَ} يَعْنِي كَالُوا لَهُمْ وَوَزَنُوا لَهُمْ كَقَوْلِهِ: {يَسْمَعُونَكُمْ} يَسْمَعُونَ لَكُمْ.
وَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «اكْتَالُوا حَتَّى تَسْتَوْفُوا». وَيُذْكَرُ عَنْ عُثْمَانَ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ قَالَ لَهُ: «إِذَا بِعْتَ فَكِلْ، وَإِذَا ابْتَعْتَ فَاكْتَلْ».

chapters.title: What is considered preferable regarding measuring
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يُسْتَحَبُّ مِنَ الْكَيْلِ

chapters.title: Allah's Blessing in the Sa' and Mudd of the Prophet saws
chapters.intro: This has been narrated by ‘Aishah رضي الله عنها on the authority of the Prophet ﷺ.
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَرَكَةِ صَاعِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم وَمُدِّهِمْ
chapters.intro_arabic: فِيهِ عَائِشَةُ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهَا عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ.

chapters.title: The selling of the foodstuff and its storage
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يُذْكَرُ فِي بَيْعِ الطَّعَامِ وَالْحُكْرَةِ

chapters.title: The selling of the foodstuff before receiving it
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيْعِ الطَّعَامِ قَبْلَ أَنْ يُقْبَضَ، وَبَيْعِ مَا لَيْسَ عِنْدَكَ

chapters.title: Whoever bought foodstuff without measuring or weighing should not sell before bringing into house
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ مَنْ رَأَى إِذَا اشْتَرَى طَعَامًا جِزَافًا أَنْ لاَ يَبِيعَهُ حَتَّى يُئْوِيَهُ إِلَى رَحْلِهِ، وَالأَدَبِ فِي ذَلِكَ

chapters.title: If somebody buys some goods or (an) animal and let it with the seller, or it dies before he takes it into his possession
chapters.intro: Ibn ‘Umar رضي الله عنهما said, “If at the time of the transaction the sold animal is living and then it dies while still in the custody of the seller, then the buyer is the loser.”
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا اشْتَرَى مَتَاعًا أَوْ دَابَّةً فَوَضَعَهُ عِنْدَ الْبَائِع، أَوْ مَاتَ قَبْلَ أَنْ يُقْبَضَ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمَا مَا أَدْرَكَتِ الصَّفْقَةُ حَيًّا مَجْمُوعًا فَهُوَ مِنَ الْمُبْتَاعِ.

chapters.title: Not to cancel a bargain already agreed upon
chapters.title_arabic: باب لاَ يَبِيعُ عَلَى بَيْعِ أَخِيهِ وَلاَ يَسُومُ عَلَى سَوْمِ أَخِيهِ، حَتَّى يَأْذَنَ لَهُ أَوْ يَتْرُكَ

chapters.title: Selling by auction
chapters.intro: ‘Ata’ said, “I saw the people seeing no harm in selling war booty by auction.”
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيْعِ الْمُزَايَدَةِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ عَطَاءٌ أَدْرَكْتُ النَّاسَ لاَ يَرَوْنَ بَأْسًا بِبَيْعِ الْمَغَانِمِ فِيمَنْ يَزِيدُ.

chapters.title: An-Naish
chapters.intro: Ibn Abi Awfa said, “One who practices Najsh is a Riba-eating traitor.” And such a practice is a false trick which is forbidden, and the Prophet ﷺ said, “Deception would lead to the Fire (Hell) and whoever does a deed which we have not ordered (anyone) to do (or is not in accord with our religion of Islamic Monotheism) then that deed will not be accepted."
chapters.title_arabic: باب النَّجْشِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَمَنْ قَالَ لاَ يَجُوزُ ذَلِكَ الْبَيْعُ.
وَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي أَوْفَى النَّاجِشُ آكِلُ رِبًا خَائِنٌ. وَهْوَ خِدَاعٌ بَاطِلٌ، لاَ يَحِلُّ. قَالَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «الْخَدِيعَةُ فِي النَّارِ، وَمَنْ عَمِلَ عَمَلاً لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ أَمْرُنَا فَهْوَ رَدٌّ».

chapters.title: Al-Gharar and Habal-il-Habala
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيْعِ الْغَرَرِ وَحَبَلِ الْحَبَلَةِ

chapters.title: Al-Limas or Mulamasa
chapters.intro: Anas said, "The Prophet ﷺ forbade it (i.e., Al-Mulamasa sale)."
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيْعِ الْمُلاَمَسَةِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ أَنَسٌ نَهَى عَنْهُ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ.

chapters.title: Munabadha
chapters.intro: And Anas said, "The Prophet ﷺ forbade such sale."
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيْعِ الْمُنَابَذَةِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ أَنَسٌ نَهَى عَنْهُ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ.

chapters.title: The seller is not allowed to keep animal unmilked for a long time
chapters.intro: The seller is not allowed to keep camels, cows, sheep or any other animal unmilked for a long time (so as to get more price by cheating)
chapters.title_arabic: باب النَّهْىِ لِلْبَائِعِ أَنْ لاَ يُحَفِّلَ الإِبِلَ وَالْبَقَرَ وَالْغَنَمَ وَكُلَّ مُحَفَّلَة
chapters.intro_arabic: وَالْمُصَرَّاةُ الَّتِي صُرِّيَ لَبَنُهَا وَحُقِنَ فِيهِ، وَجُمِعَ فَلَمْ يُحْلَبْ أَيَّامًا. وَأَصْلُ التَّصْرِيَةِ حَبْسُ الْمَاءِ يُقَالُ مِنْهُ صَرَّيْتُ الْمَاءَ إِذَا حَبَسْتَهُ.

chapters.title: Returning an animal after milking it
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِنْ شَاءَ رَدَّ الْمُصَرَّاةَ وَفِي حَلْبَتِهَا صَاعٌ مِنْ تَمْرٍ

chapters.title: The selling of an adulterer slave
chapters.intro: And Shuraih said, "The buyer can return him to the owner if he wishes because of illegal sexual intercourse."
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيْعِ الْعَبْدِ الزَّانِي‏
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ شُرَيْحٌ إِنْ شَاءَ رَدَّ مِنَ الزِّنَا.

chapters.title: Dealing with women in selling and buying
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْبَيْعِ وَالشِّرَاءِ مَعَ النِّسَاءِ

chapters.title: Is it permissible for a person from the town to sell the goods of a desert dweller
chapters.intro: The Prophet ﷺ said, "If somebody asked the advice of someone else, then the latter should advise him." ‘Ata allowed it (selling the goods of a desert dweller by a town dweller).
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ هَلْ يَبِيعُ حَاضِرٌ لِبَادٍ بِغَيْرِ أَجْرٍ وَهَلْ يُعِينُهُ أَوْ يَنْصَحُهُ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «إِذَا اسْتَنْصَحَ أَحَدُكُمْ أَخَاهُ فَلْيَنْصَحْ لَهُ». وَرَخَّصَ فِيهِ عَطَاءٌ.

chapters.title: Whoever hated that an urban person should sell the goods of a desert dweller and charge him for that
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ كَرِهَ أَنْ يَبِيعَ حَاضِرٌ لِبَادٍ بِأَجْرٍ

chapters.title: A town dweller should not buy goods for a desert dweller and charge commission as a broker
chapters.intro: Ibn Sirin and Ibrahim disliked (working as a broker for a desert dweller) whether as a seller or a buyer. Ibrahim said, "Arabs use the word 'to buy' in the meaning of 'to sell'."
chapters.title_arabic: باب لاَ يَبِيعُ حَاضِرٌ لِبَادٍ بِالسَّمْسَرَةِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَكَرِهَهُ ابْنُ سِيرِينَ وَإِبْرَاهِيمُ لِلْبَائِعِ وَالْمُشْتَرِي، وَقَالَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ إِنَّ الْعَرَبَ تَقُولُ بِعْ لِي ثَوْبًا. وَهْيَ تَعْنِي الشِّرَاءَ.

chapters.title: It is forbidden to meet the caravans on the way (to buy the goods away from the market)
chapters.intro: And the one who buys them, his bargain is invalid as he is a sinner if he knows it, for it is a kind of deceit, and deceit is forbidden.
chapters.title_arabic: باب النَّهْىِ عَنْ تَلَقِّي الرُّكْبَان
chapters.intro_arabic: وَأَنَّ بَيْعَهُ مَرْدُودٌ، لأَنَّ صَاحِبَهُ عَاصٍ آثِمٌ إِذَا كَانَ بِهِ عَالِمًا، وَهُوَ خِدَاعٌ فِي الْبَيْعِ، وَالْخِدَاعُ لاَ يَجُوزُ.

chapters.title: The limits to which one can go ahead to meet the caravan
chapters.title_arabic: باب مُنْتَهَى التَّلَقِّي

chapters.title: If somebody imposes conditions in selling against the Islamic Law
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا اشْتَرَطَ شُرُوطًا فِي الْبَيْعِ لاَ تَحِلُّ

chapters.title: Selling of dates for dates
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيْعِ التَّمْرِ بِالتَّمْرِ

chapters.title: The selling of dried grapes for dried grapes and meals for meals
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيْعِ الزَّبِيبِ بِالزَّبِيبِ وَالطَّعَامِ بِالطَّعَامِ

chapters.title: Selling of barley for barley
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيْعِ الشَّعِيرِ بِالشَّعِيرِ

chapters.title: Selling of gold for gold
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيْعِ الذَّهَبِ بِالذَّهَبِ

chapters.title: Selling of silver for silver
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيْعِ الْفِضَّةِ بِالْفِضَّةِ

chapters.title: Selling of Dinar for Dinar on credit
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيْعِ الدِّينَارِ بِالدِّينَارِ نَسْأً

chapters.title: Selling of silver for gold on delayed payment
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيْعِ الْوَرِقِ بِالذَّهَبِ نَسِيئَةً

chapters.title: Selling of gold for silver from hand to hand
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيْعِ الذَّهَبِ بِالْوَرِقِ يَدًا بِيَدٍ

chapters.title: The sale called Al-Muzabana; which is the sale of dried dates for fresh ones (that are still on the trees), and dried grapes for fresh grapes and the sale called Al-‘Araya (i.e., the selling of ripe fresh date, still over the palms, by means of estimation, for dry dates).
chapters.intro: Anas said, "The Prophet ﷺ forbade the sales called Muzabana and Muhaqala (i.e., to sell wheat in ears for pure wheat).
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيْعِ الْمُزَابَنَة، وَهْيَ بَيْعُ الثَّمَرِ بِالتَّمْرِ وَبَيْعُ الزَّبِيبِ بِالْكَرْمِ وَبَيْعُ الْعَرَايَا
chapters.intro_arabic: قَالَ أَنَسٌ نَهَى النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ عَنِ الْمُزَابَنَةِ وَالْمُحَاقَلَةِ.

chapters.title: The selling of dates still on trees
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيْعِ الثَّمَرِ عَلَى رُءُوسِ النَّخْلِ بِالذَّهَبِ وَالْفِضَّةِ

chapters.title: Araya
chapters.intro:
Malik said, “‘Ariya (plural ‘Araya) means that a person gives a date-palm (i.e., its product of dates) as a gift to another person, and then the giver is troubled by the latter’s coming to the giver’s private garden (to cut the dates), so the giver is allowed to purchase those date fruits with dried dates.”

Ibn Idris said, “The sale of the dates of an ‘Ariya should be for measured dates delivered from hand to hand and not to be done at random.” The saying of Sahl bin Hathma confirms this verdict, i.e., that the exchange of dates should not be at random but by measure of Awsuq. Ibn ‘Umar رضي الله عنهما said, “Al-‘Araya meant to give one or two date palms to someone.”

Sufyan bin Husain said, “Al-‘Araya were date-palms given as a gift to the poor who could not wait till the fruits were ripe, so they were allowed to sell them for dates as they wished.”
chapters.title_arabic: باب تَفْسِيرِ الْعَرَايَا
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَقَالَ مَالِكٌ الْعَرِيَّةُ أَنْ يُعْرِيَ الرَّجُلُ الرَّجُلَ النَّخْلَةَ، ثُمَّ يَتَأَذَّى بِدُخُولِهِ عَلَيْهِ، فَرُخِّصَ لَهُ أَنْ يَشْتَرِيَهَا مِنْهُ بِتَمْرٍ.
وَقَالَ ابْنُ إِدْرِيسَ الْعَرِيَّةُ لاَ تَكُونُ إِلاَّ بِالْكَيْلِ مِنَ التَّمْرِ يَدًا بِيَدٍ، لاَ يَكُونُ بِالْجِزَافِ. وَمِمَّا يُقَوِّيهِ قَوْلُ سَهْلِ بْنِ أَبِي حَثْمَةَ بِالأَوْسُقِ الْمُوَسَّقَةِ.
وَقَالَ ابْنُ إِسْحَاقَ فِي حَدِيثِهِ عَنْ نَافِعٍ عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمَا كَانَتِ الْعَرَايَا أَنْ يُعْرِيَ الرَّجُلُ فِي مَالِهِ النَّخْلَةَ وَالنَّخْلَتَيْنِ.
وَقَالَ يَزِيدُ عَنْ سُفْيَانَ بْنِ حُسَيْنٍ الْعَرَايَا نَخْلٌ كَانَتْ تُوهَبُ لِلْمَسَاكِينِ، فَلاَ يَسْتَطِيعُونَ أَنْ يَنْتَظِرُوا بِهَا، رُخِّصَ لَهُمْ أَنْ يَبِيعُوهَا بِمَا شَاءُوا مِنَ التَّمْرِ.

chapters.title: The sale of fruits before their benefit is evident
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيْعِ الثِّمَارِ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَبْدُوَ صَلاَحُهَا

chapters.title: The sale of date-palms before their benefit is evident
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيْعِ النَّخْلِ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَبْدُوَ صَلاَحُهَا

chapters.title: If somebody sells fruits before their benefit is evident
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا بَاعَ الثِّمَارَ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَبْدُوَ صَلاَحُهَا ثُمَّ أَصَابَتْهُ عَاهَةٌ فَهُوَ مِنَ الْبَائِع

chapters.title: To buy foodstuff on credit
chapters.title_arabic: باب شِرَاءِ الطَّعَامِ إِلَى أَجَلٍ

chapters.title: To buy dates for Riba-Al-Fadl
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا أَرَادَ بَيْعَ تَمْرٍ بِتَمْرٍ خَيْرٍ مِنْهُ

chapters.title: Sold or rented date-palms which were pollinated, or land which was sown
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ بَاعَ نَخْلاً قَدْ أُبِّرَتْ أَوْ أَرْضًا مَزْرُوعَةً أَوْ بِإِجَارَةٍ

chapters.title: The sale of unharvested crops for a measured quantity of foodstuff
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيْعِ الزَّرْعِ بِالطَّعَامِ كَيْلاً

chapters.title: The sale of datepalms completely
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيْعِ النَّخْلِ بِأَصْلِهِ

chapters.title: Bai' Al-Mukhadara
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيْعِ الْمُخَاضَرَةِ

chapters.title: The sale and eating of spadix
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيْعِ الْجُمَّارِ وَأَكْلِهِ

chapters.title: Where there is no fixed judgement, the traditions and conventions of community are to be referred
chapters.intro:
Shuraih told the weavers, “You are permitted to follow your own conventions to solve your problems (it is legal for you to stick to your traditions in bargain).”

Narrated ‘Abdul Wahab: Ayyub said he heard from Muhammad who said, “There is no harm in selling for eleven what you buy for ten, and you are allowed to take a profit for expenses.”

The Prophet ﷺ told Hind, “Take what is reasonable and sufficient for you and your sons.” Allah تعالى says: “Whoever is poor, can eat (from the orphan's property) what is just and reasonable (according to his labours).” (V.4:6)

Al-Hasan hired a donkey from ‘Abdullah bin Mirdas and asked him about the hire. The latter replied that it was for two Daniq (a Daniq equals 1/6th Dirham). So Al-Hasan rode away. Another time, Al-Hasan came to ‘Abdullah bin Mirdas and asked him to hire the donkey to him and rode away without asking him about the hire, but he sent him half a Dirham.
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ مَنْ أَجْرَى أَمْرَ الأَمْصَارِ عَلَى مَا يَتَعَارَفُونَ بَيْنَهُمْ فِي الْبُيُوعِ وَالإِجَارَةِ وَالْمِكْيَالِ، وَالْوَزْنِ، وَسُنَنِهِمْ عَلَى نِيَّاتِهِمْ وَمَذَاهِبِهِمِ الْمَشْهُورَةِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَقَالَ شُرَيْحٌ لِلْغَزَّالِينَ سُنَّتُكُمْ بَيْنَكُمْ رِبْحًا.
وَقَالَ عَبْدُ الْوَهَّابِ عَنْ أَيُّوبَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدٍ لاَ بَأْسَ الْعَشَرَةُ بِأَحَدَ عَشَرَ، وَيَأْخُذُ لِلنَّفَقَةِ رِبْحًا.
وَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ لِهِنْدٍ: «خُذِي مَا يَكْفِيكِ وَوَلَدَكِ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ».
وَقَالَ تَعَالَى: {وَمَنْ كَانَ فَقِيرًا فَلْيَأْكُلْ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ} وَاكْتَرَى الْحَسَنُ مِنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مِرْدَاسٍ حِمَارًا، فَقَالَ بِكَمْ قَالَ بِدَانَقَيْنِ. فَرَكِبَهُ، ثُمَّ جَاءَ مَرَّةً أُخْرَى، فَقَالَ الْحِمَارَ الْحِمَارَ. فَرَكِبَهُ، وَلَمْ يُشَارِطْهُ، فَبَعَثَ إِلَيْهِ بِنِصْفِ دِرْهَمٍ.

chapters.title: Selling of a jointproperty by one to the other
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيْعِ الشَّرِيكِ مِنْ شَرِيكِهِ

chapters.title: The sale of undivided common belongings
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيْعِ الأَرْضِ وَالدُّورِ وَالْعُرُوضِ مُشَاعًا غَيْرَ مَقْسُومٍ

chapters.title: If somebody buys something for another without his permission and the latter accepts it
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا اشْتَرَى شَيْئًا لِغَيْرِهِ بِغَيْرِ إِذْنِهِ فَرَضِيَ

chapters.title: Buying and selling with Mushrikun and the enemy
chapters.title_arabic: باب الشِّرَاءِ وَالْبَيْعِ مَعَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ وَأَهْلِ الْحَرْبِ

chapters.title: The purchase of a slave from the enemy
chapters.intro:
The Prophet ﷺ asked Salman to make a contract of his manumission with his masters. In reality, Salman was a free man but the Mushrikun oppressed him and sold him.

‘Ammar, Suhaib, and Bilal were taken as captives in (war) booty.

Allah تعالى said: “And Allah has preferred some of you above others in wealth and properties. Then, those who are preferred will by no means hand over their wealth and properties to those (slaves) whom their right hand possess, so that they may be equal with them in respect thereof. Do they then deny the Favour of Allah?” (V.16:71)
chapters.title_arabic: باب شِرَاءِ الْمَمْلُوكِ مِنَ الْحَرْبِيِّ وَهِبَتِهِ وَعِتْقِهِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ لِسَلْمَانَ كَاتِبْ. وَكَانَ حُرًّا فَظَلَمُوهُ وَبَاعُوهُ. وَسُبِيَ عَمَّارٌ وَصُهَيْبٌ وَبِلاَلٌ، وَقَالَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى: {وَاللَّهُ فَضَّلَ بَعْضَكُمْ عَلَى بَعْضٍ فِي الرِّزْقِ فَمَا الَّذِينَ فُضِّلُوا بِرَادِّي رِزْقِهِمْ عَلَى مَا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَانُهُمْ فَهُمْ فِيهِ سَوَاءٌ أَفَبِنِعْمَةِ اللَّهِ يَجْحَدُونَ}.

chapters.title: The hides of dead animals before tanning
chapters.title_arabic: باب جُلُودِ الْمَيْتَةِ قَبْلَ أَنْ تُدْبَغ

chapters.title: Killing of pigs
chapters.intro: And Jabir said, "The Prophet ﷺ made the sale of pigs illegal."
chapters.title_arabic: باب قَتْلِ الْخِنْزِيرِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ جَابِرٌ حَرَّمَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ بَيْعَ الْخِنْزِيرِ.

chapters.title: The fat of the dead animal should not be sold
chapters.intro: Jabir narrated this from the Prophet ﷺ.
chapters.title_arabic: باب لاَ يُذَابُ شَحْمُ الْمَيْتَةِ وَلاَ يُبَاعُ وَدَكُهُ
chapters.intro_arabic: رَوَاهُ جَابِرٌ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ.

chapters.title: The selling of the pictures
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيْعِ التَّصَاوِيرِ الَّتِي لَيْسَ فِيهَا رُوحٌ وَمَا يُكْرَهُ مِنْ ذَلِكَ

chapters.title: Trade of alcoholic drinks is illegal
chapters.intro: Jabir رضي الله عنه said, "The Prophet ﷺ made the trade of alcoholic drinks illegal."
chapters.title_arabic: باب تَحْرِيمِ التِّجَارَةِ فِي الْخَمْرِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ جَابِرٌ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ حَرَّمَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ بَيْعَ الْخَمْرِ.

chapters.title: The sin of a person who sells a free man
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِثْمِ مَنْ بَاعَ حُرًّا

chapters.title: The Prophet saws ordered the Jews to sell their land
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ أَمْرِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ الْيَهُودَ بِبَيْعِ أَرَضِيهِمْ حِينَ أَجْلاَهُمْ
chapters.intro_arabic: فِيهِ الْمَقْبُرِيُّ عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ.

chapters.title: The sale of a slave (for a slave) and an animal for an animal on credit
chapters.intro:
And Ibn ‘Umar bought a mount (riding camel) for four camels which he promised to deliver at Ar-Rabadha. Ibn ‘Abbas said, “One camel may be better than two.” Rafi‘ bin Khadij once bought a camel for two camels and he delivered one instantly and said, “If Allah will, I will bring you the other tomorrow without delay.” And said Ibn Al-Musaiyab, “There is no Riba (in animals) i.e., in selling one camel for two, or one sheep for two sheep on credit.”

Ibn Sirin said, “There is no harm in selling one camel for two on credit.”
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيْعِ الْعَبِيدِ وَالْحَيَوَانِ بِالْحَيَوَانِ نَسِيئَةً
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَاشْتَرَى ابْنُ عُمَرَ رَاحِلَةً بِأَرْبَعَةِ أَبْعِرَةٍ مَضْمُونَةٍ عَلَيْهِ، يُوفِيهَا صَاحِبَهَا بِالرَّبَذَةِ.
وَقَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ قَدْ يَكُونُ الْبَعِيرُ خَيْرًا مِنَ الْبَعِيرَيْنِ. وَاشْتَرَى رَافِعُ بْنُ خَدِيجٍ بَعِيرًا بِبَعِيرَيْنِ فَأَعْطَاهُ أَحَدَهُمَا وَقَالَ آتِيكَ بِالآخَرِ غَدًا رَهْوًا، إِنْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ.
وَقَالَ ابْنُ الْمُسَيَّبِ لاَ رِبَا فِي الْحَيَوَانِ الْبَعِيرُ بِالْبَعِيرَيْنِ، وَالشَّاةُ بِالشَّاتَيْنِ إِلَى أَجَلٍ.
وَقَالَ ابْنُ سِيرِينَ لاَ بَأْسَ بَعِيرٌ بِبَعِيرَيْنِ نَسِيئَةً.

chapters.title: The sale of slaves
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيْعِ الرَّقِيقِ

chapters.title: The sale id a slave who is promised to be manumitted
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيْعِ الْمُدَبَّرِ

chapters.title: Travel with a slave-girl
chapters.intro:
Al-Hasan found no harm in her master’s kissing or fondling with her.

Ibn ‘Umar رضي الله عنهما said, “If a slave-girl who is suitable to have sexual relations is given to somebody as a gift, or sold or manumitted, her master should not have sexual intercourse with her before she gets one menstruation so as to be sure of absence of pregnancy, and there is no such necessity for a virgin.”

‘Ata said, “There is no harm in fondling with one’s pregnant slave-girl without having sexual intercourse with her. Allah said: ‘Except with their wives and the (woman slaves) whom their right hands possess...’” (V.70:30)
chapters.title_arabic: باب هَلْ يُسَافِرُ بِالْجَارِيَةِ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَسْتَبْرِئَهَا
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَلَمْ يَرَ الْحَسَنُ بَأْسًا أَنْ يُقَبِّلَهَا أَوْ يُبَاشِرَهَا.
وَقَالَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمَا إِذَا وُهِبَتِ الْوَلِيدَةُ الَّتِي تُوطَأُ أَوْ بِيعَتْ أَوْ عَتَقَتْ فَلْيُسْتَبْرَأْ رَحِمُهَا بِحَيْضَةٍ، وَلاَ تُسْتَبْرَأُ الْعَذْرَاءُ.
وَقَالَ عَطَاءٌ لاَ بَأْسَ أَنْ يُصِيبَ مِنْ جَارِيَتِهِ الْحَامِلِ مَا دُونَ الْفَرْجِ.
وَقَالَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى: {إِلاَّ عَلَى أَزْوَاجِهِمْ أَوْ مَا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَانُهُمْ}.

chapters.title: The sale of dead animals and idols
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيْعِ الْمَيْتَةِ وَالأَصْنَامِ

chapters.title: Price of a dog
chapters.title_arabic: باب ثَمَنِ الْكَلْبِ

chapters.title: As-Salam by a definite known specified measure
chapters.title_arabic: باب السَّلَمِ فِي كَيْلٍ مَعْلُومٍ

chapters.title: As-Salam for a known specified weight
chapters.title_arabic: باب السَّلَمِ فِي وَزْنٍ مَعْلُومٍ

chapters.title: As-Salam to a person who has got nothing
chapters.title_arabic: باب السَّلَمِ إِلَى مَنْ لَيْسَ عِنْدَهُ أَصْلٌ

chapters.title: As-Salam for (the fruits of) dat-palms
chapters.title_arabic: باب السَّلَمِ فِي النَّخْلِ

chapters.title: The guarantor in As-Salam
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْكَفِيلِ فِي السَّلَمِ

chapters.title: Mortgaging in As-Salam
chapters.title_arabic: باب الرَّهْنِ فِي السَّلَمِ

chapters.title: As-Salam for a fixed specified period
chapters.intro: Ibn ‘Abbas, Abu Sa‘id, Al-Aswad and Al-Hasan permitted it. Ibn ‘Umar said, “There is no harm in buying foodstuff to be delivered within a known specified time-period, at a known fixed price provided that it is not standing crops that have not yet become ripe and free from blights and diseases.”
chapters.title_arabic: باب السَّلَمِ إِلَى أَجَلٍ مَعْلُومٍ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَبِهِ قَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ وَأَبُو سَعِيدٍ وَالأَسْوَدُ وَالْحَسَنُ.
وَقَالَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ لاَ بَأْسَ فِي الطَّعَامِ الْمَوْصُوفِ بِسِعْرٍ مَعْلُومٍ إِلَى أَجَلٍ مَعْلُومٍ، مَا لَمْ يَكُ ذَلِكَ فِي زَرْعٍ لَمْ يَبْدُ صَلاَحُهُ.

chapters.title: As-Salam in buying a she-camel to be delivered after it has given birth
chapters.title_arabic: باب السَّلَمِ إِلَى أَنْ تُنْتَجَ النَّاقَةُ

chapters.title: Shuf'a is valid if the property is undivided
chapters.title_arabic: باب الشُّفْعَةُ مَا لَمْ يُقْسَمْ، فَإِذَا وَقَعَتِ الْحُدُودُ فَلاَ شُفْعَةَ

chapters.title: The partner should inform his partner before selling
chapters.intro: Al-Hakam said, “If the pre-emptor allows his partner to sell before selling, then he has no pre-emption any more.” Ash-Sha‘bi said, “If the pre-emptor witnesses the sale of what he has the right to buy by pre-emption and does not object to that sale, he loses the right of pre-emption.”
chapters.title_arabic: باب عَرْضِ الشُّفْعَةِ عَلَى صَاحِبِهَا قَبْلَ الْبَيْعِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَقَالَ الْحَكَمُ إِذَا أَذِنَ لَهُ قَبْلَ الْبَيْعِ فَلاَ شُفْعَةَ لَهُ.
وَقَالَ الشَّعْبِيُّ مَنْ بِيعَتْ شُفْعَتُهُ وَهْوَ شَاهِدٌ لاَ يُغَيِّرُهَا فَلاَ شُفْعَةَ لَهُ.

chapters.title: Who is considered the nearer neighbour?
chapters.title_arabic: باب أَىُّ الْجِوَارِ أَقْرَبُ

chapters.title: To hire a pious man
chapters.intro:
The Statement of Allah تعالى: “… Verily, the best of men for you to hire is the strong, the trustworthy.” (V.28:26)

(And what is said about) the honest treasurer, and the person who does not employ the one who is in an earnest pursuit of a job (position).
chapters.title_arabic: باب اسْتِئْجَارِ الرَّجُلِ الصَّالِحِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {إِنَّ خَيْرَ مَنِ اسْتَأْجَرْتَ الْقَوِيُّ الأَمِينُ} وَالْخَازِنِ الأَمِينِ، وَمَنْ لَمْ يَسْتَعْمِلْ مَنْ أَرَادَهُ.

chapters.title: To shepherd sheep for Qirat
chapters.title_arabic: باب رَعْىِ الْغَنَمِ عَلَى قَرَارِيطَ

chapters.title: The employment of Mushrikun (by Muslims)
chapters.intro: And the Prophet ﷺ employed the Jews of Khaibar (for the purpose of irrigating the land).
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ اسْتِئْجَارِ الْمُشْرِكِينَ عِنْدَ الضَّرُورَةِ أَوْ إِذَا لَمْ يُوجَدْ أَهْلُ الإِسْلاَمِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَعَامَلَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يَهُودَ خَيْبَرَ.

chapters.title: It is legal if somebody hires someone to work for him after three days, or after one month or after a year. When that period elapses they should carry out their contract
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ إِذَا اسْتَأْجَرَ أَجِيرًا لِيَعْمَلَ لَهُ بَعْدَ ثَلاَثَةِ أَيَّامٍ أَوْ بَعْدَ شَهْرٍ أَوْ بَعْدَ سَنَةٍ جَازَ، وَهُمَا عَلَى شَرْطِهِمَا الَّذِي اشْتَرَطَاهُ إِذَا جَاءَ الأَجَلُ

chapters.title: Employing labourers for services in holy battles
chapters.title_arabic: باب الأَجِيرِ فِي الْغَزْوِ

chapters.title: If somebody employs someone
chapters.intro: (It is permissible, if he takes into consideration Allah’s Statement): He said: “I intend to wed one of these two daughters of mine to you... (till the end of the Verse) ‘...Allah is a Surety over what we say...” (V.<qref chapter="28" verses="27,28">28:27, 28</qref>)
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ مَنِ اسْتَأْجَرَ أَجِيرًا فَبَيَّنَ لَهُ الأَجَلَ وَلَمْ يُبَيِّنِ الْعَمَلَ
chapters.intro_arabic: لِقَوْلِهِ: {إِنِّي أُرِيدُ أَنْ أُنْكِحَكَ إِحْدَى ابْنَتَيَّ هَاتَيْنِ} إِلَى قَوْلِهِ: {عَلَى مَا نَقُولُ وَكِيلٌ} يَأْجُرُ فُلاَنًا يُعْطِيهِ أَجْرًا، وَمِنْهُ فِي التَّعْزِيَةِ أَجَرَكَ اللَّهُ.

chapters.title: To employ someone to repair a wall which is about to collapse
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا اسْتَأْجَرَ أَجِيرًا عَلَى أَنْ يُقِيمَ حَائِطًا يُرِيدُ أَنْ يَنْقَضَّ جَازَ

chapters.title: Employment up to midday
chapters.title_arabic: باب الإِجَارَةِ إِلَى نِصْفِ النَّهَارِ

chapters.title: Employment up to the Asr
chapters.title_arabic: باب الإِجَارَةِ إِلَى صَلاَةِ الْعَصْرِ

chapters.title: The sin of him who withholds the wages of employee
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِثْمِ مَنْ مَنَعَ أَجْرَ الأَجِيرِ

chapters.title: Employment from Asr till night
chapters.title_arabic: باب الإِجَارَةِ مِنَ الْعَصْرِ إِلَى اللَّيْلِ

chapters.title: Whosoever employed a labourer (and after completing the work) the labourer left the wages and went away. The employer invested that money in some way and increased it thereby, or whoever invested somebody else’s money in business and increased it thereby.
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ مَنِ اسْتَأْجَرَ أَجِيرًا فَتَرَكَ أَجْرَهُ، فَعَمِلَ فِيهِ الْمُسْتَأْجِرُ فَزَادَ، أَوْ مَنْ عَمِلَ فِي مَالِ غَيْرِهِ فَاسْتَفْضَلَ

chapters.title: One who employs himself to carry loads on his back and then gives in charity from his wages, and (what is said about) the wages of porters
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ آجَرَ نَفْسَهُ لِيَحْمِلَ عَلَى ظَهْرِهِ. ثُمَّ تَصَدَّقَ بِهِ وَأُجْرَةِ الْحَمَّالِ

chapters.title: Wages of a broker
chapters.intro:
Ibn Sirin, ‘Ata’, Ibrahim and Al-Hasan did not see any harm in them. Ibn ‘Abbas said, “There is no harm if one says (to a broker), ‘Sell this garment for such a price and whatever more you get, is for you.’”

Ibn Sirin said, “If one says to a broker, ‘Sell it for such a price and if you get more, the profit will be for you or divided between us,’ there is no harm in it.”

The Prophet ﷺ said, “Muslims should abide by their conditions.”
chapters.title_arabic: باب أَجْرِ السَّمْسَرَةِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَلَمْ يَرَ ابْنُ سِيرِينَ وَعَطَاءٌ وَإِبْرَاهِيمُ وَالْحَسَنُ بِأَجْرِ السِّمْسَارِ بَأْسًا.
وَقَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ لاَ بَأْسَ أَنْ يَقُولَ بِعْ هَذَا الثَّوْبَ فَمَا زَادَ عَلَى كَذَا وَكَذَا فَهُوَ لَكَ.
وَقَالَ ابْنُ سِيرِينَ إِذَا قَالَ بِعْهُ بِكَذَا فَمَا كَانَ مِنْ رِبْحٍ فَهْوَ لَكَ، أَوْ بَيْنِي وَبَيْنَكَ، فَلاَ بَأْسَ بِهِ.
وَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «الْمُسْلِمُونَ عِنْدَ شُرُوطِهِمْ».

chapters.title: To work as an employee for Mushrikum
chapters.title_arabic: باب هَلْ يُؤَاجِرُ الرَّجُلُ نَفْسَهُ مِنْ مُشْرِكٍ فِي أَرْضِ الْحَرْبِ

chapters.title: What is paid for Ruqya
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يُعْطَى فِي الرُّقْيَةِ عَلَى أَحْيَاءِ الْعَرَبِ بِفَاتِحَةِ الْكِتَابِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَقَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «أَحَقُّ مَا أَخَذْتُمْ عَلَيْهِ أَجْرًا كِتَابُ اللَّهِ».
وَقَالَ الشَّعْبِيُّ لاَ يَشْتَرِطُ الْمُعَلِّمُ إِلاَّ أَنْ يُعْطَى شَيْئًا فَلْيَقْبَلْهُ وَقَالَ الْحَكَمُ لَمْ أَسْمَعْ أَحَدًا كَرِهَ أَجْرَ الْمُعَلِّمِ. وَأَعْطَى الْحَسَنُ دَرَاهِمَ عَشَرَةً. وَلَمْ يَرَ ابْنُ سِيرِينَ بِأَجْرِ الْقَسَّامِ بَأْسًا.
وَقَالَ كَانَ يُقَالُ السُّحْتُ الرِّشْوَةُ فِي الْحُكْمِ. وَكَانُوا يُعْطَوْنَ عَلَى الْخَرْصِ.

chapters.title: The taxes imposed on the slaves by their masters
chapters.title_arabic: باب ضَرِيبَةِ الْعَبْدِ، وَتَعَاهُدِ ضَرَائِبِ الإِمَاءِ

chapters.title: The wages of one who has the profession of cupping
chapters.title_arabic: باب خَرَاجِ الْحَجَّامِ

chapters.title: Whoever appealed to the masters to reduce his taxes
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ كَلَّمَ مَوَالِيَ الْعَبْدِ أَنْ يُخَفِّفُوا عَنْهُ مِنْ خَرَاجِهِ

chapters.title: The earnings of prostitutes and female-slaves
chapters.intro:
Ibrahim hated the earnings of female wailers and female singers.

The Statement of Allah تعالى: “…And force not your maids to prostitution, if they desire chastity, in order that you may make a gain in the (perishable) goods of this worldly life. But if anyone compels them (to prostitution) then after such compulsion, Allah is Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful (to those women i.e., He will forgive them because they have been forced to do this evil action unwillingly)” (V.24:33).
chapters.title_arabic: باب كَسْبِ الْبَغِيِّ وَالإِمَاءِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَكَرِهَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ أَجْرَ النَّائِحَةِ وَالْمُغَنِّيَةِ. وَقَوْلُ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {وَلاَ تُكْرِهُوا فَتَيَاتِكُمْ عَلَى الْبِغَاءِ إِنْ أَرَدْنَ تَحَصُّنًا لِتَبْتَغُوا عَرَضَ الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا وَمَنْ يُكْرِهْهُنَّ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ مِنْ بَعْدِ إِكْرَاهِهِنَّ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ}.
وَقَالَ مُجَاهِدٌ: {فَتَيَاتِكُمْ} إِمَاؤُكُمْ.

chapters.title: (Charging for) the semen of a male animal
chapters.title_arabic: باب عَسْبِ الْفَحْلِ

chapters.title: If somebody rents land and he or the owner of the land dies (will the contract be cancelled)?
chapters.intro:
According to Ibn Sirin the inheritors have no right to expel the tenant before the term of the contract has elapsed.

Al-Hakam, Al-Hasan and Ayas bin Mu‘awiya said, “The contract remains valid till the end of the term.”

Ibn ‘Umar said, “The Prophet ﷺ rented the land of Khaibar on the terms that half the yield would be his share. That contract continued during the lifetime of the Prophet ﷺ, Abu Bakr, and the early part of ‘Umar’s caliphate.” It was not mentioned that Abu Bakr renewed the contract after the death of the Prophet ﷺ.
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا اسْتَأْجَرَ أَرْضًا فَمَاتَ أَحَدُهُمَا
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَقَالَ ابْنُ سِيرِينَ لَيْسَ لأَهْلِهِ أَنْ يُخْرِجُوهُ إِلَى تَمَامِ الأَجَلِ.
وَقَالَ الْحَكَمُ وَالْحَسَنُ وَإِيَاسُ بْنُ مُعَاوِيَةَ تُمْضَى الإِجَارَةُ إِلَى أَجَلِهَا.
وَقَالَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ أَعْطَى النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ خَيْبَرَ بِالشَّطْرِ، فَكَانَ ذَلِكَ عَلَى عَهْدِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ وَأَبِي بَكْرٍ وَصَدْرًا مِنْ خِلاَفَةِ عُمَرَ، وَلَمْ يُذْكَرْ أَنَّ أَبَا بَكْرٍ وَعُمَرَ جَدَّدَا الإِجَارَةَ بَعْدَ مَا قُبِضَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ.

chapters.title: Al-Hawala (the transference of a debt from one person to another. It is an agreement whereby a debtor is released from a debt by another becoming responsible for it). Can Hawala be rejected by the creditors after accepting it?
chapters.intro:
Al-Hasan and Qatada said, “If the transferee was rich when the debt was transferred, the agreement is final and irrevocable by the creditor.”

Ibn ‘Abbas said, “When two partners disassociate and one of them accepts assets while the other accepts debts as a part of his share, if the debts could not be collected (because of the death of the debtor, his bankruptcy, or his denial of the debt, etc.), the one who has accepted the debts would have no right to demand any compensation from his partner.” This is applied also in setting the affairs of inheritors.
chapters.title_arabic: باب فِي الْحَوَالَةِ، وَهَلْ يَرْجِعُ فِي الْحَوَالَةِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَقَالَ الْحَسَنُ وَقَتَادَةُ إِذَا كَانَ يَوْمَ أَحَالَ عَلَيْهِ مَلِيًّا جَازَ.
وَقَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ يَتَخَارَجُ الشَّرِيكَانِ وَأَهْلُ الْمِيرَاثِ، فَيَأْخُذُ هَذَا عَيْنًا وَهَذَا دَيْنًا، فَإِنْ تَوِيَ لأَحَدِهِمَا لَمْ يَرْجِعْ عَلَى صَاحِبِهِ.

chapters.title: If somebody's debt are transferred to a rich debtor
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا أَحَالَ عَلَى مَلِيٍّ فَلَيْسَ لَهُ رَدٌّ

chapters.title: If the debts due on a dead person are transferred to somebody, the transference is legal
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِنْ أَحَالَ دَيْنَ الْمَيِّتِ عَلَى رَجُلٍ جَازَ

chapters.title: Al-Kafala (i.e., the pledge given by somebody to the creditor to guarantee that the debtor will be present at a certain specific place to pay his debt or fine, or to undergo a punishment, etc.) in loans and debts with oneself or other things (e.g., one’s money)
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْكَفَالَةِ فِي الْقَرْضِ وَالدُّيُونِ بِالأَبْدَانِ وَغَيْرِهَا

chapters.title: The Statement of Allah jala jalaaluhu:"... To those also with whom you have made a pledge, give them their due portion by Wasiya..."
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {وَالَّذِينَ عَاقَدَتْ أَيْمَانُكُمْ فَآتُوهُمْ نَصِيبَهُمْ}

chapters.title: He who undertakes to repay the debts of a dead person
chapters.intro: Al-Hasan said the same.
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ تَكَفَّلَ عَنْ مَيِّتٍ، دَيْنًا فَلَيْسَ لَهُ أَنْ يَرْجِعَ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَبِهِ قَالَ الْحَسَنُ

chapters.title: The pledge of protection given to Abu Bakr
chapters.title_arabic: باب جِوَارِ أَبِي بَكْرٍ فِي عَهْدِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم وَعَقْدِهِ

chapters.title: Debts
chapters.title_arabic: باب الدَّيْنِ

chapters.title: A partner can deputize for another
chapters.intro: No doubt, the Prophet ﷺ shared his Hady (i.e., sacrificing animals) with ‘Ali and then ordered ‘Ali to distribute them.
chapters.title_arabic: باب وَكَالَةُ الشَّرِيكِ الشَّرِيكَ فِي الْقِسْمَةِ وَغَيْرِهَا
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَدْ أَشْرَكَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ عَلِيًّا فِي هَدْيِهِ ثُمَّ أَمَرَهُ بِقِسْمَتِهَا.

chapters.title: If a Muslim deputizes a non-Muslim
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا وَكَّلَ الْمُسْلِمُ حَرْبِيًّا فِي دَارِ الْحَرْبِ أَوْ فِي دَارِ الإِسْلاَمِ، جَازَ

chapters.title: To deputize one in exchanging money and weighing goods
chapters.intro: ‘Umar and Ibn ‘Umar deputized (a person) in money exchanges.
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْوَكَالَةِ فِي الصَّرْفِ وَالْمِيزَانِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَدْ وَكَّلَ عُمَرُ وَابْنُ عُمَرَ فِي الصَّرْفِ

chapters.title: To save the thing liable to be spoiled
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ إِذَا أَبْصَرَ الرَّاعِي أَوِ الْوَكِيلُ شَاةً تَمُوتُ أَوْ شَيْئًا يَفْسُدُ ذَبَحَ وَأَصْلَحَ مَا يَخَافُ عَلَيْهِ الْفَسَادَ

chapters.title: To depute a person, present or absent
chapters.title_arabic: باب وَكَالَةُ الشَّاهِدِ وَالْغَائِبِ جَائِزَةٌ

chapters.title: To depute a person to repay debts
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْوَكَالَةِ فِي قَضَاءِ الدُّيُونِ

chapters.title: To give a gift to a deputy or to their intercessor
chapters.intro: This is confirmed by the statement of the Prophet ﷺ to the delegates of the tribe of Hawizin when they appealed to him to return the booty to them. The Prophet ﷺ said, “I give my share to you.”
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا وَهَبَ شَيْئًا لِوَكِيلٍ أَوْ شَفِيعِ قَوْمٍ جَازَ
chapters.intro_arabic: لِقَوْلِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ لِوَفْدِ هَوَازِنَ حِينَ سَأَلُوهُ الْمَغَانِمَ فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «نَصِيبِي لَكُمْ».

chapters.title: If someone deputes a person to give something
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا وَكَّلَ رَجُلٌ أَنْ يُعْطِيَ شَيْئًا وَلَمْ يُبَيِّنْ كَمْ يُعْطِي، فَأَعْطَى عَلَى مَا يَتَعَارَفُهُ النَّاسُ

chapters.title: A woman can depute the ruler in marriage
chapters.title_arabic: باب وَكَالَةِ الْمَرْأَةِ الإِمَامَ فِي النِّكَاحِ

chapters.title: If a person deputes somebody, and the deputy leaves something
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ إِذَا وَكَّلَ رَجُلاً، فَتَرَكَ الْوَكِيلُ شَيْئًا، فَأَجَازَهُ الْمُوَكِّلُ، فَهُوَ جَائِزٌ، وَإِنْ أَقْرَضَهُ إِلَى أَجَلٍ مُسَمًّى جَازَ

chapters.title: If a deputy sells something (in an illegal manner)
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا بَاعَ الْوَكِيلُ شَيْئًا فَاسِدًا فَبَيْعُهُ مَرْدُودٌ

chapters.title: The deputyship for managing the Waaf and the expenses of the trustee. The trustee can provide his friends and can eat from it reasonably.
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْوَكَالَةِ فِي الْوَقْفِ وَنَفَقَتِهِ، وَأَنْ يُطْعِمَ صَدِيقًا لَهُ وَيَأْكُلَ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ

chapters.title: To depute a person to carry out a punishment
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْوَكَالَةِ فِي الْحُدُودِ

chapters.title: To depute someone to sacrifice Budn (camels for sacrifice) and to look after them
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْوَكَالَةِ فِي الْبُدْنِ وَتَعَاهُدِهَا

chapters.title: If a person tells his deputy, "Spend it as Allah directs you."
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ إِذَا قَالَ الرَّجُلُ لِوَكِيلِهِ ضَعْهُ حَيْثُ أَرَاكَ اللَّهُ. وَقَالَ الْوَكِيلُ قَدْ سَمِعْتُ مَا قُلْتَ

chapters.title: To depute a trustworthy treasurer
chapters.title_arabic: باب وَكَالَةِ الأَمِينِ فِي الْخِزَانَةِ وَنَحْوِهَا

chapters.title: Sowing seeds and planting trees
chapters.intro: The Statement of Allah تعالى: “Then tell Me about the seed that you sow in the ground, is it you that make it to grow, or are We the grower? Were it Our Will, We could crumble it to dry pieces...” (V.<qref chapter="56" verses="63-65">56:63-65</qref>)
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ الزَّرْعِ وَالْغَرْسِ إِذَا أُكِلَ مِنْهُ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى: {أَفَرَأَيْتُمْ مَا تَحْرُثُونَ أَأَنْتُمْ تَزْرَعُونَهُ أَمْ نَحْنُ الزَّارِعُونَ لَوْ نَشَاءُ لَجَعَلْنَاهُ حُطَامًا}.

chapters.title: The results of indulging in agricultural equipment
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ مَا يُحْذَرُ مِنْ عَوَاقِبِ الاِشْتِغَالِ بِآلَةِ الزَّرْعِ أَوْ مُجَاوَزَةِ الْحَدِّ الَّذِي أُمِرَ بِهِ

chapters.title: Keeping a watchdog for the farm
chapters.title_arabic: باب اقْتِنَاءِ الْكَلْبِ لِلْحَرْثِ

chapters.title: Employing oxen for ploughing
chapters.title_arabic: باب اسْتِعْمَالِ الْبَقَرِ لِلْحِرَاثَةِ

chapters.title: To say to another, "Look after my date-palm trees...
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا قَالَ اكْفِنِي مَئُونَةَ النَّخْلِ أَوْ غَيْرِهِ، وَتُشْرِكُنِي فِي الثَّمَرِ

chapters.title: The cutting of trees and date-palm trees
chapters.intro: Anas said, “The Prophet ﷺ ordered that the date-palm trees be cut down and they were cut down.”
chapters.title_arabic: باب قَطْعِ الشَّجَرِ وَالنَّخْلِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ أَنَسٌ أَمَرَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ بِالنَّخْلِ فَقُطِعَ.

chapters.title: Chapter:
chapters.title_arabic: باب

chapters.title: Temporary share-cropping contract
chapters.intro: Narrated Abu Ja‘far: All the emigrants in Al-Madina used to cultivate the land (for the Ansar) on the condition of having one-third or one-fourth of the yield. ‘Ali, Sa‘d bin Malik, ‘Abdullah bin Mas‘ud, ‘Umar bin ‘Abdul ‘Aziz, Al-Qasim, ‘Urwa and the families of Abu Bakr, of ‘Umar and of ‘Ali, and Ibn Sirin cultivated the land of ‘Abdur-Rahman bin Yazid on the basis of taking a portion of the yield. ‘Umar made a deal with the people that if he provided the seeds, he would get half of the yield, and if they provided the seeds, they would get so-and-so much. Al-Hasan said, “There is no harm if the land belongs to one, but both spend on it and the yield is divided between them.” Az-Zuhri had the same opinion. Al-Hasan said, “There is no harm if cotton is picked on the condition of having half the yield.” Ibrahim, Ibn Sirin, ‘Ata, Al-Hakam, Az-Zuhri and Qatada said, “There is no harm in giving the yarn to the weaver to weave into cloth on the basis that one-third of the cloth is given to the weaver for his labour.” Ma‘mar said, “There is no harm in hiring animals for a definite (fixed) period on the basis that one-third or one-fourth of the products carried by the animals is given to the owner of the animals.”
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْمُزَارَعَةِ بِالشَّطْرِ وَنَحْوِهِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَقَالَ قَيْسُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ قَالَ مَا بِالْمَدِينَةِ أَهْلُ بَيْتِ هِجْرَةٍ إِلاَّ يَزْرَعُونَ عَلَى الثُّلُثِ وَالرُّبُعِ. وَزَارَعَ عَلِيٌّ وَسَعْدُ بْنُ مَالِكٍ وَعَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ مَسْعُودٍ وَعُمَرُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ وَالْقَاسِمُ وَعُرْوَةُ وَآلُ أَبِي بَكْرٍ وَآلُ عُمَرَ وَآلُ عَلِيٍّ وَابْنُ سِيرِينَ.
وَقَالَ عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ الأَسْوَدِ كُنْتُ أُشَارِكُ عَبْدَ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنَ يَزِيدَ فِي الزَّرْعِ. وَعَامَلَ عُمَرُ النَّاسَ عَلَى إِنْ جَاءَ عُمَرُ بِالْبَذْرِ مِنْ عِنْدِهِ فَلَهُ الشَّطْرُ، وَإِنْ جَاءُوا بِالْبَذْرِ فَلَهُمْ كَذَا.
وَقَالَ الْحَسَنُ لاَ بَأْسَ أَنْ تَكُونَ الأَرْضُ لأَحَدِهِمَا فَيُنْفِقَانِ جَمِيعًا فَمَا خَرَجَ فَهْوَ بَيْنَهُمَا، وَرَأَى ذَلِكَ الزُّهْرِيُّ.
وَقَالَ الْحَسَنُ لاَ بَأْسَ أَنْ يُجْتَنَى الْقُطْنُ عَلَى النِّصْفِ.
وَقَالَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ وَابْنُ سِيرِينَ وَعَطَاءٌ وَالْحَكَمُ وَالزُّهْرِيُّ وَقَتَادَةُ لاَ بَأْسَ أَنْ يُعْطِيَ الثَّوْبَ بِالثُّلُثِ أَوِ الرُّبُعِ وَنَحْوِهِ.
وَقَالَ مَعْمَرٌ لاَ بَأْسَ أَنْ تَكُونَ الْمَاشِيَةُ عَلَى الثُّلُثِ وَالرُّبُعِ إِلَى أَجَلٍ مُسَمًّى.

chapters.title: The contract of share-cropping
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا لَمْ يَشْتَرِطِ السِّنِينَ فِي الْمُزَارَعَةِ

chapters.title: Chapter:
chapters.title_arabic: باب

chapters.title: Share-cropping with the Jews
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْمُزَارَعَةِ مَعَ الْيَهُودِ

chapters.title: What conditions are disliked in share-cropping
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يُكْرَهُ مِنَ الشُّرُوطِ فِي الْمُزَارَعَةِ

chapters.title: If a person invests the money of someone else in cultivation
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا زَرَعَ بِمَالِ قَوْمٍ بِغَيْرِ إِذْنِهِمْ وَكَانَ فِي ذَلِكَ صَلاَحٌ لَهُمْ

chapters.title: The Auqaf, Kharaj, share-cropping and other agreements
chapters.intro: The Prophet ﷺ said to ‘Umar, “Give those trees as a whole in charity (as Waqf) so that those might not be sold but their fruits can be spent and given in charity.” So, ‘Umar gave those trees in charity.
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ أَوْقَافِ أَصْحَابِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ وَأَرْضِ الْخَرَاجِ وَمُزَارَعَتِهِمْ وَمُعَامَلَتِهِمْ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ لِعُمَرَ: «تَصَدَّقْ بِأَصْلِهِ لاَ يُبَاعُ، وَلَكِنْ يُنْفَقُ ثَمَرُهُ فَتَصَدَّقَ بِهِ».

chapters.title: Cultivating the neglected land
chapters.intro: ‘Ali رضي الله عنه had the same opinion concerning such land in Kufa. ‘Umar said, “Whoever cultivates uncultivated land (belonging to nobody) will possess it.” ‘Umar and Ibn ‘Auf narrated the same from the Prophet ﷺ adding, “… provided that the land does not belong to any Muslim; otherwise one has no right to plant anything in it oppressively.”
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ أَحْيَا أَرْضًا مَوَاتًا
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَرَأَى ذَلِكَ عَلِيٌّ فِي أَرْضِ الْخَرَابِ بِالْكُوفَةِ مَوَاتٌ.
وَقَالَ عُمَرُ مَنْ أَحْيَا أَرْضًا مَيِّتَةً فَهْيَ لَهُ. وَيُرْوَى عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ عَوْفٍ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ وَقَالَ: «فِي غَيْرِ حَقِّ مُسْلِمٍ، وَلَيْسَ لِعِرْقٍ ظَالِمٍ فِيهِ حَقٌّ».

وَيُرْوَى فِيهِ عَنْ جَابِرٍ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ.

chapters.title: Chapter:
chapters.title_arabic: باب

chapters.title: The lease can be continued according to the approval of both the parties
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ إِذَا قَالَ رَبُّ الأَرْضِ أُقِرُّكَ مَا أَقَرَّكَ اللَّهُ وَلَمْ يَذْكُرْ أَجَلاً مَعْلُومًا فَهُمَا عَلَى تَرَاضِيهِمَا

chapters.title: To share the yields and fruits
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ مَا كَانَ مِنْ أَصْحَابِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يُوَاسِي بَعْضُهُمْ بَعْضًا فِي الزِّرَاعَةِ وَالثَّمَرَةِ

chapters.title: To rent the land for gold and silver
chapters.intro: Ibn ‘Abbās said, “The best thing to do is to take the uncultivated land on a yearly rental basis.”
chapters.title_arabic: باب كِرَاءِ الأَرْضِ بِالذَّهَبِ وَالْفِضَّةِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ إِنَّ أَمْثَلَ مَا أَنْتُمْ صَانِعُونَ أَنْ تَسْتَأْجِرُوا الأَرْضَ الْبَيْضَاءَ مِنَ السَّنَةِ إِلَى السَّنَةِ.

chapters.title: Chapter:
chapters.title_arabic: باب

chapters.title: What is said about planting trees
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا جَاءَ فِي الْغَرْسِ

chapters.title: The Statement of Allāh تعالى: “… And We have made from water every living thing. Will they not then believe.” (V.<qref chapter="21" verses="30">21:30</qref>)
chapters.intro: And His Statement: “Then tell me about the water that you drink. Is it you who cause it from the rainclouds to come down, or are We the Causer of it to come down? If We willed, We verily could make it salt (and undrinkable), why then do you not give thanks (to Allāh)?” (V.<qref chapter="56" verses="68-70">56:68-70</qref>).
chapters.title_arabic: بَابٌ في الشُّرْبِ وَقَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {وَجَعَلْنَا مِنَ الْمَاءِ كُلَّ شَيْءٍ حَيٍّ أَفَلاَ يُؤْمِنُونَ}
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَوْلِهِ جَلَّ ذِكْرُهُ: {أَفَرَأَيْتُمُ الْمَاءَ الَّذِي تَشْرَبُونَ أَأَنْتُمْ أَنْزَلْتُمُوهُ مِنَ الْمُزْنِ أَمْ نَحْنُ الْمُنْزِلُونَ لَوْ نَشَاءُ جَعَلْنَاهُ أُجَاجًا فَلَوْلاَ تَشْكُرُونَ}. الأُجَاجُ الْمُرُّ، الْمُزْنُ السَّحَابُ.

chapters.title: Giving water in charity, or as a gift, and whoever thinks that giving water in charity, or as a gift or by way of a testament is permissible, whether it is divided or not
chapters.intro: Narrated ‘Uthmān رضي الله عنه: The Prophet ﷺ said, “Who will buy the well of Rūmah (a well-known well in Al-Madina) (and endow it for the good of Muslims) so that he may use it as the other Muslims do without any privilege?” ‘Uthmān رضي الله عنه bought it.
chapters.title_arabic: بَابٌ في الشُّرْبِ، وَمَنْ رَأَى صَدَقَةَ الْمَاءِ وَهِبَتَهُ وَوَصِيَّتَهُ جَائِزَةً، مَقْسُومًا كَانَ أَوْ غَيْرَ مَقْسُومٍ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ عُثْمَانُ قَالَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «مَنْ يَشْتَرِي بِئْرَ رُومَةَ فَيَكُونُ دَلْوُهُ فِيهَا كَدِلاَءِ الْمُسْلِمِينَ». فَاشْتَرَاهَا عُثْمَانُ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ.

chapters.title: Whoever said, “The owner of the water has the right to drink till he is satisfied, as the Prophet ﷺ said, ‘Superfluous water should not be withheld from others.’”
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ قَالَ إِنَّ صَاحِبَ الْمَاءِ أَحَقُّ بِالْمَاءِ حَتَّى يَرْوَى لِقَوْلِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: لا يُمْنَعُ فَضْلُ الْمَاءِ

chapters.title: If one digs a well and somebody falls in it and dies
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ حَفَرَ بِئْرًا فِي مِلْكِهِ لَمْ يَضْمَنْ

chapters.title: Disputes and controversies about wells
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْخُصُومَةِ فِي الْبِئْرِ وَالْقَضَاءِ فِيهَا

chapters.title: The sin of him who witholds water from travellers
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِثْمِ مَنْ مَنَعَ ابْنَ السَّبِيلِ مِنَ الْمَاءِ

chapters.title: The dams of rivers
chapters.title_arabic: باب سَكْرِ الأَنْهَارِ

chapters.title: The land near the source of water to be irrigated first
chapters.title_arabic: باب شُرْبِ الأَعْلَى قَبْلَ الأَسْفَلِ

chapters.title: The land to be covered with water up to the ankles
chapters.title_arabic: باب شِرْبِ الأَعْلَى إِلَى الْكَعْبَيْنِ

chapters.title: The superiority of providing water
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ سَقْىِ الْمَاءِ

chapters.title: The owner of a tank or a leather water-container
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ رَأَى أَنَّ صَاحِبَ الْحَوْضِ وَالْقِرْبَةِ أَحَقُّ بِمَائِهِ

chapters.title: Hima (private pasture)
chapters.title_arabic: باب لاَ حِمَى إِلاَّ لِلَّهِ وَلِرَسُولِهِ صلى الله عليه وسلم

chapters.title: Drinking water by people and animals from rivers
chapters.title_arabic: باب شُرْبِ النَّاسِ وَالدَّوَابِّ مِنَ الأَنْهَارِ

chapters.title: The selling of wood and grass
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيْعِ الْحَطَبِ وَالْكَلإِ

chapters.title: The uncultivated pieces of land
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْقَطَائِعِ

chapters.title: Documentation of the land grants
chapters.title_arabic: باب كِتَابَةِ الْقَطَائِعِ

chapters.title: Milking she-camels at water places
chapters.title_arabic: باب حَلَبِ الإِبِلِ عَلَى الْمَاءِ

chapters.title: To pass through a garden or to have a share in datepalms
chapters.intro: The Prophet ﷺ said, "If somebody sells date-palms after pollinating them, their fruits will be for him and he has the right to enter the garden and irrigate the date-palms till he reaps the fruits. The owner of 'Ariya has a similar right."
chapters.title_arabic: باب الرَّجُلِ يَكُونُ لَهُ مَمَرٌّ، أَوْ شِرْبٌ فِي حَائِطٍ أَوْ فِي نَخْلٍ
chapters.intro_arabic: قَالَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «مَنْ بَاعَ نَخْلاً بَعْدَ أَنْ تُؤَبَّرَ فَثَمَرَتُهَا لِلْبَائِعِ». فَلِلْبَائِعِ الْمَمَرُّ وَالسَّقْيُ حَتَّى يَرْفَعَ وَكَذَلِكَ رَبُّ الْعَرِيَّةِ.

chapters.title: Whoever buys a thing on credit
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنِ اشْتَرَى بِالدَّيْنِ وَلَيْسَ عِنْدَهُ ثَمَنُهُ، أَوْ لَيْسَ بِحَضْرَتِهِ

chapters.title: Borrowing money from people
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ أَخَذَ أَمْوَالَ النَّاسِ يُرِيدُ أَدَاءَهَا أَوْ إِتْلاَفَهَا

chapters.title: Repayment of debts
chapters.intro: And the Statement of Allāh تعالى: "Verily! Allāh commands that you should render back the trusts to those, to whom they are due; and that when you judge between men, you judge with justice. Verily, how excellent is the teaching which He (Allāh) gives you! Truly, Allāh is Ever All-Hearer, All-Seer." (V.<qref chapter="4" verses="58">4:58</qref>)
chapters.title_arabic: باب أَدَاءِ الدُّيُونِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى إِنَّ اللَّهَ يَأْمُرُكُمْ أَنْ تُؤَدُّوا الْأَمَانَاتِ إِلَى أَهْلِهَا وَإِذَا حَكَمْتُمْ بَيْنَ النَّاسِ أَنْ تَحْكُمُوا بِالْعَدْلِ إِنَّ اللَّهَ نِعِمَّا يَعِظُكُمْ بِهِ إِنَّ اللَّهَ كَانَ سَمِيعًا بَصِيرًا

chapters.title: To buy camels on credit
chapters.title_arabic: باب اسْتِقْرَاضِ الإِبِلِ

chapters.title: Demanding debts handsomely
chapters.title_arabic: باب حُسْنِ التَّقَاضِي

chapters.title: Can one give an older (camel) than that he owes?
chapters.title_arabic: باب هَلْ يُعْطَى أَكْبَرَ مِنْ سِنِّهِ

chapters.title: Repaying debts handsomely
chapters.title_arabic: باب حُسْنِ الْقَضَاءِ

chapters.title: If somebody repays less than what he owes
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا قَضَى دُونَ حَقِّهِ أَوْ حَلَّلَهُ فَهْوَ جَائِزٌ

chapters.title: To settle one's accounts by repaying
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا قَاصَّ أَوْ جَازَفَهُ فِي الدَّيْنِ تَمْرًا بِتَمْرٍ أَوْ غَيْرِهِ

chapters.title: To seek refuge with Allah from being in debt
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنِ اسْتَعَاذَ مِنَ الدَّيْنِ

chapters.title: The funeral Salat (prayer) for a dead person in debt
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّلاَةِ عَلَى مَنْ تَرَكَ دَيْنًا

chapters.title: Procrastination (delay) in repaying debts by a wealthy person is injustice
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَطْلُ الْغَنِيِّ ظُلْمٌ

chapters.title: The owner of the right has the permission to demand his right
chapters.intro: The Prophet ﷺ said, "The delay in the payment of debt by one who can afford to pay, justifies his defamation and torture by the lender." Defamation means that the lender tells him in public that he has delayed the payment. Torture means legal imprisonment.
chapters.title_arabic: باب لِصَاحِبِ الْحَقِّ مَقَالٌ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَيُذْكَرُ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «لَيُّ الْوَاجِدِ يُحِلُّ عُقُوبَتَهُ وَعِرْضَهُ». قَالَ سُفْيَانُ عِرْضُهُ يَقُولُ مَطَلْتَنِي. وَعُقُوبَتُهُ الْحَبْسُ.

chapters.title: If somebody lends something and the possessor gets bankrupt
chapters.intro:
Al-Hasan said, "If somebody becomes bankrupt and he is judged to be so, he is not permitted to free his slave or sell or buy things."

Sa‘id bin Al-Musayyab said, "'Uthman gave a verdict that if a creditor took something from the debtor before the latter was declared bankrupt, it would belong to him (i.e., the other creditors would have no right to take it), and if the creditor recognized his things, he had more right to restore them (than any other creditor)."
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا وَجَدَ مَالَهُ عِنْدَ مُفْلِسٍ فِي الْبَيْعِ وَالْقَرْضِ وَالْوَدِيعَةِ فَهْوَ أَحَقُّ بِهِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَقَالَ الْحَسَنُ إِذَا أَفْلَسَ وَتَبَيَّنَ لَمْ يَجُزْ عِتْقُهُ، وَلاَ بَيْعُهُ وَلاَ شِرَاؤُهُ.
وَقَالَ سَعِيدُ بْنُ الْمُسَيَّبِ قَضَى عُثْمَانُ مَنِ اقْتَضَى مِنْ حَقِّهِ قَبْلَ أَنْ يُفْلِسَ فَهْوَ لَهُ، وَمَنْ عَرَفَ مَتَاعَهُ بِعَيْنِهِ فَهْوَ أَحَقُّ بِهِ.

chapters.title: Delay in the repayment of debts for a day or so
chapters.intro: Jabir said, "When the creditors of my father demanded their rights persistently, the Prophet requested them to take the fruits of my garden instead of the debt, but they refused. So, the Prophet neither gave them the fruits nor had the fruits plucked for them, but said, 'I will come to you tomorrow.' He came to us early in the morning and invoked Allah to bless the garden’s fruits, and so I paid the creditors their rights."
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ مَنْ أَخَّرَ الْغَرِيمَ إِلَى الْغَدِ أَوْ نَحْوِهِ، وَلَمْ يَرَ ذَلِكَ مَطْلاً
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ جَابِرٌ اشْتَدَّ الْغُرَمَاءُ فِي حُقُوقِهِمْ فِي دَيْنِ أَبِي فَسَأَلَهُمُ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ أَنْ يَقْبَلُوا ثَمَرَ حَائِطِي فَأَبَوْا، فَلَمْ يُعْطِهِمِ الْحَائِطَ وَلَمْ يَكْسِرْهُ لَهُمْ، قَالَ: «سَأَغْدُو عَلَيْكَ غَدًا». فَغَدَا عَلَيْنَا حِينَ أَصْبَحَ فَدَعَا فِي ثَمَرِهَا بِالْبَرَكَةِ فَقَضَيْتُهُمْ.

chapters.title: The property of a bankrupt
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ بَاعَ مَالَ الْمُفْلِسِ أَوِ الْمُعْدِمِ فَقَسَمَهُ بَيْنَ الْغُرَمَاءِ، أَوْ أَعْطَاهُ حَتَّى يُنْفِقَ عَلَى نَفْسِهِ

chapters.title: To lend money or sell on credit for a fixed time
chapters.intro:
Ibn Umar said concerning loans for a fixed time, "There is no objection to it, even if the debtor gives more than he owes if the creditor has not stipulated it."

Ata and Amr bin Dinar said, "The lender has no right to demand his money before the due time of payment."
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا أَقْرَضَهُ إِلَى أَجَلٍ مُسَمًّى أَوْ أَجَّلَهُ فِي الْبَيْعِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
قَالَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ فِي الْقَرْضِ إِلَى أَجَلٍ لاَ بَأْسَ بِهِ، وَإِنْ أُعْطِيَ أَفْضَلَ مِنْ دَرَاهِمِهِ، مَا لَمْ يَشْتَرِطْ.
وَقَالَ عَطَاءٌ وَعَمْرُو بْنُ دِينَارٍ هُوَ إِلَى أَجَلِهِ فِي الْقَرْضِ.

chapters.title: Intercession for the reduction of debts
chapters.title_arabic: باب الشَّفَاعَةِ فِي وَضْعِ الدَّيْنِ

chapters.title: Wasting money
chapters.intro:
And the Statement of Allah:
"... And Allah likes not mischief..." (V.2:205).
"... Verily Allah does not set right the work of Al-Mufsidun (the evil-doers, corrupters)..." (V.10:81)

And the Statement of Allah:
"Does your Salat (prayer) command that we give up what our fathers used to worship, or that we give up doing what we like with our property?..." (V.11:87)

Allah also said:
"And give not unto the foolish your property..." (V.4:5)

And to keep away from all these (things), and (also) what is forbidden as regards deceit.
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يُنْهَى عَنْ إِضَاعَةِ الْمَالِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَقَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {وَاللَّهُ لاَ يُحِبُّ الْفَسَادَ} وَ{لاَ يُصْلِحُ عَمَلَ الْمُفْسِدِينَ}.
وَقَالَ فِي قَوْلِهِ: {أَصَلَوَاتُكَ تَأْمُرُكَ أَنْ نَتْرُكَ مَا يَعْبُدُ آبَاؤُنَا أَوْ أَنْ نَفْعَلَ فِي أَمْوَالِنَا مَا نَشَاءُ}.
وَقَالَ: {وَلاَ تُؤْتُوا السُّفَهَاءَ أَمْوَالَكُمْ} وَالْحَجْرِ فِي ذَلِكَ، وَمَا يُنْهَى عَنِ الْخِدَاعِ.

chapters.title: A slave is a guardian of the property of his master
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْعَبْدُ رَاعٍ فِي مَالِ سَيِّدِهِ وَلاَ يَعْمَلُ إِلاَّ بِإِذْنِهِ

chapters.title: About the people and quarrels
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يُذْكَرُ فِي الإِشْخَاصِ وَالْخُصُومَةِ بَيْنَ الْمُسْلِمِ وَالْيَهُودِ

chapters.title: The deals done by a weak-minded or an extravagant person
chapters.intro:
Jabir narrated that the Prophet rejected the gift of charity (given by a poor man) before he prohibited him (from giving in charity).

Malik said, "If a person is in debt and he has nothing except a slave, it is not permissible for him to manumit the slave."
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ مَنْ رَدَّ أَمْرَ السَّفِيهِ وَالضَّعِيفِ الْعَقْلِ، وَإِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ حَجَرَ عَلَيْهِ الإِمَامُ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَيُذْكَرُ عَنْ جَابِرٍ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ رَدَّ عَلَى الْمُتَصَدِّقِ قَبْلَ النَّهْيِ ثُمَّ نَهَاهُ.
وَقَالَ مَالِكٌ إِذَا كَانَ لِرَجُلٍ عَلَى رَجُلٍ مَالٌ، وَلَهُ عَبْدٌ، لاَ شَيْءَ لَهُ غَيْرُهُ، فَأَعْتَقَهُ، لَمْ يَجُزْ عِتْقُهُ.

chapters.title: If somebody sells a thing for a weak-minded person and pays him the price, and advised him to utilize it in a proper way and not to spoil it, and he spoils it, he can stop him from doing so, for the Prophet had forbidden the wasting of the property. The Prophet ordered the person who said that he had always been cheated in buying, to say, at the time of the deal, "No cheating!" The Prophet did not take his money (of the person who sold his slave, because he had not proved foolish).
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ بَاعَ عَلَى الضَّعِيفِ وَنَحْوِهِ فَدَفَعَ ثَمَنَهُ إِلَيْهِ، وَأَمَرَهُ بِالإِصْلاَحِ وَالْقِيَامِ بِشَأْنِهِ، فَإِنْ أَفْسَدَ بَعْدُ مَنَعَهُ، لأَنَّ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ نَهَى عَنْ إِضَاعَةِ الْمَالِ، وَقَالَ لِلَّذِي يُخْدَعُ فِي الْبَيْعِ: «إِذَا بَايَعْتَ فَقُلْ لاَ خِلاَبَةَ». وَلَمْ يَأْخُذِ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ مَالَهُ

chapters.title: The talk of opponents against each other
chapters.title_arabic: باب كَلاَمِ الْخُصُومِ بَعْضِهِمْ فِي بَعْضٍ

chapters.title: Turning out the sinners and the quarrelsome people of the houses
chapters.intro: 'Umar turned Abu Bakr's sister (out of the house) for her wailing (over a dead person).
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِخْرَاجِ أَهْلِ الْمَعَاصِي وَالْخُصُومِ مِنَ الْبُيُوتِ بَعْدَ الْمَعْرِفَةِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَدْ أَخْرَجَ عُمَرُ أُخْتَ أَبِي بَكْرٍ حِينَ نَاحَتْ.

chapters.title: To file a case for fulfilling the will of the deceased
chapters.title_arabic: باب دَعْوَى الْوَصِيِّ لِلْمَيِّتِ

chapters.title: Tying the person liable to do mischievous things
chapters.intro: Ibn 'Abbas chained 'Ikrima to teach him the Qur'an, the Prophet's Sunna (legal ways), and the knowledge of Fara'id (laws of inheritance).
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّوَثُّقِ مِمَّنْ تُخْشَى مَعَرَّتُهُ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَيَّدَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ عِكْرِمَةَ عَلَى تَعْلِيمِ الْقُرْآنِ وَالسُّنَنِ وَالْفَرَائِضِ.

chapters.title: Fastening and imprisoning in the Haram
chapters.intro: Nafi' bin Al-Harith bought a house from Safwan bin Umayya for using it as a prison on the condition that the deal would be confirmed if 'Umar agreed to it, and if 'Umar did not agree to it, Safwan would take four hundred (Dinar). Ibn Az-Zubair imprisoned (the culprits) in Makkah.
chapters.title_arabic: باب الرَّبْطِ وَالْحَبْسِ فِي الْحَرَمِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَاشْتَرَى نَافِعُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْحَارِثِ دَارًا لِلسِّجْنِ بِمَكَّةَ مِنْ صَفْوَانَ بْنِ أُمَيَّةَ، عَلَى أَنَّ عُمَرَ إِنْ رَضِيَ فَالْبَيْعُ بَيْعُهُ، وَإِنْ لَمْ يَرْضَ عُمَرُ فَلِصَفْوَانَ أَرْبَعُمِائَةٍ. وَسَجَنَ ابْنُ الزُّبَيْرِ بِمَكَّةَ.

chapters.title: (The creditor's) pursuit (after his debtors)
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْمُلاَزَمَةِ

chapters.title: Demanding one's debts
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّقَاضِي

chapters.title: Owner's proof
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا أَخْبَرَهُ رَبُّ اللُّقَطَةِ، بِالْعَلاَمَةِ دَفَعَ إِلَيْهِ

chapters.title: Lost camels
chapters.title_arabic: باب ضَالَّةِ الإِبِلِ

chapters.title: Lost sheep
chapters.title_arabic: باب ضَالَّةِ الْغَنَمِ

chapters.title: If the owner of a lost thing is not found for one year
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا لَمْ يُوجَدْ صَاحِبُ اللُّقَطَةِ بَعْدَ سَنَةٍ فَهْىَ لِمَنْ وَجَدَهَا

chapters.title: If someone finds a thing in the sea
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا وَجَدَ خَشَبَةً فِي الْبَحْرِ أَوْ سَوْطًا أَوْ نَحْوَهُ

chapters.title: If somebody finds a date on the way
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا وَجَدَ تَمْرَةً فِي الطَّرِيقِ

chapters.title: How the Luqata at Makkah is to be announced
chapters.intro: Narrated Ibn 'Abbas: The Prophet ﷺ said, "Nobody should pick up the Luqata (lost things) of Makkah except the one who makes public announcement for it." Ibn 'Abbas said (in another narration): The Prophet ﷺ said, "None should pick up the fallen things of Makkah except that who announces it publicly."
chapters.title_arabic: باب كَيْفَ تُعَرَّفُ لُقَطَةُ أَهْلِ مَكَّةَ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَقَالَ طَاوُسٌ عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمَا عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ قَالَ: «لاَ يَلْتَقِطُ لُقَطَتَهَا إِلاَّ مَنْ عَرَّفَهَا».
وَقَالَ خَالِدٌ عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ قَالَ: «لاَ تُلْتَقَطُ لُقَطَتُهَا إِلاَّ لِمُعَرِّفٍ».

chapters.title: No animal may be milked without the permission of its owner
chapters.title_arabic: باب لاَ تُحْتَلَبُ مَاشِيَةُ أَحَدٍ بِغَيْرِ إِذْنٍهِ

chapters.title: If the owner of lost property comes back after a year
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا جَاءَ صَاحِبُ اللُّقَطَةِ بَعْدَ سَنَةٍ رَدَّهَا عَلَيْهِ، لأَنَّهَا وَدِيعَةٌ عِنْدَهُ

chapters.title: Should one pick up a fallen thing?
chapters.title_arabic: باب هَلْ يَأْخُذُ اللُّقَطَةَ وَلاَ يَدَعُهَا تَضِيعُ، حَتَّى لاَ يَأْخُذَهَا مَنْ لاَ يَسْتَحِقُّ
