chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ تَأْوِيلِ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {مِنْ بَعْدِ وَصِيَّةٍ يُوصِي بِهَا أَوْ دَيْنٍ}
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَيُذْكَرُ أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ قَضَى بِالدَّيْنِ قَبْلَ الْوَصِيَّةِ. وَقَوْلِهِ: {إِنَّ اللَّهَ يَأْمُرُكُمْ أَنْ تُؤَدُّوا الأَمَانَاتِ إِلَى أَهْلِهَا}، فَأَدَاءُ الأَمَانَةِ أَحَقُّ مِنْ تَطَوُّعِ الْوَصِيَّةِ.
وَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «لاَ صَدَقَةَ إِلاَّ عَنْ ظَهْرِ غِنًى».
وَقَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ لاَ يُوصِي الْعَبْدُ إِلاَّ بِإِذْنِ أَهْلِهِ.
وَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «الْعَبْدُ رَاعٍ فِي مَالِ سَيِّدِهِ».

chapters.title: If somebody founds an endowment (or bequeathes) his relatives by a will
chapters.intro:
Narrated Anas رضي الله عنه: The Prophet ﷺ said to Abu Talha, "Give (your garden) to the poor amongst your relatives." So he gave it to Hassan and Ubai bin Ka'b.

Anas added in another narration, "So he gave it to Hassan and Ubai bin Ka'b who were nearer relatives to him than I." The relation between Hassan and Ubai to Abu Talha was as follows: Abu Talha’s name was Zaid, the son of Sahl, the son of Al-Aswad, the son of Haram, the son of Amr, the son of Zaid Manat, the son of Adi, the son of Amr, the son of Malik, the son of An-Najjar. Hassan was the son of Thabit, the son of Al-Mundhir, the son of Haram, this means that Abu Talha and Hassan had a common great grandfather (i.e., Haram, the third in the line of descent). Hassan and Abu Talha and Ubai had a common ancestor, Amr bin Malik, the sixth in the lineage, as Ubai was the son of Ka’b, the son of Qais, the son of Ubaid, the son of Zaid, the son of Mu'awiya, the son of Amr, the son of Malik, the son of An-Najjar.

Some scholars say, "If one wants to will some of his wealth to one's relatives, they must be among those who share a Muslim common ancestor with one."
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا وَقَفَ أَوْ أَوْصَى لأَقَارِبِهِ وَمَنِ الأَقَارِبُ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ ثَابِتٌ عَنْ أَنَسٍ قَالَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ لأَبِي طَلْحَةَ: «اجْعَلْهَا لِفُقَرَاءِ أَقَارِبِكَ». فَجَعَلَهَا لِحَسَّانَ وَأُبَيِّ بْنِ كَعْبٍ. وَقَالَ الأَنْصَارِيُّ حَدَّثَنِي أَبِي، عَنْ ثُمَامَةَ، عَنْ أَنَسٍ، مِثْلَ حَدِيثِ ثَابِتٍ قَالَ ‏"‏ اجْعَلْهَا لِفُقَرَاءِ قَرَابَتِكَ ‏"‏‏.‏ قَالَ أَنَسٌ فَجَعَلَهَا لِحَسَّانَ وَأُبَىِّ بْنِ كَعْبٍ، وَكَانَا أَقْرَبَ إِلَيْهِ مِنِّي، وَكَانَ قَرَابَةُ حَسَّانَ وَأُبَىٍّ مِنْ أَبِي طَلْحَةَ وَاسْمُهُ زَيْدُ بْنُ سَهْلِ بْنِ الأَسْوَدِ بْنِ حَرَامِ بْنِ عَمْرِو بْنِ زَيْدِ مَنَاةَ بْنِ عَدِيِّ بْنِ عَمْرِو بْنِ مَالِكِ بْنِ النَّجَّارِ، وَحَسَّانُ بْنُ ثَابِتِ بْنِ الْمُنْذِرِ بْنِ حَرَامٍ فَيَجْتَمِعَانِ إِلَى حَرَامٍ، وَهْوَ الأَبُ الثَّالِثُ، وَحَرَامُ بْنُ عَمْرِو بْنِ زَيْدِ مَنَاةَ بْنِ عَدِيِّ بْنِ عَمْرِو بْنِ مَالِكِ بْنِ النَّجَّارِ، فَهْوَ يُجَامِعُ حَسَّانُ أَبَا طَلْحَةَ وَأُبَىٌّ إِلَى سِتَّةِ آبَاءٍ إِلَى عَمْرِو بْنِ مَالِكٍ، وَهْوَ أُبَىُّ بْنُ كَعْبِ بْنِ قَيْسِ بْنِ عُبَيْدِ بْنِ زَيْدِ بْنِ مُعَاوِيَةَ بْنِ عَمْرِو بْنِ مَالِكِ بْنِ النَّجَّارِ، فَعَمْرُو بْنُ مَالِكٍ يَجْمَعُ حَسَّانَ وَأَبَا طَلْحَةَ وَأُبَيًّا‏.‏ وَقَالَ بَعْضُهْمْ إِذَا أَوْصَى لِقَرَابَتِهِ فَهْوَ إِلَى آبَائِهِ فِي الإِسْلاَمِ‏.‏

chapters.title: Are children and women included under the term of relatives (concerning wills)
chapters.title_arabic: باب هَلْ يَدْخُلُ النِّسَاءُ وَالْوَلَدُ فِي الأَقَارِبِ

chapters.title: Can the founder of an endowment have the benefit of his endowment?
chapters.intro: 'Umar رضي الله عنه stipulated that the administrator of an endowment could eat from the yield of the endowment. The founder of an endowment or somebody else may be the trustee of the endowment. Similarly, if one offers a Badana (i.e., camel for sacrifice) or something else in Allah’s Cause, he is allowed to benefit by it in the same way as others benefit by it even if he did not stipulate that.
chapters.title_arabic: باب هَلْ يَنْتَفِعُ الْوَاقِفُ بِوَقْفِهِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَدِ اشْتَرَطَ عُمَرُ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ لاَ جُنَاحَ عَلَى مَنْ وَلِيَهُ أَنْ يَأْكُلَ. وَقَدْ يَلِي الْوَاقِفُ وَغَيْرُهُ. وَكَذَلِكَ مَنْ جَعَلَ بَدَنَةً أَوْ شَيْئًا لِلَّهِ، فَلَهُ أَنْ يَنْتَفِعَ بِهَا كَمَا يَنْتَفِعُ غَيْرُهُ وَإِنْ لَمْ يَشْتَرِطْ.

chapters.title: If one declares his wish to found an endowment
chapters.intro: As Umar رضي الله عنه founded an endowment and said that it was not sinful for its administrator to eat from its yield, but he did not specify whether he (i.e., Umar) or someone else would be its administrator. The Prophet ﷺ said to Abu Talha, "I recommend that you should divide it (i.e., the garden) among your relatives." So Abu Talha agreed and distributed it among his relatives and his cousins.
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ إِذَا وَقَفَ شَيْئًا فَلَمْ يَدْفَعْهُ إِلَى غَيْرِهِ، فَهُوَ جَائِزٌ
chapters.intro_arabic: لأَنَّ عُمَرَ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ أَوْقَفَ وَقَالَ لاَ جُنَاحَ عَلَى مَنْ وَلِيَهُ أَنْ يَأْكُلَ، وَلَمْ يَخُصَّ إِنْ وَلِيَهُ عُمَرُ أَوْ غَيْرُهُ. قَالَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ لأَبِي طَلْحَةَ: «أَرَى أَنْ تَجْعَلَهَا فِي الأَقْرَبِينَ». فَقَالَ أَفْعَلُ. فَقَسَمَهَا فِي أَقَارِبِهِ وَبَنِي عَمِّهِ.

chapters.title: When someone says, "My house is Sadaqa for Allah's sake,"
chapters.intro: The Prophet ﷺ allowed Abu Talha when he said, "The most beloved of my property is (the garden of) Bairuha and I wish to give it in charity for Allah’s sake." The Prophet ﷺ considered his deed valid. Some say that it is invalid unless it is specified as to whom the Sadaqa is to be given. But the first statement (i.e., that it is valid) is more correct.
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ إِذَا قَالَ دَارِي صَدَقَةٌ لِلَّهِ وَلَمْ يُبَيِّنْ لِلْفُقَرَاءِ أَوْ غَيْرِهِمْ. فَهُوَ جَائِزٌ، وَيَضَعُهَا فِي الأَقْرَبِينَ أَوْ حَيْثُ أَرَادَ
chapters.intro_arabic:
قَالَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ لأَبِي طَلْحَةَ حِينَ قَالَ أَحَبُّ أَمْوَالِي إِلَيَّ بَيْرَحَاءَ، وَإِنَّهَا صَدَقَةٌ لِلَّهِ، فَأَجَازَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ذَلِكَ.
وَقَالَ بَعْضُهُمْ لاَ يَجُوزُ حَتَّى يُبَيِّنَ لِمَنْ وَالأَوَّلُ أَصَحُّ.

chapters.title: If someone says, "My land is Sadaqa for Allah's safe on my mother's behalf,"
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ إِذَا قَالَ أَرْضِي أَوْ بُسْتَانِي صَدَقَةٌ عَنْ أُمِّي. فَهُوَ جَائِزٌ، وَإِنْ لَمْ يُبَيِّنْ لِمَنْ ذَلِكَ

chapters.title: To give part of wealth or slaves or animals in charity or as an endowment
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا تَصَدَّقَ أَوْ أَوْقَفَ بَعْضَ مَالِهِ أَوْ بَعْضَ رَقِيقِهِ أَوْ دَوَابِّهِ، فَهُوَ جَائِزٌ

chapters.title: Whoever gave something to his representative to give in charity and then the latter returned it to him
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ تَصَدَّقَ إِلَى وَكِيلِهِ ثُمَّ رَدَّ الْوَكِيلُ إِلَيْهِ

chapters.title: The Statement of Allah Taa'la: "And when the relatives and the orphans and Al-Masakin are present at the time of division..."
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {وَإِذَا حَضَرَ الْقِسْمَةَ أُولُو الْقُرْبَى وَالْيَتَامَى وَالْمَسَاكِينُ فَارْزُقُوهُمْ مِنْهُ}

chapters.title: Charity on behalf of a person who dies suddenly. And the execution of the vows.
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ مَا يُسْتَحَبُّ لِمَنْ يُتَوَفَّى فَجْأَةً أَنْ يَتَصَدَّقُوا عَنْهُ، وَقَضَاءِ النُّذُورِ عَنِ الْمَيِّتِ

chapters.title: The witnesses in the foundation of an endowment or in giving in charity
chapters.title_arabic: باب الإِشْهَادِ فِي الْوَقْفِ وَالصَّدَقَةِ

chapters.title: The Statement of Allah Taa'la: "And if you fear that you shall not be able to deal justly with the orphan-girls..."
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {وَآتُوا الْيَتَامَى أَمْوَالَهُمْ وَلاَ تَتَبَدَّلُوا الْخَبِيثَ بِالطَّيِّبِ وَلاَ تَأْكُلُوا أَمْوَالَهُمْ إِلَى أَمْوَالِكُمْ إِنَّهُ كَانَ حُوبًا كَبِيرًا وَإِنْ خِفْتُمْ أَنْ لاَ تُقْسِطُوا فِي الْيَتَامَى فَانْكِحُوا مَا طَابَ لَكُمْ مِنَ النِّسَاءِ}

chapters.title: The Statement of Allah تعالى: "And try orphans (as regards their intelligence) until they reach the age of marriage; if then you find sound judgement in them, release their property to them, but consume it not wastefully and hastily fearing that they should grow up, and whoever (amongst the guardians) is rich, he should take no wages, but if he is poor, let him have for himself what is just and reasonable (according to his labour). And when you release their property to them, take witnesses in their presence; and Allah is All-Sufficient in taking account. "There is a share for men and a share for women from what is left by parents, and those nearest related, whether the property be small or large - a legal share." (V.<qref chapter="4" verses="6,7">4:6,7</qref>)
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {وَابْتَلُوا الْيَتَامَى حَتَّى إِذَا بَلَغُوا النِّكَاحَ فَإِنْ آنَسْتُمْ مِنْهُمْ رُشْدًا فَادْفَعُوا إِلَيْهِمْ أَمْوَالَهُمْ وَلاَ تَأْكُلُوهَا إِسْرَافًا وَبِدَارًا أَنْ يَكْبَرُوا وَمَنْ كَانَ غَنِيًّا فَلْيَسْتَعْفِفْ وَمَنْ كَانَ فَقِيرًا فَلْيَأْكُلْ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ فَإِذَا دَفَعْتُمْ إِلَيْهِمْ أَمْوَالَهُمْ فَأَشْهِدُوا عَلَيْهِمْ وَكَفَى بِاللَّهِ حَسِيبًا لِلرِّجَالِ نَصِيبٌ مِمَّا تَرَكَ الْوَالِدَانِ وَالأَقْرَبُونَ وَلِلنِّسَاءِ نَصِيبٌ مِمَّا تَرَكَ الْوَالِدَانِ وَالأَقْرَبُونَ مِمَّا قَلَّ مِنْهُ أَوْ كَثُرَ نَصِيبًا مَفْرُوضًا}
chapters.intro_arabic: {حَسِيبًا} يَعْنِي كَافِيًا.

chapters.title: How a guardian is to deal with an orphan's wealth
chapters.title_arabic: باب وَمَا لِلْوَصِيِّ أَنْ يَعْمَلَ فِي مَالِ الْيَتِيمِ، وَمَا يَأْكُلُ مِنْهُ بِقَدْرِ عُمَالَتِهِ

chapters.title: The Statement of Allah Taa'la: "... those who unjustly eat up the property of orphans..."
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَأْكُلُونَ أَمْوَالَ الْيَتَامَى ظُلْمًا إِنَّمَا يَأْكُلُونَ فِي بُطُونِهِمْ نَارًا وَسَيَصْلَوْنَ سَعِيرًا}

chapters.title: Allah's Statement: "The best thing is to work honestly in their property..."
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {وَيَسْأَلُونَكَ عَنِ الْيَتَامَى قُلْ إِصْلاَحٌ لَهُمْ خَيْرٌ وَإِنْ تُخَالِطُوهُمْ فَإِخْوَانُكُمْ وَاللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ الْمُفْسِدَ مِنَ الْمُصْلِحِ وَلَوْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ لأَعْنَتَكُمْ إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌ}
chapters.intro_arabic: {لأَعْنَتَكُمْ} لأَحْرَجَكُمْ وَضَيَّقَ عَلَيْكُمْ، وَعَنَتْ خَضَعَتْ.

chapters.title: The employment of an orphan
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ اسْتِخْدَامِ الْيَتِيمِ فِي السَّفَرِ وَالْحَضَرِ إِذَا كَانَ صَلاَحًا لَهُ، وَنَظَرِ الأُمِّ وَزَوْجِهَا لِلْيَتِيمِ

chapters.title: If somebody gives a piece of land as an endowment and does not mark its boundaries
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا وَقَفَ أَرْضًا وَلَمْ يُبَيِّنِ الْحُدُودَ فَهْوَ جَائِزٌ، وَكَذَلِكَ الصَّدَقَةُ

chapters.title: A jointly-owned piece of land as an endowment
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا أَوْقَفَ جَمَاعَةٌ أَرْضًا مُشَاعًا فَهْوَ جَائِزٌ

chapters.title: How to write the endowment?
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْوَقْفِ كَيْفَ يُكْتَبُ

chapters.title: The usufruct of an endowment
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْوَقْفِ لِلْغَنِيِّ وَالْفَقِيرِ وَالضَّيْفِ

chapters.title: The foundation of an endowment for building a mosque
chapters.title_arabic: باب وَقْفِ الأَرْضِ لِلْمَسْجِدِ

chapters.title: Animals, property, gold and silver as endowments
chapters.intro: Az-Zuhri was asked, "If somebody founds an endowment of one thousand Dinar and gives the sum to his boy who is a trader to invest it in business and declares that the profit of the money will be given in charity to the poor and the relatives, does the founder have the right to use anything of the profit of the one thousand Dinar? And if he does not assign its profit to the poor?" Az-Zuhri said, "He does not have the right to use anything of it (i.e., in either case)."
chapters.title_arabic: باب وَقْفِ الدَّوَابِّ وَالْكُرَاعِ وَالْعُرُوضِ وَالصَّامِتِ
chapters.intro_arabic: قَالَ الزُّهْرِيُّ فِيمَنْ جَعَلَ أَلْفَ دِينَارٍ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ، وَدَفَعَهَا إِلَى غُلاَمٍ لَهُ تَاجِرٍ يَتْجُرُ بِهَا، وَجَعَلَ رِبْحَهُ صَدَقَةً لِلْمَسَاكِينِ وَالأَقْرَبِينَ، هَلْ لِلرَّجُلِ أَنْ يَأْكُلَ مِنْ رِبْحِ ذَلِكَ الأَلْفِ شَيْئًا، وَإِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ جَعَلَ رِبْحَهَا صَدَقَةً فِي الْمَسَاكِينِ قَالَ لَيْسَ لَهُ أَنْ يَأْكُلَ مِنْهَا.

chapters.title: The salary of the administrator of an endowment
chapters.title_arabic: باب نَفَقَةِ الْقَيِّمِ لِلْوَقْفِ

chapters.title: If somebody keeps an endowment, or stipulates that he should benefit by it as the other Muslims do
chapters.intro:
Anas kept a house as an endowment and whenever he came (to Al-Madina) he used to stay in it.

Az-Zubair gave his house in charity (i.e., as an endowment) and told his divorced daughters to dwell therein without harming or being harmed, but if any of them remarried, she would have no right to stay there.

Ibn Umar kept his share from his father's house to be inhabited by the needy amongst Abdullah's family (i.e., his own family).
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا وَقَفَ أَرْضًا أَوْ بِئْرًا وَاشْتَرَطَ لِنَفْسِهِ مِثْلَ دِلاَءِ الْمُسْلِمِينَ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَأَوْقَفَ أَنَسٌ دَارًا فَكَانَ إِذَا قَدِمَهَا نَزَلَهَا. وَتَصَدَّقَ الزُّبَيْرُ بِدُورِهِ، وَقَالَ لِلْمَرْدُودَةِ مِنْ بَنَاتِهِ أَنْ تَسْكُنَ غَيْرَ مُضِرَّةٍ وَلاَ مُضَرٍّ بِهَا، فَإِنِ اسْتَغْنَتْ بِزَوْجٍ فَلَيْسَ لَهَا حَقٌّ. وَجَعَلَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ نَصِيبَهُ مِنْ دَارِ عُمَرَ سُكْنَى لِذَوِي الْحَاجَةِ مِنْ آلِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ.

chapters.title: To say: "We will demand its price, from none but Allah."
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا قَالَ الْوَاقِفُ لاَ نَطْلُبُ ثَمَنَهُ إِلاَّ إِلَى اللَّهِ فَهْوَ جَائِزٌ

chapters.title: The Statement of Allah aaza' wajal: "When death approaches any of you, and you make a bequest..."
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا شَهَادَةُ بَيْنِكُمْ إِذَا حَضَرَ أَحَدَكُمُ الْمَوْتُ حِينَ الْوَصِيَّةِ اثْنَانِ ذَوَا عَدْلٍ مِنْكُمْ أَوْ آخَرَانِ مِنْ غَيْرِكُمْ إِنْ أَنْتُمْ ضَرَبْتُمْ فِي الأَرْضِ فَأَصَابَتْكُمْ مُصِيبَةُ الْمَوْتِ تَحْبِسُونَهُمَا مِنْ بَعْدِ الصَّلاَةِ فَيُقْسِمَانِ بِاللَّهِ إِنِ ارْتَبْتُمْ لاَ نَشْتَرِي بِهِ ثَمَنًا وَلَوْ كَانَ ذَا قُرْبَى وَلاَ نَكْتُمُ شَهَادَةَ اللَّهِ إِنَّا إِذًا لَمِنَ الآثِمِينَ فَإِنْ عُثِرَ عَلَى أَنَّهُمَا اسْتَحَقَّا إِثْمًا فَآخَرَانِ يَقُومَانِ مَقَامَهُمَا مِنَ الَّذِينَ اسْتُحِقَّ عَلَيْهِمُ الأَوْلَيَانِ فَيُقْسِمَانِ بِاللَّهِ لَشَهَادَتُنَا أَحَقُّ مِنْ شَهَادَتِهِمَا وَمَا اعْتَدَيْنَا إِنَّا إِذًا لَمِنَ الظَّالِمِينَ ذَلِكَ أَدْنَى أَنْ يَأْتُوا بِالشَّهَادَةِ عَلَى وَجْهِهَا أَوْ يَخَافُوا أَنْ تُرَدَّ أَيْمَانٌ بَعْدَ أَيْمَانِهِمْ وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ وَاسْمَعُوا وَاللَّهُ لاَ يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الْفَاسِقِينَ}الْأَوْلَيَانِ وَاحِدُهُمَا أَوْلَى وَمِنْهُ أَوْلَى بِهِ عُثِرَ أُظْهِرَ أَعْثَرْنَا أَظْهَرْنَا

chapters.title: The payments of the debts of the deceased
chapters.title_arabic: باب قَضَاءِ الْوَصِيِّ دُيُونَ الْمَيِّتِ بِغَيْرِ مَحْضَرٍ مِنَ الْوَرَثَةِ

chapters.title: The superiority of Jihad
chapters.intro: And the Statement of Allah تعالى: "Verily, Allah has purchased of the believers their lives and their properties; for the price that theirs shall be the Paradise. They fight in Allah's Cause, so they kill (others) and are killed. It is a promise in truth which is binding on Him in the Taurat (Torah) and the Injeel (Gospel) and the Qur'an. And who is truer to his covenant than Allah? Then rejoice in the bargain which you have concluded.. (up to).. And give glad tidings to the believers." (V.<qref chapter="9" verses="111,112">9:111, 112</qref>)
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلُ الْجِهَادِ وَالسِّيَرِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَوْلُ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {إِنَّ اللَّهَ اشْتَرَى مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ أَنْفُسَهُمْ وَأَمْوَالَهُمْ بِأَنَّ لَهُمُ الْجَنَّةَ يُقَاتِلُونَ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ فَيَقْتُلُونَ وَيُقْتَلُونَ وَعْدًا عَلَيْهِ حَقًّا فِي التَّوْرَاةِ وَالإِنْجِيلِ وَالْقُرْآنِ وَمَنْ أَوْفَى بِعَهْدِهِ مِنَ اللَّهِ فَاسْتَبْشِرُوا بِبَيْعِكُمُ الَّذِي بَايَعْتُمْ بِهِ} إِلَى قَوْلِهِ: {وَبَشِّرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ} قَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ الْحُدُودُ الطَّاعَةُ.

chapters.title: Striving with both, life and property
chapters.intro: And the Statement of Allah تعالى: "O you who believe! Shall I guide you to a trade that will save you from a painful torment. That you believe in Allah and His Messenger (Muhammad ﷺ), and that you strive hard and fight in the Cause of Allah with your wealth and your lives, that will be better for you, if you but know! (If you do so), He will forgive you your sins, and admit you into Gardens under which rivers flow, and pleasant dwellings in 'Adn (Eden) Paradise, that is indeed great success." (V.<qref chapter="61" verses="10-12">61:10-12</qref>)
chapters.title_arabic: باب أَفْضَلُ النَّاسِ مُؤْمِنٌ مُجَاهِدٌ بِنَفْسِهِ وَمَالِهِ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَوْلُهُ تَعَالَى: {يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا هَلْ أَدُلُّكُمْ عَلَى تِجَارَةٍ تُنْجِيكُمْ مِنْ عَذَابٍ أَلِيمٍ تُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ وَتُجَاهِدُونَ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ بِأَمْوَالِكُمْ وَأَنْفُسِكُمْ ذَلِكُمْ خَيْرٌ لَكُمْ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ يَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ ذُنُوبَكُمْ وَيُدْخِلْكُمْ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الأَنْهَارُ وَمَسَاكِنَ طَيِّبَةً فِي جَنَّاتِ عَدْنٍ ذَلِكَ الْفَوْزُ الْعَظِيمُ}.

chapters.title: The invocation that Allah may let adorn with martyrdom
chapters.intro: 'Umar said, "O Allah! Grant me martyrdom in the town of Your Messenger ﷺ."
chapters.title_arabic: باب الدُّعَاءِ بِالْجِهَادِ وَالشَّهَادَةِ لِلرِّجَالِ وَالنِّسَاءِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ عُمَرُ اللَّهُمَّ ارْزُقْنِي شَهَادَةً فِي بَلَدِ رَسُولِكَ.

chapters.title: The grades of the Mujahidun
chapters.title_arabic: باب دَرَجَاتِ الْمُجَاهِدِينَ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ يُقَالُ هَذِهِ سَبِيلِي وَهَذَا سَبِيلِي
chapters.intro_arabic: يُقَالُ هَذِهِ سَبِيلِي وَهَذَا سَبِيلِي.

chapters.title: To proceed in Allah's Cause
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْغَدْوَةِ وَالرَّوْحَةِ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ، وَقَابُ قَوْسِ أَحَدِكُمْ مِنَ الْجَنَّةِ

chapters.title: Al-Hur-ul-'Ein
chapters.intro:
They are called so as one's eyesight is perplexed while looking at them, and also because of the intense blackness of their irises and intense whiteness of the sclerotic coat of their eyes. And Allah's Statement:
"And We shall marry them to Hur (fair females) with wide lovely eyes. (V.44:54)."
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ الْحُورُ الْعِينُ وَصِفَتُهُنَّ
chapters.intro_arabic:
يَحَارُ فِيهَا الطَّرْفُ شَدِيدَةُ سَوَادِ الْعَيْنِ شَدِيدَةُ بَيَاضِ الْعَيْنِ.
{وَزَوَّجْنَاهُمْ} أَنْكَحْنَاهُمْ.

chapters.title: The wish for martyrdom
chapters.title_arabic: باب تَمَنِّي الشَّهَادَةِ

chapters.title: The superiority of him who goes in Allah's Cause and dies on the way
chapters.intro: And the Statement of Allah تعالى: "...And who-so-ever leaves his home as an emigrant unto Allah and His Messenger, and death overtakes him, his reward is then surely incumbent upon Allah..." (V.<qref chapter="4" verses="100">4:100</qref>)
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ مَنْ يُصْرَعُ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ فَمَاتَ فَهُوَ مِنْهُمْ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {وَمَنْ يَخْرُجْ مِنْ بَيْتِهِ مُهَاجِرًا إِلَى اللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ ثُمَّ يُدْرِكْهُ الْمَوْتُ فَقَدْ وَقَعَ أَجْرُهُ عَلَى اللَّهِ} وَقَعَ وَجَبَ.

chapters.title: (The reward of) the injured in Allah's Cause
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ يُنْكَبُ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ

chapters.title: (The superiority of) the wounded in Allah's Cause
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ يُجْرَحُ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ

chapters.title: The Statement of Allah Aza wa'jal: "Say: Do you wait for us except one of the two best things (martyrdom or victory)?..."
chapters.intro: Battles are always undecided and victory is shared by the opponents in turns.
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {هَلْ تَرَبَّصُونَ بِنَا إِلاَّ إِحْدَى الْحُسْنَيَيْنِ}
chapters.intro_arabic: وَالْحَرْبُ سِجَالٌ

chapters.title: The Statement of Allah Aza wa'jal: "Among the believers are men who have been true to their covenant with Allah..."
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ رِجَالٌ صَدَقُوا مَا عَاهَدُوا اللَّهَ عَلَيْهِ فَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ قَضَى نَحْبَهُ وَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ يَنْتَظِرُ وَمَا بَدَّلُوا تَبْدِيلاً}

chapters.title: Practising good deeds before taking part in a battle
chapters.intro:
Abu Ad-Darda said, "Indeed (the result of) your fighting is according to your deeds."

The Statement of Allah عز وجل: "O you who believe! Why do you say that which you do not do? Most hateful it is with Allah that you say that which you do not do. Verily, Allah loves those who fight in His Cause in rows (ranks) as if they were a solid structure" (V.61:2-4).
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ عَمَلٌ صَالِحٌ قَبْلَ الْقِتَالِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ أَبُو الدَّرْدَاءِ إِنَّمَا تُقَاتِلُونَ بِأَعْمَالِكُمْ. وَقَوْلُهُ: {يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لِمَ تَقُولُونَ مَا لاَ تَفْعَلُونَ كَبُرَ مَقْتًا عِنْدَ اللَّهِ أَنْ تَقُولُوا مَا لاَ تَفْعَلُونَ إِنَّ اللَّهَ يُحِبُّ الَّذِينَ يُقَاتِلُونَ فِي سَبِيلِهِ صَفًّا كَأَنَّهُمْ بُنْيَانٌ مَرْصُوصٌ}.

chapters.title: Whoever is killed by an arrow
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ أَتَاهُ سَهْمٌ غَرْبٌ فَقَتَلَهُ

chapters.title: Whoever fights so that Allah's Word be superior
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ قَاتَلَ لِتَكُونَ كَلِمَةُ اللَّهِ هِيَ الْعُلْيَا

chapters.title: Whose feet get covered with dust in Allah's Cause
chapters.intro: And the Statement of Allah تعالى: “It was not becoming of the people of Al-Madina and the bedouins of the neighbourhood to remain behind Allah’s Messenger (Muhammad ﷺ) when fighting in Allah’s Cause… (up to)… Surely, Allah wastes not the reward of Al-Muhsinun (the doers of good)” (V.<qref chapter="9" verses="120">9:120</qref>).
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنِ اغْبَرَّتْ قَدَمَاهُ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {مَا كَانَ لأَهْلِ الْمَدِينَةِ} إِلَى قَوْلِهِ: {إِنَّ اللَّهَ لاَ يُضِيعُ أَجْرَ الْمُحْسِنِينَ}.

chapters.title: The dust which falls on head in Allah's Cause
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَسْحِ الْغُبَارِ عَنِ النَّاسِ، فِي السَّبِيلِ

chapters.title: To take a bath after fighting
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْغَسْلِ بَعْدَ الْحَرْبِ وَالْغُبَارِ

chapters.title: The Statement of Allah Taa'la: "Think not of those who are killed in the Way of Allah as dead..."
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ فَضْلِ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {وَلاَ تَحْسِبَنَّ الَّذِينَ قُتِلُوا فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ أَمْوَاتًا بَلْ أَحْيَاءٌ عِنْدَ رَبِّهِمْ يُرْزَقُونَ فَرِحِينَ بِمَا آتَاهُمُ اللَّهُ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ وَيَسْتَبْشِرُونَ بِالَّذِينَ لَمْ يَلْحَقُوا بِهِمْ مِنْ خَلْفِهِمْ أَنْ لاَ خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلاَ هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ يَسْتَبْشِرُونَ بِنِعْمَةٍ مِنَ اللَّهِ وَفَضْلٍ وَأَنَّ اللَّهَ لاَ يُضِيعُ أَجْرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ}

chapters.title: The shade of angels on the martyr
chapters.title_arabic: باب ظِلِّ الْمَلاَئِكَةِ عَلَى الشَّهِيدِ

chapters.title: The wish of the Mujahid to return to the world
chapters.title_arabic: باب تَمَنِّي الْمُجَاهِدِ أَنْ يَرْجِعَ إِلَى الدُّنْيَا

chapters.title: Paradise is under the blades of swords
chapters.intro: Narrated Al-Mughira bin Shu'ba: Our Prophet told us about the message of our Lord that "Whoever amongst us is killed will go to Paradise." Umar asked the Prophet, "Is it not true that our men who are killed will go to Paradise and their's (i.e. those of the Pagan's) will go to the (Hell) fire?" The Prophet said, "Yes."
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْجَنَّةُ تَحْتَ بَارِقَةِ السُّيُوفِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَقَالَ الْمُغِيرَةُ بْنُ شُعْبَةَ أَخْبَرَنَا نَبِيُّنَا صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ عَنْ رِسَالَةِ رَبِّنَا: «مَنْ قُتِلَ مِنَّا صَارَ إِلَى الْجَنَّةِ».
وَقَالَ عُمَرُ لِلنَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ أَلَيْسَ قَتْلاَنَا فِي الْجَنَّةِ وَقَتْلاَهُمْ فِي النَّارِ قَالَ: «بَلَى».

chapters.title: Who wishes to beget a son to send for Jihad
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ طَلَبَ الْوَلَدَ لِلْجِهَادِ

chapters.title: Bravery and cowardice in the battle
chapters.title_arabic: باب الشَّجَاعَةِ فِي الْحَرْبِ وَالْجُبْنِ

chapters.title: Seeking refuge with Allah from cowardice
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يُتَعَوَّذُ مِنَ الْجُبْنِ

chapters.title: Whoever described what he has witnessed in the war
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ حَدَّثَ بِمَشَاهِدِهِ فِي الْحَرْبِ
chapters.intro_arabic: قَالَهُ أَبُو عُثْمَانَ عَنْ سَعْدٍ.

chapters.title: The obligation of going out for Jihad
chapters.intro:
And the Statement of Allah عز وجل: "March forth, whether you are light (being young, healthy and wealthy) or heavy (being ill, old and poor), and strive hard with your wealth and your lives in the Cause of Allah. This is better for you, if you but knew. Had it been a near gain (booty in front of them) and an easy journey, they would have followed you, but the distance (Tabuk expedition) was long for them, and they would swear by Allah (saying), ‘If we only could, we would certainly have come forth with you.’ They destroy their ownselves, and Allah knows that they are liars." (V.9:41,42)

And His Statement: "O you who believe! What is the matter with you, that when you are asked to march forth in the Cause of Allah, (i.e., Jihad), you cling heavily to the earth? Are you pleased with the life of this world rather than the Hereafter?" (up to) Able to do all things." (V.9:38-39)
chapters.title_arabic: باب وُجُوبِ النَّفِيرِ وَمَا يَجِبُ مِنَ الْجِهَادِ وَالنِّيَّةِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَوْلِهِ: {انْفِرُوا خِفَافًا وَثِقَالاً وَجَاهِدُوا بِأَمْوَالِكُمْ وَأَنْفُسِكُمْ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ ذَلِكُمْ خَيْرٌ لَكُمْ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ لَوْ كَانَ عَرَضًا قَرِيبًا وَسَفَرًا قَاصِدًا لاَتَّبَعُوكَ وَلَكِنْ بَعُدَتْ عَلَيْهِمُ الشُّقَّةُ وَسَيَحْلِفُونَ بِاللَّهِ} الآيَةَ. وَقَوْلِهِ: {يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مَا لَكُمْ إِذَا قِيلَ لَكُمُ انْفِرُوا فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ اثَّاقَلْتُمْ إِلَى الأَرْضِ أَرَضِيتُمْ بِالْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا مِنَ الآخِرَةِ} إِلَى قَوْلِهِ: {عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ}. يُذْكَرُ عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ انْفِرُوا ثُبَاتًا سَرَايَا مُتَفَرِّقِينَ، يُقَالُ أَحَدُ الثُّبَاتِ ثُبَةٌ.

chapters.title: A disbeliever kills a Muslim and later on embraces Islam
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْكَافِرِ يَقْتُلُ الْمُسْلِمَ ثُمَّ يُسْلِمُ فَيُسَدِّدُ بَعْدُ وَيُقْتَلُ

chapters.title: Whoever preferred Jihad to Saum (fasting)
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنِ اخْتَارَ الْغَزْوَ عَلَى الصَّوْمِ

chapters.title: There are seven martyrs other than killed in Jihad
chapters.title_arabic: باب الشَّهَادَةُ سَبْعٌ سِوَى الْقَتْلِ

chapters.title: The Statement of Allah Aza wa'jal: "Not equal are those of the believers who sit (at home),..."
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {لاَ يَسْتَوِي الْقَاعِدُونَ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ غَيْرُ أُولِي الضَّرَرِ وَالْمُجَاهِدُونَ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ بِأَمْوَالِهِمْ وَأَنْفُسِهِمْ فَضَّلَ اللَّهُ الْمُجَاهِدِينَ بِأَمْوَالِهِمْ وَأَنْفُسِهِمْ عَلَى الْقَاعِدِينَ دَرَجَةً وَكُلاًّ وَعَدَ اللَّهُ الْحُسْنَى وَفَضَّلَ اللَّهُ الْمُجَاهِدِينَ عَلَى الْقَاعِدِينَ} إِلَى قَوْلِهِ: {غَفُورًا رَحِيمًا}

chapters.title: Patience during fighting
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّبْرِ عِنْدَ الْقِتَالِ

chapters.title: Rousing and exhorting people to fight
chapters.intro: And the Statement of Allah جل جلاله: "Urge the believers to fight…" (V.<qref chapter="8" verses="65">8:65</qref>)
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّحْرِيضِ عَلَى الْقِتَالِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى: {حَرِّضِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَى الْقِتَالِ}.

chapters.title: The digging of the Khandaq (trench)
chapters.title_arabic: باب حَفْرِ الْخَنْدَقِ

chapters.title: Whoever is held back from Jihad by a legal cause
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ حَبَسَهُ الْعُذْرُ عَنِ الْغَزْوِ

chapters.title: The superiority of observing Saum in Allah's Cause
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ الصَّوْمِ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ

chapters.title: The Superiority of spending in Allah's Cause
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ النَّفَقَةِ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ

chapters.title: The superiority of the one who prepares a Ghazi or looks after his dependents
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ مَنْ جَهَّزَ غَازِيًا أَوْ خَلَفَهُ بِخَيْرٍ

chapters.title: To apply Hanut during the battle
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّحَنُّطِ عِنْدَ الْقِتَالِ

chapters.title: The superiority of the reconnoitrer
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ الطَّلِيعَةِ

chapters.title: Can the reconnoitrer be sent alone?
chapters.title_arabic: باب هَلْ يُبْعَثُ الطَّلِيعَةُ وَحْدَهُ

chapters.title: The travelling of two persons together
chapters.title_arabic: باب سَفَرِ الاِثْنَيْنِ

chapters.title: Good will remain in the forelocks of horses
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْخَيْلُ مَعْقُودٌ فِي نَوَاصِيهَا الْخَيْرُ إِلَى يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ

chapters.title: Jihad is to be carried on whether the Muslim ruler is good or bad
chapters.intro: By virtue of the saying of the Prophet ﷺ, "Good will remain (as a permanent quality) in the forelocks of horses (meant for Jihad), till the Day of Resurrection."
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْجِهَادُ مَاضٍ مَعَ الْبَرِّ وَالْفَاجِرِ
chapters.intro_arabic: لِقَوْلِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «الْخَيْلُ مَعْقُودٌ فِي نَوَاصِيهَا الْخَيْرُ إِلَى يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ».

chapters.title: Keeping a horse for Jihad
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ مَنِ احْتَبَسَ فَرَسًا لِقَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى: {وَمِنْ رِبَاطِ الْخَيْلِ}

chapters.title: To name a horse and a donkey
chapters.title_arabic: باب اسْمِ الْفَرَسِ وَالْحِمَارِ

chapters.title: What has been said about the evil omen of a horse
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يُذْكَرُ مِنْ شُؤْمِ الْفَرَسِ

chapters.title: Horses (are kept) for three (purposes)
chapters.intro: And the Statement of Allah جل جلاله: "And (He has created) horses, mules and donkeys, for you to ride and as an adornment. And He creates (other) things of which you have no knowledge." (V.<qref chapter="16" verses="8">16:8</qref>)
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ الْخَيْلُ لِثَلاَثَةٍ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَوْلُهُ تَعَالَى: {وَالْخَيْلَ وَالْبِغَالَ وَالْحَمِيرَ لِتَرْكَبُوهَا وَزِينَةً}.

chapters.title: Whoever beats somebody else's animal during the battle (intending to help its rider)
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ ضَرَبَ دَابَّةَ غَيْرِهِ فِي الْغَزْوِ

chapters.title: Riding on an unmanageable animal or a stallion horse
chapters.intro: Rashid bin Sa'd said, "The early Muslims preferred to ride stallions, for they were faster and more daring (than mares)."
chapters.title_arabic: باب الرُّكُوبِ عَلَى الدَّابَّةِ الصَّعْبَةِ وَالْفُحُولَةِ مِنَ الْخَيْلِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ رَاشِدُ بْنُ سَعْدٍ كَانَ السَّلَفُ يَسْتَحِبُّونَ الْفُحُولَةَ لأَنَّهَا أَجْرَى وَأَجْسَرُ.

chapters.title: The share of the horse (from the booty)
chapters.title_arabic: باب سِهَامِ الْفَرَسِ

chapters.title: Leading somebody else's animal during the battle
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ قَادَ دَابَّةَ غَيْرِهِ فِي الْحَرْبِ

chapters.title: The saddle and the stirrup of an animal
chapters.title_arabic: باب الرِّكَابِ وَالْغَرْزِ لِلدَّابَّةِ

chapters.title: The riding of an unsaddled horse
chapters.title_arabic: باب رُكُوبِ الْفَرَسِ الْعُرْىِ

chapters.title: A slow horse
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْفَرَسِ الْقَطُوفِ

chapters.title: Horse races
chapters.title_arabic: باب السَّبْقِ بَيْنَ الْخَيْلِ

chapters.title: Idmar (the preparation) of horses for racing
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِضْمَارِ الْخَيْلِ لِلسَّبْقِ

chapters.title: The extreme limit of the race of horses
chapters.title_arabic: باب غَايَةِ السَّبْقِ لِلْخَيْلِ الْمُضَمَّرَةِ

chapters.title: The she-camel of the Prophet saws
chapters.intro: Ibn Umar said, "The Prophet ﷺ made Usama ride behind him on Al-Qaswa (i.e., the Prophet’s she-camel)." Al-Miswar said, "The Prophet ﷺ said, 'Al-Qaswa has not become stubborn.'"
chapters.title_arabic: باب نَاقَةِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم
chapters.intro_arabic:
قَالَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ أَرْدَفَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ أُسَامَةَ عَلَى الْقَصْوَاءِ.
وَقَالَ الْمِسْوَرُ قَالَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «مَا خَلأَتِ الْقَصْوَاءُ».

chapters.title: Going to holy battles on a donkey
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ الْغَزْوِ عَلَى الْحَمِيرِ

chapters.title: The white mule of the Prophet saws
chapters.intro: (Anas referred to this in a narration). Abu Humaid said, "The king of Aila presented a white mule to the Prophet ﷺ."
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَغْلَةِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم الْبَيْضَاءِ
chapters.intro_arabic: قَالَهُ أَنَسٌ وَقَالَ أَبُو حُمَيْدٍ أَهْدَى مَلِكُ أَيْلَةَ لِلنَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ بَغْلَةً بَيْضَاءَ.

chapters.title: The Jihad of women
chapters.title_arabic: باب جِهَادِ النِّسَاءِ

chapters.title: The participation of a woman in a sea battle
chapters.title_arabic: باب غَزْوِ الْمَرْأَةِ فِي الْبَحْرِ

chapters.title: Selection of one wife to accompany in holy battles
chapters.title_arabic: باب حَمْلِ الرَّجُلِ امْرَأَتَهُ فِي الْغَزْوِ دُونَ بَعْضِ نِسَائِهِ

chapters.title: The Jihad of women and their fighting along with men
chapters.title_arabic: باب غَزْوِ النِّسَاءِ وَقِتَالِهِنَّ مَعَ الرِّجَالِ

chapters.title: The carrying of water by the women to the people
chapters.title_arabic: باب حَمْلِ النِّسَاءِ الْقِرَبَ إِلَى النَّاسِ فِي الْغَزْوِ

chapters.title: Treatment of the wounded by the women
chapters.title_arabic: باب مُدَاوَاةِ النِّسَاءِ الْجَرْحَى فِي الْغَزْوِ

chapters.title: The bringing back of the wounded and the killed by the women
chapters.title_arabic: باب رَدِّ النِّسَاءِ الْجَرْحَى وَالْقَتْلَى

chapters.title: Removing the arrow from the body
chapters.title_arabic: باب نَزْعِ السَّهْمِ مِنَ الْبَدَنِ

chapters.title: Vigilance during holy battles in Allah's Cause
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْحِرَاسَةِ فِي الْغَزْوِ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ

chapters.title: The service, during holy battles
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ الْخِدْمَةِ فِي الْغَزْوِ

chapters.title: The superiority of him who carries the luggage of his companions during a journey
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ مَنْ حَمَلَ مَتَاعَ صَاحِبِهِ فِي السَّفَرِ

chapters.title: The superiority of guarding (Muslims from infidels)
chapters.intro: And the Statement of Allah جل جلاله: "O you who believe! Endure and be more patient (than your enemy), and guard your territory by stationing army units permanently at the places from where the enemy can attack you, and fear Allah, so that you may be successful." (V.<qref chapter="3" verses="200">3:200</qref>)
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ فَضْلِ رِبَاطِ يَوْمٍ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اصْبِرُوا وَصَابِرُوا وَرَابِطُوا وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ

chapters.title: Whoever sets off for a holy battle accompanied by a boy-servant
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ غَزَا بِصَبِيٍّ لِلْخِدْمَةِ

chapters.title: To go on a seavoyage
chapters.title_arabic: باب رُكُوبِ الْبَحْرِ

chapters.title: The help of poor and pious men in war
chapters.intro: Narrated Ibn 'Abbas رضي الله عنهما: Abu-Sufyan said to me, "Caesar said to me, 'I asked you whether the wealthy people followed him (i.e., Muhammad ﷺ) or the poor, and you said that the poor. Really, such are the followers of the Messengers.'"
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنِ اسْتَعَانَ بِالضُّعَفَاءِ وَالصَّالِحِينَ فِي الْحَرْبِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ أَخْبَرَنِي أَبُو سُفْيَانَ قَالَ لِي قَيْصَرُ سَأَلْتُكَ أَشْرَافُ النَّاسِ اتَّبَعُوهُ أَمْ ضُعَفَاؤُهُمْ فَزَعَمْتَ ضُعَفَاؤُهُمْ وَهُمْ أَتْبَاعُ الرُّسُلِ.

chapters.title: Do not say that so-and-so is a martyr
chapters.intro: Narrated Abu Hurairah that the Prophet ﷺ said, "Allah knows him who fights in His Cause, and Allah knows him who gets wounded in His Cause".
chapters.title_arabic: باب لاَ يَقُولُ فُلاَنٌ شَهِيدٌ
chapters.intro_arabic: قَالَ أَبُو هُرَيْرَةَ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «اللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ بِمَنْ يُجَاهِدُ فِي سَبِيلِهِ، اللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ بِمَنْ يُكْلَمُ فِي سَبِيلِهِ».

chapters.title: Exhortation to archery (i.e., arrow throwing)
chapters.intro: And the Statement of Allah عز وجل: "And make ready against them all you can of power, including steeds of war (tanks, planes, missiles, artillery etc.) to threaten the enemy of Allah and your enemy…" (V.<qref chapter="8" verses="60">8:60</qref>)
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّحْرِيضِ عَلَى الرَّمْىِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {وَأَعِدُّوا لَهُمْ مَا اسْتَطَعْتُمْ مِنْ قُوَّةٍ وَمِنْ رِبَاطِ الْخَيْلِ تُرْهِبُونَ بِهِ عَدُوَّ اللَّهِ وَعَدُوَّكُمْ}.

chapters.title: To play with spears and other similar arms
chapters.title_arabic: باب اللَّهْوِ بِالْحِرَابِ وَنَحْوِهَا

chapters.title: The shield, and shielding oneself with the shield of his companion
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْمِجَنِّ وَمَنْ يَتَتَرَّسُ بِتُرْسِ صَاحِبِهِ

chapters.title: The (leather) shield
chapters.title_arabic: باب الدَّرَقِ

chapters.title: The straps for suspending sword and the hanging of the sword by the neck
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْحَمَائِلِ وَتَعْلِيقِ السَّيْفِ بِالْعُنُقِ

chapters.title: The decoration of swords (with gold and silver etc.)
chapters.title_arabic: باب حِلْيَةِ السُّيُوفِ

chapters.title: Whoever hung his sword on a tree at midday nap
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ عَلَّقَ سَيْفَهُ بِالشَّجَرِ فِي السَّفَرِ عِنْدَ الْقَائِلَةِ

chapters.title: The wearing of a helmet
chapters.title_arabic: باب لُبْسِ الْبَيْضَةِ

chapters.title: To break the weapons and to slaughter the animals of the deceased
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ لَمْ يَرَ كَسْرَ السِّلاَحِ عِنْدَ الْمَوْتِ

chapters.title: The dispersing og the people away from the Imam to rest in the shade of trees
chapters.title_arabic: باب تَفَرُّقِ النَّاسِ عَنِ الإِمَامِ، عِنْدَ الْقَائِلَةِ، وَالاِسْتِظْلاَلِ بِالشَّجَرِ

chapters.title: What is said regarding spears
chapters.intro: Narrated Ibn 'Umar that the Prophet ﷺ said, "My livelihood is under the shade of my spear, and he who disobeys my orders will be humiliated by paying Jizya."
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا قِيلَ فِي الرِّمَاحِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَيُذْكَرُ عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «جُعِلَ رِزْقِي تَحْتَ ظِلِّ رُمْحِي، وَجُعِلَ الذِّلَّةُ وَالصَّغَارُ عَلَى مَنْ خَالَفَ أَمْرِي».

chapters.title: The armour of the Prophet saws
chapters.intro: The Prophet ﷺ said, "As for Khalid, he has kept his armour for Allah's Cause."
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا قِيلَ فِي دِرْعِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم وَالْقَمِيصِ فِي الْحَرْبِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «أَمَّا خَالِدٌ فَقَدِ احْتَبَسَ أَدْرَاعَهُ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ».

chapters.title: The (wearing of a) cloak on journeys and in war
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْجُبَّةِ فِي السَّفَرِ وَالْحَرْبِ

chapters.title: The wearing of silk in war
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْحَرِيرِ فِي الْحَرْبِ

chapters.title: What is said about the knife
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يُذْكَرُ فِي السِّكِّينِ

chapters.title: The fighting against Ar-Rum (the Byzantines)
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا قِيلَ فِي قِتَالِ الرُّومِ

chapters.title: Fighting against the Jews
chapters.title_arabic: باب قِتَالِ الْيَهُودِ

chapters.title: Fighting against the Turks
chapters.title_arabic: باب قِتَالِ التُّرْكِ

chapters.title: Fighting against people wearing shoes made of hair
chapters.title_arabic: باب قِتَالِ الَّذِينَ يَنْتَعِلُونَ الشَّعَرَ

chapters.title: Whoever arranged his companions at the time of defeat, and got down from his riding animal and requested Allah for help
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ صَفَّ أَصْحَابَهُ عِنْدَ الْهَزِيمَةِ وَنَزَلَ عَنْ دَابَّتِهِ، وَاسْتَنْصَرَ

chapters.title: To invoke Allah to defeat and shake Al-Mushrikun
chapters.title_arabic: باب الدُّعَاءِ عَلَى الْمُشْرِكِينَ بِالْهَزِيمَةِ وَالزَّلْزَلَةِ

chapters.title: To preach to the people of the Scriptures, or teach them the Holy Book?
chapters.title_arabic: باب هَلْ يُرْشِدُ الْمُسْلِمُ أَهْلَ الْكِتَابِ أَوْ يُعَلِّمُهُمُ الْكِتَابَ

chapters.title: To invoke Allah to bestow guidance upon Al-Mushrikun
chapters.title_arabic: باب الدُّعَاءِ لِلْمُشْرِكِينَ بِالْهُدَى لِيَتَأَلَّفَهُمْ

chapters.title: (What is said regarding the invitation of the Jews and Christians (to Islam), and for what Muslims should declare war against them and what the Prophet ﷺ wrote to Khusrau and Caesar
chapters.intro: The invitation to Islam is essential before declaring war.
chapters.title_arabic: باب دَعْوَةِ الْيَهُودِيِّ وَالنَّصْرَانِيِّ، وَعَلَى مَا يُقَاتَلُونَ عَلَيْهِ وَمَا كَتَبَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ إِلَى كِسْرَى وَقَيْصَرَ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَالدَّعْوَةِ قَبْلَ الْقِتَالِ.

chapters.title: The invitation of Prophet (Muhammad) ﷺ to the people to embrace Islam, and to believe in his Prophethood and not to take each other as Lords instead of Allah
chapters.intro: The Statement of Allah تعالى: “It is not (possible) for any human being whom Allah has given the Book.” (V.<qref chapter="3" verses="79">3:79</qref>)
chapters.title_arabic: باب دُعَاءِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم إِلَى الإِسْلاَمِ وَالنُّبُوَّةِ، وَأَنْ لاَ يَتَّخِذَ بَعْضُهُمْ بَعْضًا أَرْبَابًا مِنْ دُونِ اللَّهِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى: {مَا كَانَ لِبَشَرٍ أَنْ يُؤْتِيَهُ اللَّهُ} إِلَى آخِرِ الآيَةِ.

chapters.title: Concealing the true destination of a Ghazwa
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ أَرَادَ غَزْوَةً فَوَرَّى بِغَيْرِهَا، وَمَنْ أَحَبَّ الْخُرُوجَ يَوْمَ الْخَمِيسِ

chapters.title: Setting out after midday
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْخُرُوجِ بَعْدَ الظُّهْرِ

chapters.title: Setting out in the last part of the month
chapters.intro: Narrated Ibn ‘Abbas رضي الله عنهما: The Prophet ﷺ set out from Al-Madina five days before the end of Dhul-Qa’da and reached Makkah on the fourth of Dhul-Hijjah.
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْخُرُوجِ آخِرَ الشَّهْرِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ كُرَيْبٌ عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمَا انْطَلَقَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ مِنَ الْمَدِينَةِ لِخَمْسٍ بَقِينَ مِنْ ذِي الْقَعْدَةِ، وَقَدِمَ مَكَّةَ لأَرْبَعِ لَيَالٍ خَلَوْنَ مِنْ ذِي الْحِجَّةِ.

chapters.title: Travelling in Ramadan
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْخُرُوجِ فِي رَمَضَانَ

chapters.title: Bidding farewell
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّوْدِيعِ

chapters.title: Listening to and obeying the Imam
chapters.title_arabic: باب السَّمْعِ وَالطَّاعَةِ لِلإِمَامِ

chapters.title: The Imam should be defended
chapters.title_arabic: باب يُقَاتَلُ مِنْ وَرَاءِ الإِمَامِ وَيُتَّقَى بِهِ

chapters.title: To give a Bai'a for not to flee during a battle
chapters.intro:
Some said, “(To give the Bai‘a pledge) for death,” according to the Statement of Allah تعالى:
“Indeed, Allah was pleased with the believers…” (V.48:18)
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْبَيْعَةِ فِي الْحَرْبِ أَنْ لاَ يَفِرُّوا
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ بَعْضُهُمْ عَلَى الْمَوْتِ لِقَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {لَقَدْ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ إِذْ يُبَايِعُونَكَ تَحْتَ الشَّجَرَةِ}.

chapters.title: The Imam should order the people to do only within their ability
chapters.title_arabic: باب عَزْمِ الإِمَامِ عَلَى النَّاسِ فِيمَا يُطِيقُونَ

chapters.title: Delay of the fighting
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ كَانَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ إِذَا لَمْ يُقَاتِلْ أَوَّلَ النَّهَارِ أَخَّرَ الْقِتَالَ حَتَّى تَزُولَ الشَّمْسُ

chapters.title: Asking the permission of the Imam
chapters.intro: As Allah’s Statement indicates: "The true believers are only those who believe in (the Oneness of) Allah and His Messenger (Muhammad ﷺ), and when they are with him on some common matter, they do not go away unless they have asked his permission. Verily! Those who ask your permission.. (to the end of verse)." (V.<qref chapter="24" verses="62">24:62</qref>)
chapters.title_arabic: باب اسْتِئْذَانِ الرَّجُلِ الإِمَامَ
chapters.intro_arabic: لِقَوْلِهِ: {إِنَّمَا الْمُؤْمِنُونَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا بِاللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ وَإِذَا كَانُوا مَعَهُ عَلَى أَمْرٍ جَامِعٍ لَمْ يَذْهَبُوا حَتَّى يَسْتَأْذِنُوهُ إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَسْتَأْذِنُونَكَ} إِلَى آخِرِ الآيَةِ.

chapters.title: Participation in the Jihad by the recently married
chapters.intro: Jabir narrated a Hadith from the Prophet ﷺ related to this chapter.
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ مَنْ غَزَا وَهُوَ حَدِيثُ عَهْدٍ بِعُرْسِهِ
chapters.intro_arabic: فِيهِ جَابِرٌ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ.

chapters.title: Participation in Jihad after the consummation of marriage
chapters.intro: Abu Hurairah narrated a Hadith from the Prophet ﷺ related to this chapter.
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ مَنِ اخْتَارَ الْغَزْوَ بَعْدَ الْبِنَاءِ
chapters.intro_arabic: فِيهِ أَبُو هُرَيْرَةَ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ.

chapters.title: The setting out of the Imam, before the people at the time of fright
chapters.title_arabic: باب مُبَادَرَةِ الإِمَامِ عِنْدَ الْفَزَعِ

chapters.title: To be quick at the time of fright
chapters.title_arabic: باب السُّرْعَةِ وَالرَّكْضِ فِي الْفَزَعِ

chapters.title: Setting out alone at a time of fright
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ الْخُرُوجِ فِي الْفَزَعِ وَحْدَهُ

chapters.title: The wages given to fight on somebody else's behalf
chapters.intro:
Mujahid said, "Once I said to Ibn 'Umar, 'Let us proceed for Jihad.' Ibn 'Umar replied, 'I would like to support you with some of my money.' I replied, 'Allah has given me enough.' He said, 'Your wealth is for you, but I like that some of my money be spent in this cause.'"

'Umar said, "Some people take money (from the Muslim’s Treasury) to strive in Allah’s Cause, but they don’t strive. So, if someone does so, we have the right to take back whatever he has taken."

Tawus and Mujahid said, "If something is given to you, so that you may strive in Allah’s Cause, then do whatever you like with it and keep it with your family."
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْجَعَائِلِ وَالْحُمْلاَنِ فِي السَّبِيلِ

chapters.title: The labourer
chapters.intro: Al-Hasan and Ibn Sirin state that a labourer should be given a share from the war booty. Atiyya bin Qais hired a horse for half of its share (of the war booty). The share of the horse amounted to four hundred Dinar, so he retained two hundred and gave two hundred to the owner of the horse.
chapters.title_arabic: باب الأَجِيرِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ الْحَسَنُ وَابْنُ سِيرِينَ يُقْسَمُ لِلأَجِيرِ مِنَ الْمَغْنَمِ. وَأَخَذَ عَطِيَّةُ بْنُ قَيْسٍ فَرَسًا عَلَى النِّصْفِ، فَبَلَغَ سَهْمُ الْفَرَسِ أَرْبَعَمِائَةِ دِينَارٍ، فَأَخَذَ مِائَتَيْنِ وَأَعْطَى صَاحِبَهُ مِائَتَيْنِ.

chapters.title: The flag of the Prophet saws
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا قِيلَ فِي لِوَاءِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم

chapters.title: "I have been made victorious..."
chapters.intro: The Statement of Allah جل جلاله: "We shall cast terror into the hearts of those who disbelieve…" (V.<qref chapter="3" verses="151">3:151</qref>)
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «نُصِرْتُ بِالرُّعْبِ مَسِيرَةَ شَهْرٍ»
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَوْلِهِ جَلَّ وَعَزَّ: {سَنُلْقِي فِي قُلُوبِ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا الرُّعْبَ بِمَا أَشْرَكُوا بِاللَّهِ}. قَالَ جَابِرٌ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ.

chapters.title: Providing oneself with food
chapters.intro: The Statement of Allah ﷻ: "…And take a provision (with you) for the journey, but the best provision is At-Taqwa (piety, righteousness)." (V.<qref chapter="2" verses="197">2:197</qref>)
chapters.title_arabic: باب حَمْلِ الزَّادِ فِي الْغَزْوِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {وَتَزَوَّدُوا فَإِنَّ خَيْرَ الزَّادِ التَّقْوَى}.

chapters.title: To carry the journey-food on one's shoulder
chapters.title_arabic: باب حَمْلِ الزَّادِ عَلَى الرِّقَابِ

chapters.title: The sitting of a woman behind her brother
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِرْدَافِ الْمَرْأَةِ خَلْفَ أَخِيهَا

chapters.title: The sitting of two men together over a riding animal
chapters.title_arabic: باب الاِرْتِدَافِ فِي الْغَزْوِ وَالْحَجِّ

chapters.title: The sitting of two men together on a donkey
chapters.title_arabic: باب الرِّدْفِ عَلَى الْحِمَارِ

chapters.title: Holding the riding animal of somebody else
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ أَخَذَ بِالرِّكَابِ وَنَحْوِهِ

chapters.title: Not to travel to a hostile country carrying copies of the Qur'an
chapters.intro: Ibn 'Umar said, "No doubt, the Prophet ﷺ and his companions travelled in the land of the enemy and they knew the Qur'an then."
chapters.title_arabic: باب السَّفَرِ بِالْمَصَاحِفِ إِلَى أَرْضِ الْعَدُوِّ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَكَذَلِكَ يُرْوَى عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ بِشْرٍ عَنْ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ نَافِعٍ عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ. وَتَابَعَهُ ابْنُ إِسْحَاقَ عَنْ نَافِعٍ عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ. وَقَدْ سَافَرَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ وَأَصْحَابُهُ فِي أَرْضِ الْعَدُوِّ وَهُمْ يَعْلَمُونَ الْقُرْآنَ.

chapters.title: The recitation of Takbir (Allahu Akbar) in the war
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّكْبِيرِ عِنْدَ الْحَرْبِ

chapters.title: Raising the voice when saying Takbir
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يُكْرَهُ مِنْ رَفْعِ الصَّوْتِ فِي التَّكْبِيرِ

chapters.title: Reciting Subhan Allah when going down a valley
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّسْبِيحِ إِذَا هَبَطَ وَادِيًا

chapters.title: To say Takbir on ascending a high place
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّكْبِيرِ إِذَا عَلاَ شَرَفًا

chapters.title: A traveller is granted reward
chapters.title_arabic: باب يُكْتَبُ لِلْمُسَافِرِ مِثْلُ مَا كَانَ يَعْمَلُ فِي الإِقَامَةِ

chapters.title: Travelling alone
chapters.title_arabic: باب السَّيْرِ وَحْدَهُ

chapters.title: Hastening in travel
chapters.intro: Narrated Abu Humaid: The Prophet ﷺ said, "I am in a hurry to reach Al-Madina; so whoever wants to hurry up with me, should hurry up".
chapters.title_arabic: باب السُّرْعَةِ فِي السَّيْرِ
chapters.intro_arabic: قَالَ أَبُو حُمَيْدٍ قَالَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «إِنِّي مُتَعَجِّلٌ إِلَى الْمَدِينَةِ، فَمَنْ أَرَادَ أَنْ يَتَعَجَّلَ مَعِي فَلْيُعَجِّلْ».

chapters.title: If someone gives his horse for Allah's Cause and then he sees it being sold
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا حَمَلَ عَلَى فَرَسٍ فَرَآهَا تُبَاعُ

chapters.title: Participation in Jihad with parent's permission
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْجِهَادِ بِإِذْنِ الأَبَوَيْنِ

chapters.title: Hanging of bells round the necks of camels
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا قِيلَ فِي الْجَرَسِ وَنَحْوِهِ فِي أَعْنَاقِ الإِبِلِ

chapters.title: If a man has enlisted himself in the army and then his wife goes out for Hajj
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنِ اكْتُتِبَ فِي جَيْشٍ فَخَرَجَتِ امْرَأَتُهُ حَاجَّةً وَكَانَ لَهُ عُذْرٌ هَلْ يُؤْذَنُ لَهُ

chapters.title: The spy
chapters.intro: Spying means secret investigations. The Statement of Allah عز وجل: "Take not My enemy and your enemy (i.e., disbelievers and polytheists, etc.) as friends..." (V.<qref chapter="60" verses="1">60:1</qref>)
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْجَاسُوسِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {لاَ تَتَّخِذُوا عَدُوِّي وَعَدُوَّكُمْ أَوْلِيَاءَ} التَّجَسُّسُ التَّبَحُّثُ.

chapters.title: Providing the prisoners of war with clothes
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْكِسْوَةِ لِلأُسَارَى

chapters.title: The superiority of the one through whom a man embraces Islam
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ مَنْ أَسْلَمَ عَلَى يَدَيْهِ رَجُلٌ

chapters.title: The prisoners of war in chains
chapters.title_arabic: باب الأُسَارَى فِي السَّلاَسِلِ

chapters.title: The superiority of the people of the Scriptures (Jews and Christians) who embrace Islam
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ مَنْ أَسْلَمَ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابَيْنِ

chapters.title: Probability of killing the babies and children
chapters.title_arabic: باب أَهْلِ الدَّارِ يُبَيَّتُونَ فَيُصَابُ الْوِلْدَانُ وَالذَّرَارِيُّ
chapters.intro_arabic: {بَيَاتًا} لَيْلاً {لَنُبَيِّتَنَّهُ} لَيْلاً، يُبَيِّتُ لَيْلاً.

chapters.title: Killing the children in the war
chapters.title_arabic: باب قَتْلِ الصِّبْيَانِ فِي الْحَرْبِ

chapters.title: Killing the women in the war
chapters.title_arabic: باب قَتْلِ النِّسَاءِ فِي الْحَرْبِ

chapters.title: Not to punish with Allah's punishment
chapters.title_arabic: باب لاَ يُعَذَّبُ بِعَذَابِ اللَّهِ

chapters.title: (Allah's Statement): "... Thereafter (is the time) either for generosity or ransom (according to what benefits Islam)...
chapters.intro: And the Statement of Allah عز وجل: "It is not for a Prophet that he should have prisoners of war (and free them with ransom) until he had made a great slaughter (among his enemies) in the land. You desire the good of this world (i.e., money of ransom for freeing the captives)." (V.<qref chapter="8" verses="67">8:67</qref>)
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ: {فَإِمَّا مَنًّا بَعْدُ وَإِمَّا فِدَاءً}
chapters.intro_arabic: فِيهِ حَدِيثُ ثُمَامَةَ، وَقَوْلُهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ: {مَا كَانَ لِنَبِيٍّ أَنْ تَكُونَ لَهُ أَسْرَى} الآيَةَ.

chapters.title: Is it legal for a Muslim captive to kill or deceive those who have captured him so that he may save himself from the infidels?
chapters.intro: Al-Miswar narrated a Hadith from the Prophet ﷺ concerning this issue.
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ هَلْ لِلأَسِيرِ أَنْ يَقْتُلَ وَيَخْدَعَ الَّذِينَ أَسَرُوهُ حَتَّى يَنْجُوَ مِنَ الْكَفَرَةِ
chapters.intro_arabic: فِيهِ الْمِسْوَرُ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ.

chapters.title: If a Mushrik burns a Muslim, should he be burnt (in retaliation)?
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا حَرَّقَ الْمُشْرِكُ الْمُسْلِمَ هَلْ يُحَرَّقُ

chapters.title: Chapter:
chapters.title_arabic: باب

chapters.title: The burning of houses and date-palms
chapters.title_arabic: باب حَرْقِ الدُّورِ وَالنَّخِيلِ

chapters.title: Killing a sleeping Mushrik
chapters.title_arabic: باب قَتْلِ النَّائِمِ الْمُشْرِكِ

chapters.title: Do not wish to meet the enemy
chapters.title_arabic: باب لاَ تَمَنَّوْا لِقَاءَ الْعَدُوِّ

chapters.title: War is deceit
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْحَرْبُ خَدْعَةٌ

chapters.title: Telling lies in war
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْكَذِبِ فِي الْحَرْبِ

chapters.title: Killing non-Muslim warriors secretly
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْفَتْكِ بِأَهْلِ الْحَرْبِ

chapters.title: What tricks and means of security may be adopted
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يَجُوزُ مِنَ الاِحْتِيَالِ وَالْحَذَرِ مَعَ مَنْ يَخْشَى مَعَرَّتَهُ

chapters.title: The recitation of poetic verses in the war
chapters.title_arabic: باب الرَّجَزِ فِي الْحَرْبِ وَرَفْعِ الصَّوْتِ فِي حَفْرِ الْخَنْدَقِ
chapters.intro_arabic: فِيهِ سَهْلٌ وَأَنَسٌ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ وَفِيهِ يَزِيدُ عَنْ سَلَمَةَ.

chapters.title: Whosoever is unable to sit firm on a horse
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ لاَ يَثْبُتُ عَلَى الْخَيْلِ

chapters.title: The treatment of a wound with the ashes of a mat (made of date-palm leaves), and the washing of blood by a lady off her father's face, and conveying water in a shield (for this purpose)
chapters.title_arabic: باب دَوَاءِ الْجُرْحِ بِإِحْرَاقِ الْحَصِيرِ وَغَسْلِ الْمَرْأَةِ عَنْ أَبِيهَا الدَّمَ عَنْ وَجْهِهِ، وَحَمْلِ الْمَاءِ فِي التُّرْسِ

chapters.title: What quarrels and differences are hated in the war
chapters.intro: And Allah said: '…And do not dispute (with one another) lest you lose courage and your strength departs…' (V.<qref chapter="8" verses="46">8:46</qref>)
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يُكْرَهُ مِنَ التَّنَازُعِ وَالاِخْتِلاَفِ فِي الْحَرْبِ وَعُقُوبَةِ مَنْ عَصَى إِمَامَهُ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى: {وَلاَ تَنَازَعُوا فَتَفْشَلُوا وَتَذْهَبَ رِيحُكُمْ}. قَالَ قَتَادَةُ الرِّيحُ الْحَرْبُ.

chapters.title: If the people get frightened at night
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا فَزِعُوا بِاللَّيْلِ

chapters.title: Shouting: "Ya Sabahah!"
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ رَأَى الْعَدُوَّ فَنَادَى بِأَعْلَى صَوْتِهِ يَا صَبَاحَاهْ. حَتَّى يُسْمِعَ النَّاسَ

chapters.title: Saying: "Take it! I am the son of so-and-so."
chapters.intro: And Salama said, "Take it! I am the son of Al-Akwa'."
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ قَالَ خُذْهَا وَأَنَا ابْنُ فُلاَنٍ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ سَلَمَةُ خُذْهَا وَأَنَا ابْنُ الأَكْوَعِ.

chapters.title: If the enemy is ready to accept the judgement of a Muslim
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا نَزَلَ الْعَدُوُّ عَلَى حُكْمِ رَجُلٍ

chapters.title: The killing of a captive
chapters.title_arabic: باب قَتْلِ الأَسِيرِ وَقَتْلِ الصَّبْرِ

chapters.title: The performance of a two Rak'a Salat before being put to death
chapters.title_arabic: باب هَلْ يَسْتَأْسِرُ الرَّجُلُ وَمَنْ لَمْ يَسْتَأْسِرْ، وَمَنْ رَكَعَ رَكْعَتَيْنِ عِنْدَ الْقَتْلِ

chapters.title: The freeing of a captive
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَكَاكِ الأَسِيرِ

chapters.title: The ransom of Al-Mushrikun
chapters.title_arabic: باب فِدَاءِ الْمُشْرِكِينَ

chapters.title: If an infidel warrior comes in an Islamic territory
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْحَرْبِيِّ إِذَا دَخَلَ دَارَ الإِسْلاَمِ بِغَيْرِ أَمَانٍ

chapters.title: One should fight for the protection of the Dhimmi
chapters.title_arabic: باب يُقَاتَلُ عَنْ أَهْلِ الذِّمَّةِ، وَلاَ يُسْتَرَقُّونَ

chapters.title: The presents given to the foreign delegates
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ جَوَائِزِ الْوَفْدِ

chapters.title: Can one intercede for the Dhimmi or deal with them?
chapters.title_arabic: باب هَلْ يُسْتَشْفَعُ إِلَى أَهْلِ الذِّمَّةِ وَمُعَامَلَتِهِمْ

chapters.title: Sprucing oneself up before receiving a delegation
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّجَمُّلِ لِلْوُفُودِ

chapters.title: How to present Islam to a (non-Muslim) boy
chapters.title_arabic: باب كَيْفَ يُعْرَضُ الإِسْلاَمُ عَلَى الصَّبِيِّ

chapters.title: The saying of the Prophet saws to the Jews, "Embrace Islam and you will be safe."
chapters.intro: This is narrated by Abu Hurairah.
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ لِلْيَهُودِ: «أَسْلِمُوا تَسْلَمُوا»
chapters.intro_arabic: قَالَهُ الْمَقْبُرِيُّ عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ.

chapters.title: If some people in a hostile non-Muslim country embrace Islam and they have possessions
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا أَسْلَمَ قَوْمٌ فِي دَارِ الْحَرْبِ، وَلَهُمْ مَالٌ وَأَرَضُونَ، فَهْىَ لَهُمْ

chapters.title: The listing of the people by the Imam
chapters.title_arabic: باب كِتَابَةِ الإِمَامِ النَّاسَ

chapters.title: Allah may supprt the religion with a Fajir
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِنَّ اللَّهَ يُؤَيِّدُ الدِّينَ بِالرَّجُلِ الْفَاجِرِ

chapters.title: To take over the leadership of the army during a battle when there is danger
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ تَأَمَّرَ فِي الْحَرْبِ مِنْ غَيْرِ إِمْرَةٍ إِذَا خَافَ الْعَدُوَّ

chapters.title: Supporting with reinforcements
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْعَوْنِ بِالْمَدَدِ

chapters.title: Staying in the (enemy) town for three (days)
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ غَلَبَ الْعَدُوَّ فَأَقَامَ عَلَى عَرْصَتِهِمْ ثَلاَثًا

chapters.title: The distribution of the war booty after a Ghazwa
chapters.intro: Narrated Rafi': We were in the company of the Prophet ﷺ at Dhul-Hulaifa and we got some camels and sheep. He distributed them, considering ten sheep as equal to one camel.
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ قَسَمَ الْغَنِيمَةَ فِي غَزْوِهِ وَسَفَرِهِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ رَافِعٌ كُنَّا مَعَ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ بِذِي الْحُلَيْفَةِ، فَأَصَبْنَا غَنَمًا وَإِبِلاً، فَعَدَلَ عَشَرَةً مِنَ الْغَنَمِ بِبَعِيرٍ.

chapters.title: If Al-Mushrikun take the property of a Muslim as war booty
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا غَنِمَ الْمُشْرِكُونَ مَالَ الْمُسْلِمِ ثُمَّ وَجَدَهُ الْمُسْلِمُ

chapters.title: Speaking with an unfamiliar accent
chapters.intro:
The Statement of Allah عز وجل: "And difference of your languages and colours..." (V.30:22)

And also His Statement: "And We sent not a Messenger except with the language of his people..." (V.14:4)
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ تَكَلَّمَ بِالْفَارِسِيَّةِ وَالرَّطَانَةِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى: {وَاخْتِلاَفُ أَلْسِنَتِكُمْ وَأَلْوَانِكُمْ}، {وَمَا أَرْسَلْنَا مِنْ رَسُولٍ إِلاَّ بِلِسَانِ قَوْمِهِ}.

chapters.title: Al-Ghulul (stealing from the war booty)
chapters.intro: And the Statement of Allah عز وجل: "...And whosoever deceives his companions as regard booty, he shall bring forth on the Day of Resurrection that which he took (illegally)..." (V.<qref chapter="3" verses="161">3:161</qref>)
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ الْغُلُولِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {وَمَنْ يَغْلُلْ يَأْتِ بِمَا غَلَّ}.

chapters.title: A little Ghulul
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْقَلِيلِ مِنَ الْغُلُولِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَلَمْ يَذْكُرْ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ عَمْرٍو عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ أَنَّهُ حَرَّقَ مَتَاعَهُ، وَهَذَا أَصَحُّ.

chapters.title: Not to slaughter the camels and sheep of the booty (before distribution)
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يُكْرَهُ مِنْ ذَبْحِ الإِبِلِ وَالْغَنَمِ فِي الْمَغَانِمِ

chapters.title: The conveyance of the good tidings of victories
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْبِشَارَةِ فِي الْفُتُوحِ

chapters.title: What may be given to the bringer of glad tidings
chapters.intro: Ka‘b bin Malik gave two garments to the person who brought the glad tidings of the acceptance of his repentance.
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ مَا يُعْطَى الْبَشِيرُ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَأَعْطَى كَعْبُ بْنُ مَالِكٍ ثَوْبَيْنِ حِينَ بُشِّرَ بِالتَّوْبَةِ.

chapters.title: There is no emigration after the Conquest
chapters.title_arabic: باب لاَ هِجْرَةَ بَعْدَ الْفَتْحِ

chapters.title: To search the Dhimmi women if there is necessity
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ إِذَا اضْطَرَّ الرَّجُلُ إِلَى النَّظَرِ فِي شُعُورِ أَهْلِ الذِّمَّةِ وَالْمُؤْمِنَاتِ إِذَا عَصَيْنَ اللَّهَ وَتَجْرِيدِهِنَّ

chapters.title: The reception of Muslim fighters after Jihad
chapters.title_arabic: باب اسْتِقْبَالِ الْغُزَاةِ

chapters.title: What to say on returning from Jihad
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يَقُولُ إِذَا رَجَعَ مِنَ الْغَزْوِ

chapters.title: The performance of Salat on returning from a journey
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّلاَةِ إِذَا قَدِمَ مِنْ سَفَرٍ

chapters.title: Taking meals on arrival (from a journey)
chapters.intro: Ibn ‘Umar used to present meals to the one who used to visit him (on returning from a journey).
chapters.title_arabic: باب الطَّعَامِ عِنْدَ الْقُدُومِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَكَانَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ يُفْطِرُ لِمَنْ يَغْشَاهُ.

chapters.title: The obligations of Khumus
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَرْضِ الْخُمُسِ

chapters.title: The payment of Khumus is a part of religion
chapters.title_arabic: باب أَدَاءُ الْخُمُسِ مِنَ الدِّينِ

chapters.title: The expenditure of the wives of the Prophet saws
chapters.title_arabic: باب نَفَقَةِ نِسَاءِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم بَعْدَ وَفَاتِهِ

chapters.title: What has been said regarding the houses of the wives of the Prophet ﷺ and that which were named after them of the houses (e.g., ‘Aishah’s house)
chapters.intro:
And the Statement of Allah ﷻ: “And stay in your houses, (O wives of the Prophet)…” (V.33:33)

And also: “Enter not the Prophet’s houses, unless permission is given to you…” (V.33:53)
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا جَاءَ فِي بُيُوتِ أَزْوَاجِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم، وَمَا نُسِبَ مِنَ الْبُيُوتِ إِلَيْهِنَّ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {وَقَرْنَ فِي بُيُوتِكُنَّ} وَ{لاَ تَدْخُلُوا بُيُوتَ النَّبِيِّ إِلاَّ أَنْ يُؤْذَنَ لَكُمْ}.

chapters.title: What has been said regarding the armour of the Prophet ﷺ, his staff, sword, cup and ring, and what the caliphs after him used of these things which had not been distributed, and also (what is said) about his hair, shoes and utensils which were considered as blessed things by his companions and others after his death
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا ذُكِرَ مِنْ دِرْعِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم وَعَصَاهُ وَسَيْفِهِ وَقَدَحِهِ وَخَاتَمِهِ وَمَا اسْتَعْمَلَ الْخُلَفَاءُ بَعْدَهُ مِنْ ذَلِكَ مِمَّا لَمْ يُذْكَرْ قِسْمَتُهُ، وَمِنْ شَعَرِهِ وَنَعْلِهِ وَآنِيَتِهِ، مِمَّا يَتَبَرَّكُ أَصْحَابُهُ وَغَيْرُهُمْ بَعْدَ وَفَاتِهِ

chapters.title: The evidence that confirms that the Khumus (i.e., one-fifth of the war booty) is meant for the needs of Allah's Messenger ﷺ and the poor
chapters.intro: And the fact that Allah's Messenger ﷺ favoured the people of As-Suffa and the widows in this matter, for when Fatima complained of suffering from grinding and the hand-mill and requested him to provide her with a slave-girl (from the booty), he (did not give her a slave-girl) but told her to ask for Allah's Aid.
chapters.title_arabic: باب الدَّلِيلِ عَلَى أَنَّ الْخُمُسَ لِنَوَائِبِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صلى الله عليه وسلم وَالْمَسَاكِينِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَإِيثَارِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ أَهْلَ الصُّفَّةِ وَالأَرَامِلَ حِينَ سَأَلَتْهُ فَاطِمَةُ وَشَكَتْ إِلَيْهِ الطَّحْنَ وَالرَّحَى أَنْ يُخْدِمَهَا مِنَ السَّبْيِ، فَوَكَلَهَا إِلَى اللَّهِ.

chapters.title: The Statement of Allah تعالى: "Verily one-fifth (1/5th) of it is assigned to Allah and to the Messenger ﷺ ..." (V.<qref chapter="8" verses="41">8:41</qref>)
chapters.intro: It means that Allah's Messenger ﷺ had the authority of distributing it (i.e., the fifth share), for Allah's Messenger ﷺ said, "I am a distributor and a treasurer, and Allah is the Giver."
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {فَأَنَّ لِلَّهِ خُمُسَهُ}
chapters.intro_arabic: يَعْنِي لِلرَّسُولِ قَسْمَ ذَلِكَ. قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «إِنَّمَا أَنَا قَاسِمٌ وَخَازِنٌ، وَاللَّهُ يُعْطِي».

chapters.title: The statement of the Prophet ﷺ: "Booty has been made legal for you Muslims."
chapters.intro:
And the Statement of Allah عز وجل: "Allah has promised you abundant spoils that you will capture..." (V.48:20)

So, the booty is for all the Muslim fighters who took part in the battle till Allah's Messenger ﷺ explained (the details) of those who deserved or not.
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «أُحِلَّتْ لَكُمُ الْغَنَائِمُ»
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى: {وَعَدَكُمُ اللَّهُ مَغَانِمَ كَثِيرَةً تَأْخُذُونَهَا فَعَجَّلَ لَكُمْ هَذِهِ} وَهْيَ لِلْعَامَّةِ حَتَّى يُبَيِّنَهُ الرَّسُولُ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ.

chapters.title: The war booty is for those who witness the battles
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْغَنِيمَةُ لِمَنْ شَهِدَ الْوَقْعَةَ

chapters.title: If somebody fights for the sake of boty
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ قَاتَلَ لِلْمَغْنَمِ هَلْ يَنْقُصُ مِنْ أَجْرِه

chapters.title: The share of those who are nor present at the time (of distribution)
chapters.title_arabic: باب قِسْمَةِ الإِمَامِ مَا يَقْدَمُ عَلَيْهِ، وَيَخْبَأُ لِمَنْ لَمْ يَحْضُرْهُ أَوْ غَابَ عَنْهُ

chapters.title: How the Prophet saws distributed the properties of Bani Quraiza and Bani An-Nadir
chapters.title_arabic: باب كَيْفَ قَسَمَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم قُرَيْظَةَ وَالنَّضِيرَ، وَمَا أَعْطَى مِنْ ذَلِكَ فِي نَوَائِبِهِ

chapters.title: Blessed is the wealth of a living or a dead Ghazi
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَرَكَةِ الْغَازِي فِي مَالِهِ حَيًّا وَمَيِّتًا مَعَ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم وَوُلاَةِ الأَمْرِ

chapters.title: If the Imam sends some messenger to carry out a certain duty, or orders one to stay at home, will he be given a share from the booty?
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا بَعَثَ الإِمَامُ رَسُولاً فِي حَاجَةٍ أَوْ أَمَرَهُ بِالْمُقَامِ هَلْ يُسْهَمُ لَهُ

chapters.title: Khumus is to be used for the needs of the Muslims
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ وَمِنَ الدَّلِيلِ عَلَى أَنَّ الْخُمُسَ لِنَوَائِبِ الْمُسْلِمِينَ مَا سَأَلَ هَوَازِنُ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ بِرَضَاعِهِ فِيهِمْ فَتَحَلَّلَ مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ

chapters.title: The free emancipation of the captives by the Prophet saws without taking out the Khumus from the booty
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا مَنَّ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم عَلَى الأُسَارَى مِنْ غَيْرِ أَنْ يُخَمَّسَ

chapters.title: The proof of the fact that Khumus is for the Imam (i.e., ruler), and that he has the right to give thereof to some of his relatives to the exclusion of others. What the Prophet ﷺ distributed to Bani Al-Muttalib and Bani Hashim from the Khumus of the Khaibar booty.
chapters.intro: 'Umar bin 'Abdul 'Aziz said, "The Prophet ﷺ did not give all of them (i.e., his relatives) in general, and he did not give to a near relative if there was a needy relative of a remoter relation. He would give the latter because of what they complained to him about their needs, and because of what they had suffered from these people (i.e., Quraish) and their allies for his sake."'Umar bin 'Abdul 'Aziz said, "The Prophet ﷺ did not give all of them (i.e., his relatives) in general, and he did not give to a near relative if there was a needy relative of a remoter relation. He would give the latter because of what they complained to him about their needs, and because of what they had suffered from these people (i.e., Quraish) and their allies for his sake."
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ وَمِنَ الدَّلِيلِ عَلَى أَنَّ الْخُمُسَ لِلإِمَامِ وَأَنَّهُ يُعْطِي بَعْضَ قَرَابَتِهِ دُونَ بَعْضٍ مَا قَسَمَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ لِبَنِي الْمُطَّلِبِ وَبَنِي هَاشِمٍ مِنْ خُمُسِ خَيْبَرَ
chapters.intro_arabic: قَالَ عُمَرُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ لَمْ يَعُمَّهُمْ بِذَلِكَ، وَلَمْ يَخُصَّ قَرِيبًا دُونَ مَنْ أَحْوَجُ إِلَيْهِ، وَإِنْ كَانَ الَّذِي أَعْطَى لِمَا يَشْكُو إِلَيْهِ مِنَ الْحَاجَةِ، وَلِمَا مَسَّتْهُمْ فِي جَنْبِهِ، مِنْ قَوْمِهِمْ وَحُلَفَائِهِمْ.

chapters.title: Not taking the Khumus from the spoils of a killed infidel
chapters.intro: And he who kills an infidel will possess his belongings without giving the Khumus, and what is the verdict of the Imam in this respect.
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ مَنْ لَمْ يُخَمِّسِ الأَسْلاَبَ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَمَنْ قَتَلَ قَتِيلاً فَلَهُ سَلَبُهُ مِنْ غَيْرِ أَنْ يُخَمِّسَ، وَحُكْمِ الإِمَامِ فِيهِ.

chapters.title: What the Prophet ﷺ used to give to those Muslims whose faith was not so firm, and to other Muslims, from the Khumus or other resources
chapters.intro: This has been said by 'Abdullah bin Zaid from the Prophet ﷺ.
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ مَا كَانَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يُعْطِي الْمُؤَلَّفَةَ قُلُوبُهُمْ وَغَيْرَهُمْ مِنَ الْخُمُسِ وَنَحْوِهِ
chapters.intro_arabic: رَوَاهُ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ زَيْدٍ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ.

chapters.title: The food gained as war booty in the battlefield
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يُصِيبُ مِنَ الطَّعَامِ فِي أَرْضِ الْحَرْبِ

chapters.title: Al-Jizya taken from the Dhimmi
chapters.intro:
And the Statement of Allah عز و جل:
"Fight against those who (1) believe not in Allah (2) nor in the Last Day, (3) nor forbid that which has been forbidden by Allah and His Messenger (Muhammad ﷺ), (4) and those who acknowledge not the religion of truth (i.e. Islam) among the people of Scripture (Jews and Christians), until they pay the Jizya with willing submission, and feel themselves subdued." (V. 9:29)

And what has been said regarding the taking of Jizya from the Jews, Christians, Magians and non-Arab infidels.

Narrated Ibn Abi Najih: I asked Mujahid, "Why are the Syrians charged four Dinars as Jizya while the Yemenites are charged one Dinar only?" Mujahid replied, "This (Jizya) has been fixed on the basis of the degree of prosperity."
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْجِزْيَةِ وَالْمُوَادَعَةِ مَعَ أَهْلِ الْحَرْبِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَقَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {قَاتِلُوا الَّذِينَ لاَ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللَّهِ وَلاَ بِالْيَوْمِ الآخِرِ وَلاَ يُحَرِّمُونَ مَا حَرَّمَ اللَّهُ وَرَسُولُهُ وَلاَ يَدِينُونَ دِينَ الْحَقِّ مِنَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتَابَ حَتَّى يُعْطُوا الْجِزْيَةَ عَنْ يَدٍ وَهُمْ صَاغِرُونَ} أَذِلاَّءُ. وَمَا جَاءَ فِي أَخْذِ الْجِزْيَةِ مِنَ الْيَهُودِ وَالنَّصَارَى وَالْمَجُوسِ وَالْعَجَمِ.
وَقَالَ ابْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي نَجِيحٍ قُلْتُ لِمُجَاهِدٍ مَا شَأْنُ أَهْلِ الشَّأْمِ، عَلَيْهِمْ أَرْبَعَةُ دَنَانِيرَ وَأَهْلُ الْيَمَنِ عَلَيْهِمْ دِينَارٌ قَالَ جُعِلَ ذَلِكَ مِنْ قِبَلِ الْيَسَارِ.

chapters.title: Truce with the king of a country
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا وَادَعَ الإِمَامُ مَلِكَ الْقَرْيَةِ هَلْ يَكُونُ ذَلِكَ لِبَقِيَّتِهِمْ

chapters.title: The advice to take care of non-Muslims who have a covenant of Allah's Messenger saws
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ الْوَصَايَا بِأَهْلِ ذِمَّةِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَالذِّمَّةُ الْعَهْدُ، وَالإِلُّ الْقَرَابَةُ.

chapters.title: To whom should the Fai and the Jizya be distributed?
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ مَا أَقْطَعَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ مِنَ الْبَحْرَيْنِ، وَمَا وَعَدَ مِنْ مَالِ الْبَحْرَيْنِ وَالْجِزْيَةِ، وَلِمَنْ يُقْسَمُ الْفَيْءُ وَالْجِزْيَةُ؟

chapters.title: The sin of one who kills an innocent person having a treaty with the Muslims
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِثْمِ مَنْ قَتَلَ مُعَاهَدًا بِغَيْرِ جُرْمٍ

chapters.title: The expelling of the Jews from the Arabian Peninsula
chapters.intro: Narrated 'Umar that the Prophet ﷺ said (to the Jews), "We shall keep you here as long as Allah keeps you here."
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِخْرَاجِ الْيَهُودِ مِنْ جَزِيرَةِ الْعَرَبِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ عُمَرُ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «أُقِرُّكُمْ مَا أَقَرَّكُمُ اللَّهُ بِهِ».

chapters.title: If Al-Mushrikun prove tracherous to the Muslims, may they be forgiven?
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا غَدَرَ الْمُشْرِكُونَ بِالْمُسْلِمِينَ هَلْ يُعْفَى عَنْهُمْ

chapters.title: The invocation of the Imam against those who break their covenant (with the Muslims)
chapters.title_arabic: باب دُعَاءِ الإِمَامِ عَلَى مَنْ نَكَثَ عَهْدًا

chapters.title: The offering of shelter and peace by women
chapters.title_arabic: باب أَمَانِ النِّسَاءِ وَجِوَارِهِنَّ

chapters.title: The asylum and protection granted by the Muslims should be respected and observed
chapters.title_arabic: باب ذِمَّةُ الْمُسْلِمِينَ وَجِوَارُهُمْ وَاحِدَةٌ يَسْعَى بِهَا أَدْنَاهُمْ

chapters.title: If non-Muslims (in war) say: "Saba'na'" and could not say "Aslamna'"
chapters.intro: And Ibn 'Umar said, "Khalid started killing such infidels (thinking that they should have said *Aslamna* to be safe). The Prophet ﷺ said, 'O Allah! I am free from what Khalid has done.' 'Umar said, 'If one says to another *Matras*, he is granting him security thereby. Allah knows all the tongues.' 'Umar said (to Al-Hurmuzan, a Persian leader), 'Speak, there is no harm.' (And that was taken as a sign of granting security.)
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ إِذَا قَالُوا صَبَأْنَا وَلَمْ يُحْسِنُوا أَسْلَمْنَا
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَقَالَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ فَجَعَلَ خَالِدٌ يَقْتُلُ فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «أَبْرَأُ إِلَيْكَ مِمَّا صَنَعَ خَالِدٌ».
وَقَالَ عُمَرُ إِذَا قَالَ مَتْرَسْ. فَقَدْ آمَنَهُ، إِنَّ اللَّهَ يَعْلَمُ الأَلْسِنَةَ كُلَّهَا.
وَقَالَ تَكَلَّمْ لاَ بَأْسَ.

chapters.title: Making peace with Al-Mushrikun and the reconciliation with them
chapters.intro: And Allah’s Statement: “But if they (enemy) incline toward peace...” (V.<qref chapter="8" verses="61">8:61</qref>)
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ الْمُوَادَعَةِ وَالْمُصَالَحَةِ مَعَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ بِالْمَالِ وَغَيْرِهِ، وَإِثْمِ مَنْ لَمْ يَفِ بِالْعَهْدِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَوْلِهِ: {وَإِنْ جَنَحُوا لِلسَّلْمِ فَاجْنَحْ لَهَا} الآيَةَ.

chapters.title: The superiority of fulfilling one's covenant
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ الْوَفَاءِ بِالْعَهْدِ

chapters.title: If a Dhimmi practises magic, can he be excused?
chapters.intro: Ibn Shihab was asked, "If one of those with whom Muslims have made a covenant bewitches people, will he be sentenced to death?" He replied, "We have been informed that Allah's Messenger ﷺ was bewitched, yet he did not kill the magician who was from the people of the Scriptures."
chapters.title_arabic: باب هَلْ يُعْفَى عَنِ الذِّمِّيِّ إِذَا سَحَرَ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَالَ ابْنُ وَهْبٍ أَخْبَرَنِي يُونُسُ عَنِ ابْنِ شِهَابٍ سُئِلَ أَعَلَى مَنْ سَحَرَ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْعَهْدِ قَتْلٌ قَالَ بَلَغَنَا أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ قَدْ صُنِعَ لَهُ ذَلِكَ، فَلَمْ يَقْتُلْ مَنْ صَنَعَهُ، وَكَانَ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ.

chapters.title: Caution against treachery
chapters.intro: And the Statement of Allah تعالى: "And if they intend to deceive you, then verily, Allah is All-Sufficient for you..." (V.<qref chapter="8" verses="62">8:62</qref>)
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ مَا يُحْذَرُ مِنَ الْغَدْرِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى: {وَإِنْ يُرِيدُوا أَنْ يَخْدَعُوكَ فَإِنَّ حَسْبَكَ اللَّهُ} الآيَةَ.

chapters.title: How to revoke a covenant
chapters.intro: And the Statement of Allah عزّ وجلّ: "If you (O Muhammad ﷺ) fear treachery from any people, throw back (their covenant) to them (so as to be) on equal terms (that there will be no more covenant between you and them)." (V.<qref chapter="8" verses="58">8:58</qref>)
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ كَيْفَ يُنْبَذُ إِلَى أَهْلِ الْعَهْدِ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَوْلُهُ: {وَإِمَّا تَخَافَنَّ مِنْ قَوْمٍ خِيَانَةً فَانْبِذْ إِلَيْهِمْ عَلَى سَوَاءٍ} الآيَةَ.

chapters.title: The sin of a person who makes a covenant and then proves treacherous
chapters.intro:
And the Statement of Allah جل جلاله:
"They are those with whom you made a covenant, but they break their covenant every time, and they do not fear Allah." (V.8:56)
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ إِثْمِ مَنْ عَاهَدَ ثُمَّ غَدَرَ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَوْلِهِ: {الَّذِينَ عَاهَدْتَ مِنْهُمْ ثُمَّ يَنْقُضُونَ عَهْدَهُمْ فِي كُلِّ مَرَّةٍ وَهُمْ لاَ يَتَّقُونَ}.

chapters.title: Chapter:
chapters.title_arabic: باب

chapters.title: It is permissible to conclude a peace treaty of three days or any other fixed period
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْمُصَالَحَةِ عَلَى ثَلاَثَةِ أَيَّامٍ، أَوْ وَقْتٍ مَعْلُومٍ

chapters.title: To make peace treaty without a limited period
chapters.intro: As the Prophet ﷺ told (the Khaibar Jews), "We will keep you as long as Allah will keep you."
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ الْمُوَادَعَةِ مِنْ غَيْرِ وَقْتٍ
chapters.intro_arabic: وَقَوْلِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «أُقِرُّكُمْ مَا أَقَرَّكُمُ اللَّهُ بِهِ».

chapters.title: The throwing of the dead bodies of Al-Mushrikun
chapters.title_arabic: باب طَرْحِ جِيَفِ الْمُشْرِكِينَ فِي الْبِئْرِ وَلاَ يُؤْخَذُ لَهُمْ ثَمَنٌ

chapters.title: The sin of a betrayer
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِثْمِ الْغَادِرِ لِلْبَرِّ وَالْفَاجِرِ

chapters.title: The Statement of Allah Taa'la: "And He it is Who originates the creation; then will repeat it and this is easier for Him..."
chapters.title_arabic: بَابُ مَا جَاءَ فِي قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {وَهُوَ الَّذِي يَبْدَأُ الْخَلْقَ ثُمَّ يُعِيدُهُ}
chapters.intro_arabic:
قَالَ الرَّبِيعُ بْنُ خُثَيْمٍ وَالْحَسَنُ كُلٌّ عَلَيْهِ هَيِّنٌ. هَيْنٌ وَهَيِّنٌ مِثْلُ لَيْنٍ وَلَيِّنٍ، وَمَيْتٍ وَمَيِّتٍ، وَضَيْقٍ وَضَيِّقٍ.
{أَفَعَيِينَا} أَفَأَعْيَا عَلَيْنَا حِينَ أَنْشَأَكُمْ وَأَنْشَأَ خَلْقَكُمْ، لُغُوبٌ النَّصَبُ.
{أَطْوَارًا} طَوْرًا كَذَا، وَطَوْرًا كَذَا، عَدَا طَوْرَهُ أَيْ قَدْرَهُ.

chapters.title: What has been said regarding the seven earths
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا جَاءَ فِي سَبْعِ أَرَضِينَ
chapters.intro_arabic:
وَقَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {اللَّهُ الَّذِي خَلَقَ سَبْعَ سَمَوَاتٍ وَمِنَ الأَرْضِ مِثْلَهُنَّ يَتَنَزَّلُ الأَمْرُ بَيْنَهُنَّ لِتَعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللَّهَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ وَأَنَّ اللَّهَ قَدْ أَحَاطَ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عِلْمًا}، {وَالسَّقْفِ الْمَرْفُوعِ} السَّمَاءُ.
{سَمْكَهَا} بِنَاءَهَا، كَانَ فِيهَا حَيَوَانٌ. الْحُبُكُ اسْتِوَاؤُهَا وَحُسْنُهَا {وَأَذِنَتْ} سَمِعَتْ وَأَطَاعَتْ.
{وَأَلْقَتْ} أَخْرَجَتْ مَا فِيهَا مِنَ الْمَوْتَى، {وَتَخَلَّتْ} عَنْهُمْ.
{طَحَاهَا} دَحَاهَا. السَّاهِرَةُ وَجْهُ الأَرْضِ، كَانَ فِيهَا الْحَيَوَانُ نَوْمُهُمْ وَسَهَرُهُمْ.

chapters.title: (About the) Stars
chapters.title_arabic: بَابٌ في النُّجُومِ
chapters.intro_arabic:
